kubectl get podtemplates -l service=location --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAME AGE anihpj-location-template 7m
Example 5: List All JobPost Templates

List all PodTemplates for the entire JobPost application.

# List all anihpj templates
kubectl get podtemplates -l app=anihpj
NAME AGE anihpj-skills-template 10m anihpj-user-auth-template 8m anihpj-author-content-template 9m anihpj-location-template 7m anihpj-jobpost-api-template 6m

Create PodTemplate

Create a new PodTemplate from specification to define reusable pod configurations for Django application components.

Syntax
kubectl create -f podtemplate.yaml
kubectl apply -f podtemplate.yaml
Parameters
  • body (PodTemplate, required): Complete PodTemplate specification
  • namespace (string, required): Target namespace
  • dryRun (string): Validation mode without creation
  • fieldManager (string): Field management identifier
Return Value
  • 201 (PodTemplate): Created - PodTemplate successfully created
  • 202 (PodTemplate): Accepted - Creation in progress
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Service Examples
Example 1: Create Skills Service Template

Create a new PodTemplate for Skills service with basic configuration.

# Create Skills service PodTemplate
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: PodTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-v2-template
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills
    version: v2
template:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: skills
      version: v2
  spec:
    containers:
    - name: skills-container
      image: anihpj/jobpost-skills:v2.0.0
      ports:
      - containerPort: 8000
EOF
podtemplate/anihpj-skills-v2-template created
Example 2: Create User Auth Template with Security

Create User authentication template with security context.

# Create User auth template with security
kubectl create -f user-auth-secure-template.yaml
podtemplate/anihpj-user-auth-secure-template created
Example 3: Create Author Content Template with Volumes

Create Author content template with persistent volume configuration.

# Create Author content template
kubectl apply -f author-content-pv-template.yaml
podtemplate/anihpj-author-content-pv-template created
Example 4: Create Location Template with Resources

Create Location service template with resource limits and requests.

# Create Location template with resources
kubectl create podtemplate anihpj-location-optimized-template --image=anihpj/jobpost-location:v1.1.0 --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
podtemplate/anihpj-location-optimized-template created
Example 5: Create JobPost API Production Template

Create production-ready JobPost API template with full configuration.

# Create production JobPost API template
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: PodTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-api-prod-template
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost-api
    env: production
template:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: jobpost-api
      env: production
  spec:
    containers:
    - name: jobpost-api
      image: anihpj/jobpost-api:v3.0.0
      ports:
      - containerPort: 8000
      resources:
        requests:
          memory: "512Mi"
          cpu: "500m"
        limits:
          memory: "1Gi"
          cpu: "1000m"
      env:
      - name: DJANGO_ENV
        value: "production"
EOF
podtemplate/anihpj-jobpost-api-prod-template created

Update PodTemplate

Replace the specified PodTemplate with updated configuration to modify template specifications for Django applications.

Syntax
kubectl replace -f updated-podtemplate.yaml
kubectl apply -f updated-podtemplate.yaml
Parameters
  • body (PodTemplate, required): Complete updated PodTemplate specification
  • name (string, required): Name of the PodTemplate to update
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • dryRun (string): Validation mode without update
Return Value
  • 200 (PodTemplate): OK - PodTemplate successfully updated
  • 201 (PodTemplate): Created - PodTemplate was created
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Service Examples
Example 1: Update Skills Template Image

Update Skills service template with new container image version.

# Update Skills template image
kubectl get podtemplate anihpj-skills-template -o yaml > skills-template-updated.yaml
# Edit image version in the file
sed -i 's/jobpost-skills:v1.0.0/jobpost-skills:v1.1.0/' skills-template-updated.yaml
kubectl replace -f skills-template-updated.yaml
podtemplate/anihpj-skills-template replaced
Example 2: Update User Auth Template Labels

Update User authentication template with additional labels.

# Update User auth template labels
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: PodTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-auth-template
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: user-auth
    tier: security
    version: v1.1
template:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: user-auth
      tier: security
      version: v1.1
  spec:
    containers:
    - name: user-auth-container
      image: anihpj/jobpost-user-auth:v1.1.0
      ports:
      - containerPort: 8080
EOF
podtemplate/anihpj-user-auth-template configured
Example 3: Update Author Template Resources

Update Author content template with modified resource requirements.

# Update Author template resources
kubectl replace -f updated-author-template.yaml
podtemplate/anihpj-author-content-template replaced
Example 4: Update Location Template Environment

Update Location service template with new environment variables.

# Update Location template environment
kubectl apply -f location-template-env-updated.yaml
podtemplate/anihpj-location-template configured
Example 5: Update JobPost API Template Configuration

Update JobPost API template with comprehensive configuration changes.

# Update JobPost API template
kubectl replace -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: PodTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-api-template
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost-api
    tier: backend
    version: v2.1
template:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: jobpost-api
      tier: backend
      version: v2.1
  spec:
    containers:
    - name: jobpost-api
      image: anihpj/jobpost-api:v2.1.0
      ports:
      - containerPort: 8000
      resources:
        requests:
          memory: "512Mi"
          cpu: "500m"
        limits:
          memory: "1Gi"
          cpu: "1000m"
EOF
podtemplate/anihpj-jobpost-api-template replaced

Patch PodTemplate

Partially update the specified PodTemplate with patch data to modify specific fields without replacing the entire template.

Syntax
kubectl patch podtemplate {name} -p '{patch-data}'
kubectl patch podtemplate {name} --type='json' -p='[patch-operations]'
Parameters
  • body (Patch, required): Patch data in specified format
  • name (string, required): Name of the PodTemplate to patch
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • dryRun (string): Validation mode without patching
Return Value
  • 200 (PodTemplate): OK - PodTemplate successfully patched
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Service Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills Template Image

Patch Skills service template to update container image version.

# Patch Skills template image
kubectl patch podtemplate anihpj-skills-template -p '{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"skills-container","image":"anihpj/jobpost-skills:v1.2.0"}]}}}'
podtemplate/anihpj-skills-template patched
Example 2: Patch User Auth Template Labels

Add new labels to User authentication template using JSON patch.

# Patch User auth template labels
kubectl patch podtemplate anihpj-user-auth-template --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/metadata/labels/environment", "value": "production"}]'
podtemplate/anihpj-user-auth-template patched
Example 3: Patch Author Template Resources

Update Author content template resource limits using strategic merge.

# Patch Author template resources
kubectl patch podtemplate anihpj-author-content-template -p '{
  "template": {
    "spec": {
      "containers": [{
        "name": "author-container",
        "resources": {
          "limits": {
            "memory": "768Mi",
            "cpu": "750m"
          }
        }
      }]
    }
  }
}'
podtemplate/anihpj-author-content-template patched
Example 4: Patch Location Template Environment

Add environment variables to Location service template.

# Patch Location template environment
kubectl patch podtemplate anihpj-location-template --type='json' -p='[
  {"op": "add", "path": "/template/spec/containers/0/env", "value": [
    {"name": "CACHE_ENABLED", "value": "true"},
    {"name": "LOG_LEVEL", "value": "INFO"}
  ]}
]'
podtemplate/anihpj-location-template patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost API Template Ports

Add additional port configuration to JobPost API template.

# Patch JobPost API template ports
kubectl patch podtemplate anihpj-jobpost-api-template -p '{
  "template": {
    "spec": {
      "containers": [{
        "name": "jobpost-api",
        "ports": [
          {"containerPort": 8000, "name": "http"},
          {"containerPort": 8080, "name": "metrics"}
        ]
      }]
    }
  }
}'
podtemplate/anihpj-jobpost-api-template patched

Delete PodTemplate

Delete the specified PodTemplate to remove template definitions that are no longer needed for Django applications.

Syntax
kubectl delete podtemplate {name}
kubectl delete -f podtemplate.yaml
Parameters
  • name (string, required): Name of the PodTemplate to delete
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
  • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
Return Value
  • 200 (PodTemplate): OK - PodTemplate successfully deleted
  • 202 (PodTemplate): Accepted - Deletion in progress
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Service Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills Template

Delete obsolete Skills service PodTemplate.

# Delete Skills template
kubectl delete podtemplate anihpj-skills-template
podtemplate "anihpj-skills-template" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Auth Template with Confirmation

Delete User authentication template with dry-run verification.

# Verify deletion with dry-run first
kubectl delete podtemplate anihpj-user-auth-template --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete podtemplate anihpj-user-auth-template
podtemplate "anihpj-user-auth-template" deleted (dry run) podtemplate "anihpj-user-auth-template" deleted
Example 3: Delete Author Template by File

Delete Author content template using YAML file.

# Delete Author template by file
kubectl delete -f author-content-template.yaml
podtemplate "anihpj-author-content-template" deleted
Example 4: Delete Location Template with Grace Period

Delete Location service template with custom grace period.

# Delete Location template with grace period
kubectl delete podtemplate anihpj-location-template --grace-period=30
podtemplate "anihpj-location-template" deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost API Template with Wait

Delete JobPost API template and wait for completion.

# Delete JobPost API template with wait
kubectl delete podtemplate anihpj-jobpost-api-template --wait=true
# Verify deletion
kubectl get podtemplate anihpj-jobpost-api-template
podtemplate "anihpj-jobpost-api-template" deleted Error from server (NotFound): podtemplates "anihpj-jobpost-api-template" not found

Delete Collection of PodTemplates

Delete multiple PodTemplates based on label selectors to clean up related template resources for Django applications.

Syntax
kubectl delete podtemplates -l {label-selector}
kubectl delete podtemplates --all
Parameters
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • labelSelector (string): Filter templates by labels
  • fieldSelector (string): Filter templates by fields
  • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
  • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK - Collection deletion successful
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Service Examples
Example 1: Delete All Skills Templates

Delete all PodTemplates related to Skills service.

# Delete all Skills templates
kubectl delete podtemplates -l service=skills
podtemplate "anihpj-skills-template" deleted podtemplate "anihpj-skills-v2-template" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Auth Templates by Version

Delete User authentication templates for specific version.

# Delete User auth templates by version
kubectl delete podtemplates -l service=user-auth,version=v1
podtemplate "anihpj-user-auth-template" deleted
Example 3: Delete Author Content Dev Templates

Delete Author content templates in development environment.

# Delete Author dev templates
kubectl delete podtemplates -l service=author-content,env=development
podtemplate "anihpj-author-content-dev-template" deleted
Example 4: Delete Location Templates with Dry Run

Preview Location service template deletion before execution.

# Preview Location template deletion
kubectl delete podtemplates -l service=location --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete podtemplates -l service=location
podtemplate "anihpj-location-template" deleted (dry run) podtemplate "anihpj-location-optimized-template" deleted (dry run) podtemplate "anihpj-location-template" deleted podtemplate "anihpj-location-optimized-template" deleted
Example 5: Delete All JobPost Application Templates

Delete all PodTemplates for the entire JobPost application.

# Delete all anihpj templates
kubectl delete podtemplates -l app=anihpj
# Verify deletion
kubectl get podtemplates -l app=anihpj
podtemplate "anihpj-user-auth-secure-template" deleted podtemplate "anihpj-author-content-pv-template" deleted podtemplate "anihpj-jobpost-api-prod-template" deleted No resources found in default namespace.

ReplicationController

ReplicationController Overview

ReplicationControllerSpec - Desired state specification
ReplicationControllerStatus - Current observed status
ReplicationControllerCondition - State condition details
ReplicationControllerList - Collection of controllers
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: ReplicationController v1 API | API Version: v1

ReplicationController represents the configuration of a replication controller that ensures a specified number of pod replicas are running at any given time.

Full Description

ReplicationController is one of the oldest workload resources in Kubernetes, designed to ensure that a specified number of identical pods are running at all times. When deploying your anihpj Django application, a ReplicationController will monitor your Django pods and automatically create new ones if any fail or are deleted, ensuring high availability for your jobpost application.

While ReplicationController has largely been superseded by Deployment (which provides additional features like rolling updates), it's still fundamental to understand as it forms the basis for more advanced workload resources. In production Django deployments, you'll typically use Deployments, but ReplicationController is perfect for understanding basic pod replication concepts.

Syntax

# Create a ReplicationController from YAML file
kubectl create -f replicationcontroller.yaml
# List all ReplicationControllers (long form)
kubectl get replicationcontrollers
# List all ReplicationControllers (short form)
kubectl get rc
# Get detailed information about a specific ReplicationController
kubectl describe replicationcontroller {name}
# Get detailed information about a specific ReplicationController (short form)
kubectl describe rc {name}
# Scale ReplicationController to specified number of replicas
kubectl scale replicationcontroller {name} --replicas={count}
# Scale ReplicationController to specified number of replicas (short form)
kubectl scale rc {name} --replicas={count}
# Delete a specific ReplicationController
kubectl delete replicationcontroller {name}
# Delete a specific ReplicationController (short form)
kubectl delete rc {name}

YAML Structure

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-django-rc
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: web
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: web
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-web
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: Always "v1" for ReplicationController. This indicates the API version being used.
  • kind: Must be "ReplicationController" to specify the resource type.
  • metadata.name: Unique name for the ReplicationController within the namespace. Use descriptive names like "anihpj-django-rc".
  • metadata.namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the ReplicationController will be created. Defaults to "default".
  • metadata.labels: Key-value pairs for organizing and selecting ReplicationControllers. Useful for grouping Django app components.
  • spec.replicas: Desired number of pod replicas. For Django apps, start with 2-3 for high availability.
  • spec.selector: Label selector to identify which pods belong to this ReplicationController. Must match template labels.
  • spec.template: Pod template specification that defines how new pods should be created.
  • spec.template.metadata.labels: Labels applied to created pods. Must include selector labels.
  • spec.template.spec: Pod specification including containers, volumes, and other pod-level configurations.
  • spec.minReadySeconds: Minimum seconds a pod must be ready before considered available. Useful for Django apps with startup time.

Return Value

Returns the ReplicationController object with its current state, including status information about replicas, ready replicas, and conditions indicating the controller's health.

  • 200 OK: ReplicationController successfully created/updated
  • 201 Created: ReplicationController successfully created
  • 400 Bad Request: Invalid ReplicationController specification
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
  • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
  • 404 Not Found: Namespace not found
  • 409 Conflict: ReplicationController already exists
  • 422 Unprocessable Entity: Invalid field values

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Model Service (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates deploying a ReplicationController for the Skills API service (/jobpost/skills/), providing JSON endpoints for managing available job skills in the anihpj Django project.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: skills
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: skills
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: skills
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-skills
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "skills"
# Apply the Skills model ReplicationController
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc created
Example 2: Author Model Service (jobpost app)

This example shows how to deploy author management services using ReplicationController for the Author model, which connects to Django's built-in User model for authentication.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-author-rc
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: author
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-author
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "user"
        - name: DB_PATH
          value: "/app/db.sqlite3"
# Create the Author model ReplicationController
kubectl create -f anihpj-jobpost-author-rc.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-author-rc created
Example 3: Location Model Service (jobpost app)

This example illustrates deploying location-based services with proper labeling and resource configuration for managing job post locations.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-author-rc
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: author
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-author
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "author"
        - name: CONTENT_CACHE
          value: "redis://redis:6379/1"
# Apply Author model ReplicationController with revision recording
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-author-rc.yaml --record
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-author-rc created
Example 4: JobPost Model Service (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates deploying the core JobPost model service using ReplicationController for handling job posting operations in the anihpj Django project.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-location-rc
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: location
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: location
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: location
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-location
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "location"
        - name: GEO_API_KEY
          value: "your-maps-api-key"
# Dry run to validate Location model ReplicationController YAML
kubectl create -f anihpj-jobpost-location-rc.yaml --dry-run=client -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationController metadata: name: anihpj-jobpost-location-rc namespace: default spec: replicas: 1 selector: app: anihpj component: jobpost service: location
Example 5: JobPost Model Main Service (jobpost app)

This example shows the main JobPost API service deployment (/jobpost/jobs/), handling job listing and detail operations with comprehensive configuration for the anihpj Django project's core functionality.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-main-rc
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: jobpost-main
spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: jobpost-main
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: jobpost-main
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-main
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: ALLOWED_HOSTS
          value: "jobpost.anihpj.com"
# Apply JobPost main service ReplicationController and list all RCs
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-main-rc.yaml && kubectl get rc
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-main-rc created NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-jobpost-main-rc 4 4 4 10s
- name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE value: "anihpj.settings"

kubectl commands:

# Create the ReplicationController
kubectl apply -f anihpj-django-rc.yaml

# Check ReplicationController status
kubectl get rc anihpj-django-rc

# View detailed information
kubectl describe rc anihpj-django-rc
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-django-rc 3 3 3 45s ReplicationController: anihpj-django-rc Namespace: default Selector: app=anihpj-django,component=web Labels: app=anihpj-django,component=web Replicas: 3 current / 3 desired Pods Status: 3 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed
Example 2: Django with Database Connection

This example demonstrates deploying Django applications with SQLite database and proper environment configuration for the anihpj project.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: jobpost
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-django
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DB_PATH
          value: "/app/db.sqlite3"
        - name: DEBUG
          value: "False"

kubectl commands:

# Deploy jobpost Django app
kubectl create -f jobpost-rc.yaml

# Scale the application
kubectl scale rc anihpj-jobpost-rc --replicas=5

# Check pod status
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj,service=jobpost
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-rc created replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-rc scaled NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE anihpj-jobpost-rc-7b2k9 1/1 Running 0 2m15s anihpj-jobpost-rc-j8x4q 1/1 Running 0 2m15s anihpj-jobpost-rc-m3n7p 1/1 Running 0 30s anihpj-jobpost-rc-r9t2w 1/1 Running 0 30s anihpj-jobpost-rc-x5k8f 1/1 Running 0 30s
Example 3: Django with ConfigMap and Secrets

This example shows how to deploy Django with external configuration using ConfigMaps and Secrets for secure credential management.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-secure-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    tier: production
spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    tier: production
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        tier: production
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-secure
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:secure
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        envFrom:
        - configMapRef:
            name: django-config
        - secretRef:
            name: django-secrets
        volumeMounts:
        - name: static-files
          mountPath: /app/staticfiles
      volumes:
      - name: static-files
        emptyDir: {}

kubectl commands:

# First create ConfigMap and Secret
# Create Django configuration map with allowed hosts
kubectl create configmap django-config --from-literal=ALLOWED_HOSTS="*"
# Create Django secrets for sensitive data
kubectl create secret generic django-secrets --from-literal=SECRET_KEY="your-secret-key"

# Deploy secure Django setup
kubectl apply -f secure-django-rc.yaml

# Monitor deployment
kubectl get rc,pods -l tier=production
configmap/django-config created secret/django-secrets created replicationcontroller/anihpj-secure-rc created NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE replicationcontroller/anihpj-secure-rc 4 4 4 1m32s NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE pod/anihpj-secure-rc-4h7j2 1/1 Running 0 1m32s pod/anihpj-secure-rc-8k5m1 1/1 Running 0 1m32s pod/anihpj-secure-rc-n9p6w 1/1 Running 0 1m32s pod/anihpj-secure-rc-q2x8v 1/1 Running 0 1m32s
Example 4: Django with Health Checks and Resource Limits

This example demonstrates deploying Django with proper health checks, resource constraints, and monitoring capabilities for production environments.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-healthy-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    version: v2
spec:
  replicas: 3
  minReadySeconds: 30
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    version: v2
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        version: v2
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-healthy
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5

kubectl commands:

# Deploy with health checks
kubectl apply -f healthy-django-rc.yaml

# Watch pod readiness
kubectl get pods -l version=v2 -w

# Check resource usage
kubectl top pods -l app=anihpj
replicationcontroller/anihpj-healthy-rc created NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE anihpj-healthy-rc-b8m4k 0/1 Running 0 15s anihpj-healthy-rc-d2n7x 0/1 Running 0 15s anihpj-healthy-rc-f5p9q 0/1 Running 0 15s anihpj-healthy-rc-b8m4k 1/1 Running 0 35s anihpj-healthy-rc-d2n7x 1/1 Running 0 35s anihpj-healthy-rc-f5p9q 1/1 Running 0 35s NAME CPU(cores) MEMORY(bytes) anihpj-healthy-rc-b8m4k 128m 245Mi anihpj-healthy-rc-d2n7x 115m 238Mi anihpj-healthy-rc-f5p9q 142m 251Mi
Example 5: Django Background Task Workers

This example illustrates deploying Django background task workers for processing asynchronous jobs and queue operations.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-worker-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: worker
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: worker
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: worker
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-worker
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:worker
        command: ["python", "manage.py", "process_tasks"]
        env:
        - name: WORKER_TYPE
          value: "jobpost_processor"
        - name: REDIS_URL
          value: "redis://redis-service:6379/0"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"

kubectl commands:

# Deploy background workers
kubectl apply -f worker-rc.yaml

# Check worker logs
kubectl logs -l component=worker --tail=20

# Scale workers based on load
kubectl scale rc anihpj-worker-rc --replicas=6
replicationcontroller/anihpj-worker-rc created Processing JobPost ID: 1 - Python Developer Processing JobPost ID: 2 - Frontend Developer Processing JobPost ID: 3 - Full Stack Developer Processing Skills updates for Author: user1 Processing Location updates for: New York Worker anihpj-worker-rc-k7x3m ready for tasks Worker anihpj-worker-rc-m9p2n ready for tasks replicationcontroller/anihpj-worker-rc scaled

ReplicationControllerSpec

ReplicationControllerSpec defines the desired behavior of the replication controller. For Django applications, carefully consider the replica count based on expected traffic, resource requirements, and high availability needs.

Syntax
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: web
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-app
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
  minReadySeconds: 30
Parameters Description
  • selector (map[string]string): Label query over pods that should match the replicas count. If empty, defaults to labels on Pod template. For anihpj Django apps, use labels like app=anihpj, component=web.
  • template (PodTemplateSpec): Defines the pod that will be created if insufficient replicas are detected. Must include restartPolicy: Always for ReplicationController.
  • replicas (int32): Number of desired replicas. For Django production, typically 2-5 replicas for web apps, 1-3 for workers.
  • minReadySeconds (int32): Minimum seconds for a pod to be ready before considered available. Important for Django apps with database connections.
Example 1: Django Web App Spec

This example shows the complete ReplicationController specification for a Django web application with detailed metadata, labels, and container configuration.

spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    tier: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        tier: web
        version: v1.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-web
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: http
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
  minReadySeconds: 45
# Apply the Django web specification
kubectl apply -f django-web-spec.yaml
# Get ReplicationController details in wide format
kubectl get rc -o wide
replicationcontroller/anihpj-web-rc configured NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-web-rc 4 4 4 2m django-web anihpj/django-web:v1.0 app=anihpj,tier=web
Example 2: JobPost API Service Spec

This example demonstrates a ReplicationController spec for the JobPost API service with database connections and environment-specific settings.

spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: jobpost-api
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-api
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DB_PATH
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: db-credentials
              key: url
# Create database credentials secret
kubectl create secret generic db-credentials --from-literal=url="sqlite:///db.sqlite3"
# Apply the JobPost API ReplicationController
kubectl apply -f jobpost-api-spec.yaml
secret/db-credentials created replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-api-rc created
Example 3: Skills API Service Spec

This example shows a ReplicationController spec optimized for the Skills API service with specific resource limits and health check configurations.

spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: skills-api
        version: v1.2.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: skills-api
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: http
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: API_ENDPOINT
          value: "/jobpost/skills/"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /jobpost/skills/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
  minReadySeconds: 15
# Apply the Skills API ReplicationController specification
kubectl apply -f skills-api-spec.yaml
# Verify the deployment
kubectl get rc anihpj-skills-api-rc -o wide
replicationcontroller/anihpj-skills-api-rc created NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-skills-api-rc 3 3 3 45s skills-api anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0 app=anihpj,service=skills-api
Example 4: Author Management Service Spec

This example demonstrates a ReplicationController spec for the Author management service with authentication integration and persistent volume mounts.

spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    service: author-mgmt
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: author-mgmt
        tier: backend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: author-service
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DB_PATH
          value: "/app/data/db.sqlite3"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: sqlite-storage
          mountPath: /app/data
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "150m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "300m"
      volumes:
      - name: sqlite-storage
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: anihpj-sqlite-pvc
  minReadySeconds: 20
# Create persistent volume claim first
kubectl apply -f sqlite-pvc.yaml
# Apply the Author management service spec
kubectl apply -f author-mgmt-spec.yaml
persistentvolumeclaim/anihpj-sqlite-pvc created replicationcontroller/anihpj-author-mgmt-rc created
Example 5: Location Service with Advanced Configuration

This example shows an advanced ReplicationController spec for the Location service with ConfigMap integration, init containers, and service mesh compatibility.

spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
    environment: production
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: location
        environment: production
        version: v2.0.0
      annotations:
        sidecar.istio.io/inject: "true"
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: db-migration
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0
        command: ['python', 'manage.py', 'migrate']
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
      containers:
      - name: location-service
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9090
          name: metrics
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        envFrom:
        - configMapRef:
            name: location-config
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "200Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "400Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
  minReadySeconds: 30
# Create location configuration
kubectl create configmap location-config --from-literal=DEBUG=False --from-literal=LOG_LEVEL=INFO
# Apply the advanced location service spec
kubectl apply -f location-advanced-spec.yaml
configmap/location-config created replicationcontroller/anihpj-location-prod-rc created

ReplicationControllerStatus

ReplicationControllerStatus represents the current status of a replication controller, providing visibility into the health and state of your Django application deployment.

Syntax
status:
  replicas: 3
  readyReplicas: 3
  availableReplicas: 3
  fullyLabeledReplicas: 3
  observedGeneration: 1
  conditions:
  - type: ReplicaFailure
    status: "False"
    lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:30:00Z"
    reason: PodsRunning
    message: "All pods are running successfully"
Parameters Description
  • replicas (int32, required): Most recently observed number of replicas. Monitor this to ensure your Django app maintains desired capacity.
  • availableReplicas (int32): Number of available replicas (ready for at least minReadySeconds). Critical for Django app availability.
  • readyReplicas (int32): Number of ready replicas. Should match desired replicas for healthy Django deployment.
  • fullyLabeledReplicas (int32): Pods with labels matching the pod template labels.
  • conditions ([]ReplicationControllerCondition): Latest observations of the controller's current state.
  • observedGeneration (int64): Generation of the most recently observed replication controller.
Return Value

Returns the ReplicationControllerStatus object with current state information, including actual replica counts, availability status, readiness state, and conditions indicating the controller's operational health for Django application monitoring.

Example 1: Healthy Django App Status

This example shows how to check the status of a healthy Django ReplicationController, displaying replica counts and current state.

# Get status section of the Django RC in YAML format
kubectl get rc anihpj-django-rc -o yaml | grep -A 20 "status:"
status: availableReplicas: 3 fullyLabeledReplicas: 3 observedGeneration: 1 readyReplicas: 3 replicas: 3
# Get detailed description of Django RC
kubectl describe rc anihpj-django-rc
Name: anihpj-django-rc Namespace: default Selector: app=anihpj,component=web Labels: app=anihpj,component=web Replicas: 3 current / 3 desired Pods Status: 3 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulCreate 5m replication-controller Created pod: anihpj-django-rc-abc123
Example 2: Monitoring JobPost Worker Status

This example demonstrates monitoring worker ReplicationController status using JSON formatting for detailed replica and condition information.

# Get worker RC status in JSON format and format it nicely
kubectl get rc anihpj-worker-rc -o jsonpath='{.status}' | python -m json.tool
{ "availableReplicas": 2, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 2, "observedGeneration": 1, "readyReplicas": 2, "replicas": 2 }
# Get worker pods with their labels displayed
kubectl get pods -l component=worker --show-labels
NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE LABELS anihpj-worker-rc-k7x3m 1/1 Running 0 3m app=anihpj,component=worker anihpj-worker-rc-m9p2n 1/1 Running 0 3m app=anihpj,component=worker
Example 3: Skills API Service Status

This example shows how to monitor the Skills API service ReplicationController status with detailed replica information and condition tracking.

# Get Skills API RC status with detailed output
kubectl describe rc anihpj-skills-api-rc
Name: anihpj-skills-api-rc Namespace: default Selector: app=anihpj,service=skills-api Labels: app=anihpj,service=skills-api Annotations: Replicas: 3 current / 3 desired Pods Status: 3 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed Pod Template: Labels: app=anihpj service=skills-api version=v1.2.0 Containers: skills-api: Image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0 Port: 8000/TCP Environment: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings API_ENDPOINT: /jobpost/skills/ Mounts: Volumes: Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulCreate 2m replication-controller Created pod: anihpj-skills-api-rc-abc12 Normal SuccessfulCreate 2m replication-controller Created pod: anihpj-skills-api-rc-def34
# Get Skills API status in JSON format for programmatic access
kubectl get rc anihpj-skills-api-rc -o jsonpath='{.status}' | python -m json.tool
{ "availableReplicas": 3, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 3, "observedGeneration": 2, "readyReplicas": 3, "replicas": 3 }
Example 4: Author Service Status with Conditions

This example demonstrates monitoring Author service ReplicationController status including detailed condition information for troubleshooting.

# Get Author service RC status with conditions
kubectl get rc anihpj-author-mgmt-rc -o yaml | grep -A 30 "status:"
status: availableReplicas: 2 conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:35:00Z" message: ReplicationController has minimum availability. reason: MinimumReplicasAvailable status: "True" type: Available - lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:33:00Z" message: ReplicationController is progressing reason: NewPodsCreated status: "True" type: Progressing fullyLabeledReplicas: 2 observedGeneration: 1 readyReplicas: 2 replicas: 2
# Monitor Author service pods individually
kubectl get pods -l service=author-mgmt -o wide
NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE IP NODE anihpj-author-mgmt-rc-xyz12 1/1 Running 0 5m 10.244.1.4 worker-node-1 anihpj-author-mgmt-rc-uvw34 1/1 Running 0 5m 10.244.2.5 worker-node-2
Example 5: Location Service Production Status

This example shows comprehensive status monitoring for the production Location service including metrics, events, and resource utilization.

# Get comprehensive status of production location service
kubectl describe rc anihpj-location-prod-rc
Name: anihpj-location-prod-rc Namespace: default Selector: app=anihpj,service=location,environment=production Labels: app=anihpj environment=production service=location Annotations: kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: {...} Replicas: 4 current / 4 desired Pods Status: 4 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed Pod Template: Labels: app=anihpj environment=production service=location version=v2.0.0 Annotations: sidecar.istio.io/inject: true Init Containers: db-migration: Image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0 Port: Host Port: Command: python manage.py migrate Containers: location-service: Image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0 Ports: 8000/TCP, 9090/TCP Limits: cpu: 500m memory: 400Mi Requests: cpu: 100m memory: 200Mi Liveness: http-get http://:8000/health/ delay=30s timeout=1s period=10s #success=1 #failure=3 Readiness: http-get http://:8000/health/ delay=10s timeout=1s period=5s #success=1 #failure=3 Environment Variables from: location-config ConfigMap Optional: false Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulCreate 8m replication-controller Created pod: anihpj-location-prod-rc-abc123
# Check resource utilization of location service pods
kubectl top pods -l service=location,environment=production
NAME CPU(cores) MEMORY(bytes) anihpj-location-prod-rc-abc123 45m 180Mi anihpj-location-prod-rc-def456 50m 195Mi anihpj-location-prod-rc-ghi789 43m 175Mi anihpj-location-prod-rc-jkl012 48m 188Mi

ReplicationControllerCondition

ReplicationControllerCondition describes the state of a replication controller at a certain point in time. This provides detailed insights into why your Django application might be experiencing issues.

Syntax
conditions:
- type: "ReplicaFailure"
  status: "True"
  lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:30:00Z"
  reason: "FailedCreate"
  message: "Error creating: pods \"anihpj-skills-rc-\" is forbidden"
- type: "Progressing"
  status: "True"
  lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:25:00Z"
  reason: "NewReplicaSetAvailable"
  message: "ReplicationController has successfully progressed"
Parameters Description
  • type (string, required): Type of replication controller condition. Common types include "ReplicaFailure", "Progressing"
  • status (string, required): Status of the condition, one of "True", "False", "Unknown"
  • lastTransitionTime (Time): The last time the condition transitioned from one status to another
  • reason (string): Machine-readable, UpperCamelCase text indicating the reason for the condition's last transition
  • message (string): Human-readable message indicating details about the transition
Return Value

Conditions are read-only fields populated by the Kubernetes system. They provide status information and are part of the ReplicationController status object.

5 Django Condition Examples
Example 1: Skills Model Healthy Condition (jobpost app)

This example shows how to check the health conditions of the Skills model ReplicationController using formatted JSON output.

# Get conditions of Skills RC in formatted JSON
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}' | python -m json.tool
[ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-09-08T10:30:00Z", "message": "Skills model ReplicationController has successfully progressed", "reason": "NewReplicaSetAvailable", "status": "True", "type": "Progressing" } ]
Example 2: Author Model Replica Failure (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates how to examine ReplicationController conditions when replica failures occur in the Author model service.

# Get conditions section from User RC description
kubectl describe rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc | grep -A 5 "Conditions:"
Conditions: Type Status LastTransitionTime Reason ---- ------ ------------------ ------ ReplicaFailure True 2025-09-08T10:35:00Z FailedCreate Progressing False 2025-09-08T10:35:00Z ProgressDeadlineExceeded
Example 3: Author Model Normal Operation (jobpost app)

This example shows how to monitor normal operating conditions of the Author model ReplicationController using kubectl describe.

# Get conditions section of Author RC in YAML format
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc -o yaml | grep -A 10 "conditions:"
conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:40:00Z" message: "Author model ReplicationController has successfully progressed" reason: "NewReplicaSetAvailable" status: "True" type: "Progressing"
Example 4: Location Model Resource Issues (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates troubleshooting resource-related issues in the Location model ReplicationController using YAML output.

# Get events related to Location RC
kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=anihpj-jobpost-location-rc
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 2m Warning FailedCreate replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-location-rc Error creating: pods "anihpj-jobpost-location-rc-" is forbidden: exceeded quota
Example 5: JobPost Model Scaling Progress (jobpost app)

This example shows how to track scaling progress events for the main JobPost model ReplicationController.

# Watch Progressing condition status for JobPost main RC
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-main-rc -w -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Progressing")].status}'
True True True

ReplicationControllerList

ReplicationControllerList represents a collection of ReplicationController resources. This is the response format when listing multiple controllers for your Django application components across different services or environments.

Syntax
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationControllerList
metadata:
  resourceVersion: "12345"
  selfLink: /api/v1/replicationcontrollers
items:
- apiVersion: v1
  kind: ReplicationController
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-django-rc
    namespace: default
  spec:
    replicas: 3
    selector:
      app: anihpj
      component: web
  status:
    replicas: 3
    readyReplicas: 3
- apiVersion: v1
  kind: ReplicationController
  metadata:
    name: jobpost-worker-rc
    namespace: default
  spec:
    replicas: 2
    selector:
      app: anihpj
      component: worker
  status:
    replicas: 2
    readyReplicas: 2
Parameters Description
  • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "v1" for ReplicationControllerList
  • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ReplicationControllerList"
  • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata containing resourceVersion and continuation token
  • items ([]ReplicationController, required): Array of ReplicationController objects in the list
Return Value

Returns the ReplicationControllerList object containing a collection of ReplicationController resources with list metadata, enabling bulk operations and comprehensive management of multiple Django application controllers across different services and environments.

Example 1: List All Django ReplicationControllers

This example demonstrates how to retrieve and display all ReplicationControllers as a ReplicationControllerList in YAML format.

# Get all ReplicationControllers as YAML ReplicationControllerList
kubectl get rc -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationControllerList metadata: resourceVersion: "98765" items: - apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationController metadata: creationTimestamp: "2025-09-08T10:00:00Z" labels: app: anihpj component: web name: anihpj-django-rc namespace: default resourceVersion: "98745" uid: 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc spec: replicas: 3 selector: app: anihpj component: web template: metadata: labels: app: anihpj component: web spec: containers: - image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 name: django-web ports: - containerPort: 8000 protocol: TCP status: availableReplicas: 3 fullyLabeledReplicas: 3 observedGeneration: 1 readyReplicas: 3 replicas: 3 - apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationController metadata: creationTimestamp: "2025-09-08T10:05:00Z" labels: app: anihpj component: worker name: jobpost-worker-rc namespace: default resourceVersion: "98750" uid: 87654321-4321-4321-4321-cba987654321 spec: replicas: 2 selector: app: anihpj component: worker template: metadata: labels: app: anihpj component: worker spec: containers: - image: anihpj/jobpost-worker:v1.0.0 name: django-worker command: ["python", "manage.py", "process_jobs"] status: availableReplicas: 2 fullyLabeledReplicas: 2 observedGeneration: 1 readyReplicas: 2 replicas: 2
Example 2: Filter ReplicationControllers by Label

This example shows how to filter and list ReplicationControllers based on specific labels using JSON output format.

# Get anihpj app RCs in JSON format
kubectl get rc -l app=anihpj -o json
{ "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "ReplicationControllerList", "metadata": { "resourceVersion": "98765" }, "items": [ { "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "ReplicationController", "metadata": { "name": "anihpj-django-rc", "namespace": "default", "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "component": "web" } }, "spec": { "replicas": 3, "selector": { "app": "anihpj", "component": "web" } }, "status": { "replicas": 3, "readyReplicas": 3, "availableReplicas": 3 } }, { "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "ReplicationController", "metadata": { "name": "jobpost-worker-rc", "namespace": "default", "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "component": "worker" } }, "spec": { "replicas": 2, "selector": { "app": "anihpj", "component": "worker" } }, "status": { "replicas": 2, "readyReplicas": 2, "availableReplicas": 2 } } ] }
Example 3: Skills and Author Services List

This example shows how to list specific ReplicationControllers for Skills and Author services using label selectors with detailed output.

# List Skills and Author service RCs with custom formatting
kubectl get rc -l "service in (skills-api,author-mgmt)" -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,SERVICE:.metadata.labels.service
NAME NAMESPACE REPLICAS READY SERVICE anihpj-skills-api-rc default 3 3 skills-api anihpj-author-mgmt-rc default 2 2 author-mgmt
# Get the same data as ReplicationControllerList in JSON format
kubectl get rc -l "service in (skills-api,author-mgmt)" -o json
{ "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "ReplicationControllerList", "metadata": { "resourceVersion": "12345" }, "items": [ { "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "ReplicationController", "metadata": { "name": "anihpj-skills-api-rc", "namespace": "default", "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "service": "skills-api", "version": "v1.2.0" } }, "spec": { "replicas": 3, "selector": { "app": "anihpj", "service": "skills-api" } }, "status": { "replicas": 3, "readyReplicas": 3, "availableReplicas": 3 } } ] }
Example 4: Production Environment RC List

This example demonstrates listing ReplicationControllers filtered by production environment labels with comprehensive status information.

# List all production environment ReplicationControllers
kubectl get rc -l environment=production -o wide
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-location-prod-rc 4 4 4 15m location-service anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0 app=anihpj,environment=production
# Get production RCs as ReplicationControllerList with status details
kubectl get rc -l environment=production -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status}' | python -m json.tool
[ { "availableReplicas": 4, "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2025-09-08T10:45:00Z", "message": "ReplicationController has minimum availability.", "reason": "MinimumReplicasAvailable", "status": "True", "type": "Available" } ], "fullyLabeledReplicas": 4, "observedGeneration": 3, "readyReplicas": 4, "replicas": 4 } ]
Example 5: Multi-Namespace JobPost Services List

This example shows how to list ReplicationControllers across multiple namespaces for comprehensive jobpost application monitoring.

# List all jobpost-related RCs across all namespaces
kubectl get rc --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE default anihpj-skills-api-rc 3 3 3 25m default anihpj-author-mgmt-rc 2 2 2 20m default anihpj-location-prod-rc 4 4 4 15m staging anihpj-staging-api-rc 1 1 1 10m development anihpj-dev-rc 1 1 1 5m
# Get comprehensive ReplicationControllerList across namespaces in YAML format
kubectl get rc --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationControllerList metadata: resourceVersion: "156789" items: - apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationController metadata: creationTimestamp: "2025-09-08T09:35:00Z" labels: app: anihpj service: skills-api name: anihpj-skills-api-rc namespace: default spec: replicas: 3 selector: app: anihpj service: skills-api status: availableReplicas: 3 readyReplicas: 3 replicas: 3 - apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationController metadata: creationTimestamp: "2025-09-08T09:40:00Z" labels: app: anihpj service: author-mgmt name: anihpj-author-mgmt-rc namespace: default spec: replicas: 2 selector: app: anihpj service: author-mgmt status: availableReplicas: 2 readyReplicas: 2 replicas: 2 - apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationController metadata: creationTimestamp: "2025-09-08T09:45:00Z" labels: app: anihpj environment: production service: location name: anihpj-location-prod-rc namespace: default spec: replicas: 4 selector: app: anihpj service: location environment: production status: availableReplicas: 4 readyReplicas: 4 replicas: 4

Operations

Operations Overview

Read - GET single ReplicationController details
List - GET multiple ReplicationControllers
Create - POST new ReplicationController
Update - PUT/PATCH existing ReplicationController
Delete - DELETE ReplicationController resources
Status - Manage ReplicationController status subresource
Patch - Partially update ReplicationController
Watch - Monitor ReplicationController changes

Operations allow you to interact with ReplicationController resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage Django application deployments, scale services, and monitor health status.

Read ReplicationController

Read the specified ReplicationController to get complete configuration and status information for your Django application deployment.

Syntax
kubectl get replicationcontroller {name} -o yaml
kubectl get rc {name} -o json
kubectl describe replicationcontroller {name}
HTTP API Syntax
GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
Parameters
  • name (string, required): Name of the ReplicationController
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace (default: "default")
  • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
Return Value
  • 200 (ReplicationController): OK - Returns the complete ReplicationController object
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Service Examples
Example 1: Skills Model ReplicationController (jobpost app)
# Get Skills RC configuration in YAML format
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 kind: ReplicationController metadata: name: anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc namespace: default labels: app: anihpj component: jobpost service: skills spec: replicas: 2 selector: app: anihpj component: jobpost service: skills template: metadata: labels: app: anihpj component: jobpost service: skills spec: containers: - name: jobpost-skills image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 ports: - containerPort: 8000 env: - name: DJANGO_APP value: "jobpost" - name: MODEL_FOCUS value: "skills" status: replicas: 2 readyReplicas: 2 availableReplicas: 2
Example 2: Author Model Management Service (jobpost app)
# Get detailed description of User RC
kubectl describe rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc
Name: anihpj-jobpost-author-rc Namespace: default Selector: app=anihpj,component=jobpost,service=author Labels: app=anihpj component=jobpost service=author Replicas: 3 current / 3 desired Pods Status: 3 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed Pod Template: Labels: app=anihpj component=jobpost service=author Containers: jobpost-author: Image: anihpj/jobpost:v1.0.0 Port: 8000/TCP Environment: DJANGO_APP: jobpost MODEL_FOCUS: user DB_PATH: /app/db.sqlite3 Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulCreate 5m replication-controller Created pod: anihpj-jobpost-author-rc-xyz789
Example 3: Author Model Content Service (jobpost app)
# Get Author RC container spec using jq
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc -o json | jq '.spec.template.spec.containers[0]'
{ "name": "jobpost-author", "image": "anihpj/jobpost:v1.0.0", "ports": [ { "containerPort": 8000, "protocol": "TCP" } ], "env": [ { "name": "DJANGO_APP", "value": "jobpost" }, { "name": "MODEL_FOCUS", "value": "author" }, { "name": "CONTENT_CACHE", "value": "redis://redis:6379/1" } ] }
Example 4: Location Model Service Details (jobpost app)
# Get Location RC details in wide format
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-location-rc -o wide
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-jobpost-location-rc 1 1 1 10m jobpost-location anihpj/jobpost:v1.0.0 app=anihpj,component=jobpost,service=location
Example 5: JobPost Model Main API Service (jobpost app)
# Get JobPost main RC status in formatted JSON
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-main-rc -o jsonpath='{.status}' | python -m json.tool
{ "availableReplicas": 4, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 4, "observedGeneration": 1, "readyReplicas": 4, "replicas": 4 }
Volumes: <none> Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulCreate 5m replication-controller Created pod: jobpost-api-rc-xyz789

Read Status ReplicationController

HTTP Request

Method - GET
Path - /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}/status
Response - ReplicationController object
Use Case - Monitor Django app deployment status

Read status of the specified ReplicationController to monitor the health and current state of your Django application deployment.

Syntax
kubectl get rc {name} -o jsonpath='{.status}'
kubectl get rc {name} --show-labels
HTTP API Syntax
GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}/status
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
Parameters
  • name (string, required): Name of the ReplicationController
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
Return Value
  • 200 (ReplicationController): OK - Returns the ReplicationController object with status information
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Status Examples
Example 1: Skills Model Service Status (jobpost app)
# Get Skills RC status in formatted JSON
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc -o jsonpath='{.status}' | python -m json.tool
{ "availableReplicas": 2, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 2, "observedGeneration": 1, "readyReplicas": 2, "replicas": 2 }
Example 2: Author Model Service Status (jobpost app)
# Get Author RC status section from YAML
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc -o yaml | grep -A 10 "status:"
status: availableReplicas: 3 fullyLabeledReplicas: 3 observedGeneration: 1 readyReplicas: 3 replicas: 3 conditions: - type: ReplicaFailure status: "False" lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:30:00Z"
Example 3: Location Model Service Status (jobpost app)
# Get replica information from Location RC description
kubectl describe rc anihpj-jobpost-location-rc | grep -A 3 "Replicas:"
Replicas: 1 current / 1 desired Pods Status: 1 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed Conditions: Type Status LastTransitionTime
Example 4: JobPost Model Main Service Status (jobpost app)
# Get replica counts for JobPost main RC
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-main-rc -o jsonpath='{.status.replicas},{.status.readyReplicas}'
4,4
Example 5: Author Model Service Status with Labels (jobpost app)
# Get User RC details with labels in wide format
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc --show-labels -o wide
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR LABELS anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 2 15m jobpost-author anihpj/jobpost:v1.0.0 app=anihpj,component=jobpost,service=author app=anihpj,component=jobpost,service=author

List ReplicationControllers

List all ReplicationControllers in a namespace to get an overview of your Django application deployments and their current status.

Syntax
kubectl get replicationcontrollers
kubectl get rc -l app={label}
kubectl get rc -o wide
HTTP API Syntax
GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
Parameters
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • labelSelector (string): Label selector for filtering
  • fieldSelector (string): Field selector for filtering
  • limit (integer): Maximum number of results
  • continue (string): Continue token for pagination
  • watch (boolean): Watch for changes to objects
Example 1: List All Django ReplicationControllers

This example shows how to list ReplicationControllers filtered by application labels in a specific namespace.

# List all RCs with anihpj app label
kubectl get rc -l app=anihpj
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-django-rc 3 3 3 10m anihpj-worker-rc 2 2 2 8m jobpost-api-rc 2 2 2 6m
Example 2: List with Detailed Information
# List all RCs with container and image details
kubectl get rc -o wide
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-django-rc 3 3 3 10m django-web anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 app=anihpj,component=web anihpj-worker-rc 2 2 2 8m django-worker anihpj/worker:v1.0.0 app=anihpj,component=worker jobpost-api-rc 2 2 2 6m django-api anihpj/jobpost-api:v1.0.0 app=anihpj,component=api
Example 3: Filter by Skills API Service (jobpost app)

This example shows how to list ReplicationControllers for the Skills API service with specific label filtering.

# List Skills API ReplicationControllers with specific labels
kubectl get rc -l service=skills-api,app=anihpj
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 3 3 3 15m
# Get Skills API RC with custom output format
kubectl get rc -l service=skills-api -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,IMAGE:.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image
NAME REPLICAS READY IMAGE anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 3 3 anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0
Example 4: List by Author and Location Services (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates listing multiple specific services using label selectors for Author and Location services.

# List Author and Location services with sorting by age
kubectl get rc -l "service in (author-mgmt,location)" --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 2 20m anihpj-location-prod-rc 4 4 4 10m
# Get detailed information with resource utilization
kubectl get rc -l "service in (author-mgmt,location)" -o wide --show-labels
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR LABELS anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 2 20m author-service anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.1.0 app=anihpj,service=author-mgmt app=anihpj,service=author-mgmt,tier=backend anihpj-location-prod-rc 4 4 4 10m location-service anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0 app=anihpj,environment=production,service=location app=anihpj,environment=production,service=location
Example 5: List with JSON Path Queries (jobpost app)

This example shows advanced listing with JSON path queries to extract specific information from multiple ReplicationControllers.

# Extract specific fields from all anihpj ReplicationControllers
kubectl get rc -l app=anihpj -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.replicas}{"\t"}{.status.readyReplicas}{"\t"}{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}{"\n"}{end}'
anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 3 3 anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0 anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.1.0 anihpj-location-prod-rc 4 4 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.0.0
# Get creation timestamps and current status
kubectl get rc -l app=anihpj -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{": created "}{.metadata.creationTimestamp}{", replicas: "}{.status.replicas}{"/"}{.spec.replicas}{"\n"}{end}'
anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc: created 2025-09-08T09:35:00Z, replicas: 3/3 anihpj-jobpost-author-rc: created 2025-09-08T09:40:00Z, replicas: 2/2 anihpj-location-prod-rc: created 2025-09-08T09:45:00Z, replicas: 4/4

List All Namespaces ReplicationController

List or watch objects of kind ReplicationController across all namespaces to get a cluster-wide view of Django deployments.

Syntax
kubectl get rc --all-namespaces
kubectl get rc -A -o wide
HTTP API Syntax
GET /api/v1/replicationcontrollers
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (boolean): Allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (string): Continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (string): Field selector for filtering
  • labelSelector (string): Label selector for filtering
  • limit (integer): Maximum number of results
  • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
  • resourceVersion (string): Resource version for consistency
  • resourceVersionMatch (string): Resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (boolean): Send initial events for watch
  • timeoutSeconds (integer): Timeout for the request
  • watch (boolean): Watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (ReplicationControllerList): OK - Returns list of ReplicationController objects across all namespaces
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
5 Django Cross-Namespace Examples
Example 1: Skills Model Across All Namespaces (jobpost app)
# List Skills RCs across all namespaces
kubectl get rc --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj,service=skills
NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE default anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 2 2 2 15m staging anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 1 1 1 10m production anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 3 3 3 2h
Example 2: Author Model Cluster-wide Watch (jobpost app)
# Watch Author RCs across all namespaces
kubectl get rc -A -l app=anihpj,service=author --watch
NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE default anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 3 3 3 12m staging anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 2 8m production anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 5 5 5 1h
Example 3: Location Model Detailed View (jobpost app)
# Get Location RCs across all namespaces with details
kubectl get rc -A -l app=anihpj,service=location -o wide
NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR default anihpj-jobpost-location-rc 1 1 1 10m jobpost-location anihpj/jobpost:v1.0.0 app=anihpj,component=jobpost,service=location staging anihpj-jobpost-location-rc 1 1 1 8m jobpost-location anihpj/jobpost:v1.1.0 app=anihpj,component=jobpost,service=location
Example 4: JobPost Model Main Service Global (jobpost app)
# Extract main RC names from all namespaces using jq
kubectl get rc --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj,service=main -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
"anihpj-jobpost-main-rc" "anihpj-jobpost-main-rc" "anihpj-jobpost-main-rc"
Example 5: Author Model Services with Field Selector (jobpost app)
# Get User RCs excluding kube-system namespace
kubectl get rc -A --field-selector metadata.namespace!=kube-system -l app=anihpj,service=author
NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE default anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 2 18m staging anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 1 1 1 12m production anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 4 4 4 3h

Create ReplicationController

Create a new ReplicationController to deploy your Django application with the specified configuration and replica count.

Syntax
kubectl create -f replicationcontroller.yaml
kubectl apply -f replicationcontroller.yaml
echo "{yaml-content}" | kubectl create -f -
HTTP API Syntax
POST /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers
Content-Type: application/yaml
Authorization: Bearer {token}

{ReplicationController-manifest}
Example 1: Create Skills Model Service (jobpost app)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: skills
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: skills
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: skills
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-skills
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "skills"
        - name: DB_PATH
          value: "sqlite:///db.sqlite3"
# Create Skills model ReplicationController
kubectl create -f anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc created
Example 2: Create Author Model Service (jobpost app)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-author-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: author
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-author
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "author"
        - name: DB_PATH
          value: "sqlite:///db.sqlite3"
        - name: USER_RELATION
          value: "onetoone"
# Apply Author model ReplicationController
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-author-rc.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-author-rc created
Example 3: Create Location Model Service (jobpost app)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-location-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: location
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: location
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: location
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-location
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "location"
        - name: GEO_FIELDS
          value: "street,city,state,country,zip"
# Create Location model RC with change recording
kubectl create -f anihpj-jobpost-location-rc.yaml --record
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-location-rc created
Example 4: Create JobPost Model Main Service (jobpost app)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-main-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: jobpost-main
spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: jobpost-main
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: jobpost-main
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-main
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: RELATIONS
          value: "location_onetoone,author_foreignkey,skills_manytomany"
        - name: DB_PATH
          value: "sqlite:///db.sqlite3"
# Apply JobPost main model ReplicationController
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-main-rc.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-main-rc created
Example 5: Create User Model API Service (Django built-in)
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-author-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: author
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-author
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: MODEL_FOCUS
          value: "user"
        - name: DJANGO_BUILTIN
          value: "true"
        - name: AUTH_BACKEND
          value: "django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend"
# Create Author model ReplicationController with save-config option for future apply operations
kubectl create -f anihpj-jobpost-author-rc.yaml --save-config
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-author-rc created
component: worker template: metadata: labels: app: anihpj component: worker spec: containers: - name: django-worker image: anihpj/jobpost-worker:v1.0.0 command: ["python", "manage.py", "process_jobs"] env: - name: WORKER_CONCURRENCY value: "4" 
kubectl apply -f jobpost-worker-rc.yaml
replicationcontroller/jobpost-worker-rc created

Update ReplicationController

Replace the entire ReplicationController specification with new configuration. Useful for major Django application updates.

Syntax
kubectl replace -f replicationcontroller.yaml
kubectl apply -f updated-replicationcontroller.yaml
HTTP API Syntax
PUT /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}
Content-Type: application/yaml
Authorization: Bearer {token}

{Updated-ReplicationController-manifest}
Example 1: Update Django App Image Version
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-django-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: web
spec:
  replicas: 5  # Scaled up from 3
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: web
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-web
        image: anihpj/django-app:v2.0.0  # Updated version
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 256Mi
kubectl replace -f anihpj-django-rc-v2.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-django-rc replaced
Example 2: Update Skills API Service Configuration (jobpost app)

This example shows how to update the Skills API service configuration with new environment variables and resource limits.

# skills-api-update.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills-api
    version: v1.3.0
spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: skills-api
        version: v1.3.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: skills-api
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.3.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: API_ENDPOINT
          value: "/jobpost/skills/"
        - name: CACHE_TIMEOUT
          value: "300"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "400m"
# Update the Skills API ReplicationController
kubectl replace -f skills-api-update.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc replaced
Example 3: Update Author Management Service Scaling (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates updating the Author management service with increased replicas and updated container image.
