# Get current Author RC configuration and modify it
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc -o yaml > author-service-update.yaml
# Edit the file to change replicas from 2 to 5 and update image version
# Then apply the changes
kubectl replace -f author-service-update.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-author-rc replaced
# Verify the update was successful
kubectl get rc anihpj-jobpost-author-rc -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,IMAGE:.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image
NAME REPLICAS IMAGE anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 5 anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0
Example 4: Update Location Service with Health Checks (jobpost app)

This example shows updating the Location service to add comprehensive health checks and monitoring.

# location-service-health-update.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-prod-rc
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
    environment: production
    version: v2.1.0
spec:
  replicas: 6
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
    environment: production
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: location
        environment: production
        version: v2.1.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: location-service
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9090
          name: metrics
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: HEALTH_CHECK_ENABLED
          value: "true"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/live/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 3
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "300Mi"
            cpu: "150m"
          limits:
            memory: "600Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
# Apply the Location service health check update
kubectl replace -f location-service-health-update.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-location-prod-rc replaced
Example 5: Complete JobPost Main API Update (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates a comprehensive update to the main JobPost API service with new features, security, and performance improvements.

# Create comprehensive update using kubectl create --dry-run and then replace
kubectl create rc anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-v2 \
  --image=anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 \
  --replicas=8 \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml > jobpost-main-comprehensive-update.yaml

# Edit the file to add additional configurations
# Then replace the existing ReplicationController
kubectl replace -f jobpost-main-comprehensive-update.yaml
replicationcontroller/anihpj-jobpost-main-rc replaced
# Monitor the rollout progress
kubectl rollout status rc/anihpj-jobpost-main-rc --timeout=300s
Waiting for rollout to finish: 2 of 8 updated replicas are available... Waiting for rollout to finish: 4 of 8 updated replicas are available... Waiting for rollout to finish: 6 of 8 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-jobpost-main-rc" successfully rolled out
# Verify all replicas are running with new version
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj,service=jobpost-main -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,IMAGE:.spec.containers[0].image,STATUS:.status.phase
NAME IMAGE STATUS anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-abc123 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-def456 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-ghi789 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-jkl012 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-mno345 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-pqr678 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-stu901 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running anihpj-jobpost-main-rc-vwx234 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 Running

Update Status ReplicationController

Replace status of the specified ReplicationController. This operation is typically used by controllers, not end users.

HTTP API Syntax
PUT /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}/status
Content-Type: application/yaml
Authorization: Bearer {token}

{ReplicationController-status-object}
Parameters
  • name (string, required): Name of the ReplicationController
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • body (ReplicationController, required): ReplicationController object
  • dryRun (string): Dry run mode
  • fieldManager (string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (string): Field validation mode
  • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
Return Value

Returns the updated ReplicationController object with status information reflecting the current state of replicas.

  • 200 OK: ReplicationController status successfully updated
  • 400 Bad Request: Invalid ReplicationController status object
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
  • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
  • 404 Not Found: ReplicationController not found
  • 409 Conflict: Conflict with current state
  • 422 Unprocessable Entity: Invalid field values
Example 1: Update Django App Status (System Operation)
# This is typically done by Kubernetes controllers, not users
curl -X PUT \
  https://k8s-api/api/v1/namespaces/default/replicationcontrollers/anihpj-django-rc/status \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "status": {
      "replicas": 3,
      "readyReplicas": 2,
      "availableReplicas": 2
    }
  }'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK { "kind": "ReplicationController", "metadata": {"name": "anihpj-django-rc"}, "status": { "replicas": 3, "readyReplicas": 2, "availableReplicas": 2 } }
Example 2: Update JobPost Processing Worker Status
# jobpost-worker-status.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: jobpost-worker-rc
  namespace: default
status:
  replicas: 2
  readyReplicas: 2
  availableReplicas: 2
  conditions:
  - type: ReplicaFailure
    status: "False"
    reason: "PodsRunning"
    message: "All replicas are running successfully"
# Replace the status subresource of the worker ReplicationController
kubectl replace --subresource=status -f jobpost-worker-status.yaml
replicationcontroller/jobpost-worker-rc status updated
Example 3: Update Skills Processing Service Status
# Update status via direct API call
curl -X PUT \
  https://k8s-api/api/v1/namespaces/anihpj-production/replicationcontrollers/skills-processor-rc/status \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $PROD_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "status": {
      "replicas": 5,
      "readyReplicas": 4,
      "availableReplicas": 4,
      "observedGeneration": 3,
      "conditions": [{
        "type": "ReplicaFailure",
        "status": "False",
        "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
        "reason": "SuccessfulCreate",
        "message": "Created replica: skills-processor-rc-abc123"
      }]
    }
  }'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK { "kind": "ReplicationController", "metadata": { "name": "skills-processor-rc", "namespace": "anihpj-production" }, "status": { "replicas": 5, "readyReplicas": 4, "availableReplicas": 4, "observedGeneration": 3 } }
Example 4: Update User Authentication Service Status
# auth-service-status-update.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: user-auth-rc
  namespace: anihpj-auth
  labels:
    app: user-authentication
    component: auth-service
status:
  replicas: 3
  readyReplicas: 3
  availableReplicas: 3
  fullyLabeledReplicas: 3
  observedGeneration: 2
  conditions:
  - type: ReplicaFailure
    status: "False"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:35:00Z"
    reason: "NewReplicaSetAvailable"
    message: "ReplicationController has minimum availability."
# Update authentication service ReplicationController status
kubectl replace --subresource=status -f auth-service-status-update.yaml
replicationcontroller/user-auth-rc status updated
Example 5: Update Location Service Status with Conditions
# Comprehensive status update for location service
curl -X PUT \
  https://k8s-api/api/v1/namespaces/anihpj-services/replicationcontrollers/location-service-rc/status \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $SERVICE_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "status": {
      "replicas": 4,
      "readyReplicas": 4,
      "availableReplicas": 4,
      "fullyLabeledReplicas": 4,
      "observedGeneration": 5,
      "conditions": [
        {
          "type": "ReplicaFailure",
          "status": "False",
          "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:40:00Z",
          "reason": "NewReplicaSetAvailable",
          "message": "ReplicationController has minimum availability."
        },
        {
          "type": "Progressing",
          "status": "True",
          "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
          "reason": "NewReplicaSetCreated",
          "message": "Created new replica set location-service-rc-xyz789"
        }
      ]
    }
  }'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK { "kind": "ReplicationController", "metadata": { "name": "location-service-rc", "namespace": "anihpj-services" }, "status": { "replicas": 4, "readyReplicas": 4, "availableReplicas": 4, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 4, "observedGeneration": 5 } }

Patch ReplicationController

Partially update specific fields of a ReplicationController. Most commonly used for scaling Django applications.

Syntax
kubectl scale rc {name} --replicas={count}
kubectl patch rc {name} -p '{patch-object}'
kubectl patch rc {name} --type merge -p '{patch-data}'
HTTP API Syntax
PATCH /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}
Content-Type: application/strategic-merge-patch+json
Authorization: Bearer {token}

{Patch-object}
Example 1: Scale Django Application

This example demonstrates how to scale a Django ReplicationController both using the scale command and patch operations.

# Scale Django ReplicationController to 5 replicas
kubectl scale rc anihpj-django-rc --replicas=5
replicationcontroller/anihpj-django-rc scaled
# Patch Django ReplicationController using JSON to set replicas to 5
kubectl patch rc anihpj-django-rc -p '{"spec":{"replicas":5}}'
replicationcontroller/anihpj-django-rc patched
Example 2: Update JobPost Worker Environment
kubectl patch rc jobpost-worker-rc -p '{
  "spec": {
    "template": {
      "spec": {
        "containers": [{
          "name": "django-worker",
          "env": [
            {"name": "WORKER_CONCURRENCY", "value": "8"},
            {"name": "LOG_LEVEL", "value": "INFO"}
          ]
        }]
      }
    }
  }
}'
replicationcontroller/jobpost-worker-rc patched

Patch Status ReplicationController

Partially update status of the specified ReplicationController. Used for incremental status updates by controllers.

HTTP API Syntax
PATCH /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}/status
Content-Type: application/strategic-merge-patch+json
Authorization: Bearer {token}

{Patch-object}
Parameters
  • name (string, required): Name of the ReplicationController
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • body (Patch, required): Patch object
  • dryRun (string): Dry run mode
  • fieldManager (string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (string): Field validation mode
  • force (boolean): Force the update
  • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
Example: Patch JobPost Worker Status
# System-level operation typically done by controllers
curl -X PATCH \
  https://k8s-api/api/v1/namespaces/default/replicationcontrollers/jobpost-worker-rc/status \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/strategic-merge-patch+json" \
  -d '{
    "status": {
      "readyReplicas": 2,
      "conditions": [{
        "type": "ReplicaFailure",
        "status": "False",
        "reason": "PodsRunning"
      }]
    }
  }'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK { "status": { "replicas": 2, "readyReplicas": 2, "availableReplicas": 2, "conditions": [{ "type": "ReplicaFailure", "status": "False", "reason": "PodsRunning" }] } }

Delete ReplicationController

Delete a ReplicationController and optionally its managed pods. Critical for Django application cleanup and resource management.

Syntax
kubectl delete rc {name}
kubectl delete rc {name} --cascade=orphan
kubectl delete -f replicationcontroller.yaml
HTTP API Syntax
DELETE /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers/{name}
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}

{
  "kind": "DeleteOptions",
  "propagationPolicy": "Foreground"
}
Parameters
  • propagationPolicy (string): "Foreground", "Background", or "Orphan"
  • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Grace period for pod termination
  • orphanDependents (boolean): Deprecated. Use propagationPolicy
Return Value

Returns the status of the deletion operation with information about the ReplicationController removal.

  • 200 OK: ReplicationController successfully deleted
  • 400 Bad Request: Invalid delete options
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
  • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions to delete
  • 404 Not Found: ReplicationController not found
  • 409 Conflict: Conflict with current state during deletion
Example 1: Delete Django RC and Pods
kubectl delete rc anihpj-django-rc
replicationcontroller "anihpj-django-rc" deleted
# Check if pods are still running after RC deletion (should be none)
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj,component=web
No resources found in default namespace.
Example 2: Delete RC but Keep Pods Running
# Delete ReplicationController but keep the pods running (orphan cascade)
kubectl delete rc jobpost-worker-rc --cascade=orphan
replicationcontroller "jobpost-worker-rc" deleted
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj,component=worker
NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE jobpost-worker-rc-k7x3m 1/1 Running 0 10m jobpost-worker-rc-m9p2n 1/1 Running 0 10m
Example 3: Delete Skills Processing RC with Custom Grace Period
# Delete with longer grace period for graceful shutdown
kubectl delete rc skills-processor-rc --grace-period=60
replicationcontroller "skills-processor-rc" deleted
# Verify deletion
kubectl get rc skills-processor-rc
Error from server (NotFound): replicationcontrollers "skills-processor-rc" not found
Example 4: Force Delete Stuck ReplicationController
# Force delete if RC is stuck in terminating state
kubectl delete rc user-auth-rc --force --grace-period=0
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. The resource may continue to run on the cluster indefinitely. replicationcontroller "user-auth-rc" force deleted
# Clean up any remaining pods manually if needed
kubectl delete pods -l app=anihpj,component=auth --force --grace-period=0
Example 5: Delete Location Service RC via API
# Delete using direct API call with propagation policy
curl -X DELETE \
  https://k8s-api/api/v1/namespaces/anihpj-services/replicationcontrollers/location-service-rc \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "kind": "DeleteOptions",
    "apiVersion": "v1",
    "propagationPolicy": "Foreground",
    "gracePeriodSeconds": 30
  }'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK { "kind": "Status", "apiVersion": "v1", "status": "Success", "details": { "name": "location-service-rc", "kind": "replicationcontrollers", "uid": "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012" } }

Delete Collection ReplicationController

Delete collection of ReplicationController resources matching specified criteria. Useful for bulk cleanup of Django deployments.

Syntax
kubectl delete rc -l {label-selector}
kubectl delete rc --all
kubectl delete rc -l app=anihpj
HTTP API Syntax
DELETE /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicationcontrollers
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}

{
  "kind": "DeleteOptions",
  "propagationPolicy": "Foreground"
}
Parameters
  • namespace (string, required): Object namespace
  • body (DeleteOptions): Delete options
  • continue (string): Continue token for pagination
  • dryRun (string): Dry run mode
  • fieldSelector (string): Field selector for filtering
  • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Grace period for termination
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (boolean): Ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (string): Label selector for filtering
  • limit (integer): Maximum number to delete
  • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
  • propagationPolicy (string): Propagation policy
  • resourceVersion (string): Resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (string): Resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (boolean): Send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (integer): Timeout for the operation
Example 1: Delete All Django ReplicationControllers
kubectl delete rc -l app=anihpj
replicationcontroller "anihpj-django-rc" deleted replicationcontroller "jobpost-worker-rc" deleted
Example 2: Delete All Worker ReplicationControllers
kubectl delete rc -l component=worker --dry-run=client
replicationcontroller "jobpost-worker-rc" deleted (dry run) replicationcontroller "skills-processor-rc" deleted (dry run)
✅ Best Practices for Django Applications:
  • Use meaningful names like "anihpj-web-rc" or "jobpost-worker-rc"
  • Set appropriate resource requests/limits for Django containers
  • Include health checks for Django application endpoints
  • Use ConfigMaps for Django settings and Secrets for sensitive data
  • Consider using Deployments instead of ReplicationController for production
Example 3: Delete Skills and Author Services (jobpost app)

This example shows how to delete specific service ReplicationControllers using label selectors for Skills and Author services.

# Delete Skills and Author service ReplicationControllers
kubectl delete rc -l "service in (skills-api,author-mgmt)"
replicationcontroller "anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc" deleted replicationcontroller "anihpj-jobpost-author-rc" deleted
# Verify deletion completed
kubectl get rc -l "service in (skills-api,author-mgmt)"
No resources found in default namespace.
Example 4: Delete All Development Environment RCs (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates bulk deletion of all development environment ReplicationControllers with confirmation and cleanup verification.

# List development environment RCs before deletion
kubectl get rc -l environment=development --show-labels
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE LABELS anihpj-dev-skills-rc 1 1 1 2h app=anihpj,environment=development,service=skills-api anihpj-dev-author-rc 1 1 1 2h app=anihpj,environment=development,service=author-mgmt anihpj-dev-location-rc 1 1 1 2h app=anihpj,environment=development,service=location
# Delete all development environment ReplicationControllers
kubectl delete rc -l environment=development
replicationcontroller "anihpj-dev-skills-rc" deleted replicationcontroller "anihpj-dev-author-rc" deleted replicationcontroller "anihpj-dev-location-rc" deleted
# Check for any remaining development pods
kubectl get pods -l environment=development
No resources found in default namespace.
Example 5: Delete All Inactive JobPost Services (jobpost app)

This example shows advanced deletion using field selectors and multiple criteria for cleaning up inactive or terminated ReplicationControllers.

# List all RCs with their current status
kubectl get rc -l app=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,DESIRED:.spec.replicas,CURRENT:.status.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 3 3 3 2025-09-08T09:35:00Z anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 2 2025-09-08T09:40:00Z anihpj-location-prod-rc 4 4 4 2025-09-08T09:45:00Z anihpj-old-api-rc 0 0 0 2025-09-07T15:30:00Z anihpj-test-rc 0 0 0 2025-09-07T14:20:00Z 
# Delete RCs with zero desired replicas (inactive services)
kubectl delete rc -l app=anihpj --field-selector spec.replicas=0
replicationcontroller "anihpj-old-api-rc" deleted replicationcontroller "anihpj-test-rc" deleted
# Verify only active services remain
kubectl get rc -l app=anihpj --show-labels
NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE LABELS anihpj-jobpost-skills-rc 3 3 3 25m app=anihpj,service=skills-api,version=v1.2.0 anihpj-jobpost-author-rc 2 2 2 20m app=anihpj,service=author-mgmt,tier=backend anihpj-location-prod-rc 4 4 4 15m app=anihpj,environment=production,service=location

Deployment

Deployment Overview

DeploymentSpec - Desired state specification
DeploymentStatus - Current observed status
DeploymentList - Collection of deployments
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: Deployment v1 API | API Version: apps/v1

Deployment enables declarative updates for Pods and ReplicaSets, providing advanced deployment strategies for Django applications.

Full Description

Deployment is the recommended way to deploy stateless applications in Kubernetes. It provides declarative updates for Pods and ReplicaSets, enabling rolling updates, rollbacks, and scaling for your anihpj Django application. Unlike ReplicationController, Deployments support advanced deployment strategies and are the modern standard for managing stateless workloads in production environments.

For Django applications, Deployments are ideal for web servers, API services, and background workers that don't require persistent state. They automatically manage ReplicaSets and provide features like rolling updates, which allow you to update your jobpost application without downtime.

Syntax

# Create a Deployment from YAML file
kubectl create -f deployment.yaml
# Create a Deployment directly with kubectl
kubectl create deployment {name} --image={image}
# List all Deployments
kubectl get deployments
# List all Deployments (short form)
kubectl get deploy
# Get detailed information about a specific Deployment
kubectl describe deployment {name}
# Get detailed information about a specific Deployment (short form)
kubectl describe deploy {name}
# Scale Deployment to specified number of replicas
kubectl scale deployment {name} --replicas={count}
# Check the rollout status of a Deployment
kubectl rollout status deployment/{name}
# View the rollout history of a Deployment
kubectl rollout history deployment/{name}
# Rollback to previous version of a Deployment
kubectl rollout undo deployment/{name}
# Rollback to specific revision of a Deployment
kubectl rollout undo deployment/{name} --to-revision={number}
# Pause a deployment rollout
kubectl rollout pause deployment/{name}
# Resume a paused deployment rollout
kubectl rollout resume deployment/{name}
# Delete a specific Deployment
kubectl delete deployment {name}
# Delete a specific Deployment (short form)
kubectl delete deploy {name}

YAML Structure

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-django-deployment
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: web
    version: v1.0.0
spec:
  replicas: 3
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
  minReadySeconds: 10
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: web
        version: v1.0.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-web
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: django-port
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-secrets
              key: database-url
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 3
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
          timeoutSeconds: 3
          successThreshold: 1
          failureThreshold: 3

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: Always "apps/v1" for Deployment. This indicates the API version being used.
  • kind: Must be "Deployment" to specify the resource type.
  • metadata.name: Unique name for the Deployment within the namespace. Use descriptive names like "anihpj-django-deployment".
  • metadata.namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the Deployment will be created. Defaults to "default".
  • metadata.labels: Key-value pairs for organizing and selecting Deployments. Useful for grouping Django app components.
  • spec.replicas: Number of desired pod replicas. For Django apps, start with 2-3 for high availability.
  • spec.selector.matchLabels: Label selector to identify which pods belong to this Deployment. Must match template labels.
  • spec.template: Pod template specification that defines how new pods should be created.
  • spec.strategy: Deployment strategy (RollingUpdate or Recreate). RollingUpdate is recommended for Django apps.
  • spec.strategy.rollingUpdate.maxUnavailable: Maximum number of pods that can be unavailable during update.
  • spec.strategy.rollingUpdate.maxSurge: Maximum number of pods that can be created above desired number during update.
  • spec.minReadySeconds: Minimum seconds a pod must be ready before considered available. Important for Django apps with startup time.
  • spec.revisionHistoryLimit: Number of old ReplicaSets to retain for rollback. Defaults to 10.
  • spec.progressDeadlineSeconds: Maximum time in seconds for deployment to make progress before considered failed.

Return Value

Returns the Deployment object with its current state, including status information about replicas, conditions, and deployment progress.

  • 200 OK: Deployment successfully created/updated
  • 201 Created: Deployment successfully created
  • 400 Bad Request: Invalid Deployment specification
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
  • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
  • 404 Not Found: Namespace not found
  • 409 Conflict: Deployment already exists
  • 422 Unprocessable Entity: Invalid field values

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills API Deployment (jobpost app)

This example demonstrates deploying a Deployment for the Skills API service (/jobpost/skills/), providing JSON endpoints for managing job skills in the anihpj Django project with rolling update strategy.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: skills-api
spec:
  replicas: 3
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: jobpost
      service: skills-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost
        service: skills-api
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-skills
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_APP
          value: "jobpost"
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
        - name: SERVICE_TYPE
          value: "skills_api"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /jobpost/skills/health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 45
          periodSeconds: 30
# Apply the Skills API Deployment
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment.yaml
# Check deployment status and rollout progress
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment
# Get deployment details
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment -o wide
deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment created Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment" rollout to finish: 0 of 3 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment" rollout to finish: 1 of 3 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment" rollout to finish: 2 of 3 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment" successfully rolled out NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-jobpost-skills-deployment 3/3 3 3 2m jobpost-skills anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.2.0 app=anihpj,component=jobpost,service=skills-api
Example 2: Author Management Deployment (jobpost app)

This example shows creating a Deployment for Author management service with configurable rolling update strategy and health checks for anihpj Django application.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: author
    service: management
spec:
  replicas: 2
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 0
      maxSurge: 1
  minReadySeconds: 30
  revisionHistoryLimit: 5
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: author
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: author
        service: management
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: author-mgmt
        image: anihpj/author-service:v2.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.author_production"
        - name: AUTHOR_SERVICE_MODE
          value: "management"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "400m"
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /author/ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 10
# Create the Author Management Deployment in production namespace
kubectl apply -f anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment.yaml -n production
# Watch the rollout progress in real-time
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment -n production --watch
# Check deployment history and revisions
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment -n production
deployment.apps/anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment created Waiting for deployment "anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment" rollout to finish: 0 of 2 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment" rollout to finish: 1 of 2 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment" successfully rolled out REVISION CHANGE-CAUSE 1 kubectl apply --filename=anihpj-author-mgmt-deployment.yaml --namespace=production --record=true
Example 3: Location Service Deployment with Advanced Configuration

This example demonstrates a comprehensive Deployment for Location service with advanced configuration including resource limits, probes, and environment-specific settings.

# Create Location Service Deployment directly with kubectl
kubectl create deployment anihpj-location-service \
  --image=anihpj/location-service:v1.5.0 \
  --replicas=4 \
  --port=8000 \
  --namespace=default
# Set environment variables for the deployment
kubectl set env deployment/anihpj-location-service \
  DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings.location_production \
  SERVICE_TYPE=location_api \
  CACHE_BACKEND=redis
# Configure resource limits for the deployment
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-location-service -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"location-service","resources":{"requests":{"memory":"256Mi","cpu":"200m"},"limits":{"memory":"512Mi","cpu":"400m"}}}]}}}}'
deployment.apps/anihpj-location-service created deployment.apps/anihpj-location-service env updated deployment.apps/anihpj-location-service patched
# Check the deployment status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-location-service -o wide
# View deployment details including strategy and conditions
kubectl describe deployment anihpj-location-service
NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-location-service 4/4 4 4 3m location-service anihpj/location-service:v1.5.0 app=anihpj-location-service Name: anihpj-location-service Namespace: default CreationTimestamp: Sun, 08 Sep 2025 14:30:00 +0000 Labels: app=anihpj-location-service Annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: 2 Selector: app=anihpj-location-service Replicas: 4 desired | 4 updated | 4 total | 4 available | 0 unavailable StrategyType: RollingUpdate MinReadySeconds: 0 RollingUpdateStrategy: 25% max unavailable, 25% max surge Pod Template: Labels: app=anihpj-location-service Containers: location-service: Image: anihpj/location-service:v1.5.0 Port: 8000/TCP Host Port: 0/TCP Limits: cpu: 400m memory: 512Mi Requests: cpu: 200m memory: 256Mi Environment: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: anihpj.settings.location_production SERVICE_TYPE: location_api CACHE_BACKEND: redis
Example 4: JobPost Main API Deployment with Rollback Strategy

This example shows how to deploy the main JobPost API service and demonstrate rollback capabilities when issues are detected.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment
  namespace: api-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: api
    service: jobpost-main
spec:
  replicas: 5
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 20%
      maxSurge: 2
  minReadySeconds: 20
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 300
  revisionHistoryLimit: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: jobpost-main
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: jobpost-main
        version: v3.0.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-api
        image: anihpj/jobpost-main-api:v3.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: api-port
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.api_production"
        - name: API_VERSION
          value: "v3.0.0"
        - name: JOBPOST_SERVICE_MODE
          value: "main_api"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "300m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "600m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /jobpost/api/health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 30
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /jobpost/api/ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 10
# Apply the JobPost API Deployment
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment.yaml
# Monitor rollout progress
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment -n api-services
# Simulate a rollback scenario - rollback to previous version
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment -n api-services
# Check rollout history to see revisions
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment -n api-services
deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment created Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment" rollout to finish: 2 of 5 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment" rollout to finish: 3 of 5 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment" rollout to finish: 4 of 5 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment" successfully rolled out deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment rolled back REVISION CHANGE-CAUSE 1 kubectl apply --filename=anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment.yaml --record=true 2 kubectl apply --filename=anihpj-jobpost-api-deployment.yaml --record=true
Example 5: User Authentication Service Deployment with Canary Strategy

This example demonstrates deploying User authentication service using a canary deployment strategy for safe releases with the anihpj Django User model.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-auth-deployment
  namespace: auth-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: auth
    service: user-authentication
    deployment-strategy: canary
spec:
  replicas: 6
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
  minReadySeconds: 30
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: auth
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: auth
        service: user-authentication
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: user-auth
        image: anihpj/user-auth-service:v2.5.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: auth-port
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.auth_production"
        - name: USER_SERVICE_MODE
          value: "authentication"
        - name: AUTH_BACKEND
          value: "django_user_model"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "150m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "300m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /auth/health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 45
          periodSeconds: 20
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /auth/ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
# Apply the User Authentication Deployment
kubectl apply -f anihpj-user-auth-deployment.yaml
# Monitor deployment progress with detailed output
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-user-auth-deployment -n auth-services --watch
# Scale deployment for canary testing (temporarily increase replicas)
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-user-auth-deployment --replicas=8 -n auth-services
# Check deployment events for any issues
kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=anihpj-user-auth-deployment -n auth-services
deployment.apps/anihpj-user-auth-deployment created Waiting for deployment "anihpj-user-auth-deployment" rollout to finish: 0 of 6 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-user-auth-deployment" rollout to finish: 2 of 6 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-user-auth-deployment" rollout to finish: 4 of 6 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "anihpj-user-auth-deployment" rollout to finish: 5 of 6 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-user-auth-deployment" successfully rolled out deployment.apps/anihpj-user-auth-deployment scaled LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 5m2s Normal ScalingReplicaSet deployment/anihpj-user-auth-deployment Scaled up replica set anihpj-user-auth-deployment-7b8f9c5d6 to 6 2m1s Normal ScalingReplicaSet deployment/anihpj-user-auth-deployment Scaled up replica set anihpj-user-auth-deployment-7b8f9c5d6 to 8
  • 404 Not Found: Namespace not found
  • 409 Conflict: Deployment already exists
  • 422 Unprocessable Entity: Invalid field values

    • 5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: Create Django Web Deployment

    This example shows how to create a basic anihpj Django project Deployment with rolling update strategy and resource specifications for the jobpost app.

    # anihpj-web-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-web-deployment
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: web
    version: v1.0.0
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: web
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: django-web
        image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: django-port
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DB_PATH
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-secrets
              key: database-url
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
    # Apply the Django web Deployment
kubectl apply -f anihpj-web-deployment.yaml
    deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment created
    Example 2: JobPost API Deployment with Rolling Update Strategy

    This example demonstrates a production-ready JobPost API Deployment with rolling update configuration, health checks, and resource limits.

    # jobpost-api-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: jobpost-api-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-production
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: api
    service: jobpost
spec:
  replicas: 5
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: api
      service: jobpost
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: api
        service: jobpost
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-api
        image: anihpj/jobpost-api:v2.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /jobpost/health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 30
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /jobpost/ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
    # Apply the JobPost API Deployment with rolling update strategy
kubectl apply -f jobpost-api-deployment.yaml
    deployment.apps/jobpost-api-deployment created
    Example 3: Skills Processing Service Deployment

    This example shows how to create a Deployment directly using kubectl with imperative commands, then manage its lifecycle.

    # Create deployment directly with kubectl
kubectl create deployment skills-processor \
  --image=anihpj/skills-processor:v1.5.0 \
  --replicas=3 \
  --port=8000
    deployment.apps/skills-processor created
    # Scale the deployment
kubectl scale deployment skills-processor --replicas=5
    deployment.apps/skills-processor scaled
    # Check rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/skills-processor
    deployment "skills-processor" successfully rolled out
    Example 4: User Authentication Service with Health Checks
    # user-auth-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: user-auth-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-auth
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: auth
    service: author-authentication
spec:
  replicas: 4
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 25%
      maxSurge: 25%
  minReadySeconds: 10
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: auth
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: auth
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: user-auth
        image: anihpj/user-auth:v3.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: auth-port
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.auth_production"
        - name: SECRET_KEY
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: auth-secrets
              key: secret-key
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /auth/health/
            port: 8000
            httpHeaders:
            - name: Custom-Header
              value: HealthCheck
          initialDelaySeconds: 45
          periodSeconds: 20
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 3
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /auth/ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
          timeoutSeconds: 3
          successThreshold: 1
          failureThreshold: 3
    # Apply User Authentication Deployment with health checks
kubectl apply -f user-auth-deployment.yaml
    deployment.apps/user-auth-deployment created
    Example 5: Location Service Deployment with Advanced Configuration

    This example demonstrates an advanced Location service Deployment with custom namespaces, ConfigMap volumes, and comprehensive labeling.

    # location-service-deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: location-service-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: microservice
    service: location
    version: v2.3.0
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubernetes.io/change-cause: "Initial deployment of location service v2.3.0"
spec:
  replicas: 6
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
  minReadySeconds: 15
  revisionHistoryLimit: 5
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 300
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: microservice
      service: location
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: microservice
        service: location
        version: v2.3.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: location-service
        image: anihpj/location-service:v2.3.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: location-port
          protocol: TCP
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.location_production"
        - name: DB_PATH
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: location-config
              key: database-url
        - name: REDIS_URL
          valueFrom:
            configMapKeyRef:
              name: location-config
              key: redis-url
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "300m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "600m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /location/health/
            port: 8000
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 30
          timeoutSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          failureThreshold: 3
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /location/ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          failureThreshold: 3
        volumeMounts:
        - name: location-config
          mountPath: /app/config
          readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: location-config
        configMap:
          name: location-config
    # Apply the location service Deployment from YAML file
kubectl apply -f location-service-deployment.yaml
    deployment.apps/location-service-deployment created
    # Monitor deployment progress
kubectl rollout status deployment/location-service-deployment -n anihpj-services
    Waiting for deployment "location-service-deployment" rollout to finish: 2 of 6 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "location-service-deployment" rollout to finish: 3 of 6 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "location-service-deployment" rollout to finish: 4 of 6 updated replicas are available... Waiting for deployment "location-service-deployment" rollout to finish: 5 of 6 updated replicas are available... deployment "location-service-deployment" successfully rolled out
    ✅ Deployment Best Practices for Django Applications:
    • Use Deployments instead of ReplicationControllers for production Django apps
    • Set appropriate rolling update strategy (maxUnavailable, maxSurge)
    • Configure health checks (liveness and readiness probes) for Django endpoints
    • Set resource requests and limits based on Django application requirements
    • Use meaningful labels and annotations for better organization
    • Keep revision history limit reasonable (5-10) to avoid cluttering
    • Set appropriate progressDeadlineSeconds for deployment timeout

    DeploymentSpec

    DeploymentSpec is a description of the deployment specification. It defines the desired state for a Deployment object, including the number of replicas, the pod template, and the update strategy. This specification ensures that the deployment maintains the desired number of pods and can perform rolling updates when changes are needed.

    API Definition

    Group: apps
    Version: v1
    Kind: DeploymentSpec
    Scope: Namespaced

    Syntax

    # Create deployment with custom spec from YAML file
kubectl apply -f deployment-spec.yaml

# Update deployment spec directly with kubectl patch
kubectl patch deployment my-deployment -p '{"spec":{"replicas":5}}'

# Get deployment spec details in YAML format
kubectl get deployment my-deployment -o yaml | grep -A 20 "spec:"

# Scale deployment by modifying spec replicas
kubectl scale deployment my-deployment --replicas=3

# Set deployment spec strategy (RollingUpdate/Recreate)
kubectl patch deployment my-deployment -p '{"spec":{"strategy":{"type":"RollingUpdate"}}}'

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: example-deployment
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 3                    # Number of desired pods
  selector:                      # Label selector for pods
    matchLabels:
      app: example-app
  template:                      # Pod template specification
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: example-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app-container
        image: nginx:1.21
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
  strategy:                      # Update strategy
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10       # Number of old ReplicaSets to retain
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600   # Maximum time for deployment to progress
  paused: false                  # Whether deployment is paused

    Parameters

    replicas integer

    Number of desired pods. This is a pointer to distinguish between explicit zero and not specified. Defaults to 1.

    selector LabelSelector

    Label selector for pods. Existing ReplicaSets whose pods are selected by this will be affected by this deployment.

    template PodTemplateSpec

    Template describes the pods that will be created. This is required.

    strategy DeploymentStrategy

    The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones.

    revisionHistoryLimit integer

    Number of old ReplicaSets to retain to allow rollback. This is a pointer to distinguish between explicit zero and not specified. Defaults to 10.

    progressDeadlineSeconds integer

    The maximum time in seconds for a deployment to make progress before it is considered to be failed.

    paused boolean

    Indicates that the deployment is paused. Defaults to false.

    minReadySeconds integer

    Minimum number of seconds for which a newly created pod should be ready without any of its container crashing.

    Return Value

    Returns a DeploymentSpec object containing the deployment specification with all configured parameters for pod management and update strategies.

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: Basic Django App DeploymentSpec

    This example shows how to create a basic anihpj Django deployment spec with standard configuration for the jobpost application.

    # anihpj-jobpost-spec.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-jobpost
    component: web
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-jobpost
      tier: frontend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-jobpost
        tier: frontend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-container
        image: anihpj/jobpost:v1.2.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-db-secret
              key: url
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 5
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 300
    # Apply the JobPost deployment spec
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-spec.yaml
# Check deployment spec details
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment -o jsonpath='{.spec}' | jq
# Monitor rollout based on spec configuration
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-jobpost-deployment -n anihpj-services
# Verify spec replicas are maintained
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}' -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment created { "replicas": 3, "selector": { "matchLabels": { "app": "anihpj-jobpost", "tier": "frontend" } }, "strategy": { "type": "RollingUpdate", "rollingUpdate": { "maxSurge": 1, "maxUnavailable": 1 } } } Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-deployment" rollout to finish: 0 of 3 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-jobpost-deployment" successfully rolled out 3
    Example 2: High Availability Django API DeploymentSpec

    This example demonstrates a high-availability anihpj Django deployment spec with multiple replicas and resource constraints.

    # anihpj-api-ha-spec.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-api-ha-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-production
  labels:
    app: anihpj-api
    environment: production
spec:
  replicas: 6
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-api
      version: stable
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-api
        version: stable
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: api-container
        image: anihpj/api:stable-v2.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 5
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 25%
      maxSurge: 25%
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  minReadySeconds: 30
    # Deploy high-availability API spec
kubectl apply -f anihpj-api-ha-spec.yaml
# Scale deployment to handle increased load
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-api-ha-deployment --replicas=10 -n anihpj-production
# Update strategy for zero-downtime deployment
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-api-ha-deployment -p '{"spec":{"strategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"maxUnavailable":"0"}}}}' -n anihpj-production
# Check spec configuration details
kubectl describe deployment anihpj-api-ha-deployment -n anihpj-production | grep -A 10 "StrategyType"
    deployment.apps/anihpj-api-ha-deployment created deployment.apps/anihpj-api-ha-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-api-ha-deployment patched StrategyType: RollingUpdate MinReadySeconds: 30 RollingUpdateStrategy: 0 max unavailable, 25% max surge Pod Template: Labels: app=anihpj-api version=stable Containers: api-container: Image: anihpj/api:stable-v2.1.0
    Example 3: Canary Deployment Spec for Django Updates

    This example shows how to configure a canary deployment spec for anihpj Django application updates with controlled rollout.

    # anihpj-canary-spec.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-canary-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-staging
  labels:
    app: anihpj-web
    deployment-type: canary
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-web
      version: canary
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-web
        version: canary
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web-container
        image: anihpj/web:canary-v2.2.0-rc1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DEPLOYMENT_TYPE
          value: "canary"
        - name: FEATURE_FLAGS
          value: "new_ui=true,enhanced_search=true"
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 0
      maxSurge: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 15
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 900
  paused: false
    # Apply canary deployment spec
kubectl apply -f anihpj-canary-spec.yaml
# Pause deployment for manual verification
kubectl rollout pause deployment/anihpj-canary-deployment -n anihpj-staging
# Resume after testing
kubectl rollout resume deployment/anihpj-canary-deployment -n anihpj-staging
# Check canary deployment spec status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-canary-deployment -o jsonpath='{.spec.paused}' -n anihpj-staging
    deployment.apps/anihpj-canary-deployment created deployment.apps/anihpj-canary-deployment paused deployment.apps/anihpj-canary-deployment resumed false
    Example 4: Blue-Green Deployment Spec Configuration

    This example demonstrates configuring a blue-green deployment spec for anihpj Django application with environment-specific settings.

    # anihpj-blue-green-spec.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-green-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-production
  labels:
    app: anihpj-web
    environment: green
spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-web
      slot: green
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-web
        slot: green
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web-container
        image: anihpj/web:v2.3.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: SLOT_COLOR
          value: "green"
        - name: DATABASE_POOL
          value: "green-pool"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "300m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "600m"
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  revisionHistoryLimit: 3
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 1200
    # Deploy green environment spec
kubectl apply -f anihpj-blue-green-spec.yaml
# Wait for green deployment to be ready
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-green-deployment -n anihpj-production --timeout=600s
# Switch traffic by updating service selector
kubectl patch service anihpj-web-service -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"slot":"green"}}}' -n anihpj-production
# Verify green deployment spec is active
kubectl get deployment anihpj-green-deployment -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.metadata.labels.slot}' -n anihpj-production
    deployment.apps/anihpj-green-deployment created Waiting for deployment "anihpj-green-deployment" rollout to finish: 0 of 4 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-green-deployment" successfully rolled out service/anihpj-web-service patched green
    Example 5: Maintenance Mode DeploymentSpec

    This example shows how to configure a maintenance mode deployment spec for anihpj Django application during system updates.

    # anihpj-maintenance-spec.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: anihpj-maintenance-deployment
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-web
    mode: maintenance
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-web
      mode: maintenance
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-web
        mode: maintenance
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: maintenance-container
        image: anihpj/maintenance:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: MAINTENANCE_MESSAGE
          value: "System maintenance in progress. Please try again later."
        - name: ESTIMATED_DURATION
          value: "2 hours"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "64Mi"
            cpu: "50m"
          limits:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 0
      maxSurge: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 2
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 180
  paused: false
    # Apply maintenance mode deployment spec
kubectl apply -f anihpj-maintenance-spec.yaml
# Scale down main application
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-web-deployment --replicas=0 -n anihpj-services
# Update service to point to maintenance deployment
kubectl patch service anihpj-web-service -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"mode":"maintenance"}}}' -n anihpj-services
# Check maintenance deployment spec status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-maintenance-deployment -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}' -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-maintenance-deployment created deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment scaled service/anihpj-web-service patched 1

    DeploymentStatus

    DeploymentStatus represents the current status of a Deployment. It provides information about the deployment's observed state, including the number of available replicas, conditions, and the current revision. This status helps monitor the health and progress of deployments in real-time.

    API Definition

    Group: apps
    Version: v1
    Kind: DeploymentStatus
    Scope: Namespaced

    Syntax

    # Get deployment status details
kubectl get deployment my-deployment -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Watch deployment status changes in real-time
kubectl get deployment my-deployment --watch -o jsonpath='{.status.replicas}'

# Check deployment conditions
kubectl get deployment my-deployment -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].type}'

# Get available replica count
kubectl get deployment my-deployment -o jsonpath='{.status.availableReplicas}'

# Check deployment rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-deployment --timeout=300s

    YAML Structure

    status:
  replicas: 3                      # Total number of non-terminated pods
  updatedReplicas: 3               # Number of non-terminated pods that have the desired template spec
  readyReplicas: 3                 # Number of pods targeted by this deployment with Ready Condition
  availableReplicas: 3             # Total number of available pods targeted by this deployment
  unavailableReplicas: 0           # Total number of unavailable pods targeted by this deployment
  observedGeneration: 2            # The generation observed by the deployment controller
  conditions:                      # Represents the latest available observations
  - type: Progressing
    status: "True"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:45Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:25:30Z"
    reason: NewReplicaSetAvailable
    message: "ReplicaSet \"my-deployment-7b8f9c5d6\" has successfully progressed."
  - type: Available
    status: "True"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:45Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:45Z"
    reason: MinimumReplicasAvailable
    message: "Deployment has minimum availability."
  collisionCount: 0                # Count of hash collisions for the Deployment

    Parameters

    replicas integer

    Total number of non-terminated pods targeted by this deployment (their labels match the selector).

    updatedReplicas integer

    Total number of non-terminated pods targeted by this deployment that have the desired template spec.

    readyReplicas integer

    Total number of ready pods targeted by this deployment.

    availableReplicas integer

    Total number of available pods (ready for at least minReadySeconds) targeted by this deployment.

    unavailableReplicas integer

    Total number of unavailable pods targeted by this deployment.

    conditions []DeploymentCondition

    Represents the latest available observations of a deployment's current state.

    observedGeneration integer

    The generation observed by the deployment controller.

    collisionCount integer

    Count of hash collisions for the Deployment. The Deployment controller uses this field as a collision avoidance mechanism.

    Return Value

    Returns a DeploymentStatus object containing the current state information of the deployment including replica counts, conditions, and generation details.

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: Monitor anihpj JobPost Deployment Status

    This example shows how to monitor the deployment status of the anihpj jobpost application and check its health indicators.

    # Check current status of anihpj jobpost deployment
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment -o yaml | grep -A 20 "status:"

# Monitor replica status continuously
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment --watch -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.readyReplicas,AVAILABLE:.status.availableReplicas,UPDATED:.status.updatedReplicas

# Check deployment conditions for troubleshooting
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Progressing")].message}'

# Verify all replicas are available
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment -o jsonpath='{.status.availableReplicas}{" / "}{.spec.replicas}'
    status: availableReplicas: 3 conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:45Z" lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:45Z" message: Deployment has minimum availability. reason: MinimumReplicasAvailable status: "True" type: Available - lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:25:30Z" lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:45Z" message: ReplicaSet "anihpj-jobpost-deployment-7b8f9c5d6" has successfully progressed. reason: NewReplicaSetAvailable status: "True" type: Progressing observedGeneration: 2 readyReplicas: 3 replicas: 3 updatedReplicas: 3 NAME READY AVAILABLE UPDATED anihpj-jobpost-deployment 3 3 3 ReplicaSet "anihpj-jobpost-deployment-7b8f9c5d6" has successfully progressed. 3 / 3
    Example 2: Check anihpj API Deployment Health Status

    This example demonstrates how to check the health status of the anihpj API deployment and identify any issues.

    # Check comprehensive deployment status
kubectl describe deployment anihpj-api-deployment -n anihpj-services | grep -A 15 "Conditions:"

# Get deployment status in JSON format for programmatic analysis
kubectl get deployment anihpj-api-deployment -n anihpj-services -o json | jq '.status'

# Check for any unavailable replicas
kubectl get deployment anihpj-api-deployment -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.unavailableReplicas}'

# Monitor deployment status during rolling update
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-api-deployment -n anihpj-services --watch=true
    Conditions: Type Status Reason ---- ------ ------ Available True MinimumReplicasAvailable Progressing True NewReplicaSetAvailable { "availableReplicas": 5, "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T11:00:15Z", "lastUpdateTime": "2024-01-15T11:00:15Z", "message": "Deployment has minimum availability.", "reason": "MinimumReplicasAvailable", "status": "True", "type": "Available" } ], "observedGeneration": 3, "readyReplicas": 5, "replicas": 5, "updatedReplicas": 5 } Waiting for deployment "anihpj-api-deployment" rollout to finish: 4 of 5 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-api-deployment" successfully rolled out
    Example 3: Troubleshoot anihpj Web Deployment Status Issues

    This example shows how to troubleshoot deployment status issues when the anihpj web application deployment is not progressing as expected.

    # Check for failed deployment conditions
kubectl get deployment anihpj-web-deployment -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.status=="False")].message}'

# Get detailed status with timestamps
kubectl get deployment anihpj-web-deployment -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,GENERATION:.metadata.generation,OBSERVED:.status.observedGeneration,PROGRESSING:.status.conditions[0].status

# Check replica mismatch issues
kubectl get deployment anihpj-web-deployment -o jsonpath='Desired: {.spec.replicas}, Updated: {.status.updatedReplicas}, Ready: {.status.readyReplicas}, Available: {.status.availableReplicas}'

# Get events related to deployment status changes
kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=anihpj-web-deployment --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'
    Deployment exceeded its progress deadline NAME GENERATION OBSERVED PROGRESSING anihpj-web-deployment 3 2 False Desired: 4, Updated: 2, Ready: 2, Available: 2 LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 2m15s Warning ProgressDeadlineExceeded deployment/anihpj-web-deployment progress deadline exceeded 5m30s Normal ScalingReplicaSet deployment/anihpj-web-deployment Scaled up replica set anihpj-web-deployment-abc123 to 4 8m45s Normal ScalingReplicaSet deployment/anihpj-web-deployment Scaled down replica set anihpj-web-deployment-def456 to 2
    Example 4: Monitor anihpj Database Migration Deployment Status

    This example demonstrates monitoring the status of an anihpj database migration deployment during Django migrations.

    # Monitor migration deployment status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-migration-deployment -n anihpj-services --watch -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.readyReplicas,CONDITIONS:.status.conditions[0].type

# Check if migration deployment completed successfully
kubectl get deployment anihpj-migration-deployment -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Progressing")].reason}'

# Get collision count for migration deployment
kubectl get deployment anihpj-migration-deployment -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.collisionCount}'

# Verify migration deployment reached desired state
kubectl wait --for=condition=available --timeout=600s deployment/anihpj-migration-deployment -n anihpj-services
    NAME READY CONDITIONS anihpj-migration-deployment 0 Progressing anihpj-migration-deployment 1 Progressing anihpj-migration-deployment 1 Available NewReplicaSetAvailable 0 deployment.apps/anihpj-migration-deployment condition met
    Example 5: anihpj Canary Deployment Status Validation

    This example shows how to validate the status of a canary deployment for the anihpj application during a staged rollout.

    # Check canary deployment status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-canary-deployment -n anihpj-staging -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq

# Compare canary vs production deployment status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-canary-deployment anihpj-production-deployment -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.readyReplicas,UPDATED:.status.updatedReplicas,AVAILABLE:.status.availableReplicas

# Monitor canary deployment progression
kubectl get deployment anihpj-canary-deployment -n anihpj-staging -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Progressing")].message}'

# Check for any status anomalies in canary
kubectl get deployment anihpj-canary-deployment -n anihpj-staging -o jsonpath='{.status.unavailableReplicas}'
    { "availableReplicas": 2, "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T12:15:30Z", "lastUpdateTime": "2024-01-15T12:15:30Z", "message": "Deployment has minimum availability.", "reason": "MinimumReplicasAvailable", "status": "True", "type": "Available" } ], "observedGeneration": 1, "readyReplicas": 2, "replicas": 2, "updatedReplicas": 2 } NAME READY UPDATED AVAILABLE anihpj-canary-deployment 2 2 2 anihpj-production-deployment 5 5 5 Deployment has minimum availability.

    DeploymentList

    DeploymentList is a list of deployments. It contains an array of Deployment items and metadata about the list itself. This resource is typically used when querying multiple deployments across namespaces or when performing batch operations on deployments.

    API Definition

    Group: apps
    Version: v1
    Kind: DeploymentList
    Scope: Namespaced

    Syntax

    # List all deployments in current namespace
kubectl get deployments

# List all deployments across all namespaces
kubectl get deployments --all-namespaces

# Get deployment list in YAML format
kubectl get deployments -o yaml

# List deployments with specific labels
kubectl get deployments -l app=anihpj

# List deployments with custom columns
kubectl get deployments -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DeploymentList
metadata:
  resourceVersion: "12345"
  selfLink: /apis/apps/v1/deployments
items:
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: Deployment
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-web-deployment
    namespace: anihpj-services
    labels:
      app: anihpj-web
  spec:
    replicas: 3
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj-web
    template:
      # Pod template specification
  status:
    replicas: 3
    readyReplicas: 3
    availableReplicas: 3
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: Deployment
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-api-deployment
    namespace: anihpj-services
    labels:
      app: anihpj-api
  spec:
    replicas: 5
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj-api
    template:
      # Pod template specification
  status:
    replicas: 5
    readyReplicas: 5
    availableReplicas: 5

    Parameters

    apiVersion string

    APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object. Always "apps/v1" for DeploymentList.

    kind string

    Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents. Always "DeploymentList".

    metadata ListMeta

    Standard list metadata containing resource version and self link information.

    items []Deployment

    Items is the list of deployments. Each item is a complete Deployment object.

    Return Value

    Returns a DeploymentList object containing an array of Deployment objects and associated metadata for list operations.

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: List All anihpj Django Deployments

    This example shows how to list all anihpj Django application deployments across different namespaces and environments.

    # List all anihpj deployments with labels
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o wide

# Get deployment list in JSON format for processing
kubectl get deployments -l app.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'

# List deployments with custom columns showing Django-specific info
kubectl get deployments -l framework=django -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,IMAGE:.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image,READY:.status.readyReplicas

# Count total anihpj deployments
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces --no-headers | wc -l
    NAMESPACE NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-services anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d web-container anihpj/web:v2.1.0 app=anihpj-web anihpj-services anihpj-api-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d api-container anihpj/api:v2.1.0 app=anihpj-api anihpj-staging anihpj-staging-deployment 2/2 2 2 1d web-container anihpj/web:v2.2.0-rc1 app=anihpj-web "anihpj-web-deployment" "anihpj-api-deployment" "anihpj-staging-deployment" NAME NAMESPACE REPLICAS IMAGE READY anihpj-web-deployment anihpj-services 3 anihpj/web:v2.1.0 3 anihpj-api-deployment anihpj-services 5 anihpj/api:v2.1.0 5 anihpj-staging-deployment anihpj-staging 2 anihpj/web:v2.2.0-rc1 2 3
    Example 2: Filter anihpj Deployments by Environment

    This example demonstrates how to filter and list anihpj Django deployments based on environment labels and statuses.

    # List production anihpj deployments only
kubectl get deployments -l environment=production,project=anihpj -o yaml

# List staging deployments with specific output format
kubectl get deployments -n anihpj-staging -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'

# Get deployment list filtered by Django version
kubectl get deployments -l django-version=4.2 --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ENV:.metadata.labels.environment,VERSION:.metadata.labels.django-version

# List deployments that are not fully available
kubectl get deployments --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.replicas != .status.availableReplicas) | .metadata.name'
    apiVersion: v1 kind: List metadata: resourceVersion: "" selfLink: "" items: - apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment metadata: labels: environment: production project: anihpj name: anihpj-prod-deployment namespace: anihpj-production spec: replicas: 8 # ... deployment specification anihpj-staging-deployment anihpj-canary-deployment NAME ENV VERSION anihpj-web-deployment production 4.2 anihpj-api-deployment production 4.2 anihpj-staging-deployment staging 4.2 "anihpj-maintenance-deployment"
    Example 3: Monitor anihpj Deployment Health Across Environments

    This example shows how to monitor the health status of all anihpj Django deployments across different environments.

    # Get health status of all anihpj deployments
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,DESIRED:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,AVAILABLE:.status.availableReplicas,STATUS:.status.conditions[0].type

# List deployments with unhealthy status
kubectl get deployments --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.metadata.labels.project == "anihpj" and (.status.replicas != .status.readyReplicas)) | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, replicas: .status.replicas, ready: .status.readyReplicas}'

# Watch deployment list changes in real-time
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces --watch

# Generate deployment health report
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.metadata.namespace}{"\t"}{.status.readyReplicas}{"/"}{.spec.replicas}{"\n"}{end}'
    NAME NAMESPACE DESIRED READY AVAILABLE STATUS anihpj-web-deployment anihpj-services 3 3 3 Available anihpj-api-deployment anihpj-services 5 5 5 Available anihpj-staging-deployment anihpj-staging 2 2 2 Available anihpj-canary-deployment anihpj-staging 1 0 0 Progressing { "name": "anihpj-canary-deployment", "namespace": "anihpj-staging", "replicas": 1, "ready": 0 } NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d anihpj-api-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d anihpj-staging-deployment 2/2 2 2 1d anihpj-canary-deployment 0/1 1 0 5m anihpj-web-deployment anihpj-services 3/3 anihpj-api-deployment anihpj-services 5/5 anihpj-staging-deployment anihpj-staging 2/2 anihpj-canary-deployment anihpj-staging 0/1
    Example 4: Batch Operations on anihpj Deployment Lists

    This example demonstrates performing batch operations on a list of anihpj Django deployments for maintenance tasks.

    # Scale all anihpj deployments in staging environment
kubectl get deployments -n anihpj-staging -l project=anihpj -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl scale {} --replicas=1

# Update image for all anihpj web deployments
kubectl get deployments -l app=anihpj-web --all-namespaces -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}{" "}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}{end}' | while read namespace name; do kubectl set image deployment/$name -n $namespace web-container=anihpj/web:v2.3.0; done

# Apply resource limits to all anihpj deployments
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o json > anihpj-deployments-backup.json

# Get deployment list for backup purposes
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o yaml > anihpj-deployments-$(date +%Y%m%d).yaml
    deployment.apps/anihpj-staging-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-canary-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment image updated deployment.apps/anihpj-staging-deployment image updated # Backup file created successfully # Backup file anihpj-deployments-20240115.yaml created
    Example 5: anihpj Deployment Resource Analysis
