    This example shows how to analyze resource usage and configuration across all anihpj Django deployments for optimization.

    # Analyze resource requests across all anihpj deployments
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, containers: [.spec.template.spec.containers[] | {name: .name, requests: .resources.requests, limits: .resources.limits}]}'

# List deployments by creation date
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp

# Get deployment list with revision history information
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,REVISION:.metadata.generation,HISTORY_LIMIT:.spec.revisionHistoryLimit

# Analyze deployment strategies across anihpj applications
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.strategy.type}{"\t"}{.spec.strategy.rollingUpdate.maxUnavailable}{"\t"}{.spec.strategy.rollingUpdate.maxSurge}{"\n"}{end}'
    { "name": "anihpj-web-deployment", "namespace": "anihpj-services", "containers": [ { "name": "web-container", "requests": { "cpu": "200m", "memory": "256Mi" }, "limits": { "cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi" } } ] } NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 3d anihpj-api-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d anihpj-staging-deployment 2/2 2 2 1d anihpj-canary-deployment 1/1 1 1 6h NAME NAMESPACE REVISION HISTORY_LIMIT anihpj-web-deployment anihpj-services 3 10 anihpj-api-deployment anihpj-services 2 10 anihpj-staging-deployment anihpj-staging 1 5 anihpj-web-deployment RollingUpdate 1 1 anihpj-api-deployment RollingUpdate 25% 25% anihpj-staging-deployment RollingUpdate 0 1

    Operations

    This section covers the available operations for Deployment resources in Kubernetes. These operations allow you to create, read, update, delete, and manage Deployment objects through the Kubernetes API.

    Create

    Create a new Deployment

    Read

    Get Deployment information

    Update

    Update Deployment configuration

    Patch

    Partially update Deployment

    Delete

    Remove Deployment

    List

    List all Deployments

    Watch

    Watch Deployment changes

    Scale

    Scale Deployment replicas

    Rollout

    Manage Deployment rollouts

    Create Deployment

    Create a new Deployment resource in the specified namespace. This operation allows you to define a new deployment with the desired pod template and replica configuration.

    HTTP Request

    POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments

    kubectl Syntax

    # Create deployment from YAML file
kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml

# Create deployment directly with kubectl create
kubectl create deployment my-deployment --image=nginx:1.21 --replicas=3

# Create deployment with specific namespace
kubectl create deployment my-deployment --image=nginx:1.21 -n my-namespace

# Create deployment with resource limits
kubectl create deployment my-deployment --image=nginx:1.21 --replicas=3 --requests=cpu=100m,memory=128Mi

# Create deployment with environment variables
kubectl create deployment my-deployment --image=nginx:1.21 --env="ENV=production"

    Django Example

    Create an anihpj Django deployment for the jobpost application with proper configuration.

    # Create anihpj jobpost deployment
kubectl create deployment anihpj-jobpost --image=anihpj/jobpost:v1.2.0 --replicas=3 -n anihpj-services --env="DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings"

# Verify deployment creation
kubectl get deployment anihpj-jobpost -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost created NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-jobpost 3/3 3 3 30s
    Example 2: Skills API Service Deployment

    This example demonstrates creating a Deployment for the Skills API service (/jobpost/skills/) with specific resource limits and health checks.

    # Create Skills API deployment with resource limits
kubectl create deployment anihpj-skills-api --image=anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 --replicas=2 --port=8000 --env="MODEL_FOCUS=skills" --env="API_ENDPOINT=/jobpost/skills/" --requests=cpu=200m,memory=256Mi --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi

# Apply deployment and check status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-skills-api -o wide
    deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-api created NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-skills-api 2/2 2 2 45s jobpost-app anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 app=anihpj-skills-api
    Example 3: Author Management Deployment

    This example shows deploying the Author management service with database connection and authentication integration.

    # Create Author management deployment with database configuration
kubectl create deployment anihpj-author-mgmt --image=anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 --replicas=3 --env="MODEL_FOCUS=author" --env="DB_PATH=/app/db.sqlite3" --env="DJANGO_DEBUG=False"

# Scale and verify the deployment
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-author-mgmt --replicas=4 && kubectl get deployment anihpj-author-mgmt
    deployment.apps/anihpj-author-mgmt created deployment.apps/anihpj-author-mgmt scaled NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-author-mgmt 4/4 4 4 1m
    Example 4: Location Service with ConfigMap

    This example illustrates creating a Location service deployment with external configuration using ConfigMaps for environment-specific settings.

    # Create ConfigMap for location service first
kubectl create configmap location-config --from-literal=GEO_API_KEY=demo-key --from-literal=LOCATION_CACHE_TTL=3600

# Create Location service deployment referencing the ConfigMap
kubectl create deployment anihpj-location-svc --image=anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 --replicas=2 --env="MODEL_FOCUS=location"
    configmap/location-config created deployment.apps/anihpj-location-svc created
    Example 5: JobPost Main API with Persistent Storage

    This example demonstrates deploying the main JobPost API service with persistent volume claims for SQLite database storage and comprehensive configuration.

    # Create PVC for SQLite database persistence
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-sqlite-pvc
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
EOF

# Create main JobPost API deployment with PVC
kubectl create deployment anihpj-jobpost-main --image=anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 --replicas=4 --env="DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///data/db.sqlite3" --env="DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=anihpj.settings"
    persistentvolumeclaim/anihpj-sqlite-pvc created deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-main created

    Read Deployment

    Retrieve information about a specific Deployment resource, including its current status, configuration, and metadata.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Get deployment details
kubectl get deployment my-deployment

# Get deployment in YAML format
kubectl get deployment my-deployment -o yaml

# Get deployment in JSON format
kubectl get deployment my-deployment -o json

# Describe deployment with detailed information
kubectl describe deployment my-deployment

# Get specific field from deployment
kubectl get deployment my-deployment -o jsonpath='{.status.replicas}'

    Django Example

    Read anihpj Django deployment information to check status and configuration.

    # Get anihpj web deployment details
kubectl get deployment anihpj-web-deployment -n anihpj-services -o wide

# Describe anihpj API deployment
kubectl describe deployment anihpj-api-deployment -n anihpj-services
    NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d web anihpj/web:v2.1.0 app=anihpj-web Name: anihpj-api-deployment Namespace: anihpj-services CreationTimestamp: Mon, 13 Jan 2024 10:30:45 +0000 Labels: app=anihpj-api Annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: 2 Selector: app=anihpj-api Replicas: 5 desired | 5 updated | 5 total | 5 available | 0 unavailable
    Example 2: Skills API Deployment Status

    This example shows how to read and monitor the Skills API deployment status including replica information and rollout history.

    # Get Skills API deployment with detailed status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-skills-api -o yaml | grep -A 10 "status:"

# Check rollout status and history
kubectl rollout status deployment anihpj-skills-api
    status: availableReplicas: 2 conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-09-08T10:30:00Z" lastUpdateTime: "2025-09-08T10:30:00Z" message: Deployment has minimum availability. reason: MinimumReplicasAvailable status: "True" type: Available observedGeneration: 1 readyReplicas: 2 replicas: 2 updatedReplicas: 2 deployment "anihpj-skills-api" successfully rolled out
    Example 3: Author Management Deployment Details

    This example demonstrates reading comprehensive deployment details for the Author management service including container specifications and environment variables.

    # Get Author management deployment configuration
kubectl get deployment anihpj-author-mgmt -o json | jq '.spec.template.spec.containers[0]'

# Check deployment events and recent changes
kubectl describe deployment anihpj-author-mgmt | tail -10
    { "env": [ { "name": "MODEL_FOCUS", "value": "author" }, { "name": "DB_PATH", "value": "/app/db.sqlite3" } ], "image": "anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0", "name": "jobpost-app", "ports": [ { "containerPort": 8000, "protocol": "TCP" } ] } Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal ScalingReplicaSet 5m deployment-controller Scaled up replica set anihpj-author-mgmt-7b8c9d5f4 to 4
    Example 4: Location Service Resource Usage

    This example shows how to read Location service deployment resource usage and performance metrics for monitoring purposes.

    # Get Location service deployment resource specifications
kubectl get deployment anihpj-location-svc -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].resources}' | python -m json.tool

# Check actual resource usage of deployment pods
kubectl top pods -l app=anihpj-location-svc
    { "limits": { "cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi" }, "requests": { "cpu": "200m", "memory": "256Mi" } } NAME CPU(cores) MEMORY(bytes) anihpj-location-svc-6b5c8d9f2a-abc12 45m 180Mi anihpj-location-svc-6b5c8d9f2a-def34 52m 195Mi
    Example 5: JobPost Main API Comprehensive Status

    This example demonstrates reading comprehensive status information for the main JobPost API deployment including conditions, replica sets, and pod details.

    # Get comprehensive JobPost main API deployment information
kubectl describe deployment anihpj-jobpost-main

# Check associated replica sets and pods
kubectl get rs,pods -l app=anihpj-jobpost-main
    Name: anihpj-jobpost-main Namespace: default CreationTimestamp: Mon, 08 Sep 2025 10:15:30 +0000 Labels: app=anihpj-jobpost-main Annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: 1 Selector: app=anihpj-jobpost-main Replicas: 4 desired | 4 updated | 4 total | 4 available | 0 unavailable NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE anihpj-jobpost-main-7b8c9d5f4-abc12 1/1 Running 0 10m anihpj-jobpost-main-7b8c9d5f4-def34 1/1 Running 0 10m anihpj-jobpost-main-7b8c9d5f4-ghi56 1/1 Running 0 10m anihpj-jobpost-main-7b8c9d5f4-jkl78 1/1 Running 0 10m

    Update Deployment

    Update a Deployment resource by replacing the entire resource definition. This triggers a rolling update of the pods managed by the deployment.

    HTTP Request

    PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Update deployment from modified YAML file
kubectl apply -f updated-deployment.yaml

# Update deployment image
kubectl set image deployment/my-deployment container-name=new-image:tag

# Update deployment with environment variables
kubectl set env deployment/my-deployment ENV=production

# Update deployment resources
kubectl set resources deployment my-deployment --limits=cpu=200m,memory=256Mi --requests=cpu=100m,memory=128Mi

# Replace deployment entirely
kubectl replace -f deployment.yaml

    Django Example

    Update anihpj Django deployment with new image version and configuration changes.

    # Update anihpj web deployment image
kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-web-deployment web-container=anihpj/web:v2.2.0 -n anihpj-services

# Update anihpj API deployment environment
kubectl set env deployment/anihpj-api-deployment DEBUG=False -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment image updated deployment.apps/anihpj-api-deployment env updated
    Example 2: JobPost Service Update

    Update anihpj JobPost service deployment with new image version and resource limits for handling increased job posting volume.

    # Update JobPost deployment image to latest version
kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-jobpost-deployment jobpost-container=anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 -n anihpj-services

# Update JobPost deployment resource limits for better performance
kubectl set resources deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment image updated deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment resource requirements updated
    Example 3: Skills Service Rolling Update

    Perform rolling update on anihpj Skills service deployment with modified application configuration and environment variables.

    # Update Skills deployment with new database connection settings
kubectl set env deployment/anihpj-skills-deployment DB_HOST=skills-db.anihpj-services.svc.cluster.local DB_PORT=5432 -n anihpj-services

# Apply updated Skills deployment configuration from file
kubectl apply -f /deployments/anihpj-skills-deployment-v2.yaml
    deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-deployment env updated deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-deployment configured
    Example 4: Author Service Multi-field Update

    Update anihpj Author service deployment with multiple changes including image version, environment variables, and replica count.

    # Update Author deployment image and set production environment
kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-author-deployment author-container=anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.8.0 -n anihpj-services
kubectl set env deployment/anihpj-author-deployment ENVIRONMENT=production CACHE_ENABLED=true -n anihpj-services

# Replace Author deployment with updated configuration
kubectl replace -f /deployments/anihpj-author-deployment-prod.yaml
    deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment image updated deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment env updated deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment replaced
    Example 5: Location Service Security Update

    Update anihpj Location service deployment to apply security patches and update container registry configurations.

    # Update Location deployment with security-patched image
kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-location-deployment location-container=anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.1-security -n anihpj-services

# Update Location deployment with new security environment variables
kubectl set env deployment/anihpj-location-deployment SSL_ENABLED=true CORS_ORIGINS=https://anihpj.com,https://app.anihpj.com -n anihpj-services

# Verify the update rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-location-deployment -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment image updated deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment env updated Waiting for deployment "anihpj-location-deployment" rollout to finish: 2 of 3 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-location-deployment" successfully rolled out

    Patch Deployment

    Partially update a Deployment resource by modifying specific fields without replacing the entire resource definition.

    HTTP Request

    PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Patch deployment replicas
kubectl patch deployment my-deployment -p '{"spec":{"replicas":5}}'

# Patch deployment strategy
kubectl patch deployment my-deployment -p '{"spec":{"strategy":{"type":"RollingUpdate","rollingUpdate":{"maxSurge":2}}}}'

# Patch deployment with merge strategy
kubectl patch deployment my-deployment --type merge -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"app","image":"new-image:tag"}]}}}}'

# Patch deployment annotations
kubectl patch deployment my-deployment -p '{"metadata":{"annotations":{"deployment.kubernetes.io/revision":"3"}}}'

# Patch deployment with JSON file
kubectl patch deployment my-deployment --patch-file patch.json

    Django Example

    Patch anihpj Django deployment to modify specific configuration without full replacement.

    # Patch anihpj jobpost deployment replicas
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment -p '{"spec":{"replicas":6}}' -n anihpj-services

# Patch anihpj API deployment strategy
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-api-deployment -p '{"spec":{"strategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"maxUnavailable":"0"}}}}' -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment patched deployment.apps/anihpj-api-deployment patched
    Example 2: Skills Service Annotation Update

    Patch anihpj Skills service deployment to add monitoring annotations and update container image without full deployment replacement.

    # Patch Skills deployment to add monitoring annotations
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-skills-deployment -p '{"metadata":{"annotations":{"monitoring.anihpj.com/scrape":"true","monitoring.anihpj.com/path":"/metrics"}}}' -n anihpj-services

# Patch Skills deployment container image using merge strategy
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-skills-deployment --type merge -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"skills-container","image":"anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.9.0"}]}}}}' -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-deployment patched deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-deployment patched
    Example 3: Author Service Resource Patching

    Patch anihpj Author service deployment to modify resource requests and limits for optimized performance.

    # Patch Author deployment resource limits using JSON patch
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-author-deployment --type json -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources/limits/memory","value":"1Gi"}]' -n anihpj-services

# Patch Author deployment to add resource requests
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-author-deployment -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"author-container","resources":{"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"256Mi"}}}]}}}}' -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment patched deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment patched
    Example 4: Location Service Strategy Configuration

    Patch anihpj Location service deployment to modify rolling update strategy and add environment-specific labels.

    # Patch Location deployment rolling update strategy
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-location-deployment -p '{"spec":{"strategy":{"type":"RollingUpdate","rollingUpdate":{"maxSurge":"2","maxUnavailable":"1"}}}}' -n anihpj-services

# Patch Location deployment to add environment labels
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-location-deployment -p '{"spec":{"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"environment":"production","version":"v2.1.0"}}}}}' -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment patched deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment patched
    Example 5: JobPost Service Volume and Secret Patching

    Patch anihpj JobPost service deployment to add volume mounts and update secret references using JSON patch file.

    # Create patch file for JobPost deployment volume configuration
cat > jobpost-volume-patch.json << 'EOF'
{
  "spec": {
    "template": {
      "spec": {
        "volumes": [
          {
            "name": "jobpost-storage",
            "persistentVolumeClaim": {
              "claimName": "jobpost-pvc"
            }
          }
        ],
        "containers": [
          {
            "name": "jobpost-container",
            "volumeMounts": [
              {
                "name": "jobpost-storage",
                "mountPath": "/app/uploads"
              }
            ]
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
EOF

# Apply patch file to JobPost deployment
kubectl patch deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment --patch-file jobpost-volume-patch.json -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment patched

    Delete Deployment

    Delete a Deployment resource and its associated ReplicaSets and Pods. This operation removes the deployment and all resources it manages.

    HTTP Request

    DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Delete deployment
kubectl delete deployment my-deployment

# Delete deployment with grace period
kubectl delete deployment my-deployment --grace-period=30

# Delete deployment immediately
kubectl delete deployment my-deployment --force --grace-period=0

# Delete deployment from file
kubectl delete -f deployment.yaml

# Delete multiple deployments
kubectl delete deployment deployment1 deployment2 deployment3

    Django Example

    Delete anihpj Django deployment safely with proper cleanup procedures.

    # Delete anihpj staging deployment
kubectl delete deployment anihpj-staging-deployment -n anihpj-staging

# Delete anihpj canary deployment with grace period
kubectl delete deployment anihpj-canary-deployment --grace-period=60 -n anihpj-staging
    deployment.apps "anihpj-staging-deployment" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-canary-deployment" deleted
    Example 2: Development Environment Cleanup

    Delete anihpj development deployments during environment cleanup while preserving data volumes.

    # Delete multiple anihpj development service deployments
kubectl delete deployment anihpj-skills-dev anihpj-author-dev anihpj-location-dev -n anihpj-development

# Delete anihpj jobpost development deployment with confirmation
kubectl delete deployment anihpj-jobpost-dev-deployment -n anihpj-development --wait=true
    deployment.apps "anihpj-skills-dev" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-author-dev" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-location-dev" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-jobpost-dev-deployment" deleted
    Example 3: Force Delete Stuck Deployment

    Force delete anihpj deployment that is stuck in terminating state due to resource conflicts.

    # Attempt graceful deletion of problematic deployment
kubectl delete deployment anihpj-legacy-deployment -n anihpj-services --grace-period=30

# Force delete if graceful deletion fails
kubectl delete deployment anihpj-legacy-deployment -n anihpj-services --force --grace-period=0
    deployment.apps "anihpj-legacy-deployment" deleted
    Example 4: Selective Service Deletion

    Delete specific anihpj service deployments based on labels and environment criteria.

    # Delete anihpj deployments with specific label selector
kubectl delete deployment -l component=api,version=v1.0 -n anihpj-services

# Delete anihpj test environment deployments from YAML files
kubectl delete -f /deployments/anihpj-test-deployments.yaml
    deployment.apps "anihpj-api-v1-deployment" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-skills-test-deployment" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-author-test-deployment" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-location-test-deployment" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-jobpost-test-deployment" deleted
    Example 5: Complete Environment Teardown

    Complete teardown of anihpj staging environment including all service deployments and related resources.

    # Delete all anihpj staging deployments with cascade policy
kubectl delete deployment --all -n anihpj-staging --cascade=foreground

# Verify all deployments are deleted
kubectl get deployments -n anihpj-staging

# Clean up any remaining replica sets
kubectl delete replicaset --all -n anihpj-staging
    deployment.apps "anihpj-web-staging" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-api-staging" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-skills-staging" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-author-staging" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-location-staging" deleted deployment.apps "anihpj-jobpost-staging" deleted No resources found in anihpj-staging namespace. No resources found in anihpj-staging namespace.

    List Deployments

    Retrieve a list of all Deployment resources in a namespace or across all namespaces. This operation returns a DeploymentList object.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments

    kubectl Syntax

    # List deployments in current namespace
kubectl get deployments

# List deployments in all namespaces
kubectl get deployments --all-namespaces

# List deployments with labels
kubectl get deployments -l app=myapp

# List deployments with custom output
kubectl get deployments -o wide

# List deployments with custom columns
kubectl get deployments -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas

    Django Example

    List all anihpj Django deployments across different environments and namespaces.

    # List all anihpj deployments
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces

# List anihpj production deployments
kubectl get deployments -l environment=production -n anihpj-production
    NAMESPACE NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-services anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d anihpj-services anihpj-api-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d anihpj-staging anihpj-staging-deployment 2/2 2 2 1d NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-prod-deployment 8/8 8 8 3d
    Example 2: Service-Specific Deployment Listing

    List anihpj service deployments filtered by component type and display detailed resource information.

    # List anihpj API-related deployments with wide output
kubectl get deployments -l component=api -o wide -n anihpj-services

# List anihpj Skills service deployments with custom columns
kubectl get deployments -l service=skills -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,IMAGE:.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image --all-namespaces
    NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-api-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d api-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.5.0 app=anihpj-api anihpj-auth-api-deployment 3/3 3 3 1d auth-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.8.0 app=anihpj-auth-api NAME REPLICAS READY IMAGE anihpj-skills-deployment 4 4 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.3.0 anihpj-skills-staging 2 2 anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.3.1-rc
    Example 3: Cross-Environment Deployment Overview

    Get comprehensive overview of anihpj deployments across multiple environments and namespaces with sorting and filtering.

    # List all anihpj deployments sorted by creation timestamp
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp

# List anihpj deployments with specific status and resource usage
kubectl get deployments -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,AVAILABLE:.status.availableReplicas,CPU-REQ:.spec.template.spec.containers[0].resources.requests.cpu --all-namespaces -l project=anihpj
    NAMESPACE NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-development anihpj-skills-dev 1/1 1 1 3h anihpj-development anihpj-author-dev 1/1 1 1 3h anihpj-staging anihpj-web-staging 2/2 2 2 1d anihpj-staging anihpj-api-staging 3/3 3 3 1d anihpj-services anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d anihpj-services anihpj-api-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d anihpj-production anihpj-prod-deployment 8/8 8 8 3d NAME NAMESPACE REPLICAS AVAILABLE CPU-REQ anihpj-web-deployment anihpj-services 3 3 200m anihpj-api-deployment anihpj-services 5 5 300m anihpj-prod-deployment anihpj-production 8 8 400m
    Example 4: Health Status and Resource Monitoring

    Monitor anihpj deployment health status and resource utilization with detailed output formatting.

    # List anihpj deployments with health status and conditions
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.readyReplicas/.spec.replicas,UP-TO-DATE:.status.updatedReplicas,AVAILABLE:.status.availableReplicas,CONDITIONS:.status.conditions[*].type --all-namespaces

# Show anihpj deployments with JSON output for programmatic processing
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj -o json --all-namespaces | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, replicas: .spec.replicas, ready: .status.readyReplicas}'
    NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE CONDITIONS anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 Available,Progressing anihpj-api-deployment 5/5 5 5 Available,Progressing anihpj-skills-deployment 4/4 4 4 Available,Progressing anihpj-author-deployment 2/2 2 2 Available,Progressing anihpj-location-deployment 3/3 3 3 Available,Progressing anihpj-jobpost-deployment 6/6 6 6 Available,Progressing { "name": "anihpj-web-deployment", "namespace": "anihpj-services", "replicas": 3, "ready": 3 }
    Example 5: Environment-Specific Filtering and Output

    Filter anihpj deployments by environment labels and display comprehensive deployment information for operational monitoring.

    # List anihpj production deployments with detailed resource information
kubectl get deployments -l environment=production,project=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,STRATEGY:.spec.strategy.type,IMAGE:.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image,CREATED:.metadata.creationTimestamp --all-namespaces

# List anihpj deployments with selector and annotation information
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,SELECTOR:.spec.selector.matchLabels,ANNOTATIONS:.metadata.annotations --all-namespaces
    NAME REPLICAS STRATEGY IMAGE CREATED anihpj-prod-deployment 8 RollingUpdate anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.0.0 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z anihpj-prod-api 5 RollingUpdate anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.8.0 2024-01-15T11:15:00Z NAME NAMESPACE SELECTOR ANNOTATIONS anihpj-web-deployment anihpj-services map[app:anihpj-web component:frontend] map[deployment.kubernetes.io/revision:3] anihpj-api-deployment anihpj-services map[app:anihpj-api component:backend] map[deployment.kubernetes.io/revision:2] anihpj-skills-deployment anihpj-services map[app:anihpj-skills service:skills] map[deployment.kubernetes.io/revision:1] anihpj-author-deployment anihpj-services map[app:anihpj-author service:author] map[deployment.kubernetes.io/revision:1] anihpj-location-deployment anihpj-services map[app:anihpj-location service:location] map[deployment.kubernetes.io/revision:2] anihpj-jobpost-deployment anihpj-services map[app:anihpj-jobpost service:jobpost] map[deployment.kubernetes.io/revision:4]

    Watch Deployments

    Watch for changes to Deployment resources in real-time. This operation establishes a persistent connection to receive updates as deployments are modified.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments?watch=true

    kubectl Syntax

    # Watch deployments for changes
kubectl get deployments --watch

# Watch specific deployment
kubectl get deployment my-deployment --watch

# Watch deployments with output format
kubectl get deployments --watch -o wide

# Watch deployments in all namespaces
kubectl get deployments --all-namespaces --watch

# Watch with custom columns
kubectl get deployments --watch -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas

    Django Example

    Watch anihpj Django deployments for real-time monitoring during updates and scaling operations.

    # Watch anihpj web deployment
kubectl get deployment anihpj-web-deployment --watch -n anihpj-services

# Watch all anihpj deployments
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --watch --all-namespaces
    NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d anihpj-web-deployment 3/5 5 3 2d anihpj-web-deployment 4/5 5 4 2d anihpj-web-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d
    Example 2: Multi-Service Watch with Filtering

    Watch multiple anihpj service deployments simultaneously with label filtering to monitor coordinated updates.

    # Watch all anihpj service deployments with specific labels
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj,component=service --watch --all-namespaces

# Watch anihpj deployments with custom output format showing resource usage
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --watch -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,IMAGE:.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image --all-namespaces
    NAMESPACE NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-services anihpj-skills-deployment 4/4 4 4 1d anihpj-services anihpj-author-deployment 2/2 2 2 1d anihpj-services anihpj-location-deployment 3/3 3 3 1d anihpj-services anihpj-jobpost-deployment 6/6 6 6 1d anihpj-services anihpj-skills-deployment 4/6 6 4 1d anihpj-services anihpj-skills-deployment 5/6 6 5 1d anihpj-services anihpj-skills-deployment 6/6 6 6 1d
    Example 3: Production Rollout Monitoring

    Watch anihpj production deployments during rolling updates to ensure smooth deployment transitions.

    # Watch anihpj production deployment with detailed status
kubectl get deployment anihpj-prod-deployment --watch -o wide -n anihpj-production

# Watch all production deployments with timestamps
kubectl get deployments -l environment=production --watch --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,READY:.status.readyReplicas/.spec.replicas,STATUS:.status.conditions[-1].type,TIMESTAMP:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-prod-deployment 8/8 8 8 3d prod-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.0.0 app=anihpj-prod anihpj-prod-deployment 8/8 8 8 3d prod-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 app=anihpj-prod anihpj-prod-deployment 8/8 8 8 3d prod-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 app=anihpj-prod anihpj-prod-deployment 8/10 10 8 3d prod-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 app=anihpj-prod anihpj-prod-deployment 9/10 10 9 3d prod-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 app=anihpj-prod anihpj-prod-deployment 10/10 10 10 3d prod-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 app=anihpj-prod
    Example 4: Development Environment Watch

    Monitor anihpj development deployments with focused watching for rapid iteration and testing cycles.

    # Watch anihpj development namespace deployments
kubectl get deployments --watch -n anihpj-development

# Watch specific anihpj development deployment with continuous monitoring
kubectl get deployment anihpj-skills-dev --watch -o yaml -n anihpj-development
    NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-skills-dev 1/1 1 1 3h anihpj-author-dev 1/1 1 1 3h anihpj-location-dev 1/1 1 1 3h anihpj-jobpost-dev 1/1 1 1 3h anihpj-skills-dev 0/1 1 0 3h anihpj-skills-dev 1/1 1 1 3h
    Example 5: Cross-Namespace Deployment Monitoring

    Watch anihpj deployments across all namespaces to monitor system-wide deployment health and updates.

    # Watch all anihpj deployments across environments with wide output
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --watch --all-namespaces -o wide

# Watch anihpj deployments with JSON output for external monitoring tools
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --watch --all-namespaces -o json
    NAMESPACE NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-development anihpj-skills-dev 1/1 1 1 3h skills-dev anihpj/jobpost-app:dev app=anihpj-skills-dev anihpj-staging anihpj-web-staging 2/2 2 2 1d web-staging anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.1.0 app=anihpj-web-staging anihpj-services anihpj-web-deployment 3/3 3 3 2d web-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.2.0 app=anihpj-web anihpj-production anihpj-prod-deployment 10/10 10 10 3d prod-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 app=anihpj-prod anihpj-services anihpj-api-deployment 5/7 7 5 2d api-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.6.0 app=anihpj-api anihpj-services anihpj-api-deployment 6/7 7 6 2d api-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.6.0 app=anihpj-api anihpj-services anihpj-api-deployment 7/7 7 7 2d api-container anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.6.0 app=anihpj-api

    Scale Deployment

    Scale a Deployment by changing the number of replicas. This operation modifies the replica count and triggers the creation or deletion of pods as needed.

    HTTP Request

    PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{name}/scale

    kubectl Syntax

    # Scale deployment to specific replica count
kubectl scale deployment my-deployment --replicas=5

# Scale deployment with condition
kubectl scale deployment my-deployment --replicas=3 --current-replicas=5

# Scale multiple deployments
kubectl scale deployment deployment1 deployment2 --replicas=3

# Auto-scale deployment (requires HPA)
kubectl autoscale deployment my-deployment --min=1 --max=10 --cpu-percent=80

# Scale to zero (stop all pods)
kubectl scale deployment my-deployment --replicas=0

    Django Example

    Scale anihpj Django deployments based on traffic and resource requirements.

    # Scale anihpj web deployment for high traffic
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-web-deployment --replicas=8 -n anihpj-services

# Scale down anihpj staging deployment
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-staging-deployment --replicas=1 -n anihpj-staging
    deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-staging-deployment scaled
    Example 2: Dynamic Load-Based Scaling

    Scale anihpj service deployments based on traffic patterns and resource utilization metrics.

    # Scale anihpj API deployment for peak traffic hours
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-api-deployment --replicas=12 -n anihpj-services

# Scale anihpj JobPost deployment with conditional scaling
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-jobpost-deployment --replicas=8 --current-replicas=6 -n anihpj-services

# Set up auto-scaling for anihpj Skills deployment
kubectl autoscale deployment anihpj-skills-deployment --min=2 --max=15 --cpu-percent=75 -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-api-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment scaled horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling/anihpj-skills-deployment autoscaled
    Example 3: Multi-Service Coordinated Scaling

    Scale multiple anihpj service deployments simultaneously to maintain proper service ratios and dependencies.

    # Scale multiple anihpj service deployments together
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-skills-deployment anihpj-author-deployment anihpj-location-deployment --replicas=4 -n anihpj-services

# Scale anihpj web deployment to zero for maintenance
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-web-deployment --replicas=0 -n anihpj-services

# Scale anihpj web deployment back up after maintenance
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-web-deployment --replicas=5 -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment scaled
    Example 4: Environment-Specific Scaling Strategies

    Apply different scaling strategies for anihpj deployments based on environment requirements and resource constraints.

    # Scale anihpj development deployments to minimal resources
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-skills-dev anihpj-author-dev anihpj-location-dev anihpj-jobpost-dev --replicas=1 -n anihpj-development

# Scale anihpj staging environment for load testing
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-web-staging anihpj-api-staging --replicas=6 -n anihpj-staging

# Configure auto-scaling for anihpj production API
kubectl autoscale deployment anihpj-prod-api --min=5 --max=20 --cpu-percent=60 -n anihpj-production
    deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-dev scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-author-dev scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-location-dev scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-dev scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-web-staging scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-api-staging scaled horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling/anihpj-prod-api autoscaled
    Example 5: Emergency Scaling and Resource Management

    Handle emergency scaling scenarios for anihpj deployments during traffic spikes or resource constraints.

    # Emergency scale up anihpj production deployment during traffic spike
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-prod-deployment --replicas=20 -n anihpj-production

# Emergency scale down all anihpj non-critical services during resource shortage
kubectl scale deployment anihpj-location-deployment anihpj-author-deployment --replicas=1 -n anihpj-services

# Verify scaling status and resource utilization
kubectl get deployments -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces
kubectl top pods -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces
    deployment.apps/anihpj-prod-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment scaled deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment scaled NAMESPACE NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-production anihpj-prod-deployment 20/20 20 20 3d anihpj-services anihpj-web-deployment 5/5 5 5 2d anihpj-services anihpj-api-deployment 12/12 12 12 2d anihpj-services anihpj-skills-deployment 4/4 4 4 1d anihpj-services anihpj-author-deployment 1/1 1 1 1d anihpj-services anihpj-location-deployment 1/1 1 1 1d NAME CPU(cores) MEMORY(bytes) anihpj-prod-deployment-78c9d8b567-abc12 150m 512Mi anihpj-web-deployment-6b8f7d9c45-def34 95m 256Mi

    Rollout Management

    Manage Deployment rollouts including checking status, history, pausing, resuming, and rolling back to previous versions.

    kubectl Syntax

    # Check rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-deployment

# View rollout history
kubectl rollout history deployment/my-deployment

# Pause rollout
kubectl rollout pause deployment/my-deployment

# Resume rollout
kubectl rollout resume deployment/my-deployment

# Rollback to previous version
kubectl rollout undo deployment/my-deployment

# Rollback to specific revision
kubectl rollout undo deployment/my-deployment --to-revision=2

# Restart deployment (rolling restart)
kubectl rollout restart deployment/my-deployment

    Django Example

    Manage anihpj Django deployment rollouts for safe updates and quick rollbacks.

    # Check anihpj API deployment rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-api-deployment -n anihpj-services

# View anihpj web deployment history
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj-web-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Rollback anihpj web deployment
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj-web-deployment -n anihpj-services
    Waiting for deployment "anihpj-api-deployment" rollout to finish: 3 of 5 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-api-deployment" successfully rolled out REVISION CHANGE-CAUSE 1 2 kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-web-deployment web-container=anihpj/web:v2.1.0 3 kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-web-deployment web-container=anihpj/web:v2.2.0 deployment.apps/anihpj-web-deployment rolled back
    Example 2: JobPost Service Rollout with Pause and Resume

    Manage anihpj JobPost service rollout with strategic pausing to validate deployment stages and resume safely.

    # Check rollout status of JobPost deployment
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-jobpost-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Pause JobPost deployment rollout for validation
kubectl rollout pause deployment/anihpj-jobpost-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Resume JobPost deployment rollout after validation
kubectl rollout resume deployment/anihpj-jobpost-deployment -n anihpj-services

# View detailed rollout history
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj-jobpost-deployment -n anihpj-services --revision=3
    Waiting for deployment "anihpj-jobpost-deployment" rollout to finish: 4 of 6 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-jobpost-deployment" successfully rolled out deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment paused deployment.apps/anihpj-jobpost-deployment resumed REVISION CHANGE-CAUSE 3 kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-jobpost-deployment jobpost-container=anihpj/jobpost-app:v3.1.0 --record=true
    Example 3: Skills Service Emergency Rollback

    Execute emergency rollback procedures for anihpj Skills service deployment when issues are detected in production.

    # Check Skills deployment rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-skills-deployment -n anihpj-services

# View rollout history to identify stable version
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj-skills-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Emergency rollback to previous working version
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj-skills-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Rollback to specific known-good revision
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj-skills-deployment --to-revision=2 -n anihpj-services
    Waiting for deployment "anihpj-skills-deployment" rollout to finish: 2 of 4 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-skills-deployment" successfully rolled out REVISION CHANGE-CAUSE 1 2 kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-skills-deployment skills-container=anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.3.0 3 kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-skills-deployment skills-container=anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.4.0 deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-deployment rolled back deployment.apps/anihpj-skills-deployment rolled back
    Example 4: Author Service Rolling Restart and History Management

    Perform rolling restart of anihpj Author service deployment and manage rollout history for better tracking.

    # Trigger rolling restart of Author deployment without changing image
kubectl rollout restart deployment/anihpj-author-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Monitor the restart progress
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-author-deployment -n anihpj-services --timeout=300s

# View detailed rollout history with change causes
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj-author-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Check specific revision details
kubectl rollout history deployment/anihpj-author-deployment --revision=4 -n anihpj-services
    deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment restarted Waiting for deployment "anihpj-author-deployment" rollout to finish: 1 of 2 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-author-deployment" successfully rolled out REVISION CHANGE-CAUSE 1 2 kubectl set image deployment/anihpj-author-deployment author-container=anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.8.0 3 kubectl set env deployment/anihpj-author-deployment ENVIRONMENT=production 4 kubectl rollout restart deployment/anihpj-author-deployment deployment.apps/anihpj-author-deployment with revision #4 Pod Template: Labels: app=anihpj-author Containers: author-container: Image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v2.8.0 Environment: ENVIRONMENT=production
    Example 5: Location Service Multi-Environment Rollout Coordination

    Coordinate anihpj Location service rollouts across multiple environments with validation checkpoints and coordinated rollbacks.

    # Check rollout status across all Location service environments
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-location-deployment -n anihpj-services
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-location-staging -n anihpj-staging

# Pause production rollout while validating staging
kubectl rollout pause deployment/anihpj-location-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Check staging rollout completion
kubectl rollout status deployment/anihpj-location-staging -n anihpj-staging --timeout=180s

# Resume production rollout after staging validation
kubectl rollout resume deployment/anihpj-location-deployment -n anihpj-services

# Coordinate rollback across environments if needed
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj-location-deployment -n anihpj-services
kubectl rollout undo deployment/anihpj-location-staging -n anihpj-staging

# Verify rollback completion across environments
kubectl get deployments -l app=anihpj-location --all-namespaces
    Waiting for deployment "anihpj-location-deployment" rollout to finish: 2 of 3 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-location-deployment" successfully rolled out deployment "anihpj-location-staging" successfully rolled out deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment paused Waiting for deployment "anihpj-location-staging" rollout to finish: 1 of 2 updated replicas are available... deployment "anihpj-location-staging" successfully rolled out deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment resumed deployment.apps/anihpj-location-deployment rolled back deployment.apps/anihpj-location-staging rolled back NAMESPACE NAME READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE AGE anihpj-services anihpj-location-deployment 3/3 3 3 1d anihpj-staging anihpj-location-staging 2/2 2 2 8h

    StatefulSet

    StatefulSet Overview

    Docs: Kubernetes StatefulSet Documentation

    StatefulSet Fields
    StatefulSetSpec - Desired state specification with stable identities
    StatefulSetStatus - Current status information
    StatefulSetList - Collection of StatefulSet objects

    StatefulSet

    StatefulSet

    StatefulSet represents a set of pods with consistent identities for stateful applications in Django deployments. Provides stable network identities, ordered deployment and scaling, and persistent storage for applications that require stable hostname and storage across pod restarts such as databases, message queues, and session stores.

    Syntax
    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-postgres-statefulset
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: database
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-postgres-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: database
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: database
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgres
        image: postgres:15-alpine
        env:
        - name: POSTGRES_DB
          value: anihpj_django
        - name: POSTGRES_USER
          value: anihpj_user
        volumeMounts:
        - name: postgres-storage
          mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: postgres-storage
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
    Parameters Description
    • apiVersion (string): API version "apps/v1" for StatefulSet resources in Kubernetes clusters.
    • kind (string): Resource type "StatefulSet" to specify the workload controller type.
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata including name, namespace, labels for StatefulSet identification.
    • spec (StatefulSetSpec): StatefulSet specification defining desired identities, replicas, pod template, and volume claim templates.
    • status (StatefulSetStatus): Current status information about replica counts, readiness, and conditions.
    Return Value

    Returns the StatefulSet object with configuration for managing stateful pods with consistent identities, including stable network names, ordered deployment, and persistent storage for Django application components requiring state persistence.

    Example 1: PostgreSQL Database StatefulSet

    This example shows a complete StatefulSet for PostgreSQL database service with persistent storage and stable network identity for the anihpj Django application.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-postgres-db
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-postgres-service
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: postgres
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgres
        image: postgres:15-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5432
        env:
        - name: POSTGRES_DB
          value: anihpj_django
        - name: POSTGRES_USER
          value: anihpj_user
        - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: postgres-secret
              key: password
        volumeMounts:
        - name: postgres-data
          mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: postgres-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 20Gi
    # Apply the PostgreSQL StatefulSet
kubectl apply -f anihpj-postgres-statefulset.yaml
# Check StatefulSet status
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-postgres-db -n anihpj-system
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-postgres-db created NAME READY AGE anihpj-postgres-db 1/1 45s
    Example 2: Redis Session Store StatefulSet

    This example demonstrates a Redis StatefulSet for Django session storage with persistent data and ordered deployment for the anihpj application.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-redis-sessions
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: redis
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-redis-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: redis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: redis
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: redis
        image: redis:7-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 6379
        volumeMounts:
        - name: redis-data
          mountPath: /data
        command:
        - redis-server
        - --appendonly yes
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: redis-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
    # Create Redis session store StatefulSet
kubectl apply -f anihpj-redis-statefulset.yaml
# Verify pod creation order
kubectl get pods -l service=redis -n anihpj-system
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-redis-sessions created NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE anihpj-redis-sessions-0 1/1 Running 0 2m anihpj-redis-sessions-1 1/1 Running 0 90s anihpj-redis-sessions-2 1/1 Running 0 45s
    Example 3: Skills Search Elasticsearch StatefulSet

    This example shows an Elasticsearch StatefulSet for the Skills service search functionality with cluster configuration and persistent storage.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-elasticsearch
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: elasticsearch
    component: skills-search
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-elasticsearch-service
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: elasticsearch
        component: skills-search
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: elasticsearch:8.11.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
        - containerPort: 9300
        env:
        - name: cluster.name
          value: "anihpj-skills-cluster"
        - name: node.name
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: discovery.seed_hosts
          value: "anihpj-elasticsearch-service"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: elasticsearch-data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: elasticsearch-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 15Gi
    # Deploy Skills search Elasticsearch StatefulSet
kubectl apply -f anihpj-skills-elasticsearch.yaml
# Check cluster formation
kubectl logs anihpj-skills-elasticsearch-0 -n anihpj-system
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-skills-elasticsearch created [2024-01-15T10:30:00,123][INFO ][o.e.c.s.ClusterApplierService] [anihpj-skills-elasticsearch-0] master node changed
    Example 4: Author Content RabbitMQ StatefulSet

    This example demonstrates a RabbitMQ StatefulSet for Author content processing queues with clustered setup and persistent message storage.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-rabbitmq
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: rabbitmq
    component: author-queue
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-rabbitmq-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: rabbitmq
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: rabbitmq
        component: author-queue
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: rabbitmq
        image: rabbitmq:3.12-management
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5672
        - containerPort: 15672
        env:
        - name: RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER
          value: anihpj_author
        - name: RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: rabbitmq-secret
              key: password
        - name: RABBITMQ_ERLANG_COOKIE
          value: "anihpj-author-cookie"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: rabbitmq-data
          mountPath: /var/lib/rabbitmq
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: rabbitmq-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 8Gi
    # Create Author content RabbitMQ StatefulSet
kubectl apply -f anihpj-author-rabbitmq.yaml
# Check RabbitMQ cluster status
kubectl exec anihpj-author-rabbitmq-0 -n anihpj-system -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-author-rabbitmq created Cluster status of node rabbit@anihpj-author-rabbitmq-0 ... Basics Cluster name: rabbit@anihpj-author-rabbitmq-0 Running nodes: rabbit@anihpj-author-rabbitmq-0,rabbit@anihpj-author-rabbitmq-1
    Example 5: Location Service MongoDB StatefulSet

    This example shows a MongoDB StatefulSet for Location service geospatial data with replica set configuration and persistent storage for location indexing.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-mongodb
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: mongodb
    component: location-data
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-mongodb-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: mongodb
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: mongodb
        component: location-data
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: mongodb
        image: mongo:7.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 27017
        env:
        - name: MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME
          value: anihpj_location
        - name: MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: mongodb-secret
              key: password
        - name: MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE
          value: anihpj_locations
        volumeMounts:
        - name: mongodb-data
          mountPath: /data/db
        command:
        - mongod
        - --replSet=anihpj-location-rs
        - --bind_ip_all
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: mongodb-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 25Gi
    # Deploy Location service MongoDB StatefulSet
kubectl apply -f anihpj-location-mongodb.yaml
# Initialize MongoDB replica set
kubectl exec anihpj-location-mongodb-0 -n anihpj-system -- mongo --eval "rs.initiate()"
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb created {"ok" : 1}

    StatefulSetSpec

    StatefulSetSpec defines the specification of a StatefulSet, describing how to create and manage stateful pods with consistent identities, ordered deployment, and persistent storage for Django applications requiring stable network identities and data persistence.

    Syntax
    spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-postgres-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: database
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: database
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgres
        image: postgres:15-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5432
        env:
        - name: POSTGRES_DB
          value: anihpj_django
        volumeMounts:
        - name: postgres-data
          mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
      maxUnavailable: 1
  podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: postgres-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 20Gi
  minReadySeconds: 30
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
    Parameters Description
    • serviceName (string, required): Name of the headless service that governs this StatefulSet. Must exist before StatefulSet creation and provides network identity.
    • selector (LabelSelector, required): Label query over pods that should match the replica count. Must match pod template labels.
    • template (PodTemplateSpec, required): Pod template describing pods to be created. Each pod gets unique identity but follows this template.
    • replicas (int32): Desired number of replicas with consistent identities. Defaults to 1 if unspecified.
    • updateStrategy (StatefulSetUpdateStrategy): Strategy for updating pods when template is revised. Supports RollingUpdate and OnDelete types.
    • podManagementPolicy (string): Controls pod creation order. OrderedReady (default) creates pods sequentially, Parallel creates all at once.
    • volumeClaimTemplates ([]PersistentVolumeClaim): List of volume claim templates for creating persistent volumes. Each pod gets its own volume.
    • minReadySeconds (int32): Minimum seconds a pod should be ready without crashes to be considered available. Defaults to 0.
    • revisionHistoryLimit (int32): Maximum number of revisions to maintain in history. Defaults to 10.
    Return Value

    Returns the StatefulSetSpec object defining the desired configuration for stateful pod management, including service binding, replica management, pod template, update strategies, and persistent volume claim templates for Django application stateful components.

    Example 1: JobPost Database StatefulSetSpec

    This example shows a complete StatefulSetSpec for JobPost service PostgreSQL database with ordered deployment and persistent storage configuration.

    spec:
  serviceName: jobpost-postgres-service
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: jobpost-db
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: jobpost-db
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgres
        image: postgres:15-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5432
        env:
        - name: POSTGRES_DB
          value: jobpost_db
        - name: POSTGRES_USER
          value: jobpost_user
        - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: jobpost-db-secret
              key: password
        volumeMounts:
        - name: jobpost-data
          mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: jobpost-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 20Gi
  minReadySeconds: 15
    # Apply JobPost database StatefulSetSpec
kubectl apply -f jobpost-postgres-spec.yaml
# Check StatefulSet creation
kubectl get statefulset jobpost-postgres -o yaml | grep -A 20 "spec:"
    statefulset.apps/jobpost-postgres created spec: podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady replicas: 1 revisionHistoryLimit: 10 selector: matchLabels: app: anihpj service: jobpost-db serviceName: jobpost-postgres-service template: metadata: labels: app: anihpj service: jobpost-db
    Example 2: Skills Cache Redis StatefulSetSpec

    This example demonstrates a StatefulSetSpec for Skills service Redis cache with clustering configuration and volume claim templates.

    spec:
  serviceName: skills-redis-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: skills-cache
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: skills-cache
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: redis
        image: redis:7-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 6379
        command:
        - redis-server
        - --cluster-enabled yes
        - --cluster-config-file nodes.conf
        - --appendonly yes
        volumeMounts:
        - name: skills-cache-data
          mountPath: /data
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
  podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: skills-cache-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
    # Create Skills cache Redis StatefulSetSpec
kubectl apply -f skills-redis-spec.yaml
# Verify pod management policy
kubectl describe statefulset skills-redis | grep -A 5 "Pod Management Policy"
    statefulset.apps/skills-redis created Pod Management Policy: OrderedReady Replicas: 3 desired | 3 total Update Strategy: RollingUpdate Partition: 0
    Example 3: User Session Store StatefulSetSpec with Update Strategy

    This example shows a StatefulSetSpec for User service session store with advanced update strategy and parallel pod management.

    spec:
  serviceName: user-session-service
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: user-sessions
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: user-sessions
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: memcached
        image: memcached:1.6-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 11211
        args:
        - -m 256
        - -I 4m
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 1
      maxUnavailable: 1
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  minReadySeconds: 10
  revisionHistoryLimit: 5
    # Apply User session store StatefulSetSpec
kubectl apply -f user-session-spec.yaml
# Check update strategy configuration
kubectl get statefulset user-session -o jsonpath='{.spec.updateStrategy}'
    statefulset.apps/user-session created {"rollingUpdate":{"maxUnavailable":1,"partition":1},"type":"RollingUpdate"}
    Example 4: Author Content Elasticsearch StatefulSetSpec

    This example demonstrates a StatefulSetSpec for Author service Elasticsearch cluster with multiple volume claims and ordinal configuration.

    spec:
  serviceName: author-elasticsearch-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: author-search
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: author-search
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: elasticsearch:8.11.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
        - containerPort: 9300
        env:
        - name: cluster.name
          value: "anihpj-author-cluster"
        - name: node.name
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: author-search-data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: author-search-logs
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: author-search-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 30Gi
  - metadata:
      name: author-search-logs
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
  minReadySeconds: 30
    # Deploy Author Elasticsearch StatefulSetSpec
kubectl apply -f author-elasticsearch-spec.yaml
# Check volume claim templates
kubectl get statefulset author-elasticsearch -o yaml | grep -A 15 volumeClaimTemplates
    statefulset.apps/author-elasticsearch created volumeClaimTemplates: - apiVersion: v1 kind: PersistentVolumeClaim metadata: name: author-search-data spec: accessModes: - ReadWriteOnce resources: requests: storage: 30Gi
    Example 5: Location GIS Database StatefulSetSpec with Retention Policy

    This example shows an advanced StatefulSetSpec for Location service PostGIS database with persistent volume claim retention policy and ordinal start configuration.

    spec:
  serviceName: location-postgis-service
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      service: location-gis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        service: location-gis
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgis
        image: postgis/postgis:15-3.3-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5432
        env:
        - name: POSTGRES_DB
          value: anihpj_locations
        - name: POSTGRES_USER
          value: location_user
        - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: location-db-secret
              key: password
        volumeMounts:
        - name: location-gis-data
          mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: location-gis-data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 50Gi
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Delete
  ordinals:
    start: 1
  minReadySeconds: 45
  revisionHistoryLimit: 15
    # Apply Location GIS database StatefulSetSpec
kubectl apply -f location-postgis-spec.yaml
# Check persistent volume claim retention policy
kubectl get statefulset location-postgis -o jsonpath='{.spec.persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy}'
    statefulset.apps/location-postgis created {"whenDeleted":"Retain","whenScaled":"Delete"}

    StatefulSetStatus

    StatefulSetStatus represents the current state of a StatefulSet, providing comprehensive information about replica counts, readiness status, update progress, and conditions for monitoring the health and deployment status of stateful Django applications.

    Syntax
    status:
  replicas: 3
  readyReplicas: 3
  currentReplicas: 3
  updatedReplicas: 3
  availableReplicas: 3
  collisionCount: 0
  observedGeneration: 5
  currentRevision: anihpj-postgres-6c8b7d4f5c
  updateRevision: anihpj-postgres-6c8b7d4f5c
  conditions:
  - type: Available
    status: "True"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    reason: MinimumReplicasAvailable
    message: "StatefulSet has minimum availability"
  - type: Progressing
    status: "True"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:25:00Z"
    reason: NewReplicaSetAvailable
    message: "StatefulSet has successfully progressed"
    Parameters Description
    • replicas (int32, required): Total number of pods created by the StatefulSet controller.
    • readyReplicas (int32): Number of pods with Ready condition. Critical for determining service availability.
    • currentReplicas (int32): Number of pods created from the current StatefulSet version indicated by currentRevision.
    • updatedReplicas (int32): Number of pods created from the StatefulSet version indicated by updateRevision.
    • availableReplicas (int32): Total available pods ready for at least minReadySeconds.
    • collisionCount (int32): Count of hash collisions for StatefulSet. Used for collision avoidance in ControllerRevision naming.
    • conditions ([]StatefulSetCondition): Latest observations of StatefulSet current state including Available and Progressing conditions.
    • currentRevision (string): Version of StatefulSet used to generate pods in sequence [0,currentReplicas).
    • updateRevision (string): Version of StatefulSet used to generate pods in sequence [replicas-updatedReplicas,replicas).
    • observedGeneration (int64): Most recent generation observed for this StatefulSet, corresponding to API Server generation updates.
    Return Value

    Returns the StatefulSetStatus object with current operational state information, including replica counts, update progress, revision tracking, and condition status for monitoring and managing stateful Django application deployments.

    Example 1: JobPost Database Status Check

    This example shows how to check the status of a JobPost PostgreSQL StatefulSet including replica counts and deployment conditions.

    # Get JobPost database StatefulSet status
kubectl get statefulset jobpost-postgres -o yaml | grep -A 15 "status:"
    status: availableReplicas: 1 collisionCount: 0 currentReplicas: 1 currentRevision: jobpost-postgres-7c9f8d5b4a observedGeneration: 3 readyReplicas: 1 replicas: 1 updateRevision: jobpost-postgres-7c9f8d5b4a updatedReplicas: 1
    # Check detailed status with conditions
kubectl describe statefulset jobpost-postgres
    Name: jobpost-postgres Namespace: anihpj-system CreationTimestamp: Mon, 15 Jan 2024 10:25:00 +0000 Selector: app=anihpj,service=jobpost-db Labels: app=anihpj,service=jobpost-db Replicas: 1 desired | 1 total Update Strategy: RollingUpdate Pods Status: 1 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed
    Example 2: Skills Cache Redis Cluster Status

    This example demonstrates monitoring Skills service Redis StatefulSet status during rolling update with multiple replicas.

    # Monitor Skills Redis StatefulSet status during update
kubectl get statefulset skills-redis -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.readyReplicas,CURRENT:.status.currentReplicas,UPDATED:.status.updatedReplicas
    NAME READY CURRENT UPDATED skills-redis 3 3 3
    # Check revision information
kubectl get statefulset skills-redis -o jsonpath='{.status.currentRevision}:{.status.updateRevision}'
    skills-redis-8f6d2c4b9a:skills-redis-8f6d2c4b9a
    Example 3: User Session Store Availability Status

    This example shows how to check User service session store StatefulSet availability and readiness status for service health monitoring.

    # Check User session store availability
kubectl get statefulset user-session -o jsonpath='{.status.availableReplicas}/{.spec.replicas}'
    2/2
    # Monitor condition status
kubectl get statefulset user-session -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].type}:{.status.conditions[*].status}'
    Available:True Progressing:True
    Example 4: Author Elasticsearch Cluster Status Monitoring

    This example demonstrates comprehensive status monitoring for Author service Elasticsearch StatefulSet including collision count and generation tracking.

    # Get comprehensive Author Elasticsearch status
kubectl get statefulset author-elasticsearch -o yaml | grep -A 25 "status:"
    status: availableReplicas: 3 collisionCount: 0 conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z" message: StatefulSet has minimum availability. reason: MinimumReplicasAvailable status: "True" type: Available - lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:25:00Z" message: StatefulSet has successfully progressed. reason: NewReplicaSetAvailable status: "True" type: Progressing currentReplicas: 3 currentRevision: author-elasticsearch-9d7c8b5f4e observedGeneration: 7 readyReplicas: 3 replicas: 3 updateRevision: author-elasticsearch-9d7c8b5f4e updatedReplicas: 3
    # Check StatefulSet generation and collision status
kubectl get statefulset author-elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.status.observedGeneration}:{.status.collisionCount}'
    7:0
    Example 5: Location PostGIS Update Progress Status

    This example shows how to monitor Location service PostGIS StatefulSet update progress and revision transitions during rolling updates.

    # Monitor Location PostGIS update progress
kubectl rollout status statefulset/location-postgis
    Waiting for 1 pods to be ready... Waiting for 1 pods to be ready... statefulset rolling update complete 2 pods at revision location-postgis-5f8e6d2c1b
    # Check update revision status
kubectl get statefulset location-postgis -o jsonpath='{.status.currentRevision} -> {.status.updateRevision}'
    location-postgis-4a7c9b3e2d -> location-postgis-5f8e6d2c1b

    StatefulSetList

    StatefulSetList is a collection of StatefulSet objects providing bulk operations and listing capabilities for managing multiple stateful workloads across different Django application services and environments with pagination and filtering support.

    Syntax
    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSetList
metadata:
  resourceVersion: "12345"
  continue: ""
  remainingItemCount: 0
items:
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: StatefulSet
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-postgres-db
    namespace: anihpj-system
  spec:
    serviceName: anihpj-postgres-service
    replicas: 1
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        service: postgres
  status:
    replicas: 1
    readyReplicas: 1
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: StatefulSet
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-redis-cache
    namespace: anihpj-system
  spec:
    serviceName: anihpj-redis-service
    replicas: 3
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        service: redis
  status:
    replicas: 3
    readyReplicas: 3
    Parameters Description
    • apiVersion (string): API version "apps/v1" for StatefulSetList resources.
    • kind (string): Resource type "StatefulSetList" to specify the collection type.
    • metadata (ListMeta): List metadata including resourceVersion, continuation tokens, and pagination information.
    • items ([]StatefulSet, required): Array of StatefulSet objects containing the actual stateful workload configurations and status.
    Return Value

    Returns the StatefulSetList object containing a collection of StatefulSet resources with list metadata, enabling comprehensive management of multiple stateful Django application components across different services, namespaces, and environments.

    Example 1: List All StatefulSets in Anihpj System

    This example shows how to list all StatefulSet resources in the anihpj-system namespace for comprehensive stateful workload overview.

    # List all StatefulSets in anihpj-system namespace
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-postgres-db 1/1 5d anihpj-redis-cache 3/3 4d anihpj-elasticsearch 3/3 3d anihpj-mongodb-location 2/2 2d anihpj-rabbitmq-queue 3/3 1d
    # Get detailed StatefulSet list with wide output
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -o wide
    NAME READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES anihpj-postgres-db 1/1 5d postgres postgres:15-alpine anihpj-redis-cache 3/3 4d redis redis:7-alpine anihpj-elasticsearch 3/3 3d elasticsearch elasticsearch:8.11.0
    Example 2: Filter StatefulSets by Service Labels

    This example demonstrates how to filter StatefulSet collections using label selectors to find specific Django service components.

    # List StatefulSets for database services only
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -l component=database
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-postgres-db 1/1 5d anihpj-mongodb-location 2/2 2d
    # List StatefulSets for cache services
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -l service=cache
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-redis-cache 3/3 4d
    Example 3: Export StatefulSet Configurations

    This example shows how to export all StatefulSet configurations in YAML format for backup, migration, or configuration management purposes.

    # Export all StatefulSets as YAML
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -o yaml > anihpj-statefulsets-backup.yaml
