    apiVersion: v1 items: - apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: StatefulSet metadata: name: anihpj-postgres-db namespace: anihpj-system spec: replicas: 1 serviceName: anihpj-postgres-service status: readyReplicas: 1 replicas: 1 - apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: StatefulSet metadata: name: anihpj-redis-cache namespace: anihpj-system spec: replicas: 3 serviceName: anihpj-redis-service status: readyReplicas: 3 replicas: 3 kind: List
    # Count total StatefulSets
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system --no-headers | wc -l
    5
    Example 4: Monitor StatefulSet Status Across Services

    This example demonstrates comprehensive monitoring of StatefulSet status across different Django application services using custom output formatting.

    # Monitor StatefulSet readiness across all services
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,SERVICE:.metadata.labels.service,READY:.status.readyReplicas,DESIRED:.spec.replicas,STATUS:.status.conditions[0].type
    NAME SERVICE READY DESIRED STATUS anihpj-postgres-db postgres 1 1 Available anihpj-redis-cache redis 3 3 Available anihpj-elasticsearch elasticsearch 3 3 Available anihpj-mongodb-location mongodb 2 2 Available anihpj-rabbitmq-queue rabbitmq 3 3 Available
    # Check StatefulSet age and revisions
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp,CURRENT_REVISION:.status.currentRevision
    NAME AGE CURRENT_REVISION anihpj-postgres-db 2024-01-10T10:25:00Z postgres-db-7c9f8d5b4a anihpj-redis-cache 2024-01-11T14:30:00Z redis-cache-8f6d2c4b9a
    Example 5: Watch StatefulSet Changes in Real-Time

    This example shows how to monitor StatefulSet changes in real-time across the anihpj Django application infrastructure for live operational awareness.

    # Watch StatefulSet changes in real-time
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -w
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-postgres-db 1/1 5d anihpj-redis-cache 3/3 4d anihpj-elasticsearch 3/3 3d anihpj-mongodb-location 2/2 2d anihpj-rabbitmq-queue 3/3 1d anihpj-redis-cache 2/3 4d anihpj-redis-cache 3/3 4d
    # Watch StatefulSet events
kubectl get events -n anihpj-system --field-selector involvedObject.kind=StatefulSet -w
    LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 2m15s Normal SuccessfulCreate StatefulSet/anihpj-redis-cache Created pod: anihpj-redis-cache-2 1m45s Normal SuccessfulCreate StatefulSet/anihpj-redis-cache Created pod: anihpj-redis-cache-3

    ⚙️ Operations

    StatefulSet Operations Overview

    Docs: Kubernetes StatefulSet API Operations

    HTTP API operations for managing StatefulSet resources with persistent storage and stable network identities in anihpj Django applications. StatefulSets provide ordered deployment, scaling, and deletion of pods with persistent volumes.

    Read StatefulSet - Get detailed StatefulSet information
    List StatefulSets - List and watch StatefulSet resources
    Create StatefulSet - Deploy new stateful applications
    Update StatefulSet - Modify StatefulSet configurations
    Delete StatefulSet - Remove StatefulSet resources
    🔍 Get StatefulSet

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets/{name}

    Read the specified StatefulSet with detailed configuration and status information.

    # Get specific StatefulSet details
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-postgres-db -n anihpj-system -o yaml
    apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: StatefulSet metadata: name: anihpj-postgres-db namespace: anihpj-system spec: serviceName: anihpj-postgres-service replicas: 1 selector: matchLabels: app: anihpj service: postgres status: availableReplicas: 1 currentReplicas: 1 readyReplicas: 1 replicas: 1
    📋 List StatefulSets

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets

    List or watch StatefulSet objects in the specified namespace.

    # List all StatefulSets in namespace
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system
# Watch StatefulSet changes
kubectl get statefulset -n anihpj-system -w
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-postgres-db 1/1 5d anihpj-redis-cache 3/3 4d anihpj-elasticsearch 3/3 3d
    ✨ Create StatefulSet

    HTTP Request: POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets

    Create a new StatefulSet resource for stateful Django applications.

    # Create StatefulSet from YAML
kubectl apply -f anihpj-statefulset.yaml
# Create with dry-run
kubectl apply -f anihpj-statefulset.yaml --dry-run=client
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-postgres-db created
    🔄 Update StatefulSet

    HTTP Request: PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets/{name}

    Replace the specified StatefulSet configuration.

    # Update StatefulSet configuration
kubectl apply -f anihpj-statefulset-updated.yaml
# Patch StatefulSet
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-postgres-db -p '{"spec":{"replicas":2}}'
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-postgres-db configured
    🗑️ Delete StatefulSet

    HTTP Request: DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets/{name}

    Delete a StatefulSet resource and optionally its managed pods.

    # Delete StatefulSet keeping pods
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-postgres-db --cascade=orphan
# Delete StatefulSet and pods
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-postgres-db
    statefulset.apps "anihpj-postgres-db" deleted

    Create StatefulSet

    Create a new StatefulSet resource in a namespace. This operation deploys a stateful application with ordered pod creation and persistent storage.

    HTTP Request

    POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets

    kubectl Syntax

    # Create StatefulSet from YAML file
kubectl create -f statefulset.yaml

# Create StatefulSet from YAML with custom namespace
kubectl create -f statefulset.yaml -n my-namespace

# Create StatefulSet with dry-run
kubectl create -f statefulset.yaml --dry-run=client

# Apply StatefulSet configuration
kubectl apply -f statefulset.yaml

# Create StatefulSet with field manager
kubectl apply -f statefulset.yaml --field-manager=kubectl

    Django Example

    Create anihpj Django StatefulSet for database services requiring persistent storage and stable network identities.

    # Create anihpj PostgreSQL StatefulSet for persistent database
kubectl apply -f anihpj-postgres-statefulset.yaml -n anihpj-services

# Create anihpj Redis StatefulSet for session storage
kubectl apply -f anihpj-redis-statefulset.yaml -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-postgres-statefulset created statefulset.apps/anihpj-redis-statefulset created
    Example 2: Skills Service Database StatefulSet

    Create anihpj Skills service dedicated PostgreSQL StatefulSet with persistent volume claims for skills data storage.

    # Create Skills service PostgreSQL StatefulSet
cat << 'EOF' | kubectl apply -f - -n anihpj-services
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-postgres
  labels:
    app: anihpj-skills-db
    service: skills
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-skills-postgres-service
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-skills-db
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-skills-db
        service: skills
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: postgres
        image: postgres:15
        env:
        - name: POSTGRES_DB
          value: "anihpj_skills"
        - name: POSTGRES_USER
          value: "anihpj_user"
        - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-skills-db-secret
              key: password
        volumeMounts:
        - name: postgres-storage
          mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: postgres-storage
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
EOF
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-skills-postgres created
    Example 3: Author Service Elasticsearch StatefulSet

    Create anihpj Author service Elasticsearch StatefulSet cluster for author search indexing with persistent storage.

    # Create Author service Elasticsearch StatefulSet cluster
kubectl apply -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-elasticsearch
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-author-search
    service: author
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-author-elasticsearch-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-author-search
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-author-search
        service: author
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: elasticsearch:8.9.0
        env:
        - name: cluster.name
          value: "anihpj-author-cluster"
        - name: node.name
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: discovery.seed_hosts
          value: "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-0,anihpj-author-elasticsearch-1,anihpj-author-elasticsearch-2"
        - name: cluster.initial_master_nodes
          value: "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-0,anihpj-author-elasticsearch-1,anihpj-author-elasticsearch-2"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: elasticsearch-storage
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: elasticsearch-storage
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch created
    Example 4: Location Service MongoDB StatefulSet

    Create anihpj Location service MongoDB StatefulSet with replica set configuration for geographic data storage.

    # Create Location service MongoDB StatefulSet
kubectl create -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-mongodb
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-location-db
    service: location
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-location-mongodb-service
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-location-db
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-location-db
        service: location
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: mongodb
        image: mongo:6.0
        env:
        - name: MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME
          value: "anihpj_location_admin"
        - name: MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-location-mongodb-secret
              key: password
        - name: MONGO_INITDB_DATABASE
          value: "anihpj_locations"
        command:
        - mongod
        - --replSet
        - anihpj-location-rs
        - --bind_ip_all
        volumeMounts:
        - name: mongodb-storage
          mountPath: /data/db
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: mongodb-storage
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 8Gi
EOF
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb created
    Example 5: JobPost Service ClickHouse StatefulSet

    Create anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse StatefulSet for analytics and job posting metrics with persistent storage and clustering.

    # Create JobPost service ClickHouse StatefulSet for analytics
kubectl apply -n anihpj-services -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse
  labels:
    app: anihpj-jobpost-analytics
    service: jobpost
spec:
  serviceName: anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-service
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-jobpost-analytics
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-jobpost-analytics
        service: jobpost
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: clickhouse
        image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:23.8
        env:
        - name: CLICKHOUSE_DB
          value: "anihpj_jobpost_analytics"
        - name: CLICKHOUSE_USER
          value: "anihpj_analytics_user"
        - name: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-secret
              key: password
        - name: CLICKHOUSE_DEFAULT_ACCESS_MANAGEMENT
          value: "1"
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8123
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9000
          name: native
        volumeMounts:
        - name: clickhouse-storage
          mountPath: /var/lib/clickhouse
        - name: clickhouse-logs
          mountPath: /var/log/clickhouse-server
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: clickhouse-storage
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 20Gi
  - metadata:
      name: clickhouse-logs
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]  
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 2Gi
EOF
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse created

    Get StatefulSet

    Read the specified StatefulSet resource to retrieve its current configuration and status information.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Get specific StatefulSet
kubectl get statefulset my-statefulset

# Get StatefulSet with detailed output
kubectl get statefulset my-statefulset -o wide

# Get StatefulSet in YAML format
kubectl get statefulset my-statefulset -o yaml

# Get StatefulSet in JSON format
kubectl get statefulset my-statefulset -o json

# Describe StatefulSet for detailed information
kubectl describe statefulset my-statefulset

    Django Example

    Retrieve anihpj Django StatefulSet information for database services monitoring and troubleshooting.

    # Get anihpj PostgreSQL StatefulSet
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-postgres-statefulset -n anihpj-services

# Get anihpj Redis StatefulSet with wide output
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-redis-statefulset -o wide -n anihpj-services
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-postgres-statefulset 1/1 2d NAME READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES anihpj-redis-statefulset 1/1 1d redis redis:7.0
    Example 2: Skills Service Database StatefulSet Details

    Get detailed information about anihpj Skills service PostgreSQL StatefulSet including pod status and storage information.

    # Get Skills PostgreSQL StatefulSet with full details
kubectl describe statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres -n anihpj-services

# Get Skills StatefulSet YAML configuration
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres -o yaml -n anihpj-services

# Check Skills StatefulSet pods status
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj-skills-db -n anihpj-services
    Name: anihpj-skills-postgres Namespace: anihpj-services CreationTimestamp: Mon, 25 Sep 2025 10:30:00 +0000 Selector: app=anihpj-skills-db Labels: app=anihpj-skills-db service=skills Replicas: 1 desired | 1 total Update Strategy: RollingUpdate Pod Management Policy: OrderedReady Pods Status: 1 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE anihpj-skills-postgres-0 1/1 Running 0 2d
    Example 3: Author Service Elasticsearch Cluster Status

    Monitor anihpj Author service Elasticsearch StatefulSet cluster health and node status for search functionality.

    # Get Author Elasticsearch StatefulSet status
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-author-elasticsearch -n anihpj-services

# Check all Elasticsearch pods in the cluster
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj-author-search -n anihpj-services -o wide

# Get StatefulSet events for troubleshooting
kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=anihpj-author-elasticsearch -n anihpj-services
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3/3 1d NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE IP NODE anihpj-author-elasticsearch-0 1/1 Running 0 1d 10.244.1.12 worker-1 anihpj-author-elasticsearch-1 1/1 Running 0 1d 10.244.2.8 worker-2 anihpj-author-elasticsearch-2 1/1 Running 0 1d 10.244.3.15 worker-3 LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 45m Normal SuccessfulCreate statefulset/anihpj-author-elasticsearch create Pod anihpj-author-elasticsearch-0 45m Normal SuccessfulCreate statefulset/anihpj-author-elasticsearch create Pod anihpj-author-elasticsearch-1
    Example 4: Location Service MongoDB Replica Set Information

    Retrieve anihpj Location service MongoDB StatefulSet information including replica set status and storage utilization.

    # Get Location MongoDB StatefulSet detailed information
kubectl describe statefulset anihpj-location-mongodb -n anihpj-services

# Check MongoDB StatefulSet JSON configuration
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-location-mongodb -o json -n anihpj-services | jq '.status'

# Get persistent volume claims for MongoDB
kubectl get pvc -l app=anihpj-location-db -n anihpj-services
    Name: anihpj-location-mongodb Namespace: anihpj-services Selector: app=anihpj-location-db Labels: app=anihpj-location-db service=location Replicas: 3 desired | 3 total Update Strategy: RollingUpdate Volume Claim Templates: Name: mongodb-storage Storage Class: Access Modes: ReadWriteOnce Storage: 8Gi NAME STATUS VOLUME CAPACITY ACCESS MODES STORAGECLASS AGE mongodb-storage-anihpj-location-mongodb-0 Bound pvc-abc123-def456-789 8Gi RWO gp2 1d mongodb-storage-anihpj-location-mongodb-1 Bound pvc-def456-789abc-123 8Gi RWO gp2 1d mongodb-storage-anihpj-location-mongodb-2 Bound pvc-789abc-123def-456 8Gi RWO gp2 1d
    Example 5: JobPost Service ClickHouse Analytics Status

    Monitor anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse StatefulSet for analytics performance and storage metrics.

    # Get JobPost ClickHouse StatefulSet comprehensive status
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.readyReplicas/.spec.replicas,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp

# Check ClickHouse pods with resource usage
kubectl top pods -l app=anihpj-jobpost-analytics -n anihpj-services

# Get StatefulSet storage usage details
kubectl get pvc -l app=anihpj-jobpost-analytics -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.phase,CAPACITY:.status.capacity.storage,USED:.status.used
    NAME READY AGE anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 2/2 2024-09-25T10:30:00Z NAME CPU(cores) MEMORY(bytes) anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-0 250m 1024Mi anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-1 200m 896Mi NAME STATUS CAPACITY USED clickhouse-storage-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-0 Bound 20Gi 12Gi clickhouse-logs-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-0 Bound 2Gi 1.2Gi clickhouse-storage-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-1 Bound 20Gi 11Gi clickhouse-logs-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-1 Bound 2Gi 1.1Gi

    Update StatefulSet

    Replace the specified StatefulSet resource with a new configuration. This operation allows you to modify StatefulSet specifications including pod templates, replicas, and update strategies.

    HTTP Request

    PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Update StatefulSet from file
kubectl replace -f statefulset.yaml

# Update StatefulSet image
kubectl set image statefulset/my-statefulset container-name=new-image:tag

# Update StatefulSet replicas
kubectl scale statefulset my-statefulset --replicas=5

# Apply updated StatefulSet configuration
kubectl apply -f updated-statefulset.yaml

# Update StatefulSet with patch
kubectl patch statefulset my-statefulset -p '{"spec":{"replicas":3}}'

    Django Example

    Update anihpj Django StatefulSet configurations for database services to handle increased load and new requirements.

    # Update anihpj PostgreSQL StatefulSet image version
kubectl set image statefulset/anihpj-postgres-statefulset postgres=postgres:16 -n anihpj-services

# Scale anihpj Redis StatefulSet replicas
kubectl scale statefulset anihpj-redis-statefulset --replicas=3 -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-postgres-statefulset image updated statefulset.apps/anihpj-redis-statefulset scaled
    Example 2: Skills Service Database Performance Update

    Update anihpj Skills service PostgreSQL StatefulSet with enhanced configuration for better performance and resource allocation.

    # Update Skills PostgreSQL StatefulSet with new resource limits
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres -n anihpj-services -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"postgres","resources":{"limits":{"memory":"2Gi","cpu":"1000m"},"requests":{"memory":"1Gi","cpu":"500m"}}}]}}}}'

# Update Skills PostgreSQL with new environment variables for optimization
kubectl set env statefulset/anihpj-skills-postgres POSTGRES_SHARED_BUFFERS=256MB POSTGRES_EFFECTIVE_CACHE_SIZE=1GB -n anihpj-services

# Apply updated Skills PostgreSQL configuration from file
kubectl apply -f anihpj-skills-postgres-updated.yaml -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-skills-postgres patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-skills-postgres env updated statefulset.apps/anihpj-skills-postgres configured
    Example 3: Author Service Elasticsearch Cluster Scaling

    Scale anihpj Author service Elasticsearch StatefulSet cluster and update configuration for increased search performance.

    # Scale Author Elasticsearch cluster to 5 nodes
kubectl scale statefulset anihpj-author-elasticsearch --replicas=5 -n anihpj-services

# Update Elasticsearch StatefulSet with new memory configuration
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-author-elasticsearch -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"elasticsearch","env":[{"name":"ES_JAVA_OPTS","value":"-Xms1g -Xmx1g"}]}]}}}}'

# Update Elasticsearch image to latest version
kubectl set image statefulset/anihpj-author-elasticsearch elasticsearch=elasticsearch:8.10.0 -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch scaled statefulset.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch image updated
    Example 4: Location Service MongoDB Replica Set Enhancement

    Update anihpj Location service MongoDB StatefulSet with new version and enhanced replica set configuration for geographic data requirements.

    # Update Location MongoDB StatefulSet to new version
kubectl set image statefulset/anihpj-location-mongodb mongodb=mongo:7.0 -n anihpj-services

# Update MongoDB with new configuration for geographic indexing
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-location-mongodb -n anihpj-services -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"mongodb","env":[{"name":"MONGO_GEO_INDEXING","value":"true"},{"name":"MONGO_TEXT_SEARCH","value":"enabled"}]}]}}}}'

# Replace Location MongoDB StatefulSet with updated configuration
kubectl replace -f anihpj-location-mongodb-v2.yaml -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb image updated statefulset.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb replaced
    Example 5: JobPost Service ClickHouse Analytics Optimization

    Update anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse StatefulSet with optimized configuration for better analytics performance and storage efficiency.

    # Update JobPost ClickHouse StatefulSet with new image and configuration
kubectl set image statefulset/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse clickhouse=clickhouse/clickhouse-server:23.9 -n anihpj-services

# Update ClickHouse with optimized memory settings for analytics
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse -n anihpj-services --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/env", "value": [{"name":"CLICKHOUSE_DB","value":"anihpj_jobpost_analytics"},{"name":"CLICKHOUSE_USER","value":"anihpj_analytics_user"},{"name":"CLICKHOUSE_MAX_MEMORY_USAGE","value":"4000000000"},{"name":"CLICKHOUSE_MAX_THREADS","value":"4"}]}]'

# Scale ClickHouse StatefulSet for increased analytics workload
kubectl scale statefulset anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse --replicas=3 -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse image updated statefulset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse scaled

    Patch StatefulSet

    Partially update a StatefulSet resource by modifying specific fields without replacing the entire resource definition. This operation allows targeted updates to StatefulSet configuration.

    HTTP Request

    PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Patch StatefulSet replicas
kubectl patch statefulset my-statefulset -p '{"spec":{"replicas":5}}'

# Patch StatefulSet with JSON file
kubectl patch statefulset my-statefulset --patch-file patch.json

# Patch StatefulSet with merge strategy
kubectl patch statefulset my-statefulset --type merge -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"app","image":"new-image:tag"}]}}}}'

# Patch StatefulSet annotations
kubectl patch statefulset my-statefulset -p '{"metadata":{"annotations":{"deployment.kubernetes.io/revision":"3"}}}'

# Patch StatefulSet update strategy
kubectl patch statefulset my-statefulset -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"type":"RollingUpdate","rollingUpdate":{"partition":2}}}}'

    Django Example

    Patch anihpj Django StatefulSet to modify specific configuration without full replacement for database services.

    # Patch anihpj PostgreSQL StatefulSet replicas
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-postgres-statefulset -p '{"spec":{"replicas":2}}' -n anihpj-services

# Patch anihpj Redis StatefulSet update strategy
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-redis-statefulset -p '{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"partition":1}}}}' -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-postgres-statefulset patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-redis-statefulset patched
    Example 2: Skills Service Database Resource Updates

    Patch anihpj Skills service PostgreSQL StatefulSet to update resource limits and environment variables for optimal performance.

    # Patch Skills PostgreSQL StatefulSet resource limits
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"postgres","resources":{"limits":{"memory":"4Gi","cpu":"2000m"},"requests":{"memory":"2Gi","cpu":"1000m"}}}]}}}}'

# Patch Skills PostgreSQL with new environment variables for performance tuning
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres -n anihpj-services -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"postgres","env":[{"name":"POSTGRES_SHARED_BUFFERS","value":"512MB"},{"name":"POSTGRES_EFFECTIVE_CACHE_SIZE","value":"2GB"},{"name":"POSTGRES_WORK_MEM","value":"16MB"}]}]}}}}'
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-skills-postgres patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-skills-postgres patched
    Example 3: Author Service Elasticsearch Configuration Tuning

    Patch anihpj Author service Elasticsearch StatefulSet to optimize cluster configuration and memory settings for better search performance.

    # Patch Author Elasticsearch StatefulSet with memory optimization
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-author-elasticsearch -n anihpj-services --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/env", "value": [{"name":"cluster.name","value":"anihpj-author-cluster"},{"name":"ES_JAVA_OPTS","value":"-Xms2g -Xmx2g"},{"name":"cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.low","value":"85%"}]}]'

# Patch Elasticsearch StatefulSet to add monitoring annotations
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-author-elasticsearch -n anihpj-services -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"monitoring.anihpj.com/scrape":"true","monitoring.anihpj.com/port":"9200","monitoring.anihpj.com/path":"/_metrics"}}}'
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch patched
    Example 4: Location Service MongoDB Replica Set Optimization

    Patch anihpj Location service MongoDB StatefulSet to enhance replica set configuration and add geographic indexing optimizations.

    # Patch Location MongoDB StatefulSet with enhanced configuration
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-location-mongodb -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"mongodb","env":[{"name":"MONGO_OPLOG_SIZE","value":"1024"},{"name":"MONGO_GEO_INDEXING","value":"enabled"},{"name":"MONGO_TEXT_SEARCH","value":"enabled"}]}]}}}}'

# Patch MongoDB StatefulSet to update readiness probe for better reliability
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-location-mongodb -n anihpj-services -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"mongodb","readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["mongo","--eval","db.adminCommand('\''ping'\'')"]},"initialDelaySeconds":30,"periodSeconds":10}}]}}}}'
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb patched statefulset.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb patched
    Example 5: JobPost Service ClickHouse Analytics Enhancement

    Patch anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse StatefulSet to optimize analytics performance and add advanced configuration for data processing.

    # Create patch file for JobPost ClickHouse optimization
cat > jobpost-clickhouse-patch.json << 'EOF'
{
  "spec": {
    "template": {
      "spec": {
        "containers": [
          {
            "name": "clickhouse",
            "env": [
              {
                "name": "CLICKHOUSE_MAX_MEMORY_USAGE",
                "value": "8000000000"
              },
              {
                "name": "CLICKHOUSE_MAX_THREADS",
                "value": "8"
              },
              {
                "name": "CLICKHOUSE_BACKGROUND_POOL_SIZE",
                "value": "16"
              },
              {
                "name": "CLICKHOUSE_MERGE_TREE_MAX_ROWS_TO_USE_CACHE",
                "value": "1000000"
              }
            ],
            "resources": {
              "limits": {
                "memory": "8Gi",
                "cpu": "4000m"
              },
              "requests": {
                "memory": "4Gi", 
                "cpu": "2000m"
              }
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
EOF

# Apply patch file to JobPost ClickHouse StatefulSet
kubectl patch statefulset anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse --patch-file jobpost-clickhouse-patch.json -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse patched

    Delete StatefulSet

    Delete a StatefulSet resource and optionally its associated pods and persistent volume claims. This operation removes the StatefulSet but can preserve or delete persistent storage based on configuration.

    HTTP Request

    DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Delete StatefulSet
kubectl delete statefulset my-statefulset

# Delete StatefulSet and pods immediately
kubectl delete statefulset my-statefulset --cascade=foreground

# Delete StatefulSet but keep pods
kubectl delete statefulset my-statefulset --cascade=orphan

# Delete StatefulSet from file
kubectl delete -f statefulset.yaml

# Delete StatefulSet with grace period
kubectl delete statefulset my-statefulset --grace-period=30

    Django Example

    Delete anihpj Django StatefulSet resources safely while considering data persistence requirements.

    # Delete anihpj Redis StatefulSet (cache data can be lost)
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-redis-statefulset -n anihpj-services

# Delete anihpj test PostgreSQL StatefulSet with immediate cleanup
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-test-postgres --cascade=foreground -n anihpj-testing
    statefulset.apps "anihpj-redis-statefulset" deleted statefulset.apps "anihpj-test-postgres" deleted
    Example 2: Skills Service Database Cleanup

    Clean up anihpj Skills service development PostgreSQL StatefulSet while preserving production data in other environments.

    # Delete Skills development PostgreSQL StatefulSet
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres-dev -n anihpj-development

# Delete Skills staging database with graceful shutdown
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres-staging --grace-period=60 -n anihpj-staging

# Verify Skills production StatefulSet is preserved
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-skills-postgres -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps "anihpj-skills-postgres-dev" deleted statefulset.apps "anihpj-skills-postgres-staging" deleted NAME READY AGE anihpj-skills-postgres 1/1 5d
    Example 3: Author Service Elasticsearch Test Cluster Removal

    Remove anihpj Author service test Elasticsearch StatefulSet cluster while preserving persistent volumes for potential data recovery.

    # Delete Author test Elasticsearch StatefulSet but keep pods for data migration
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test --cascade=orphan -n anihpj-testing

# List orphaned pods for manual cleanup after data migration
kubectl get pods -l app=anihpj-author-search-test -n anihpj-testing

# Delete test Elasticsearch StatefulSet from YAML file
kubectl delete -f anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test.yaml -n anihpj-testing
    statefulset.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test" deleted NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test-0 1/1 Running 0 2h anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test-1 1/1 Running 0 2h anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test-2 1/1 Running 0 2h statefulset.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test" deleted
    Example 4: Location Service MongoDB Legacy Cleanup

    Clean up anihpj Location service legacy MongoDB StatefulSet and associated resources during system migration.

    # Delete legacy Location MongoDB StatefulSet with immediate cleanup
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-location-mongodb-legacy --cascade=foreground -n anihpj-services

# Clean up associated persistent volume claims
kubectl delete pvc -l app=anihpj-location-db-legacy -n anihpj-services

# Verify current Location MongoDB StatefulSet is running
kubectl get statefulset anihpj-location-mongodb -n anihpj-services
    statefulset.apps "anihpj-location-mongodb-legacy" deleted persistentvolumeclaim "mongodb-storage-anihpj-location-mongodb-legacy-0" deleted persistentvolumeclaim "mongodb-storage-anihpj-location-mongodb-legacy-1" deleted persistentvolumeclaim "mongodb-storage-anihpj-location-mongodb-legacy-2" deleted NAME READY AGE anihpj-location-mongodb 3/3 3d
    Example 5: JobPost Service ClickHouse Development Environment Teardown

    Complete teardown of anihpj JobPost service development ClickHouse StatefulSet including all associated resources and storage.

    # Delete JobPost development ClickHouse StatefulSet
kubectl delete statefulset anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-dev -n anihpj-development

# Delete all associated persistent volume claims
kubectl delete pvc -l app=anihpj-jobpost-analytics-dev -n anihpj-development

# Clean up development namespace if empty
kubectl get all -n anihpj-development
kubectl delete namespace anihpj-development --ignore-not-found
    statefulset.apps "anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-dev" deleted persistentvolumeclaim "clickhouse-storage-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-dev-0" deleted persistentvolumeclaim "clickhouse-logs-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-dev-0" deleted persistentvolumeclaim "clickhouse-storage-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-dev-1" deleted persistentvolumeclaim "clickhouse-logs-anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-dev-1" deleted No resources found in anihpj-development namespace. namespace "anihpj-development" deleted

    List StatefulSets

    Retrieve a list of all StatefulSet resources in a namespace or across all namespaces. This operation returns a StatefulSetList object with filtering and formatting options.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/statefulsets

    kubectl Syntax

    # List StatefulSets in current namespace
kubectl get statefulsets

# List StatefulSets in all namespaces
kubectl get statefulsets --all-namespaces

# List StatefulSets with labels
kubectl get statefulsets -l app=myapp

# List StatefulSets with custom output
kubectl get statefulsets -o wide

# List StatefulSets with custom columns
kubectl get statefulsets -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas

    Django Example

    List all anihpj Django StatefulSets across different environments to monitor database and stateful services.

    # List all anihpj StatefulSets
kubectl get statefulsets -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces

# List anihpj production StatefulSets
kubectl get statefulsets -l environment=production -n anihpj-production
    NAMESPACE NAME READY AGE anihpj-services anihpj-postgres-statefulset 1/1 2d anihpj-services anihpj-redis-statefulset 3/3 1d anihpj-services anihpj-skills-postgres 1/1 2d anihpj-services anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3/3 1d NAME READY AGE anihpj-prod-mongodb 3/3 5d anihpj-prod-clickhouse 2/2 3d
    Example 2: Service-Specific StatefulSet Listing

    List anihpj service StatefulSets filtered by component type and display detailed resource information for monitoring.

    # List anihpj database StatefulSets with wide output
kubectl get statefulsets -l component=database -o wide -n anihpj-services

# List anihpj analytics StatefulSets with custom columns
kubectl get statefulsets -l service=analytics -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,SERVICE:.metadata.labels.service --all-namespaces
    NAME READY AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES anihpj-skills-postgres 1/1 2d postgres postgres:15 anihpj-location-mongodb 3/3 1d mongodb mongo:6.0 anihpj-postgres-main 1/1 3d postgres postgres:16 NAME REPLICAS READY SERVICE anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 2 2 analytics anihpj-metrics-influxdb 1 1 analytics
    Example 3: Cross-Environment StatefulSet Overview

    Get comprehensive overview of anihpj StatefulSets across multiple environments with resource status and health monitoring.

    # List all anihpj StatefulSets sorted by creation timestamp
kubectl get statefulsets -l project=anihpj --all-namespaces --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp

# List anihpj StatefulSets with detailed status information
kubectl get statefulsets -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,DESIRED:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,UPDATED:.status.updatedReplicas,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp --all-namespaces -l project=anihpj
    NAMESPACE NAME READY AGE anihpj-development anihpj-skills-postgres-dev 1/1 3h anihpj-staging anihpj-postgres-staging 1/1 1d anihpj-staging anihpj-redis-staging 1/1 1d anihpj-services anihpj-skills-postgres 1/1 2d anihpj-services anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3/3 1d anihpj-services anihpj-location-mongodb 3/3 1d anihpj-production anihpj-prod-mongodb 3/3 5d anihpj-production anihpj-prod-clickhouse 2/2 3d NAME NAMESPACE DESIRED READY UPDATED AGE anihpj-skills-postgres anihpj-services 1 1 1 2024-09-23T10:30:00Z anihpj-author-elasticsearch anihpj-services 3 3 3 2024-09-24T14:20:00Z anihpj-location-mongodb anihpj-services 3 3 3 2024-09-24T16:45:00Z anihpj-prod-mongodb anihpj-production 3 3 3 2024-09-20T09:15:00Z
    Example 4: StatefulSet Storage and Resource Monitoring

    Monitor anihpj StatefulSet storage utilization and resource consumption for capacity planning and optimization.

    # List anihpj StatefulSets with storage information
kubectl get statefulsets -l project=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,VOLUME-CLAIMS:.spec.volumeClaimTemplates[*].metadata.name,STORAGE:.spec.volumeClaimTemplates[*].spec.resources.requests.storage --all-namespaces

# Show StatefulSets with resource requests and limits
kubectl get statefulsets -l project=anihpj -o json --all-namespaces | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, replicas: .spec.replicas, containers: .spec.template.spec.containers[].name}'
    NAME REPLICAS VOLUME-CLAIMS STORAGE anihpj-skills-postgres 1 postgres-storage 5Gi anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3 elasticsearch-storage 10Gi anihpj-location-mongodb 3 mongodb-storage 8Gi anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 2 clickhouse-storage,clickhouse-logs 20Gi,2Gi { "name": "anihpj-skills-postgres", "namespace": "anihpj-services", "replicas": 1, "containers": "postgres" } { "name": "anihpj-author-elasticsearch", "namespace": "anihpj-services", "replicas": 3, "containers": "elasticsearch" }
    Example 5: Environment-Specific Filtering and Health Status

    Filter anihpj StatefulSets by environment labels and display comprehensive health status for operational monitoring.

    # List anihpj production StatefulSets with health status
kubectl get statefulsets -l environment=production,project=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,DESIRED:.spec.replicas,CURRENT:.status.currentReplicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,UPDATED:.status.updatedReplicas,CONDITIONS:.status.conditions[*].type --all-namespaces

# List anihpj StatefulSets with pod management policy and update strategy
kubectl get statefulsets -l project=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,POD-POLICY:.spec.podManagementPolicy,UPDATE-STRATEGY:.spec.updateStrategy.type,PARTITION:.spec.updateStrategy.rollingUpdate.partition --all-namespaces
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UPDATED CONDITIONS anihpj-prod-mongodb 3 3 3 3 anihpj-prod-clickhouse 2 2 2 2 anihpj-prod-postgres 1 1 1 1 NAME NAMESPACE POD-POLICY UPDATE-STRATEGY PARTITION anihpj-skills-postgres anihpj-services OrderedReady RollingUpdate 0 anihpj-author-elasticsearch anihpj-services OrderedReady RollingUpdate 0 anihpj-location-mongodb anihpj-services OrderedReady RollingUpdate 0 anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse anihpj-services OrderedReady RollingUpdate 0 anihpj-prod-mongodb anihpj-production OrderedReady RollingUpdate 0

    ControllerRevision

    ControllerRevision Overview

    ControllerRevision - Immutable snapshot of state data
    ControllerRevisionList - Collection of controller revisions
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: ControllerRevision v1 API | API Version: apps/v1

    ControllerRevision implements an immutable snapshot of state data. Used by DaemonSet and StatefulSet controllers for update and rollback operations.

    Description

    ControllerRevision implements an immutable snapshot of state data. Clients are responsible for serializing and deserializing the objects that contain their internal state. Once a ControllerRevision has been successfully created, it cannot be updated. The API Server will fail validation of all requests that attempt to mutate the Data field. ControllerRevisions may, however, be deleted. Note that, due to its use by both the DaemonSet and StatefulSet controllers for update and rollback, this object is beta. However, it may be subject to name and representation changes in future releases, and clients should not depend on its stability. It is primarily for internal use by controllers.

    Syntax

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevision
metadata:
  name: controller-revision-name
  namespace: namespace-name
revision: 1
data:
  # RawExtension - serialized representation of the state

    Parameters

    apiVersion (string): API version, always "apps/v1"
    kind (string): Resource type, always "ControllerRevision"
    metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata containing name, namespace, labels, and annotations
    revision (int64, required): Revision indicates the revision of the state represented by Data
    data (RawExtension): Data is the serialized representation of the state

    Return Value

    Returns a ControllerRevision object representing an immutable snapshot of controller state data with revision tracking for rollback operations.

    Examples

    Example 1: Skills Service DaemonSet Revision

    View ControllerRevision for anihpj Skills service DaemonSet to track configuration changes and enable rollback functionality.

    # Get ControllerRevisions for Skills DaemonSet
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-skills-daemonset

# View specific ControllerRevision details
kubectl describe controllerrevision anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 -n anihpj-services
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-skills-daemonset 1 2d anihpj-skills-daemonset-9c4e2f8a67 anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 1d
    Example 2: Author Service StatefulSet Revision History

    Examine ControllerRevision history for anihpj Author service StatefulSet to understand deployment evolution and rollback points.

    # List all ControllerRevisions for Author StatefulSet
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services --selector=controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-author-elasticsearch

# Get detailed revision information
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-author-elasticsearch-5f8c9d7e23 -n anihpj-services -o yaml
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-author-elasticsearch-5f8c9d7e23 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 1 3d anihpj-author-elasticsearch-7a1b4e9f56 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 2 2d anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3 1d
    Example 3: Location Service MongoDB StatefulSet Revision Comparison

    Compare ControllerRevisions for anihpj Location service MongoDB StatefulSet to identify configuration differences between versions.

    # Get revision details for comparison
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-6d4a8b9c12 -n anihpj-services -o json | jq .data

# List revisions with creation timestamps
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-location-mongodb --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-location-mongodb-6d4a8b9c12 anihpj-location-mongodb 1 4d anihpj-location-mongodb-8f2c1e5a47 anihpj-location-mongodb 2 3d anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 anihpj-location-mongodb 3 2d
    Example 4: JobPost Service ClickHouse StatefulSet Revision Management

    Manage ControllerRevisions for anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse StatefulSet including cleanup of old revisions to maintain history.

    # Get all revisions for JobPost ClickHouse
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse

# Delete old revision (cleanup)
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93 -n anihpj-services
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93 anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 1 5d anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-7d5f2a9c46 anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 2 4d anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 3 3d
    Example 5: Multi-Service Revision Monitoring

    Monitor ControllerRevisions across all anihpj services to track deployment history and identify rollback opportunities.

    # Get all ControllerRevisions in anihpj services namespace
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services

# Filter revisions created in last 24 hours
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services --field-selector metadata.creationTimestamp>$(date -u -d '1 day ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 1d anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3 1d anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 anihpj-location-mongodb 3 2d anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 3 3d

    ControllerRevisionList

    ControllerRevisionList is a resource containing a list of ControllerRevision objects.

    Description

    ControllerRevisionList is a resource containing a list of ControllerRevision objects. It provides a way to list and manage multiple controller revisions as a collection, useful for batch operations and comprehensive revision management.

    Syntax

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevisionList
metadata:
  resourceVersion: "string"
items:
  - # Array of ControllerRevision objects

    Parameters

    apiVersion (string): API version, always "apps/v1"
    kind (string): Resource type, always "ControllerRevisionList"
    metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata
    items ([]ControllerRevision, required): Items is the list of ControllerRevision objects

    Return Value

    Returns a ControllerRevisionList object containing an array of ControllerRevision objects matching the specified criteria.

    Examples

    Example 1: Skills Service Revision List

    List all ControllerRevisions for anihpj Skills service to review deployment history and available rollback points.

    # Get ControllerRevisionList for Skills service
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-skills -o yaml
    apiVersion: v1 kind: List items: - apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ControllerRevision metadata: name: anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 namespace: anihpj-services
    Example 2: Author Service Elasticsearch Revision History

    Retrieve comprehensive ControllerRevisionList for anihpj Author service Elasticsearch StatefulSet with detailed revision metadata.

    # Get detailed ControllerRevisionList for Author Elasticsearch
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-author-elasticsearch -o json
    { "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "List", "items": [ { "apiVersion": "apps/v1", "kind": "ControllerRevision", "metadata": { "name": "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89", "namespace": "anihpj-services" }, "revision": 3 } ] }
    Example 3: Location Service MongoDB Revision Collection

    Query ControllerRevisionList for anihpj Location service MongoDB StatefulSet to analyze revision patterns and deployment frequency.

    # Get ControllerRevisionList with custom columns
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-location-mongodb -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REVISION:.revision,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME REVISION AGE anihpj-location-mongodb-6d4a8b9c12 1 2024-09-04T10:15:30Z anihpj-location-mongodb-8f2c1e5a47 2 2024-09-05T14:22:15Z anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 3 2024-09-06T09:45:22Z
    Example 4: JobPost Service ClickHouse Revision Management

    Manage ControllerRevisionList for anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse StatefulSet including filtering and sorting operations.

    # Get sorted ControllerRevisionList by revision number
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse --sort-by=.revision
    NAME REVISION AGE anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93 1 5d anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-7d5f2a9c46 2 4d anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 3 3d
    Example 5: Multi-Service Revision List Aggregation

    Aggregate ControllerRevisionList across all anihpj services to provide comprehensive revision management and monitoring capabilities.

    # Get comprehensive ControllerRevisionList across all services
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services --show-labels
    NAME REVISION AGE LABELS anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 2 1d controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-skills-daemonset anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 3 1d controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-author-elasticsearch anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 3 2d controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-location-mongodb anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 3 3d controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse

    ControllerRevision Operations

    Read ControllerRevision

    Read the specified ControllerRevision resource to retrieve detailed information about a specific revision snapshot.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/controllerrevisions/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Get specific ControllerRevision
kubectl get controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace

# Get ControllerRevision with output formatting
kubectl get controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace -o yaml

# Get ControllerRevision details
kubectl describe controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace

# Get ControllerRevision with custom columns
kubectl get controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REVISION:.revision

# Get ControllerRevision with JSON path
kubectl get controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace -o jsonpath='{.revision}'

    Django Example

    Read anihpj Django ControllerRevision to examine specific revision details for StatefulSet rollback analysis.

    # Get specific Skills DaemonSet ControllerRevision
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 -n anihpj-services

# Get Author Elasticsearch StatefulSet revision details
kubectl describe controllerrevision anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 -n anihpj-services
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 1d
    Example 2: Author Service Elasticsearch Revision Analysis

    Read anihpj Author service Elasticsearch ControllerRevision to analyze specific configuration snapshots for deployment tracking.

    # Get Author Elasticsearch ControllerRevision in YAML format
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 -n anihpj-services -o yaml

# Extract revision data for comparison
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.data}' | base64 -d
    apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ControllerRevision metadata: name: anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 namespace: anihpj-services revision: 3
    Example 3: Location Service MongoDB Revision Details

    Read anihpj Location service MongoDB ControllerRevision to understand configuration changes and rollback points for database management.

    # Get Location MongoDB ControllerRevision details
kubectl describe controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 -n anihpj-services

# Get revision with creation timestamp
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REVISION:.revision,CREATED:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    Name: anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 Namespace: anihpj-services Labels: controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-location-mongodb Revision: 3 Data:
    Example 4: JobPost Service ClickHouse Revision Inspection

    Read anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse ControllerRevision to inspect analytics database configuration snapshots for performance tuning.

    # Get JobPost ClickHouse ControllerRevision
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 -n anihpj-services -o wide

# Extract and decode revision data
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 -n anihpj-services -o json | jq '.data | @base64d'
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE LABELS anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 3 3d controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse
    Example 5: Cross-Service Revision Comparison

    Read multiple anihpj service ControllerRevisions to compare configuration differences across Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

    # Get multiple ControllerRevisions for comparison
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 -n anihpj-services

# Compare revision numbers across services
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,CONTROLLER:.metadata.ownerReferences[0].name,REVISION:.revision
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3

    List ControllerRevision

    List or watch ControllerRevision objects in a specific namespace to monitor revision history and track deployment changes.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/controllerrevisions

    kubectl Syntax

    # List all ControllerRevisions in namespace
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n my-namespace

# List with labels
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n my-namespace -l app=my-app

# List with field selector
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n my-namespace --field-selector metadata.name=my-revision

# Watch ControllerRevisions
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n my-namespace -w

# List with custom output
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n my-namespace -o wide

    Django Example

    List anihpj Django ControllerRevisions to monitor revision history across all services for deployment tracking.

    # List all ControllerRevisions in anihpj services namespace
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services

# List ControllerRevisions for specific controller
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-skills-daemonset
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 1d anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3 1d
    Example 2: Author Service Elasticsearch Revision History

    List anihpj Author service Elasticsearch ControllerRevisions to track search service deployment evolution and rollback points.

    # List Author Elasticsearch ControllerRevisions
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-author-elasticsearch

# List with detailed output showing creation time
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-author-elasticsearch -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REVISION:.revision,CREATED:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME REVISION CREATED anihpj-author-elasticsearch-5f8c9d7e23 1 2024-09-03T10:15:30Z anihpj-author-elasticsearch-7a1b4e9f56 2 2024-09-04T14:22:15Z anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 3 2024-09-05T09:45:22Z
    Example 3: Location Service MongoDB Revision Monitoring

    List anihpj Location service MongoDB ControllerRevisions to monitor geographic database configuration changes and version history.

    # List Location MongoDB ControllerRevisions with watch
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-location-mongodb -w

# List sorted by revision number
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-location-mongodb --sort-by=.revision
    NAME REVISION AGE anihpj-location-mongodb-6d4a8b9c12 1 4d anihpj-location-mongodb-8f2c1e5a47 2 3d anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 3 2d
    Example 4: JobPost Service ClickHouse Revision Analytics

    List anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse ControllerRevisions to analyze analytics database deployment patterns and revision frequency.

    # List JobPost ClickHouse ControllerRevisions with labels
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse --show-labels

# List recent revisions (last 7 days)
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse --field-selector metadata.creationTimestamp>$(date -u -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    NAME REVISION AGE LABELS anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93 1 5d app=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse,controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-7d5f2a9c46 2 4d app=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse,controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse
    Example 5: Multi-Service Revision Overview

    List all anihpj service ControllerRevisions to provide comprehensive revision overview across Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

    # List all ControllerRevisions across services
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -o wide

# List with controller information
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,CONTROLLER:.metadata.ownerReferences[0].name,REVISION:.revision,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 1d anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3 1d anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 anihpj-location-mongodb 3 2d anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse 3 3d

    List All Namespaces ControllerRevision

    List or watch ControllerRevision objects across all namespaces to provide cluster-wide revision monitoring and management.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/controllerrevisions

    kubectl Syntax

    # List ControllerRevisions across all namespaces
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces

# List with specific labels across all namespaces
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -l app=my-app

# Watch ControllerRevisions across all namespaces
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -w

# List with custom output format
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -o wide

# List with field selector
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces --field-selector metadata.namespace=production

    Django Example

    List anihpj Django ControllerRevisions across all namespaces to monitor deployment history in production, staging, and development environments.

    # List all anihpj ControllerRevisions across namespaces
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -l app.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj

# List ControllerRevisions in production and services namespaces
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces --field-selector metadata.namespace=anihpj-production,metadata.namespace=anihpj-services
    NAMESPACE NAME CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-services anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 1d anihpj-production anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3 1d
    Example 2: Cross-Environment Revision Tracking

    List anihpj ControllerRevisions across development, staging, and production namespaces to track deployment progression and version consistency.

    # List ControllerRevisions across all anihpj environments
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -l project=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,REVISION:.revision

# Filter by specific anihpj environments
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces | grep -E 'anihpj-(dev|staging|production)'
    NAMESPACE NAME REVISION anihpj-dev anihpj-skills-daemonset-5a6b7c8d94 1 anihpj-staging anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 2 anihpj-production anihpj-skills-daemonset-9c4e2f1a67 3
    Example 3: Multi-Namespace Service Monitoring

    Monitor anihpj service ControllerRevisions across multiple namespaces to ensure consistent deployment versions and identify discrepancies.

    # Watch ControllerRevisions across all anihpj namespaces
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=anihpj -w

# List with controller and namespace information
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,CONTROLLER:.metadata.ownerReferences[0].name,REVISION:.revision,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAMESPACE CONTROLLER REVISION AGE anihpj-services anihpj-skills-daemonset 2 2024-09-07T10:15:30Z anihpj-services anihpj-author-elasticsearch 3 2024-09-07T14:22:15Z anihpj-production anihpj-location-mongodb 3 2024-09-06T09:45:22Z
    Example 4: Cluster-Wide Revision Analysis

    Analyze anihpj ControllerRevisions across the entire cluster to identify deployment patterns, version distribution, and rollback opportunities.

    # Get comprehensive cluster-wide ControllerRevision overview
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces --show-labels | grep anihpj

# List recent revisions across all namespaces
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces --field-selector metadata.creationTimestamp>$(date -u -d '1 day ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
    NAMESPACE NAME REVISION AGE LABELS anihpj-services anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 2 1d app=anihpj-skills,controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-skills-daemonset anihpj-services anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 3 1d app=anihpj-author,controller.kubernetes.io/name=anihpj-author-elasticsearch
    Example 5: Production vs Staging Revision Comparison

    Compare anihpj ControllerRevisions between production and staging environments to ensure deployment synchronization and version alignment.

    # Compare revisions between production and staging
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -l environment=production -o custom-columns=SERVICE:.metadata.labels.app,REVISION:.revision
kubectl get controllerrevisions --all-namespaces -l environment=staging -o custom-columns=SERVICE:.metadata.labels.app,REVISION:.revision
    SERVICE REVISION anihpj-skills 3 anihpj-author 3 anihpj-location 2 anihpj-jobpost 2

    Create ControllerRevision

    Create a new ControllerRevision resource to capture an immutable snapshot of controller state data for rollback and versioning purposes.

    HTTP Request

    POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/controllerrevisions

    kubectl Syntax

    # Create ControllerRevision from YAML file
kubectl apply -f controller-revision.yaml

# Create ControllerRevision with inline definition
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevision
metadata:
  name: my-revision
  namespace: my-namespace
revision: 1
data:
  # serialized controller state
EOF

# Create with dry run
kubectl apply -f controller-revision.yaml --dry-run=client

# Create with field manager
kubectl apply -f controller-revision.yaml --field-manager=my-manager

# Create and validate
kubectl apply -f controller-revision.yaml --validate=true

    Django Example

    Create anihpj Django ControllerRevision to manually capture configuration snapshots for critical deployment states.

    # Create Skills service ControllerRevision manually
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevision
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-manual-snapshot-v1
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-skills
    revision-type: manual
revision: 10
data:
  # Manual snapshot of Skills DaemonSet configuration
EOF

# Create Author Elasticsearch ControllerRevision with backup
kubectl apply -f anihpj-author-elasticsearch-revision.yaml -n anihpj-services
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-skills-manual-snapshot-v1 created controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch-backup-v3 created
    Example 2: Location Service MongoDB Revision Creation

    Create anihpj Location service MongoDB ControllerRevision to preserve critical database configuration states for disaster recovery.

    # Create Location MongoDB ControllerRevision with backup configuration
cat > anihpj-location-mongodb-revision.yaml << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevision
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-mongodb-backup-v4
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-location-mongodb
    backup-type: critical-state
    created-by: admin
revision: 4
data:
  # Serialized MongoDB StatefulSet configuration
  # with geographic indexing and replication settings
EOF

kubectl apply -f anihpj-location-mongodb-revision.yaml
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb-backup-v4 created
    Example 3: JobPost Service ClickHouse Revision Backup

    Create anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse ControllerRevision to backup analytics database configuration before major performance tuning.

    # Create JobPost ClickHouse pre-optimization ControllerRevision
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevision
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-pre-optimization
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse
    snapshot-reason: pre-performance-tuning
    optimization-phase: baseline
revision: 5
data:
  # Pre-optimization ClickHouse StatefulSet configuration
  # baseline performance settings
EOF
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-pre-optimization created
    Example 4: Multi-Service Revision Synchronization

    Create synchronized anihpj ControllerRevisions across Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services for coordinated deployment snapshots.

    # Create synchronized revisions for all services
for service in skills author location jobpost; do
  kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevision
metadata:
  name: anihpj-$service-sync-snapshot-v6
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-$service
    sync-group: release-v2.1.0
    coordination: synchronized
revision: 6
data:
  # Synchronized $service configuration
EOF
done
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-skills-sync-snapshot-v6 created controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-author-sync-snapshot-v6 created controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-sync-snapshot-v6 created controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-jobpost-sync-snapshot-v6 created
    Example 5: Emergency Rollback Revision Creation

    Create emergency anihpj ControllerRevisions to capture known-good states before critical system updates and migrations.

    # Create emergency rollback revisions
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ControllerRevision
metadata:
  name: anihpj-emergency-rollback-point
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    emergency: "true"
    rollback-point: stable-v2.0.5
    criticality: high
revision: 999
data:
  # Emergency rollback configuration
  # stable anihpj platform state
EOF
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-emergency-rollback-point created

    Update ControllerRevision

    Replace the specified ControllerRevision resource. Note: ControllerRevisions are typically immutable, so updates are generally not allowed for the data field.

    HTTP Request

    PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/controllerrevisions/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Update ControllerRevision from YAML file
kubectl apply -f controller-revision-updated.yaml

# Replace ControllerRevision completely
kubectl replace -f controller-revision.yaml

# Update with force
kubectl replace -f controller-revision.yaml --force

# Update with cascade deletion
kubectl replace -f controller-revision.yaml --cascade=foreground

# Update with dry run
kubectl apply -f controller-revision.yaml --dry-run=client

    Django Example

    Update anihpj Django ControllerRevision metadata such as labels and annotations while preserving immutable data field.

    # Update Skills ControllerRevision labels only
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"updated":"true","maintenance":"completed"}}}'

# Update Author ControllerRevision annotations
kubectl annotate controllerrevision anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 -n anihpj-services deployment.status="verified"
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 annotated
    Example 2: Location Service MongoDB Revision Metadata Update

    Update anihpj Location service MongoDB ControllerRevision metadata to add operational tracking and maintenance information.

    # Update Location MongoDB ControllerRevision with maintenance info
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"maintenance.window":"2024-09-08T02:00:00Z","verified.by":"ops-team","rollback.tested":"true"}}}'

# Add operational labels
kubectl label controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 -n anihpj-services environment=production stability=verified
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 labeled
    Example 3: JobPost Service ClickHouse Revision Annotation Update

    Update anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse ControllerRevision with performance metrics and optimization tracking annotations.

    # Update JobPost ClickHouse ControllerRevision with performance data
kubectl annotate controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 -n anihpj-services \
  performance.baseline="established" \
  optimization.applied="memory-tuning" \
  benchmark.score="95"

# Update with rollback safety annotation
kubectl annotate controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 -n anihpj-services rollback.safe="true"
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 annotated controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 annotated
    Example 4: Multi-Service Revision Labeling

    Update anihpj ControllerRevisions across Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services with coordinated release labeling.

    # Add release labels to all service revisions
for service in skills author location jobpost; do
  kubectl label controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-$service release.version=v2.1.0 release.stage=production
done

# Add stability certification
kubectl label controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l release.version=v2.1.0 stability.certified=true
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 labeled controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 labeled controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 labeled controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 labeled
    Example 5: Emergency Revision Status Update

    Update anihpj emergency ControllerRevision with incident response tracking and resolution status annotations.

    # Update emergency revision with incident tracking
kubectl annotate controllerrevision anihpj-emergency-rollback-point -n anihpj-services \
  incident.id="INC-2024-0908-001" \
  incident.status="resolved" \
  rollback.executed="false" \
  resolution.time="2024-09-08T15:30:00Z"

# Mark as emergency resolution point
kubectl label controllerrevision anihpj-emergency-rollback-point -n anihpj-services emergency.resolution=available
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-emergency-rollback-point annotated controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-emergency-rollback-point labeled

    Patch ControllerRevision

    Partially update the specified ControllerRevision resource by modifying specific fields without replacing the entire resource definition. Typically used for metadata updates.

    HTTP Request

    PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/controllerrevisions/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Patch ControllerRevision with JSON
kubectl patch controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace -p '{"metadata":{"labels":{"updated":"true"}}}'

# Patch with YAML
kubectl patch controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --type merge -p 'metadata: labels: environment: production'

# Patch with JSON file
kubectl patch controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --patch-file patch.json

# Patch with strategic merge
kubectl patch controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --type strategic -p '{"metadata":{"annotations":{"note":"updated"}}}'

# Patch with JSON merge
kubectl patch controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --type merge -p '{"metadata":{"finalizers":["custom-finalizer"]}}'

    Django Example

    Patch anihpj Django ControllerRevision to add operational metadata and tracking information without modifying immutable data.

    # Patch Skills ControllerRevision with deployment tracking
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"deployment.phase":"completed","verification.status":"passed"}}}'

# Patch Author ControllerRevision with performance annotations
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 -n anihpj-services -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"performance.baseline":"established","load.tested":"true"}}}'
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 patched
    Example 2: Location Service MongoDB Revision Operational Patch

    Patch anihpj Location service MongoDB ControllerRevision with geographic indexing verification and operational readiness status.

    # Patch Location MongoDB ControllerRevision with geo-indexing status
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 -n anihpj-services --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/metadata/annotations/geo-indexing.verified", "value": "true"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/metadata/annotations/replication.status", "value": "healthy"}]'

# Patch with operational readiness
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 -n anihpj-services -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"operational.ready":"true","backup.verified":"true"}}}'
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 patched
    Example 3: JobPost Service ClickHouse Revision Performance Patch

    Patch anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse ControllerRevision with analytics performance metrics and optimization tracking data.

    # Create patch file for JobPost ClickHouse performance tracking
cat > jobpost-clickhouse-patch.json << 'EOF'
{
  "metadata": {
    "annotations": {
      "analytics.performance.baseline": "1000 queries/sec",
      "memory.optimization.applied": "true",
      "cpu.utilization.target": "75%",
      "storage.compression.enabled": "true"
    },
    "labels": {
      "performance.tier": "high",
      "optimization.level": "advanced"
    }
  }
}
EOF

kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 -n anihpj-services --patch-file jobpost-clickhouse-patch.json
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 patched
    Example 4: Cross-Service Revision Coordination Patch

    Patch anihpj ControllerRevisions across Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services with coordinated release and synchronization metadata.

    # Patch all service revisions with release coordination
for service in skills author location jobpost; do
  kubectl patch controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-$service --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"release.coordination":"v2.1.0","sync.group":"anihpj-platform","deployment.wave":"1"}}}'
done

# Patch with environment synchronization status
kubectl patch controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l release.coordination=v2.1.0 --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"sync.status":"coordinated","deployment.verified":"true"}}}'
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-skills-daemonset-7b9f8d6c45 patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-author-elasticsearch-8c2d5f1a89 patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-location-mongodb-9e3b7d6f28 patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-9b1e6c4f72 patched
    Example 5: Emergency Response Revision Patch

    Patch anihpj emergency ControllerRevision with incident response metadata and rollback preparation status for crisis management.

    # Patch emergency revision with incident response data
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-emergency-rollback-point -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"incident.response.plan":"activated","rollback.preparation":"complete","emergency.contact":"ops-team@anihpj.com","escalation.level":"2"}}}'

# Patch with crisis management labels
kubectl patch controllerrevision anihpj-emergency-rollback-point -n anihpj-services -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"crisis.management":"active","rollback.ready":"true","emergency.verified":"true"}}}'
    controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-emergency-rollback-point patched controllerrevision.apps/anihpj-emergency-rollback-point patched

    Delete ControllerRevision

    Delete the specified ControllerRevision resource to remove outdated revision snapshots and clean up revision history.

    HTTP Request

    DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/controllerrevisions/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Delete specific ControllerRevision
kubectl delete controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace

# Delete with grace period
kubectl delete controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --grace-period=30

# Delete immediately
kubectl delete controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --grace-period=0 --force

# Delete with cascade
kubectl delete controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --cascade=foreground

# Delete with dry run
kubectl delete controllerrevision my-revision -n my-namespace --dry-run=client

    Django Example

    Delete outdated anihpj Django ControllerRevisions to maintain clean revision history and optimize storage usage.

    # Delete old Skills ControllerRevision
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-skills-daemonset-5a6b7c8d94 -n anihpj-services

# Delete obsolete Author ControllerRevision
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-author-elasticsearch-5f8c9d7e23 -n anihpj-services
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-daemonset-5a6b7c8d94" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-5f8c9d7e23" deleted
    Example 2: Location Service MongoDB Revision Cleanup
