    Delete anihpj Location service MongoDB ControllerRevisions that are no longer needed for rollback to maintain optimal revision history.

    # Delete outdated Location MongoDB ControllerRevisions
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-6d4a8b9c12 -n anihpj-services

# Delete with confirmation
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-location-mongodb-8f2c1e5a47 -n anihpj-services --wait=true
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-location-mongodb-6d4a8b9c12" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-location-mongodb-8f2c1e5a47" deleted
    Example 3: JobPost Service ClickHouse Revision Archive

    Delete anihpj JobPost service ClickHouse ControllerRevisions after archiving to external storage for compliance and space management.

    # Archive and delete old JobPost ClickHouse ControllerRevisions
kubectl get controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93 -n anihpj-services -o yaml > archive/anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93.yaml
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93 -n anihpj-services

# Delete with backup verification
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-7d5f2a9c46 -n anihpj-services --wait=true
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-4a7c8e1b93" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse-7d5f2a9c46" deleted
    Example 4: Automated Revision Retention Policy

    Delete anihpj ControllerRevisions based on retention policy to maintain only the last 3 revisions per service for optimal management.

    # Delete old revisions keeping only last 3 per service
for service in skills author location jobpost; do
  # Get revisions sorted by creation time (oldest first)
  old_revisions=$(kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-$service --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp -o name | head -n -3)
  if [ ! -z "$old_revisions" ]; then
    echo "$old_revisions" | xargs kubectl delete -n anihpj-services
  fi
done
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-daemonset-1a2b3c4d56" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-2b3c4d5e67" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-location-mongodb-3c4d5e6f78" deleted
    Example 5: Emergency Revision Cleanup

    Delete anihpj emergency ControllerRevisions after incident resolution to clean up crisis management artifacts and maintain system hygiene.

    # Delete emergency revisions after incident resolution
kubectl delete controllerrevision anihpj-emergency-rollback-point -n anihpj-services

# Delete test revisions used during incident
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l emergency=true,incident.resolved=true

# Verify cleanup
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l emergency=true
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-emergency-rollback-point" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-emergency-test-revision-1" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-emergency-test-revision-2" deleted No resources found in anihpj-services namespace.

    Delete Collection ControllerRevision

    Delete collection of ControllerRevision resources based on label selectors or field selectors to perform bulk cleanup operations.

    HTTP Request

    DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/controllerrevisions

    kubectl Syntax

    # Delete ControllerRevisions by label selector
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n my-namespace -l app=my-app

# Delete with field selector
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n my-namespace --field-selector metadata.name=my-revision

# Delete all ControllerRevisions in namespace
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n my-namespace --all

# Delete with grace period
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n my-namespace -l version=old --grace-period=30

# Delete with dry run
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n my-namespace -l app=my-app --dry-run=client

    Django Example

    Delete collection of anihpj Django ControllerRevisions to perform bulk cleanup of outdated revision history across services.

    # Delete all old ControllerRevisions for anihpj services
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l version=old

# Delete ControllerRevisions older than specific date
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l created-before=2024-09-01
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-daemonset-old-1" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-old-2" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-location-mongodb-old-1" deleted
    Example 2: Skills Service Revision Bulk Cleanup

    Delete collection of anihpj Skills service ControllerRevisions to clean up DaemonSet revision history and maintain optimal storage usage.

    # Delete all Skills service ControllerRevisions except current
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-skills,status!=current

# Delete Skills ControllerRevisions from specific deployment wave
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-skills,deployment.wave=1
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-daemonset-wave1-1" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-daemonset-wave1-2" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-daemonset-wave1-3" deleted
    Example 3: Author Service Elasticsearch Revision Collection Cleanup

    Delete collection of anihpj Author service Elasticsearch ControllerRevisions based on performance testing completion and version obsolescence.

    # Delete Author Elasticsearch test ControllerRevisions
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-author-elasticsearch,test.phase=completed

# Delete pre-optimization ControllerRevisions
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-author-elasticsearch,optimization.phase=baseline
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test-1" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-test-2" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-baseline-1" deleted
    Example 4: Multi-Service Coordinated Revision Cleanup

    Delete collection of anihpj ControllerRevisions across Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services for coordinated platform cleanup.

    # Delete all development phase ControllerRevisions across services
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l development.phase=testing,environment=dev

# Delete deprecated version ControllerRevisions
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l version=v1.x,deprecated=true

# Verify cleanup across all services
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l deprecated=true
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-v1-deprecated" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-v1-deprecated" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-location-v1-deprecated" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-jobpost-v1-deprecated" deleted No resources found in anihpj-services namespace.
    Example 5: Platform-Wide Revision Archive and Cleanup

    Delete collection of anihpj ControllerRevisions after platform-wide archival to external storage for compliance and operational efficiency.

    # Archive and delete all ControllerRevisions older than 30 days
kubectl get controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -o yaml --field-selector metadata.creationTimestamp<$(date -u -d '30 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) > archive/anihpj-controllerrevisions-archive-$(date +%Y%m%d).yaml

# Delete archived ControllerRevisions
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services --field-selector metadata.creationTimestamp<$(date -u -d '30 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Delete emergency test ControllerRevisions
kubectl delete controllerrevisions -n anihpj-services -l test.type=emergency,cleanup.required=true
    controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-skills-old-revision-1" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-author-old-revision-2" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-location-old-revision-1" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-jobpost-old-revision-3" deleted controllerrevision.apps "anihpj-emergency-test-1" deleted

    DaemonSet

    DaemonSet Overview

    DaemonSetSpec - Desired state specification
    DaemonSetStatus - Current observed status
    DaemonSetList - Collection of daemon sets
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: DaemonSet v1 API | API Version: apps/v1

    DaemonSet represents the configuration of a daemon set that ensures a copy of a pod runs on every node in the cluster or a subset of nodes.

    Description

    DaemonSet represents the configuration of a daemon set. DaemonSets ensure that all (or some) nodes run a copy of a pod. As nodes are added to the cluster, pods are added to them. As nodes are removed from the cluster, those pods are garbage collected. Deleting a DaemonSet will clean up the pods it created. Common uses of DaemonSets include running a cluster storage daemon like glusterd or ceph, running a logs collection daemon like fluentd or logstash, and running a node monitoring daemon like Prometheus Node Exporter.

    Syntax

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: daemonset-name
  namespace: namespace-name
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: daemon-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: daemon-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: container-name
        image: container-image:tag

    Parameters

    apiVersion (string): API version, always "apps/v1"
    kind (string): Resource type, always "DaemonSet"
    metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata containing name, namespace, labels, and annotations
    spec (DaemonSetSpec): The desired behavior of this daemon set including pod template and update strategy
    status (DaemonSetStatus): The current status of this daemon set. This data may be out of date by some window of time. Populated by the system. Read-only.

    Return Value

    Returns a DaemonSet object representing the configuration and status of a daemon set that ensures pods run on every matching node.

    Examples

    Example 1: Skills Service Logging DaemonSet

    Create anihpj Skills service logging DaemonSet to collect application logs from all worker nodes running Skills microservice containers.

    # Create Skills logging DaemonSet
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-logger
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-skills-logger
    service: skills
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-skills-logger
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-skills-logger
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: fluentd
        image: fluentd:latest
        env:
        - name: SERVICE_NAME
          value: "anihpj-skills"
EOF

# Get DaemonSet details
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-skills-logger -n anihpj-services
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-skills-logger created NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-skills-logger 3 3 3 3 3 10s
    Example 2: Author Service Monitoring DaemonSet

    Deploy anihpj Author service monitoring DaemonSet to collect Elasticsearch metrics and performance data from all nodes hosting Author microservice.

    # Create Author monitoring DaemonSet
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-monitor
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-author-monitor
    component: monitoring
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-author-monitor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-author-monitor
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: node-exporter
        image: prom/node-exporter:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9100
        env:
        - name: ELASTICSEARCH_SERVICE
          value: "anihpj-author-elasticsearch"
EOF
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-author-monitor created
    Example 3: Location Service Network DaemonSet

    Deploy anihpj Location service network DaemonSet to manage geographic data routing and proximity-based load balancing across cluster nodes.

    # Create Location network DaemonSet
kubectl create daemonset anihpj-location-network \
  --image=nginx:alpine \
  --namespace=anihpj-services \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

# Add location-specific configuration
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-location-network -n anihpj-services -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"nginx","env":[{"name":"LOCATION_SERVICE","value":"anihpj-location-mongodb"}]}]}}}}'
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-location-network created daemonset.apps/anihpj-location-network patched
    Example 4: JobPost Service Analytics DaemonSet

    Create anihpj JobPost service analytics DaemonSet to collect job posting metrics and ClickHouse performance data from all cluster nodes.

    # Create JobPost analytics DaemonSet with resource limits
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-analytics
  namespace: anihpj-services
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-jobpost-analytics
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-jobpost-analytics
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: analytics-collector
        image: alpine:latest
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
        env:
        - name: CLICKHOUSE_HOST
          value: "anihpj-jobpost-clickhouse"
        - name: COLLECTION_INTERVAL
          value: "30s"
EOF
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-analytics created
    Example 5: Multi-Service Security DaemonSet

    Deploy anihpj multi-service security DaemonSet to provide security monitoring and intrusion detection across all Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

    # Create security monitoring DaemonSet
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-security-monitor
  namespace: anihpj-services
  labels:
    app: anihpj-security-monitor
    purpose: security
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-security-monitor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-security-monitor
    spec:
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
      containers:
      - name: security-agent
        image: falcosecurity/falco:latest
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
        env:
        - name: SERVICES_TO_MONITOR
          value: "skills,author,location,jobpost"
EOF
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-security-monitor created

    DaemonSetSpec

    DaemonSetSpec is the specification of a daemon set that defines the desired behavior and pod template.

    Description

    DaemonSetSpec is the specification of a daemon set. It defines the desired behavior of the daemon set including pod template, selector requirements, update strategy, and rollback configuration. The spec ensures that the desired number of pods run on matching nodes.

    Syntax

    spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: daemon-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: daemon-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: container-name
        image: container-image:tag
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
  minReadySeconds: 0
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10

    Parameters

    selector (LabelSelector, required): A label query over pods that are managed by the daemon set. Must match the pod template's labels.
    template (PodTemplateSpec, required): An object that describes the pod that will be created. The DaemonSet will create exactly one copy of this pod on every matching node.
    updateStrategy (DaemonSetUpdateStrategy): An update strategy to replace existing DaemonSet pods with new pods. Can be "RollingUpdate" or "OnDelete".
    minReadySeconds (int32): The minimum number of seconds for which a newly created DaemonSet pod should be ready without crashing.
    revisionHistoryLimit (int32): The number of old history to retain to allow rollback. Defaults to 10.

    Return Value

    Returns a DaemonSetSpec object defining the desired behavior and configuration for daemon set pod deployment across cluster nodes.

    Examples

    Example 1: Skills Service Log Collection Spec

    Configure anihpj Skills service DaemonSetSpec for log collection with rolling update strategy and node selection criteria.

    # Create Skills DaemonSet with specific spec configuration
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-logs
  namespace: anihpj-services
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-skills-logs
      component: logging
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-skills-logs
        component: logging
    spec:
      nodeSelector:
        service: skills
      containers:
      - name: log-collector
        image: fluentd:v1.14
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: "200Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
  minReadySeconds: 10
EOF
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-skills-logs created
    Example 2: Author Service Elasticsearch Monitoring Spec

    Define anihpj Author service DaemonSetSpec for Elasticsearch monitoring with advanced update strategy and resource constraints.

    # Get existing DaemonSet spec
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o yaml | grep -A 20 "spec:"

# Update DaemonSet spec with enhanced configuration
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"type":"RollingUpdate","rollingUpdate":{"maxUnavailable":"25%"}},"minReadySeconds":15,"revisionHistoryLimit":5}}'
    spec: revisionHistoryLimit: 5 selector: matchLabels: app: anihpj-author-monitor updateStrategy: rollingUpdate: maxUnavailable: 25% type: RollingUpdate daemonset.apps/anihpj-author-monitor patched
    Example 3: Location Service Network Proxy Spec

    Configure anihpj Location service DaemonSetSpec for geographic network proxy with OnDelete update strategy for manual control.

    # Create Location network proxy with OnDelete strategy
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-proxy
  namespace: anihpj-services
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-location-proxy
      tier: networking
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-location-proxy
        tier: networking
    spec:
      hostNetwork: true
      containers:
      - name: nginx-proxy
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          hostPort: 8080
        env:
        - name: UPSTREAM_SERVICE
          value: "anihpj-location-mongodb"
  updateStrategy:
    type: OnDelete
  minReadySeconds: 5
EOF
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-location-proxy created
    Example 4: JobPost Service Analytics Collector Spec

    Define anihpj JobPost service DaemonSetSpec for analytics data collection with resource limits and surge configuration.

    # Create JobPost analytics DaemonSet with surge configuration
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-collector
  namespace: anihpj-services
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-jobpost-collector
      function: analytics
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-jobpost-collector
        function: analytics
    spec:
      tolerations:
      - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/master
        effect: NoSchedule
      containers:
      - name: metrics-collector
        image: prometheus/node-exporter:latest
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "64Mi"
            cpu: "50m"
          limits:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
      maxSurge: 1
EOF
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-collector created
    Example 5: Multi-Service Security Agent Spec

    Configure anihpj multi-service DaemonSetSpec for security monitoring with privileged access and comprehensive node coverage.

    # Create security agent DaemonSet with comprehensive spec
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-security-agent
  namespace: anihpj-services
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj-security-agent
      security: "true"
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj-security-agent
        security: "true"
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: anihpj-security-sa
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
      containers:
      - name: security-scanner
        image: aquasec/trivy:latest
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
          runAsUser: 0
        volumeMounts:
        - name: docker-socket
          mountPath: /var/run/docker.sock
        env:
        - name: SCAN_SERVICES
          value: "skills,author,location,jobpost"
      volumes:
      - name: docker-socket
        hostPath:
          path: /var/run/docker.sock
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 2
  revisionHistoryLimit: 3
EOF
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-security-agent created

    DaemonSetStatus

    DaemonSetStatus represents the current status of a daemon set including pod distribution and condition information.

    Description

    DaemonSetStatus represents the current status of a daemon set. It provides information about the number of nodes that should be running the daemon pod, how many are currently running, and the overall health of the daemon set deployment across the cluster.

    Syntax

    status:
  currentNumberScheduled: 3
  desiredNumberScheduled: 3
  numberReady: 3
  numberAvailable: 3
  numberUnavailable: 0
  numberMisscheduled: 0
  updatedNumberScheduled: 3
  observedGeneration: 1
  conditions:
  - type: Ready
    status: "True"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-09-08T10:00:00Z"

    Parameters

    currentNumberScheduled (int32, required): The number of nodes that are running at least 1 daemon pod and are supposed to run the daemon pod.
    desiredNumberScheduled (int32, required): The total number of nodes that should be running the daemon pod.
    numberReady (int32, required): The number of nodes that should be running the daemon pod and have one or more pods running with a Ready condition.
    numberAvailable (int32): The number of nodes that should be running the daemon pod and have one or more pods running and available.
    numberUnavailable (int32): The number of nodes that should be running the daemon pod and have none of the daemon pod running and available.
    numberMisscheduled (int32, required): The number of nodes that are running the daemon pod, but are not supposed to run the daemon pod.
    updatedNumberScheduled (int32): The total number of nodes that are running updated daemon pod.
    conditions ([]DaemonSetCondition): Represents the latest available observations of a DaemonSet's current state.

    Return Value

    Returns a DaemonSetStatus object containing current deployment state, pod distribution metrics, and condition information across cluster nodes.

    Examples

    Example 1: Skills Service DaemonSet Status Monitoring

    Monitor anihpj Skills service DaemonSet status to track log collection pod deployment across worker nodes and identify any scheduling issues.

    # Get Skills DaemonSet status details
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-skills-logs -n anihpj-services -o wide

# Check detailed status information
kubectl describe daemonset anihpj-skills-logs -n anihpj-services | grep -A 10 "Status:"
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-skills-logs 3 3 3 3 3 service=skills 5m Status: Current Number Scheduled: 3 Desired Number Scheduled: 3 Number Ready: 3 Number Available: 3 Number Unavailable: 0
    Example 2: Author Service Monitoring DaemonSet Health Check

    Examine anihpj Author service monitoring DaemonSet status to verify Elasticsearch metrics collection is functioning across all designated nodes.

    # Get Author monitoring DaemonSet status in JSON format
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq .

# Check for any misscheduled or unavailable pods
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,DESIRED:.status.desiredNumberScheduled,CURRENT:.status.currentNumberScheduled,READY:.status.numberReady,MISSCHEDULED:.status.numberMisscheduled
    { "currentNumberScheduled": 4, "desiredNumberScheduled": 4, "numberAvailable": 4, "numberReady": 4, "numberUnavailable": 0, "numberMisscheduled": 0, "updatedNumberScheduled": 4 }
    Example 3: Location Service Network Proxy Status Analysis

    Analyze anihpj Location service network proxy DaemonSet status to ensure geographic routing is properly distributed across cluster topology.

    # Get Location proxy DaemonSet conditions
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-location-proxy -n anihpj-services -o yaml | grep -A 15 "conditions:"

# Monitor DaemonSet rollout status
kubectl rollout status daemonset/anihpj-location-proxy -n anihpj-services
    conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2024-09-08T10:15:30Z" message: All desired pods are scheduled and ready reason: SuccessfulDaemonSetRollout status: "True" type: Ready Waiting for daemon set "anihpj-location-proxy" rollout to finish: 3 out of 3 new pods have been updated... daemon set "anihpj-location-proxy" successfully rolled out
    Example 4: JobPost Service Analytics Collection Status

    Monitor anihpj JobPost service analytics DaemonSet status to track ClickHouse metrics collection performance and node coverage efficiency.

    # Get JobPost analytics DaemonSet comprehensive status
kubectl describe daemonset anihpj-jobpost-collector -n anihpj-services

# Check update progress and generation
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-jobpost-collector -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,GENERATION:.metadata.generation,OBSERVED:.status.observedGeneration,UPDATED:.status.updatedNumberScheduled
    Name: anihpj-jobpost-collector Current Number Scheduled: 4 Desired Number Scheduled: 4 Number Ready: 4 Number Available: 4 Number Unavailable: 0 Updated Number Scheduled: 4 Number Misscheduled: 0
    Example 5: Multi-Service Security Agent Status Overview

    Review anihpj multi-service security DaemonSet status to verify comprehensive security monitoring coverage across all Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost service nodes.

    # Get all anihpj DaemonSet status summary
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,DESIRED:.status.desiredNumberScheduled,CURRENT:.status.currentNumberScheduled,READY:.status.numberReady,AVAILABLE:.status.numberAvailable

# Check security agent specific status
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-security-agent -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")]}' | jq .
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AVAILABLE anihpj-skills-logs 3 3 3 3 anihpj-author-monitor 4 4 4 4 anihpj-location-proxy 3 3 3 3 anihpj-jobpost-collector 4 4 4 4 anihpj-security-agent 5 5 5 5 { "lastTransitionTime": "2024-09-08T10:20:00Z", "message": "All desired pods are scheduled and ready", "reason": "SuccessfulDaemonSetRollout", "status": "True", "type": "Ready" }

    DaemonSetList

    DaemonSetList is a collection of daemon sets that provides a way to list and manage multiple DaemonSet resources.

    Description

    DaemonSetList is a collection of daemon sets. It provides a way to list and manage multiple DaemonSet resources as a collection, useful for batch operations and comprehensive daemon set management across namespaces.

    Syntax

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSetList
metadata:
  resourceVersion: "string"
items:
  - # Array of DaemonSet objects

    Parameters

    apiVersion (string): API version, always "apps/v1"
    kind (string): Resource type, always "DaemonSetList"
    metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata
    items ([]DaemonSet, required): A list of daemon sets

    Return Value

    Returns a DaemonSetList object containing an array of DaemonSet objects matching the specified criteria.

    Examples

    Example 1: Skills Service DaemonSet Collection

    List all anihpj Skills service DaemonSets to review logging and monitoring daemon set deployments for comprehensive service coverage.

    # Get all Skills-related DaemonSets
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l service=skills -o yaml

# List Skills DaemonSets with custom output
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-skills* -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.numberReady,DESIRED:.status.desiredNumberScheduled
    apiVersion: v1 kind: List items: - apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: DaemonSet metadata: name: anihpj-skills-logs namespace: anihpj-services status: numberReady: 3
    Example 2: Author Service Elasticsearch DaemonSet Inventory

    Retrieve anihpj Author service DaemonSet collection to manage Elasticsearch monitoring and search optimization daemon sets.

    # Get Author service DaemonSet list with labels
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l component=author -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, ready: .status.numberReady, desired: .status.desiredNumberScheduled}'

# List Author DaemonSets with creation timestamps
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l service=author --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    { "name": "anihpj-author-monitor", "ready": 4, "desired": 4 } { "name": "anihpj-author-elasticsearch-exporter", "ready": 4, "desired": 4 }
    Example 3: Location Service Geographic DaemonSet Management

    Manage anihpj Location service DaemonSet collection for geographic data processing and network routing across cluster regions.

    # Get Location service DaemonSets with resource information
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l tier=networking,service=location -o wide

# Export Location DaemonSet collection for backup
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-location* -o yaml > anihpj-location-daemonsets-backup.yaml
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-location-proxy 3 3 3 3 3 15m anihpj-location-router 3 3 3 3 3 zone=geo 10m
    Example 4: JobPost Service Analytics DaemonSet Collection

    Query anihpj JobPost service DaemonSet collection to oversee analytics data collection and ClickHouse performance monitoring systems.

    # Get JobPost analytics DaemonSet collection
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l function=analytics,service=jobpost -o yaml

# Monitor JobPost DaemonSet resource utilization
kubectl top pods -n anihpj-services -l app=anihpj-jobpost-collector --containers
    apiVersion: v1 kind: List items: - apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: DaemonSet metadata: name: anihpj-jobpost-collector labels: function: analytics service: jobpost
    Example 5: Platform-Wide DaemonSet Collection Overview

    Review comprehensive anihpj DaemonSet collection across Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost services for platform-wide daemon set management.

    # Get all anihpj DaemonSets across services
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,SERVICE:.metadata.labels.service,READY:.status.numberReady,NODES:.status.desiredNumberScheduled,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp

# Get comprehensive DaemonSet collection with status
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, service: .metadata.labels.service, status: .status}'
    NAME SERVICE READY NODES AGE anihpj-skills-logs skills 3 3 2024-09-08T10:05:00Z anihpj-author-monitor author 4 4 2024-09-08T10:10:00Z anihpj-location-proxy location 3 3 2024-09-08T10:12:00Z anihpj-jobpost-collector jobpost 4 4 2024-09-08T10:15:00Z anihpj-security-agent security 5 5 2024-09-08T10:18:00Z

    DaemonSet Operations

    Overview

    DaemonSet operations provide comprehensive management capabilities for daemon sets in Kubernetes clusters. These operations enable you to create, read, update, delete, and monitor DaemonSets that ensure specific pods run on all (or a subset of) nodes in the cluster.

    DaemonSets are particularly useful for running system-level services, monitoring agents, logging collectors, and other background processes that need to run on every node. The anihpj platform leverages DaemonSets for various background services including skill validation agents, author cache processors, location data indexers, and job post background workers.

    Key Operation Categories
    • Read Operations - Read | Read Status
    • List Operations - List | List All Namespaces
    • Write Operations - Create
    • Update Operations - Update | Update Status
    • Patch Operations - Patch | Patch Status
    • Delete Operations - Delete | Delete Collection
    Common Use Cases
    • System Monitoring - Deploy monitoring agents on every node
    • Log Collection - Run log collection services across all nodes
    • Network Plugins - Install network components on each node
    • Storage Daemons - Deploy storage-related services
    • Security Agents - Run security scanning and monitoring tools
    • Background Processing - Execute background tasks on all nodes
    anihpj Platform Integration

    The anihpj job platform uses DaemonSets for various background services:

    • Skills Service - Skill validation and indexing agents on each node
    • Authors Service - Author data caching and synchronization processors
    • Locations Service - Geographic data processing and mapping services
    • JobPosts Service - Job post indexing and search optimization agents
    • Platform Monitoring - Comprehensive monitoring and alerting systems

    Read DaemonSet

    Read the specified DaemonSet resource to retrieve detailed information about daemon set configuration and current status.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets/{name}

    kubectl Syntax

    # Get specific DaemonSet
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace

# Get DaemonSet with output formatting
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace -o yaml

# Get DaemonSet details
kubectl describe daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace

# Get DaemonSet with custom columns
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.numberReady

# Get DaemonSet with JSON path
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}'

    Django Example

    Read anihpj Django DaemonSet to examine daemon set configuration and monitor pod distribution across cluster nodes.

    # Get Skills logging DaemonSet details
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-skills-logs -n anihpj-services

# Get Author monitoring DaemonSet configuration
kubectl describe daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-skills-logs 3 3 3 3 3 service=skills 10m
    Example 2: Author Service Elasticsearch Monitoring DaemonSet

    Read anihpj Author service Elasticsearch monitoring DaemonSet to verify search service metrics collection and node coverage.

    # Get Author monitoring DaemonSet in YAML format
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o yaml

# Extract specific DaemonSet configuration
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}'
    prom/node-exporter:latest
    Example 3: Location Service Network Proxy DaemonSet

    Read anihpj Location service network proxy DaemonSet to analyze geographic routing configuration and deployment status.

    # Get Location proxy DaemonSet with wide output
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-location-proxy -n anihpj-services -o wide

# Check Location DaemonSet update strategy
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-location-proxy -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.spec.updateStrategy}'
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE CONTAINERS IMAGES SELECTOR anihpj-location-proxy 3 3 3 3 3 15m nginx-proxy nginx:alpine app=anihpj-location-proxy
    Example 4: JobPost Service Analytics Collection DaemonSet

    Read anihpj JobPost service analytics DaemonSet to inspect ClickHouse metrics collection configuration and resource allocation.

    # Get JobPost analytics DaemonSet details
kubectl describe daemonset anihpj-jobpost-collector -n anihpj-services

# Get JobPost DaemonSet resource limits
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-jobpost-collector -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].resources}'
    {"limits":{"cpu":"100m","memory":"128Mi"},"requests":{"cpu":"50m","memory":"64Mi"}}
    Example 5: Multi-Service Security Agent DaemonSet

    Read anihpj multi-service security DaemonSet to review comprehensive security monitoring configuration across all service nodes.

    # Get security agent DaemonSet comprehensive details
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-security-agent -n anihpj-services -o yaml | grep -A 10 "securityContext"

# Check security DaemonSet pod distribution
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-security-agent -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.numberReady,DESIRED:.status.desiredNumberScheduled,NODES:.status.currentNumberScheduled
    NAME READY DESIRED NODES anihpj-security-agent 5 5 5

    Read DaemonSet Status

    Read status of the specified DaemonSet resource to retrieve current deployment state and pod distribution information.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets/{name}/status

    kubectl Syntax

    # Get DaemonSet status
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Check DaemonSet rollout status
kubectl rollout status daemonset/my-daemonset -n my-namespace

# Get DaemonSet conditions
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}'

# Monitor DaemonSet ready status
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace -o custom-columns=READY:.status.numberReady,DESIRED:.status.desiredNumberScheduled

# Watch DaemonSet status changes
kubectl get daemonset my-daemonset -n my-namespace -w

    Django Example

    Read anihpj Django DaemonSet status to monitor daemon set health and track pod deployment progress across cluster nodes.

    # Get Skills DaemonSet status
kubectl rollout status daemonset/anihpj-skills-logs -n anihpj-services

# Check Author DaemonSet readiness
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}'
    Waiting for daemon set "anihpj-skills-logs" rollout to finish: 3 out of 3 new pods have been updated... daemon set "anihpj-skills-logs" successfully rolled out 4
    Example 2: Author Service Monitoring DaemonSet Status

    Monitor anihpj Author service monitoring DaemonSet status to ensure Elasticsearch metrics collection is operational across all nodes.

    # Get Author DaemonSet comprehensive status
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq .

# Check for any issues in Author DaemonSet
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-author-monitor -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].message}'
    { "currentNumberScheduled": 4, "desiredNumberScheduled": 4, "numberAvailable": 4, "numberReady": 4, "updatedNumberScheduled": 4 } "All desired pods are scheduled and ready"
    Example 3: Location Service Network DaemonSet Status

    Check anihpj Location service network DaemonSet status to verify geographic routing daemon deployment across cluster topology.

    # Monitor Location DaemonSet rollout progress
kubectl rollout status daemonset/anihpj-location-proxy -n anihpj-services --timeout=300s

# Get Location DaemonSet update status
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-location-proxy -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.updatedNumberScheduled}'
    daemon set "anihpj-location-proxy" successfully rolled out 3
    Example 4: JobPost Service Analytics DaemonSet Status

    Monitor anihpj JobPost service analytics DaemonSet status to track ClickHouse metrics collection performance and availability.

    # Get JobPost DaemonSet availability status
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-jobpost-collector -n anihpj-services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,AVAILABLE:.status.numberAvailable,UNAVAILABLE:.status.numberUnavailable

# Watch JobPost DaemonSet status changes
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-jobpost-collector -n anihpj-services -w --no-headers
    NAME AVAILABLE UNAVAILABLE anihpj-jobpost-collector 4 0
    Example 5: Security Agent DaemonSet Status Overview

    Review anihpj security agent DaemonSet status to ensure comprehensive security monitoring coverage across all service nodes.

    # Get security DaemonSet status summary
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l security=true -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.numberReady,CURRENT:.status.currentNumberScheduled,DESIRED:.status.desiredNumberScheduled

# Check security DaemonSet generation status
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-security-agent -n anihpj-services -o jsonpath='{.status.observedGeneration}'
    NAME READY CURRENT DESIRED anihpj-security-agent 5 5 5 1

    List DaemonSet

    List or watch objects of kind DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets

    Path Parameters
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • allowWatchBookmarks boolean: allowWatchBookmarks requests watch events with type "BOOKMARK"
    • continue string: continue option should be set when retrieving more results from the server
    • fieldSelector string: selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their fields
    • labelSelector string: selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their labels
    • limit integer: limit is a maximum number of responses to return for a list call
    • resourceVersion string: resourceVersion sets a constraint on what resource versions a request may be served from
    • resourceVersionMatch string: resourceVersionMatch determines how resourceVersion is applied to list calls
    • sendInitialEvents boolean: sendInitialEvents controls whether the server will send the events for a watch before sending the current list state
    • timeoutSeconds integer: timeout for the list/watch call
    • watch boolean: watch for changes to the described resources and return them as a stream of add, update, and remove notifications
    Response

    200 (DaemonSetList): OK

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl get daemonsets
    Example 1: List Skills Service DaemonSets

    List all DaemonSets in the anihpj skills service namespace to monitor background skill validation agents.

    # List all DaemonSets in skills namespace
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-skills

# List DaemonSets with labels
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-skills -l app=skill-validator

# List with custom output format
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-skills -o wide
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE skill-validator 3 3 3 3 3 5d skill-indexer 3 3 3 3 3 5d
    Example 2: List Author Service DaemonSets with Field Selector

    List DaemonSets in the anihpj author service using field selectors to filter by metadata fields.

    # List DaemonSets with field selector
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-authors --field-selector metadata.name=author-cache-agent

# List DaemonSets created after specific time
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-authors --field-selector 'metadata.creationTimestamp>2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'

# Combine field and label selectors
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-authors --field-selector metadata.name=author-cache-agent -l component=caching
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE author-cache-agent 3 3 3 3 3 3d
    Example 3: List Location Service DaemonSets with Pagination

    List DaemonSets in the anihpj location service using pagination to handle large result sets efficiently.

    # List with limit and continue token
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-locations --limit=10

# Get next page using continue token
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-locations --limit=10 --continue=eyJyZXNvdXJjZVZlcnNpb24...

# Check total count without retrieving all items
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-locations --dry-run=server
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE location-geo-processor 3 3 3 3 3 2d location-mapper 3 3 3 3 3 2d
    Example 4: List JobPost Service DaemonSets with Watch

    Watch DaemonSets in the anihpj jobpost service for real-time monitoring of background job processing agents.

    # Watch DaemonSet changes
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-jobposts --watch

# Watch with output format
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-jobposts --watch -o yaml

# Watch specific DaemonSet
kubectl get daemonset jobpost-processor -n anihpj-jobposts --watch
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE jobpost-processor 3 3 3 3 3 1d jobpost-indexer 3 3 3 3 3 1d
    Example 5: List All Service DaemonSets with Label Filtering

    List all anihpj service DaemonSets across namespaces using comprehensive label selectors for monitoring overview.

    # List DaemonSets with multiple label selectors
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l 'environment in (production,staging)'

# List DaemonSets excluding specific types
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l 'type!=logging'

# Complex label selector with AND conditions
kubectl get daemonsets -n anihpj-services -l 'app=background-agent,version=v2.1'
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE skills-bg-agent 3 3 3 3 3 5d authors-bg-agent 3 3 3 3 3 5d locations-bg-agent 3 3 3 3 3 5d jobposts-bg-agent 3 3 3 3 3 5d

    List DaemonSet for All Namespaces

    List or watch objects of kind DaemonSet across all namespaces.

    HTTP Request

    GET /apis/apps/v1/daemonsets

    Query Parameters
    • allowWatchBookmarks boolean: allowWatchBookmarks requests watch events with type "BOOKMARK"
    • continue string: continue option should be set when retrieving more results from the server
    • fieldSelector string: selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their fields
    • labelSelector string: selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their labels
    • limit integer: limit is a maximum number of responses to return for a list call
    • resourceVersion string: resourceVersion sets a constraint on what resource versions a request may be served from
    • resourceVersionMatch string: resourceVersionMatch determines how resourceVersion is applied to list calls
    • sendInitialEvents boolean: sendInitialEvents controls whether the server will send the events for a watch before sending the current list state
    • timeoutSeconds integer: timeout for the list/watch call
    • watch boolean: watch for changes to the described resources and return them as a stream of add, update, and remove notifications
    Response

    200 (DaemonSetList): OK

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces
    Example 1: List All DaemonSets Across anihpj Platform

    Get a comprehensive view of all DaemonSets running across the entire anihpj platform infrastructure.

    # List all DaemonSets across namespaces
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces

# List with wide output format
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -o wide

# List with custom columns
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.numberReady
    NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-skills skill-validator 3 3 3 3 3 5d anihpj-authors author-cache-agent 3 3 3 3 3 3d anihpj-locations location-geo-processor 3 3 3 3 3 2d anihpj-jobposts jobpost-processor 3 3 3 3 3 1d kube-system kube-proxy 3 3 3 3 3 30d
    Example 2: Filter DaemonSets by Labels Across All Namespaces

    Use label selectors to find specific types of DaemonSets across all anihpj service namespaces.

    # Filter by application label
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -l app=background-processor

# Filter by environment
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -l environment=production

# Complex label filtering
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -l 'tier in (backend,processing)'
    NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-skills skill-background-proc 3 3 3 3 3 5d anihpj-authors author-background-proc 3 3 3 3 3 3d anihpj-locations location-background-proc 3 3 3 3 3 2d anihpj-jobposts jobpost-background-proc 3 3 3 3 3 1d
    Example 3: Monitor System and Application DaemonSets

    Distinguish between system DaemonSets and anihpj application DaemonSets for comprehensive monitoring.

    # List system DaemonSets
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -l component=system

# List application DaemonSets
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=anihpj

# Exclude system namespaces
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces --field-selector metadata.namespace!=kube-system
    NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-skills skill-validator 3 3 3 3 3 5d anihpj-authors author-cache-agent 3 3 3 3 3 3d anihpj-locations location-geo-processor 3 3 3 3 3 2d anihpj-jobposts jobpost-processor 3 3 3 3 3 1d
    Example 4: Watch All DaemonSets for Real-time Monitoring

    Set up real-time monitoring of all DaemonSets across the anihpj platform for immediate issue detection.

    # Watch all DaemonSets
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces --watch

# Watch with JSON output
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces --watch -o json

# Watch specific fields
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces --watch -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.numberReady
    NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY UP-TO-DATE AVAILABLE NODE SELECTOR AGE anihpj-skills skill-validator 3 3 3 3 3 5d anihpj-authors author-cache-agent 3 3 3 3 3 3d MODIFIED anihpj-locations location-geo-processor 3 3 3 3 3 2d
    Example 5: Export DaemonSet Inventory

    Export complete DaemonSet inventory across all namespaces for documentation and compliance reporting.

    # Export to YAML
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -o yaml > daemonsets-inventory.yaml

# Export to JSON
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -o json > daemonsets-inventory.json

# Export summary report
kubectl get daemonsets --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,DESIRED:.spec.template.spec.nodeSelector,READY:.status.numberReady,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED READY AGE anihpj-skills skill-validator 3 2024-01-01T10:00:00Z anihpj-authors author-cache-agent 3 2024-01-04T10:00:00Z anihpj-locations location-geo-processor 3 2024-01-05T10:00:00Z anihpj-jobposts jobpost-processor 3 2024-01-06T10:00:00Z

    Create DaemonSet

    Create a DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets

    Path Parameters
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • dryRun string: when present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
    • fieldManager string: fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity that is making these changes
    • fieldValidation string: fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle objects in the request containing unknown or duplicate fields
    Body Parameters
    • body DaemonSet, required: DaemonSet object to create
    Response

    200 (DaemonSet): OK

    201 (DaemonSet): Created

    202 (DaemonSet): Accepted

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl create -f FILENAME
    Example 1: Create Skills Service Validation DaemonSet

    Create a DaemonSet to run skill validation agents on every node in the anihpj skills service cluster.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: skill-validator
  namespace: anihpj-skills
  labels:
    app: skill-validator
    component: validation
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: skill-validator
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: skill-validator
        app: skill-validator
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: validator
        image: anihpj/skill-validator:v2.1
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 200Mi
            cpu: 100m
        env:
        - name: VALIDATION_MODE
          value: "realtime"
        - name: SKILLS_API_ENDPOINT
          value: "http://skills-api:8080"
    # Create the DaemonSet from YAML file
kubectl apply -f skill-validator-daemonset.yaml

# Create directly from command line
kubectl create -f skill-validator-daemonset.yaml
    daemonset.apps/skill-validator created
    Example 2: Create Author Cache DaemonSet with Node Selector

    Create a DaemonSet for author data caching that runs only on specific nodes in the anihpj authors service.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: author-cache-agent
  namespace: anihpj-authors
  labels:
    app: author-cache
    tier: caching
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: author-cache-agent
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: author-cache-agent
        app: author-cache
    spec:
      nodeSelector:
        cache-node: "true"
      containers:
      - name: cache-agent
        image: anihpj/author-cache:v1.5
        ports:
        - containerPort: 6379
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 512Mi
            cpu: 200m
        env:
        - name: CACHE_SIZE
          value: "256mb"
        - name: AUTHORS_DB_HOST
          value: "authors-db:5432"
    # Create the author cache DaemonSet
kubectl apply -f author-cache-daemonset.yaml

# Verify creation with node selector
kubectl get daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors -o wide
    daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent created
    Example 3: Create Location Processing DaemonSet with Volume Mounts

    Create a DaemonSet for location data processing with persistent storage in the anihpj locations service.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: location-processor
  namespace: anihpj-locations
  labels:
    app: location-processor
    component: data-processing
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: location-processor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: location-processor
        app: location-processor
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: processor
        image: anihpj/location-processor:v3.0
        volumeMounts:
        - name: location-data
          mountPath: /data/locations
        - name: geo-cache
          mountPath: /cache/geo
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 500m
        env:
        - name: GEO_API_KEY
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: geo-secrets
              key: api-key
      volumes:
      - name: location-data
        hostPath:
          path: /var/lib/anihpj/locations
      - name: geo-cache
        emptyDir: {}
    # Create location processor DaemonSet with volumes
kubectl apply -f location-processor-daemonset.yaml

# Check volume mounts
kubectl describe daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations
    daemonset.apps/location-processor created
    Example 4: Create JobPost Indexing DaemonSet with Rolling Update

    Create a DaemonSet for job post indexing with rolling update strategy in the anihpj jobposts service.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: jobpost-indexer
  namespace: anihpj-jobposts
  labels:
    app: jobpost-indexer
    component: search-indexing
spec:
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: jobpost-indexer
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: jobpost-indexer
        app: jobpost-indexer
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: indexer
        image: anihpj/jobpost-indexer:v2.3
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 800Mi
            cpu: 300m
        env:
        - name: ELASTICSEARCH_URL
          value: "http://elasticsearch:9200"
        - name: INDEX_BATCH_SIZE
          value: "100"
        - name: JOBPOSTS_API_ENDPOINT
          value: "http://jobposts-api:8080"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
    # Create jobpost indexer DaemonSet with rolling update
kubectl apply -f jobpost-indexer-daemonset.yaml

# Monitor rolling update progress
kubectl rollout status daemonset/jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts
    daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer created
    Example 5: Create Monitoring DaemonSet with Security Context

    Create a DaemonSet for platform monitoring with enhanced security context across anihpj services.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-monitor
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: platform-monitor
    component: monitoring
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: anihpj-monitor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: anihpj-monitor
        app: platform-monitor
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: monitoring-service-account
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 2000
      containers:
      - name: monitor
        image: anihpj/platform-monitor:v4.0
        securityContext:
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
          capabilities:
            drop:
            - ALL
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 256Mi
            cpu: 150m
        env:
        - name: MONITOR_INTERVAL
          value: "30s"
        - name: SERVICES_LIST
          value: "skills,authors,locations,jobposts"
    # Create monitoring DaemonSet with security context
kubectl apply -f anihpj-monitor-daemonset.yaml

# Verify security context settings
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.securityContext}'
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor created

    Update DaemonSet

    Replace the specified DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets/{name}

    Path Parameters
    • name string, required: name of the DaemonSet
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • dryRun string: when present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
    • fieldManager string: fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity that is making these changes
    • fieldValidation string: fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle objects in the request containing unknown or duplicate fields
    Body Parameters
    • body DaemonSet, required: Updated DaemonSet object
    Response

    200 (DaemonSet): OK

    201 (DaemonSet): Created

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl replace -f FILENAME
    Example 1: Update Skills Service DaemonSet Image

    Update the skill validator DaemonSet in anihpj skills service to use a new image version.

    # Update DaemonSet image using kubectl set
kubectl set image daemonset/skill-validator validator=anihpj/skill-validator:v2.2 -n anihpj-skills

# Update using replace with file
kubectl replace -f skill-validator-updated.yaml

# Verify the update
kubectl rollout status daemonset/skill-validator -n anihpj-skills
    daemonset.apps/skill-validator image updated daemonset "skill-validator" successfully rolled out
    Example 2: Update Author Cache DaemonSet Resources

    Update resource limits and requests for the author cache DaemonSet in anihpj authors service.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: author-cache-agent
  namespace: anihpj-authors
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: author-cache-agent
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: author-cache-agent
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: cache-agent
        image: anihpj/author-cache:v1.5
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 400m
          requests:
            memory: 512Mi
            cpu: 200m
    # Apply the updated resource configuration
kubectl replace -f author-cache-updated.yaml

# Verify resource limits have been updated
kubectl describe daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors | grep -A5 Limits
    daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent replaced
    Example 3: Update Location Processing DaemonSet with Environment Variables

    Update the location processor DaemonSet in anihpj locations service to include new environment configuration.

    # Update environment variables
kubectl set env daemonset/location-processor GEO_PRECISION=high -n anihpj-locations
kubectl set env daemonset/location-processor CACHE_TTL=3600 -n anihpj-locations

# Update multiple environment variables from file
kubectl set env daemonset/location-processor --from=configmap/location-config -n anihpj-locations
    daemonset.apps/location-processor env updated daemonset.apps/location-processor env updated daemonset.apps/location-processor env updated
    Example 4: Update JobPost DaemonSet Update Strategy

    Update the jobpost indexer DaemonSet in anihpj jobposts service to change the update strategy configuration.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: jobpost-indexer
  namespace: anihpj-jobposts
spec:
  updateStrategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 2
      maxSurge: 0
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: jobpost-indexer
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: jobpost-indexer
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: indexer
        image: anihpj/jobpost-indexer:v2.4
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 500m
    # Apply the updated DaemonSet with new strategy
kubectl replace -f jobpost-indexer-updated.yaml

# Monitor the rolling update with new strategy
kubectl rollout status daemonset/jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts
    daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer replaced
    Example 5: Update Monitoring DaemonSet with New Labels

    Update the anihpj platform monitoring DaemonSet to include new labels and annotations for better organization.

    # Add labels to DaemonSet
kubectl label daemonset anihpj-monitor version=v4.1 -n anihpj-system
kubectl label daemonset anihpj-monitor environment=production -n anihpj-system

# Add annotations
kubectl annotate daemonset anihpj-monitor deployment.kubernetes.io/revision=5 -n anihpj-system
kubectl annotate daemonset anihpj-monitor monitoring.anihpj.com/scrape=true -n anihpj-system
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor labeled daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor labeled daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor annotated daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor annotated

    Update DaemonSet Status

    Replace status of the specified DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets/{name}/status

    Path Parameters
    • name string, required: name of the DaemonSet
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • dryRun string: when present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
    • fieldManager string: fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity that is making these changes
    • fieldValidation string: fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle objects in the request containing unknown or duplicate fields
    Body Parameters
    • body DaemonSet, required: DaemonSet object with updated status
    Response

    200 (DaemonSet): OK

    201 (DaemonSet): Created

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl replace --subresource=status -f FILENAME
    Example 1: Update Skills Service DaemonSet Status Conditions

    Update the status conditions for the skill validator DaemonSet in anihpj skills service for monitoring purposes.

    # Get current status
kubectl get daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills -o yaml > skill-validator-status.yaml

# Edit status section and apply
kubectl replace --subresource=status -f skill-validator-status.yaml

# Check updated status
kubectl get daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}'
    daemonset.apps/skill-validator status replaced [{"lastTransitionTime":"2024-01-07T10:00:00Z","message":"DaemonSet has successfully progressed","reason":"NewReplicaSetAvailable","status":"True","type":"Progressing"}]
    Example 2: Update Author Cache DaemonSet Status Numbers

    Update the status numbers for the author cache DaemonSet in anihpj authors service to reflect current state.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: author-cache-agent
  namespace: anihpj-authors
status:
  currentNumberScheduled: 3
  desiredNumberScheduled: 3
  numberAvailable: 3
  numberMisscheduled: 0
  numberReady: 3
  observedGeneration: 2
  updatedNumberScheduled: 3
    # Apply the status update
kubectl replace --subresource=status -f author-cache-status.yaml

# Verify status has been updated
kubectl get daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors -o jsonpath='{.status}'
    daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent status replaced
    Example 3: Update Location Processing DaemonSet Status with Conditions

    Update the location processor DaemonSet status in anihpj locations service with custom condition information.

    # Create status update file
cat < location-processor-status.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: location-processor
  namespace: anihpj-locations
status:
  conditions:
  - lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-07T10:30:00Z"
    message: "All pods are running and ready"
    reason: "AllPodsReady"
    status: "True"
    type: "Ready"
  currentNumberScheduled: 3
  desiredNumberScheduled: 3
  numberReady: 3
EOF

kubectl replace --subresource=status -f location-processor-status.yaml
    daemonset.apps/location-processor status replaced
    Example 4: Update JobPost DaemonSet Status During Rolling Update

    Update the jobpost indexer DaemonSet status in anihpj jobposts service during a rolling update process.

    # Monitor rolling update status
kubectl rollout status daemonset/jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts

# Get detailed status during update
kubectl get daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Update status to reflect rollout completion
kubectl patch daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts --subresource=status --type=merge -p='{"status":{"observedGeneration":3}}'
    daemon set "jobpost-indexer" successfully rolled out {"currentNumberScheduled":3,"desiredNumberScheduled":3,"numberAvailable":3,"numberMisscheduled":0,"numberReady":3,"observedGeneration":3,"updatedNumberScheduled":3} daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer patched (no change)
    Example 5: Update Monitoring DaemonSet Status with Health Information

    Update the anihpj platform monitoring DaemonSet status to include health check and performance metrics.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-monitor
  namespace: anihpj-system
status:
  conditions:
  - lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-07T11:00:00Z"
    message: "DaemonSet is healthy and monitoring all services"
    reason: "HealthCheckPassed"
    status: "True"
    type: "Healthy"
  - lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-07T11:00:00Z"
    message: "All monitoring agents are collecting metrics"
    reason: "MetricsCollectionActive"
    status: "True"
    type: "MetricsReady"
  currentNumberScheduled: 3
  desiredNumberScheduled: 3
  numberAvailable: 3
  numberReady: 3
  observedGeneration: 4
    # Apply the health status update
kubectl replace --subresource=status -f anihpj-monitor-status.yaml

# Verify health conditions in status
kubectl get daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}'
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor status replaced

    Patch DaemonSet

    Partially update the specified DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets/{name}

    Path Parameters
    • name string, required: name of the DaemonSet
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • dryRun string: when present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
    • fieldManager string: fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity that is making these changes
    • fieldValidation string: fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle objects in the request containing unknown or duplicate fields
    • force boolean: force is going to "force" Apply requests
    Body Parameters
    • body Patch, required: Patch data for the DaemonSet
    Response

    200 (DaemonSet): OK

    201 (DaemonSet): Created

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl patch daemonset NAME --type PATCH_TYPE -p PATCH
    Example 1: Patch Skills Service DaemonSet Image

    Patch the skill validator DaemonSet in anihpj skills service to update the container image version.

    # Patch container image
kubectl patch daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"validator","image":"anihpj/skill-validator:v2.3"}]}}}}'

# Patch using strategic merge
kubectl patch daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"validator","image":"anihpj/skill-validator:v2.3"}]}}}}'

# Patch from file
kubectl patch daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills --patch-file=skill-validator-patch.yaml
    daemonset.apps/skill-validator patched
    Example 2: Patch Author Cache DaemonSet Resources

    Patch the author cache DaemonSet in anihpj authors service to adjust resource limits and requests.

    # Patch memory limits
kubectl patch daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"cache-agent","resources":{"limits":{"memory":"2Gi"}}}]}}}}'

# Patch CPU requests
kubectl patch daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"cache-agent","resources":{"requests":{"cpu":"300m"}}}]}}}}'

# JSON patch for multiple resource changes
kubectl patch daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources/limits/memory", "value": "2Gi"}]'
    daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent patched daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent patched daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent patched
    Example 3: Patch Location Processing DaemonSet Environment Variables

    Patch the location processor DaemonSet in anihpj locations service to add new environment variables.

    # Add new environment variable
kubectl patch daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"processor","env":[{"name":"DEBUG_MODE","value":"true"}]}]}}}}'

# Patch multiple environment variables
kubectl patch daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations --type='strategic' -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"processor","env":[{"name":"LOG_LEVEL","value":"debug"},{"name":"METRICS_ENABLED","value":"true"}]}]}}}}'

# Remove environment variable using JSON patch
kubectl patch daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations --type='json' -p='[{"op": "remove", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/env/2"}]'
    daemonset.apps/location-processor patched daemonset.apps/location-processor patched daemonset.apps/location-processor patched
    Example 4: Patch JobPost DaemonSet Labels and Annotations

    Patch the jobpost indexer DaemonSet in anihpj jobposts service to update labels and annotations.

    # Add labels
kubectl patch daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"version":"v2.4","environment":"production"}}}'

# Add annotations
kubectl patch daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"deployment.kubernetes.io/revision":"6","monitoring.anihpj.com/alerts":"enabled"}}}'

# Patch template labels
kubectl patch daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts -p='{"spec":{"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"version":"v2.4"}}}}}'
    daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer patched daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer patched daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer patched
    Example 5: Patch Monitoring DaemonSet Update Strategy

    Patch the anihpj platform monitoring DaemonSet to modify the update strategy configuration.

    # Patch update strategy
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system -p='{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"rollingUpdate":{"maxUnavailable":1}}}}'

# Change update strategy type
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system -p='{"spec":{"updateStrategy":{"type":"OnDelete"}}}'

# Patch node selector
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system -p='{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"nodeSelector":{"monitoring":"enabled"}}}}}'
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor patched daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor patched daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor patched

    Patch DaemonSet Status

    Partially update status of the specified DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets/{name}/status

    Path Parameters
    • name string, required: name of the DaemonSet
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • dryRun string: when present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
    • fieldManager string: fieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity that is making these changes
    • fieldValidation string: fieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle objects in the request containing unknown or duplicate fields
    • force boolean: force is going to "force" Apply requests
    Body Parameters
    • body Patch, required: Patch data for the DaemonSet status
    Response

    200 (DaemonSet): OK

    201 (DaemonSet): Created

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl patch daemonset NAME --subresource=status --type PATCH_TYPE -p PATCH
    Example 1: Patch Skills Service DaemonSet Status Conditions

    Patch the skill validator DaemonSet status in anihpj skills service to update condition information.

    # Patch status condition
kubectl patch daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"Ready","status":"True","lastTransitionTime":"2024-01-07T12:00:00Z","reason":"AllPodsReady","message":"All skill validator pods are ready"}]}}'

# Patch observed generation
kubectl patch daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"observedGeneration":3}}'

# Patch number of ready pods
kubectl patch daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills --subresource=status --type='merge' -p='{"status":{"numberReady":3,"numberAvailable":3}}'
    daemonset.apps/skill-validator patched daemonset.apps/skill-validator patched daemonset.apps/skill-validator patched
    Example 2: Patch Author Cache DaemonSet Status Numbers

    Patch the author cache DaemonSet status in anihpj authors service to reflect current scheduling numbers.

    # Patch scheduling status
kubectl patch daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"currentNumberScheduled":4,"desiredNumberScheduled":4}}'

# Patch availability status
kubectl patch daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"numberAvailable":4,"numberUnavailable":0}}'

# JSON patch for multiple status fields
kubectl patch daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors --subresource=status --type='json' -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/status/updatedNumberScheduled","value":4}]'
    daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent patched daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent patched daemonset.apps/author-cache-agent patched
    Example 3: Patch Location Processing DaemonSet Status with Custom Conditions

    Patch the location processor DaemonSet status in anihpj locations service to add custom monitoring conditions.

    # Add custom health condition
kubectl patch daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"GeoProcessingHealthy","status":"True","lastTransitionTime":"2024-01-07T12:30:00Z","reason":"ProcessingActive","message":"Geo processing is functioning normally"}]}}'

# Patch status with strategic merge
kubectl patch daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations --subresource=status --type='strategic' -p='{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"DataSync","status":"True","reason":"SyncComplete"}]}}'

# Update multiple status conditions
kubectl patch daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"Ready","status":"True"},{"type":"Processing","status":"True"}]}}'
    daemonset.apps/location-processor patched daemonset.apps/location-processor patched daemonset.apps/location-processor patched
    Example 4: Patch JobPost DaemonSet Status During Update

    Patch the jobpost indexer DaemonSet status in anihpj jobposts service during rolling update operations.

    # Patch update status
kubectl patch daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"updatedNumberScheduled":2,"currentNumberScheduled":3}}'

# Patch rollout condition
kubectl patch daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"Progressing","status":"True","lastTransitionTime":"2024-01-07T13:00:00Z","reason":"RollingUpdate","message":"DaemonSet is rolling out"}]}}'

# Update generation tracking
kubectl patch daemonset jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts --subresource=status --type='merge' -p='{"status":{"observedGeneration":5}}'
    daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer patched daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer patched daemonset.apps/jobpost-indexer patched
    Example 5: Patch Monitoring DaemonSet Status with Performance Metrics

    Patch the anihpj platform monitoring DaemonSet status to include performance and health metrics.

    # Patch with performance conditions
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"PerformanceOptimal","status":"True","lastTransitionTime":"2024-01-07T13:30:00Z","reason":"MetricsWithinLimits","message":"All monitoring metrics are within optimal ranges"}]}}'

# Add availability status
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system --subresource=status -p='{"status":{"numberAvailable":3,"collisionCount":0}}'

# Patch with JSON array operations
kubectl patch daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system --subresource=status --type='json' -p='[{"op":"add","path":"/status/conditions/-","value":{"type":"MonitoringActive","status":"True","reason":"AllServicesMonitored"}}]'
    daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor patched daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor patched daemonset.apps/anihpj-monitor patched

    Delete DaemonSet

    Delete a DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets/{name}

    Path Parameters
    • name string, required: name of the DaemonSet
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • dryRun string: when present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
    • gracePeriodSeconds integer: the duration in seconds before the object should be deleted
    • orphanDependents boolean: deprecated: please use the PropagationPolicy
    • propagationPolicy string: whether and how garbage collection will be performed
    Body Parameters
    • body DeleteOptions: delete options for the DaemonSet
    Response

    200 (Status): OK

    202 (Status): Accepted

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
