    kubectl delete daemonset NAME
    Example 1: Delete Skills Service DaemonSet

    Delete the skill validator DaemonSet from anihpj skills service when no longer needed.

    # Delete DaemonSet immediately
kubectl delete daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills

# Delete with confirmation
kubectl delete daemonset skill-validator -n anihpj-skills --wait=true

# Delete using file
kubectl delete -f skill-validator.yaml
    daemonset.apps "skill-validator" deleted
    Example 2: Delete Author Cache DaemonSet with Grace Period

    Delete the author cache DaemonSet from anihpj authors service with a specified grace period for clean shutdown.

    # Delete with grace period
kubectl delete daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors --grace-period=60

# Delete immediately (grace period 0)
kubectl delete daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors --grace-period=0 --force

# Delete with cascade policy
kubectl delete daemonset author-cache-agent -n anihpj-authors --cascade=foreground
    daemonset.apps "author-cache-agent" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Location Processing DaemonSet with Dry Run

    Delete the location processor DaemonSet from anihpj locations service using dry run to verify before actual deletion.

    # Dry run delete to check what would be deleted
kubectl delete daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations --dry-run=client

# Server dry run
kubectl delete daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations --dry-run=server

# Actual delete after verification
kubectl delete daemonset location-processor -n anihpj-locations
    daemonset.apps "location-processor" deleted (dry run) daemonset.apps "location-processor" deleted (server dry run) daemonset.apps "location-processor" deleted
    Example 4: Delete JobPost DaemonSet with Label Selector

    Delete jobpost indexer DaemonSet from anihpj jobposts service using label selectors for targeted deletion.

    # Delete by label selector
kubectl delete daemonset -l app=jobpost-indexer -n anihpj-jobposts

# Delete by multiple labels
kubectl delete daemonset -l app=jobpost-indexer,version=v2.4 -n anihpj-jobposts

# Delete all DaemonSets in namespace
kubectl delete daemonset --all -n anihpj-jobposts
    daemonset.apps "jobpost-indexer" deleted
    Example 5: Delete Monitoring DaemonSet with Background Propagation

    Delete the anihpj platform monitoring DaemonSet using background propagation policy for efficient cleanup.

    # Delete with background propagation
kubectl delete daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system --cascade=background

# Delete with orphan propagation (keep pods)
kubectl delete daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system --cascade=orphan

# Delete with timeout
kubectl delete daemonset anihpj-monitor -n anihpj-system --timeout=30s
    daemonset.apps "anihpj-monitor" deleted

    Delete Collection of DaemonSets

    Delete collection of DaemonSet.

    HTTP Request

    DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/daemonsets

    Path Parameters
    • namespace string, required: object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects
    Query Parameters
    • continue string: continue option should be set when retrieving more results from the server
    • dryRun string: when present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
    • fieldSelector string: selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their fields
    • gracePeriodSeconds integer: the duration in seconds before the object should be deleted
    • labelSelector string: selector to restrict the list of returned objects by their labels
    • limit integer: limit is a maximum number of responses to return for a list call
    • orphanDependents boolean: deprecated: please use the PropagationPolicy
    • propagationPolicy string: whether and how garbage collection will be performed
    • resourceVersion string: resourceVersion sets a constraint on what resource versions a request may be served from
    • resourceVersionMatch string: resourceVersionMatch determines how resourceVersion is applied to list calls
    • sendInitialEvents boolean: sendInitialEvents controls whether the server will send the events for a watch before sending the current list state
    • timeoutSeconds integer: timeout for the list/watch call
    Body Parameters
    • body DeleteOptions: delete options for the collection
    Response

    200 (Status): OK

    401: Unauthorized

    kubectl syntax
    kubectl delete daemonsets --all
    Example 1: Delete All Skills Service DaemonSets

    Delete all DaemonSets in the anihpj skills service namespace for complete cleanup.

    # Delete all DaemonSets in namespace
kubectl delete daemonsets --all -n anihpj-skills

# Delete with confirmation and wait
kubectl delete daemonsets --all -n anihpj-skills --wait=true

# Dry run to check what would be deleted
kubectl delete daemonsets --all -n anihpj-skills --dry-run=client
    daemonset.apps "skill-validator" deleted daemonset.apps "skill-indexer" deleted daemonset.apps "skill-cache" deleted
    Example 2: Delete Author Service DaemonSets by Label

    Delete multiple DaemonSets in the anihpj authors service using label selectors for targeted bulk deletion.

    # Delete by single label
kubectl delete daemonsets -l app=author-service -n anihpj-authors

# Delete by multiple labels
kubectl delete daemonsets -l tier=caching,environment=development -n anihpj-authors

# Delete excluding specific labels
kubectl delete daemonsets -l '!critical' -n anihpj-authors
    daemonset.apps "author-cache-agent" deleted daemonset.apps "author-indexer" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Location Service DaemonSets with Field Selector

    Delete DaemonSets in the anihpj locations service using field selectors for specific criteria-based deletion.

    # Delete by field selector
kubectl delete daemonsets --field-selector metadata.name=location-old-processor -n anihpj-locations

# Delete by creation time
kubectl delete daemonsets --field-selector 'metadata.creationTimestamp<2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' -n anihpj-locations

# Combine field and label selectors
kubectl delete daemonsets -l version=old --field-selector 'metadata.creationTimestamp<2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' -n anihpj-locations
    daemonset.apps "location-old-processor" deleted daemonset.apps "location-legacy-indexer" deleted
    Example 4: Delete JobPost Service DaemonSets with Grace Period

    Delete multiple DaemonSets in the anihpj jobposts service with specified grace period for controlled shutdown.

    # Delete with grace period
kubectl delete daemonsets --all -n anihpj-jobposts --grace-period=120

# Delete immediately with force
kubectl delete daemonsets --all -n anihpj-jobposts --grace-period=0 --force

# Delete with specific propagation policy
kubectl delete daemonsets -l component=processing -n anihpj-jobposts --cascade=foreground
    daemonset.apps "jobpost-processor" deleted daemonset.apps "jobpost-indexer" deleted daemonset.apps "jobpost-cache" deleted
    Example 5: Delete System DaemonSets with Comprehensive Options

    Delete multiple DaemonSets in the anihpj system namespace using comprehensive deletion options for cleanup operations.

    # Delete with timeout and background cascade
kubectl delete daemonsets -l type=monitoring -n anihpj-system --timeout=60s --cascade=background

# Delete with limit and continue
kubectl delete daemonsets --all -n anihpj-system --limit=5

# Delete with dry run and output format
kubectl delete daemonsets --all -n anihpj-system --dry-run=server -o yaml
    daemonset.apps "anihpj-monitor" deleted daemonset.apps "log-collector" deleted daemonset.apps "metrics-agent" deleted

    Job

    Job Overview

    JobSpec - Job specification and configuration
    JobStatus - Current execution status and conditions
    JobList - Collection of Job resources
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: Job v1 API | API Version: batch/v1

    Job represents the configuration of a single job that runs one or more pods to completion.

    Full Description

    A Job creates one or more Pods and will continue to retry execution of the Pods until a specified number of them successfully terminate. Jobs are useful for running batch processes, data processing tasks, database migrations, and other run-to-completion workloads in your anihpj Django application.

    Unlike ReplicationController or Deployment which manage long-running services, Jobs are designed for finite tasks that need to complete successfully. Common use cases in Django applications include data migrations, batch processing of user uploads, generating reports, cleaning up temporary files, and sending bulk notifications. Jobs ensure these tasks complete reliably, even if individual pods fail.

    Syntax

    # Create a Job from YAML file
kubectl create -f job.yaml
# List all Jobs
kubectl get jobs
# Get detailed information about a specific Job
kubectl describe job {name}
# Delete a specific Job
kubectl delete job {name}
# Monitor Job progress
kubectl get jobs -w
# Get Job logs from completed pods
kubectl logs job/{name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: anihpj-batch-job
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: batch
spec:
  completions: 1          # Number of successful completions needed
  parallelism: 1          # Number of pods running simultaneously  
  backoffLimit: 3         # Number of retries before marking as failed
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        job: batch-processing
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
      - name: worker
        image: anihpj/batch-worker:latest
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings"
status:
  active: 0               # Number of active pods
  succeeded: 1            # Number of successful completions
  failed: 0               # Number of failed pods
  startTime: "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"
  completionTime: "2024-01-15T10:05:30Z"

    Key Components

    • metadata - Standard object metadata including name, namespace, labels
    • spec (JobSpec) - Desired behavior of the job including pod template and completion policy
    • status (JobStatus) - Current status including active, succeeded, and failed pod counts
    Example 1: Skills Data Migration Job

    Create a job to migrate legacy skills data in the anihpj platform to the new database schema.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: skills-migration
  namespace: anihpj-skills
  labels:
    app: data-migration
    component: skills
spec:
  completions: 1
  parallelism: 1
  backoffLimit: 3
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: data-migration
        job: skills-migration
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
      - name: migrator
        image: anihpj/skills-migrator:v1.2
        env:
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: database-secrets
              key: url
        - name: MIGRATION_TYPE
          value: "skills-schema-v2"
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 512Mi
            cpu: 200m
          requests:
            memory: 256Mi
            cpu: 100m
    # Create the skills migration job
kubectl apply -f skills-migration-job.yaml

# Monitor job progress
kubectl get job skills-migration -n anihpj-skills --watch
    job.batch/skills-migration created NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-migration 0/1 5s 5s skills-migration 1/1 45s 45s
    Example 2: Author Profile Processing Job

    Run a job to process and update author profile data with enhanced metadata in the anihpj authors service.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: author-profile-processor
  namespace: anihpj-authors
  labels:
    app: data-processing
    component: authors
spec:
  completions: 5
  parallelism: 2
  backoffLimit: 2
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: data-processing
        job: author-profile-processor
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: processor
        image: anihpj/author-processor:v2.1
        env:
        - name: BATCH_SIZE
          value: "100"
        - name: AUTHORS_API_ENDPOINT
          value: "http://authors-api:8080"
        - name: PROFILE_ENHANCEMENT
          value: "enabled"
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 500m
    # Create the author processing job
kubectl create -f author-profile-job.yaml

# Check job status and pods
kubectl get job,pods -l job-name=author-profile-processor -n anihpj-authors
    job.batch/author-profile-processor created NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE job.batch/author-profile-processor 2/5 30s 30s NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE pod/author-profile-processor-abc123 1/1 Running 0 25s pod/author-profile-processor-def456 1/1 Running 0 25s
    Example 3: Location Data Geocoding Job

    Execute a job to geocode location data and update coordinates for the anihpj locations service.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: location-geocoding
  namespace: anihpj-locations
  labels:
    app: geocoding
    component: locations
spec:
  completions: 10
  parallelism: 3
  backoffLimit: 5
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: geocoding
        job: location-geocoding
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
      - name: geocoder
        image: anihpj/location-geocoder:v1.8
        env:
        - name: GEO_API_KEY
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: geo-secrets
              key: api-key
        - name: BATCH_SIZE
          value: "50"
        - name: LOCATIONS_DB_HOST
          value: "locations-db:5432"
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 768Mi
            cpu: 300m
    # Start the geocoding job
kubectl apply -f location-geocoding-job.yaml

# Monitor job completion
kubectl logs -f job/location-geocoding -n anihpj-locations
    job.batch/location-geocoding created Processing batch 1/10: 50 locations Processing batch 2/10: 50 locations Geocoding completed successfully
    Example 4: JobPost Indexing Job

    Create a job to rebuild search indexes for job posts in the anihpj jobposts service.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: jobpost-indexing
  namespace: anihpj-jobposts
  labels:
    app: search-indexing
    component: jobposts
spec:
  completions: 1
  parallelism: 1
  backoffLimit: 2
  ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 3600
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: search-indexing
        job: jobpost-indexing
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: indexer
        image: anihpj/jobpost-indexer:v3.0
        env:
        - name: ELASTICSEARCH_URL
          value: "http://elasticsearch:9200"
        - name: JOBPOSTS_API_ENDPOINT
          value: "http://jobposts-api:8080"
        - name: INDEX_NAME
          value: "jobposts-v3"
        - name: REBUILD_MODE
          value: "full"
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 2Gi
            cpu: 800m
        volumeMounts:
        - name: temp-storage
          mountPath: /tmp/indexing
      volumes:
      - name: temp-storage
        emptyDir:
          sizeLimit: 1Gi
    # Launch the indexing job
kubectl create -f jobpost-indexing-job.yaml

# Check job logs
kubectl logs job/jobpost-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts
    job.batch/jobpost-indexing created Starting full index rebuild... Processed 1000 job posts Processed 2000 job posts Index rebuild completed successfully
    Example 5: Database Backup Job

    Run a scheduled database backup job for the anihpj platform databases.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: database-backup
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: backup
    component: database
spec:
  completions: 1
  parallelism: 1
  backoffLimit: 1
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 7200
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: backup
        job: database-backup
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      serviceAccountName: backup-service-account
      containers:
      - name: backup
        image: anihpj/db-backup:v2.5
        env:
        - name: BACKUP_TIMESTAMP
          value: "20240908-120000"
        - name: DATABASES
          value: "skills,authors,locations,jobposts"
        - name: S3_BUCKET
          value: "anihpj-backups"
        - name: AWS_REGION
          value: "us-west-2"
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 500m
        volumeMounts:
        - name: backup-storage
          mountPath: /backups
      volumes:
      - name: backup-storage
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: backup-pvc
    # Execute the backup job
kubectl apply -f database-backup-job.yaml

# Monitor backup progress
kubectl get job database-backup -n anihpj-system -w
    job.batch/database-backup created NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE database-backup 0/1 10s 10s database-backup 1/1 5m30s 5m30s

    JobSpec

    JobSpec describes how the job execution will look like, including pod template, completion requirements, and retry policies.

    The JobSpec defines the desired behavior of a Job, including the number of completions needed, parallelism settings, retry policies, and the pod template that will be used to run the job's workload. This is essential for configuring batch processing tasks in your anihpj Django application.

    Key JobSpec Fields

    • template - Pod template specification (required)
    • completions - Number of successful pod completions needed
    • parallelism - Maximum number of pods running simultaneously
    • backoffLimit - Number of retries before marking job as failed
    • activeDeadlineSeconds - Maximum duration for job execution
    • ttlSecondsAfterFinished - Time to live after job completion
    Example 1: Skills Validation Job with Parallel Processing

    Configure a JobSpec for parallel validation of skills data in the anihpj skills service.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: skills-validation
  namespace: anihpj-skills
spec:
  completions: 10
  parallelism: 3
  backoffLimit: 2
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 1800
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: skills-validation
        batch: skills-processing
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
      - name: validator
        image: anihpj/skills-validator:v2.0
        command: ["python", "validate_skills.py"]
        args: ["--batch-size=100", "--validate-duplicates"]
        env:
        - name: VALIDATION_LEVEL
          value: "strict"
        - name: SKILLS_DB_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: skills-db-secret
              key: database-url
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 512Mi
            cpu: 200m
          requests:
            memory: 256Mi
            cpu: 100m
    # Create skills validation job
kubectl apply -f skills-validation-job.yaml

# Monitor parallel execution
kubectl get pods -l app=skills-validation -n anihpj-skills
    job.batch/skills-validation created NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE skills-validation-abc123 1/1 Running 0 30s skills-validation-def456 1/1 Running 0 30s skills-validation-ghi789 1/1 Running 0 30s
    Example 2: Author Data Processing with Completion Mode

    Set up a JobSpec with indexed completion mode for processing author data in batches.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: author-data-processing
  namespace: anihpj-authors
spec:
  completions: 5
  parallelism: 2
  completionMode: Indexed
  backoffLimit: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: author-processing
        batch: data-processing
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: processor
        image: anihpj/author-processor:v1.5
        command: ["python", "process_authors.py"]
        args: ["--index=$(JOB_COMPLETION_INDEX)", "--batch-size=50"]
        env:
        - name: JOB_COMPLETION_INDEX
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.annotations['batch.kubernetes.io/job-completion-index']
        - name: AUTHORS_API_URL
          value: "http://authors-api:8080"
        - name: PROCESSING_MODE
          value: "enhanced"
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 768Mi
            cpu: 300m
    # Deploy indexed job
kubectl create -f author-processing-job.yaml

# Check indexed pod names
kubectl get pods -l app=author-processing -n anihpj-authors -o wide
    job.batch/author-data-processing created NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE author-data-processing-0-abc123 1/1 Running 0 20s author-data-processing-1-def456 1/1 Running 0 20s
    Example 3: Location Geocoding with TTL and Deadline

    Configure a JobSpec with time-based controls for location geocoding tasks.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: location-geocoding-batch
  namespace: anihpj-locations
spec:
  completions: 1
  parallelism: 1
  backoffLimit: 3
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600  # 1 hour maximum
  ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 1800  # Clean up after 30 minutes
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: location-geocoding
        batch: geocoding-batch
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
      - name: geocoder
        image: anihpj/location-geocoder:v2.2
        command: ["python", "geocode_locations.py"]
        env:
        - name: GEOCODING_PROVIDER
          value: "google-maps"
        - name: BATCH_SIZE
          value: "1000"
        - name: RATE_LIMIT
          value: "10"  # requests per second
        - name: GEO_API_KEY
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: geocoding-secrets
              key: google-api-key
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 500m
        livenessProbe:
          exec:
            command: ["python", "health_check.py"]
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 60
    # Start geocoding job with TTL
kubectl apply -f location-geocoding-batch-job.yaml

# Monitor job with deadline
kubectl describe job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations
    job.batch/location-geocoding-batch created Name: location-geocoding-batch Namespace: anihpj-locations Active Deadline Seconds: 3600 Ttl Seconds After Finished: 1800
    Example 4: JobPost Search Index with Failure Policy

    Implement a JobSpec with pod failure policy for jobpost search indexing operations.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: jobpost-search-indexing
  namespace: anihpj-jobposts
spec:
  completions: 3
  parallelism: 1
  backoffLimit: 2
  podFailurePolicy:
    rules:
    - action: FailJob
      onExitCodes:
        containerName: indexer
        operator: In
        values: [1, 2, 3]  # Critical failure codes
    - action: Ignore
      onExitCodes:
        containerName: indexer
        operator: In
        values: [42]  # Ignorable warning code
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: jobpost-indexing
        batch: search-indexing
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: indexer
        image: anihpj/jobpost-indexer:v2.8
        command: ["python", "index_jobposts.py"]
        args: ["--incremental", "--optimize"]
        env:
        - name: ELASTICSEARCH_URL
          value: "http://elasticsearch:9200"
        - name: INDEX_SETTINGS
          value: "high-performance"
        - name: JOBPOSTS_API_ENDPOINT
          value: "http://jobposts-api:8080"
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 2Gi
            cpu: 800m
          requests:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 400m
    # Deploy job with failure policy
kubectl create -f jobpost-indexing-job.yaml

# Monitor failure policy application
kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts
    job.batch/jobpost-search-indexing created LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 30s Normal Created job/jobpost-search-indexing Created pod: jobpost-search-indexing-abc123
    Example 5: Multi-Service Backup with Suspend Control

    Create a JobSpec with suspend functionality for coordinated backup operations across anihpj services.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: multi-service-backup
  namespace: anihpj-system
spec:
  completions: 4  # One for each service
  parallelism: 1  # Sequential backups
  backoffLimit: 1
  suspend: false  # Can be set to true to pause
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: multi-backup
        batch: system-backup
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      serviceAccountName: backup-service-account
      containers:
      - name: backup
        image: anihpj/multi-service-backup:v1.3
        command: ["bash", "backup_services.sh"]
        env:
        - name: SERVICES
          value: "skills,authors,locations,jobposts"
        - name: BACKUP_TYPE
          value: "incremental"
        - name: S3_BUCKET
          value: "anihpj-backups"
        - name: NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: backup-secrets
              key: webhook-url
        resources:
          limits:
            memory: 1Gi
            cpu: 400m
        volumeMounts:
        - name: backup-config
          mountPath: /config
          readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: backup-config
        configMap:
          name: backup-configuration
    # Create backup job
kubectl apply -f multi-service-backup-job.yaml

# Suspend job if needed
kubectl patch job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -p '{"spec":{"suspend":true}}'

# Resume job
kubectl patch job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
    job.batch/multi-service-backup created job.batch/multi-service-backup patched job.batch/multi-service-backup patched

    JobStatus

    JobStatus represents the current state of a Job, including completion status, failure conditions, and runtime information.

    The JobStatus provides real-time information about job execution, including active pods, completion counts, start and completion times, and detailed conditions that help monitor and troubleshoot job execution in your anihpj Django application.

    Key JobStatus Fields

    • conditions - Array of job conditions (Complete, Failed, Suspended)
    • active - Number of active pods
    • succeeded - Number of pods that terminated successfully
    • failed - Number of pods that terminated with failure
    • startTime - Time when job started execution
    • completionTime - Time when job completed
    Example 1: Skills Processing Job Status Monitoring

    Monitor JobStatus for skills processing operations in the anihpj skills service.

    # Check skills processing job status
kubectl get job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills -o yaml
    status:
  active: 2
  succeeded: 8
  failed: 0
  startTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  conditions:
  - type: Progressing
    status: "True"
    lastProbeTime: "2024-01-15T10:35:30Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    reason: JobRunning
    message: "Job is progressing: 8 of 10 completions"
  - type: Complete
    status: "False"
    lastProbeTime: "2024-01-15T10:35:30Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    reason: JobNotComplete
    message: "Job has not completed yet"
    # Monitor job progress in real-time
kubectl get job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills -w

# Get detailed job events
kubectl describe job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills
    NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 8/10 5m30s 6m skills-validation 9/10 6m15s 6m45s skills-validation 10/10 6m30s 7m
    Example 2: Author Processing Job Failure Analysis

    Analyze JobStatus conditions for failed author processing jobs.

    # Check failed author processing job
kubectl get job author-data-processing -n anihpj-authors -o jsonpath='{.status}'
    {
  "active": 0,
  "succeeded": 3,
  "failed": 2,
  "startTime": "2024-01-15T11:00:00Z",
  "completionTime": "2024-01-15T11:45:30Z",
  "conditions": [
    {
      "type": "Failed",
      "status": "True",
      "lastProbeTime": "2024-01-15T11:45:30Z",
      "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T11:45:30Z",
      "reason": "BackoffLimitExceeded",
      "message": "Job has reached the specified backoff limit"
    }
  ]
}
    # Get failed pod logs for debugging
kubectl get pods -l app=author-processing -n anihpj-authors --field-selector=status.phase=Failed

# Examine failure details
kubectl logs author-data-processing-failed-pod -n anihpj-authors
    NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE author-data-processing-2-abc123 0/1 Failed 0 45m author-data-processing-4-def456 0/1 Failed 0 40m Error: Database connection timeout after 30 seconds Traceback: authors/models.py line 45 in process_batch()
    Example 3: Location Geocoding Job Completion Status

    Track completion status and timing for location geocoding operations.

    # Check location geocoding completion
kubectl get job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -o yaml
    status:
  active: 0
  succeeded: 1
  failed: 0
  startTime: "2024-01-15T12:00:00Z"
  completionTime: "2024-01-15T12:55:42Z"
  conditions:
  - type: Complete
    status: "True"
    lastProbeTime: "2024-01-15T12:55:42Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T12:55:42Z"
    reason: JobComplete
    message: "Job completed successfully"
  ready: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}
  completedIndexes: "0"
    # Calculate job duration
kubectl get job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,START:.status.startTime,COMPLETION:.status.completionTime"

# Check if TTL cleanup is active
kubectl get job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -o jsonpath='{.spec.ttlSecondsAfterFinished}'
    NAME START COMPLETION location-geocoding-batch 2024-01-15T12:00:00Z 2024-01-15T12:55:42Z 1800
    Example 4: JobPost Indexing Status with Parallel Execution

    Monitor parallel execution status for jobpost search indexing tasks.

    # Check parallel execution status
kubectl get job jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts -o yaml
    status:
  active: 1
  succeeded: 2
  failed: 0
  startTime: "2024-01-15T13:15:00Z"
  conditions:
  - type: Progressing
    status: "True"
    lastProbeTime: "2024-01-15T13:25:15Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T13:15:00Z"
    reason: JobRunning
    message: "Job is progressing: 2 of 3 completions, 1 active pod"
  ready: 1
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}
  completedIndexes: "0,1"
    # Monitor active pods during parallel execution
kubectl get pods -l app=jobpost-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts --show-labels

# Check completion progress
kubectl get job jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,COMPLETIONS:.spec.completions,SUCCEEDED:.status.succeeded,ACTIVE:.status.active"
    NAME READY STATUS LABELS jobpost-search-indexing-abc123 1/1 Running app=jobpost-indexing,batch=search-indexing NAME COMPLETIONS SUCCEEDED ACTIVE jobpost-search-indexing 3 2 1
    Example 5: Multi-Service Backup Suspended Job Status

    Examine JobStatus for suspended backup operations across anihpj services.

    # Check suspended job status
kubectl get job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -o yaml
    status:
  active: 0
  succeeded: 0
  failed: 0
  startTime: "2024-01-15T14:00:00Z"
  conditions:
  - type: Suspended
    status: "True"
    lastProbeTime: "2024-01-15T14:05:30Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T14:05:30Z"
    reason: JobSuspended
    message: "Job is suspended"
  ready: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

    # Resume suspended job and monitor status change
kubectl patch job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'

# Watch status transition from suspended to running
kubectl get job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -w -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,SUSPENDED:.spec.suspend,CONDITIONS:.status.conditions[*].type"

# Check final backup completion status
kubectl get job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Complete")].message}'
    job.batch/multi-service-backup patched NAME SUSPENDED CONDITIONS multi-service-backup false Suspended multi-service-backup false Progressing multi-service-backup false Complete "Job completed successfully"

    JobList

    JobList is a collection of Jobs that provides bulk operations and filtering capabilities for batch workloads.

    The JobList allows you to manage multiple jobs collectively, perform bulk operations, and apply filters to find specific jobs across different namespaces in your anihpj Django application. This is essential for managing batch processing workflows at scale.

    Key JobList Operations

    • List All Jobs - Get all jobs across namespaces
    • Filter by Labels - Select jobs using label selectors
    • Filter by Field - Select jobs using field selectors
    • Bulk Operations - Apply operations to multiple jobs
    • Watch Changes - Monitor job collection changes
    Example 1: List All Skills Service Jobs

    Retrieve and filter all jobs related to the anihpj skills service.

    # List all jobs in skills namespace
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-skills

# List jobs with detailed output
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-skills -o wide
    NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h skills-data-import 1/1 2m15s 2h skills-cleanup 1/1 1m05s 3h skills-export 0/1 45s 45s
    # Get jobs with custom columns
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-skills -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.conditions[0].type,COMPLETIONS:.spec.completions,ACTIVE:.status.active"

# Filter jobs by completion status
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-skills --field-selector=status.successful=1
    NAME STATUS COMPLETIONS ACTIVE skills-validation Complete 10 0 skills-data-import Complete 1 0 skills-cleanup Complete 1 0 skills-export Failed 1 0 NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h skills-data-import 1/1 2m15s 2h skills-cleanup 1/1 1m05s 3h
    NAME STATUS COMPLETIONS ACTIVE skills-validation Complete 10 0 skills-data-import Complete 1 0 skills-cleanup Complete 1 0 skills-export Failed 1 0 NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h skills-data-import 1/1 2m15s 2h skills-cleanup 1/1 1m05s 3h
    Example 2: Label-Based Job Filtering Across Services

    Use label selectors to filter jobs across all anihpj service namespaces.

    # List jobs with specific labels across all namespaces
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -l batch=data-processing

# Filter jobs by application type
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -l app=author-processing,jobType=batch
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-skills skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h anihpj-authors author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m anihpj-locations location-data-sync 1/1 3m45s 30m anihpj-jobposts jobpost-batch-update 0/1 2m10s 2m10s
    # Complex label selector for production jobs
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -l 'environment=production,batch in (data-processing,indexing)'

# Get jobs not in specific environments
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -l 'environment!=development'
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-skills skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h anihpj-jobposts jobpost-search-indexing 3/3 12m30s 5m NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m location-geocoding-batch 1/1 55m42s 15m jobpost-search-indexing 3/3 12m30s 5m
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-skills skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h anihpj-jobposts jobpost-search-indexing 3/3 12m30s 5m NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m location-geocoding-batch 1/1 55m42s 15m jobpost-search-indexing 3/3 12m30s 5m
    Example 3: Job Status Collection and Monitoring

    Monitor job collections with status filtering for anihpj batch operations.

    # List running jobs across all anihpj namespaces
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.active=1
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-jobposts jobpost-batch-update 0/1 3m45s 3m45s anihpj-locations location-geocoding-batch 0/1 1m20s 1m20s
    # List failed jobs for debugging
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.failed=1

# Watch job collection changes in real-time
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -w

# Get job collection with JSON output for automation
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.succeeded > 0) | .metadata.name'
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-authors author-retry 0/5 8m20s 8m20s anihpj-skills skills-export 0/1 45s 45s NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m "skills-validation" "author-data-processing" "location-geocoding-batch" "jobpost-search-indexing"
    Example 4: Time-Based Job Collection Queries

    Filter and sort job collections by creation time and completion status.

    # Sort jobs by creation time (newest first)
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-jobposts jobpost-search-indexing 3/3 12m30s 5m anihpj-locations location-geocoding-batch 1/1 55m42s 15m anihpj-authors author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m anihpj-skills skills-validation 10/10 5m30s 1h
    # Get jobs created in the last hour
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --field-selector=metadata.creationTimestamp>"$(date -d '1 hour ago' --iso-8601)"

# List jobs with completion time information
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,CREATED:.metadata.creationTimestamp,COMPLETED:.status.completionTime"

# Find long-running jobs (over 30 minutes)
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.startTime != null and (.status.completionTime == null or ((.status.completionTime | fromdateiso8601) - (.status.startTime | fromdateiso8601)) > 1800)) | .metadata.name'
    NAME NAMESPACE COMPLETIONS AGE jobpost-search-indexing anihpj-jobposts 3/3 5m location-geocoding-batch anihpj-locations 1/1 15m NAME NAMESPACE CREATED COMPLETED skills-validation anihpj-skills 2024-01-15T14:00:00Z 2024-01-15T14:05:30Z author-data-processing anihpj-authors 2024-01-15T14:15:00Z 2024-01-15T14:23:20Z "location-geocoding-batch" "author-data-processing"
    NAME NAMESPACE COMPLETIONS AGE jobpost-search-indexing anihpj-jobposts 3/3 5m location-geocoding-batch anihpj-locations 1/1 15m NAME NAMESPACE CREATED COMPLETED skills-validation anihpj-skills 2024-01-15T14:00:00Z 2024-01-15T14:05:30Z author-data-processing anihpj-authors 2024-01-15T14:15:00Z 2024-01-15T14:23:20Z "location-geocoding-batch" "author-data-processing"
    Example 5: Bulk Job Operations and Cleanup

    Perform bulk operations on job collections for maintenance and cleanup.

    # Delete all completed jobs in skills namespace
kubectl delete jobs -n anihpj-skills --field-selector=status.successful=1
    job.batch "skills-validation" deleted job.batch "skills-data-import" deleted job.batch "skills-cleanup" deleted
    # Delete jobs older than 24 hours across all namespaces
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.creationTimestamp < "'$(date -d '24 hours ago' --iso-8601)'") | "\(.metadata.namespace) \(.metadata.name)"' | while read ns name; do kubectl delete job $name -n $ns; done

# Apply configuration to multiple jobs using labels
kubectl label jobs -l batch=data-processing cleanup=enabled --all-namespaces

# Suspend all jobs matching a pattern
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -l app=author-processing -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl patch {} -p '{"spec":{"suspend":true}}'

# Resume all suspended jobs
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --field-selector=spec.suspend=true -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl patch {} -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
    job.batch/author-data-processing labeled job.batch/author-profile-sync labeled job.batch/author-data-processing patched job.batch/author-profile-sync patched

    Job Operations

    Operations Overview

    Read - GET single Job details
    Read Status - GET Job status subresource
    List - GET multiple Jobs in namespace
    List All Namespaces - GET Jobs across all namespaces
    Create - POST new Job
    Update - PUT existing Job
    Update Status - PUT Job status subresource
    Patch - PATCH existing Job
    Patch Status - PATCH Job status subresource
    Delete - DELETE single Job
    Delete Collection - DELETE multiple Jobs

    Operations allow you to interact with Job resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage Django application batch processing workloads, monitor execution progress, and handle job lifecycle in your anihpj application.

    Read Job

    Read the specified Job resource and retrieve its current configuration and status.

    Example 1: Read Skills Validation Job Configuration
    # Read specific job in skills namespace
kubectl get job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills -o yaml
    apiVersion: batch/v1 kind: Job metadata: name: skills-validation namespace: anihpj-skills spec: completions: 10 parallelism: 3 status: succeeded: 10 conditions: - type: Complete status: "True"
    Example 2: Read Author Processing Job with Custom Output
    # Get job with specific fields
kubectl get job author-data-processing -n anihpj-authors -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,COMPLETIONS:.spec.completions,STATUS:.status.conditions[0].type"
    NAME COMPLETIONS STATUS author-data-processing 5 Complete
    Example 3: Read Location Geocoding Job JSON
    # Read job as JSON for API integration
kubectl get job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -o json
    { "apiVersion": "batch/v1", "kind": "Job", "metadata": { "name": "location-geocoding-batch", "namespace": "anihpj-locations" }, "status": { "succeeded": 1, "completionTime": "2024-01-15T12:55:42Z" } }
    Example 4: Read JobPost Indexing Job Details
    # Get detailed job information
kubectl describe job jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts
    Name: jobpost-search-indexing Namespace: anihpj-jobposts Selector: controller-uid=abc123-def456 Labels: app=jobpost-indexing Completions: 3 Parallelism: 1 Pod Template: Labels: app=jobpost-indexing Containers: indexer: Image: anihpj/jobpost-indexer:v2.8 Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulCreate 2m job-controller Created pod: jobpost-search-indexing-abc123
    Example 5: Read Multi-Service Backup Job Status
    # Read job status only
kubectl get job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status}'
    {"active":0,"succeeded":4,"startTime":"2024-01-15T14:00:00Z","completionTime":"2024-01-15T14:15:30Z","conditions":[{"type":"Complete","status":"True","lastProbeTime":"2024-01-15T14:15:30Z","lastTransitionTime":"2024-01-15T14:15:30Z","reason":"JobComplete","message":"Job completed successfully"}]}

    Read Job Status

    Read the status subresource of the specified Job to monitor execution progress and conditions.

    Example 1: Monitor Skills Processing Job Progress
    # Read job status with conditions
kubectl get job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*]}'
    {"type":"Complete","status":"True","lastProbeTime":"2024-01-15T10:35:30Z","lastTransitionTime":"2024-01-15T10:35:30Z","reason":"JobComplete","message":"Job completed successfully"}
    Example 2: Check Author Job Completion Count
    # Get completion metrics
kubectl get job author-data-processing -n anihpj-authors -o jsonpath='{.status.succeeded}/{.spec.completions}'
    5/5
    Example 3: Monitor Location Job Active Pods
    # Check active pod count
kubectl get job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -o jsonpath='{.status.active}'
    0
    Example 4: Track JobPost Indexing Duration
    # Calculate job runtime
kubectl get job jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts -o custom-columns="START:.status.startTime,COMPLETION:.status.completionTime"
    START COMPLETION 2024-01-15T13:15:00Z 2024-01-15T13:27:30Z
    Example 5: Check Backup Job Failure Status
    # Get failure information
kubectl get job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Failed")]}'
    # No output - job completed successfully without failure conditions

    List Jobs

    List Job resources in a specific namespace with filtering and sorting options.

    Example 1: List All Skills Namespace Jobs
    # List all jobs in skills namespace
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-skills
    NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE skills-validation 10/10 6m30s 1h skills-data-import 1/1 2m15s 2h skills-cleanup 1/1 1m05s 3h skills-export 0/1 45s 45s
    Example 2: List Author Jobs with Labels
    # Filter jobs by labels
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-authors -l batch=data-processing
    NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m author-profile-sync 1/1 3m10s 30m
    Example 3: List Location Jobs by Status
    # Filter by completion status
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-locations --field-selector=status.successful=1
    NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE location-geocoding-batch 1/1 55m42s 1h15m location-data-sync 1/1 3m45s 2h
    Example 4: List JobPost Jobs with Custom Columns
    # Custom output format
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-jobposts -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,ACTIVE:.status.active,SUCCEEDED:.status.succeeded"
    NAME ACTIVE SUCCEEDED jobpost-search-indexing 0 3 jobpost-batch-update 1 0 jobpost-data-migration 0 1
    Example 5: List System Jobs Sorted by Age
    # Sort by creation time
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-system --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE multi-service-backup 4/4 15m30s 2h system-maintenance 1/1 5m20s 1h log-rotation 1/1 2m45s 30m

    List Jobs for All Namespaces

    List Job resources across all namespaces in the cluster with cross-namespace filtering.

    Example 1: List All anihpj Service Jobs
    # List jobs across all anihpj namespaces
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -l project=anihpj
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-skills skills-validation 10/10 6m30s 1h anihpj-authors author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m anihpj-locations location-geocoding-batch 1/1 55m42s 1h15m anihpj-jobposts jobpost-search-indexing 3/3 12m30s 5m anihpj-system multi-service-backup 4/4 15m30s 2h
    Example 2: Find Running Jobs Cluster-wide
    # List all active jobs
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.active=1
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-jobposts jobpost-batch-update 0/1 3m45s 3m45s anihpj-locations location-sync-retry 0/1 1m20s 1m20s
    Example 3: List Failed Jobs Across Services
    # Find failed jobs for troubleshooting
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.failed=1
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-skills skills-export 0/1 5m45s 45m anihpj-authors author-retry 0/5 8m20s 8m20s
    Example 4: List Jobs by Batch Type
    # Filter by batch processing type
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces -l batch=data-processing
    NAMESPACE NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE anihpj-skills skills-validation 10/10 6m30s 1h anihpj-authors author-data-processing 5/5 8m20s 45m anihpj-locations location-data-sync 1/1 3m45s 2h anihpj-jobposts jobpost-batch-update 0/1 3m45s 3m45s
    Example 5: List Recent Jobs Cluster-wide
    # Get jobs created in last 24 hours
kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp

    Create Job

    Create a new Job resource in the specified namespace to execute batch workloads.

    Example 1: Create Skills Validation Job
    # Create job from YAML file
kubectl apply -f skills-validation-job.yaml -n anihpj-skills
    job.batch/skills-validation created
    Example 2: Create Author Processing Job with Imperative Command
    # Create job using kubectl run
kubectl create job author-batch-process --image=anihpj/author-processor:v1.5 -n anihpj-authors
    job.batch/author-batch-process created
    Example 3: Create Location Geocoding Job from CronJob
    # Create job from existing cronjob
kubectl create job location-geocoding-manual --from=cronjob/location-geocoding-schedule -n anihpj-locations
    job.batch/location-geocoding-manual created
    Example 4: Create JobPost Indexing Job with Environment Variables
    # Create job with environment variables
kubectl create job jobpost-index --image=anihpj/jobpost-indexer:v2.8 --env="INDEX_TYPE=full" --env="OPTIMIZE=true" -n anihpj-jobposts
    job.batch/jobpost-index created
    Example 5: Create Multi-Service Backup Job
    # Create job from manifest
kubectl create -f multi-service-backup-job.yaml -n anihpj-system
    job.batch/multi-service-backup created

    Update Job

    Update the specified Job resource configuration (limited fields can be updated).

    Example 1: Update Skills Job Suspend Status
    # Suspend running job
kubectl patch job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills -p '{"spec":{"suspend":true}}'
    job.batch/skills-validation patched
    Example 2: Update Author Job Labels
    # Add label to running job
kubectl label job author-data-processing -n anihpj-authors priority=high
    job.batch/author-data-processing labeled
    Example 3: Update Location Job TTL
    # Update TTL for automatic cleanup
kubectl patch job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -p '{"spec":{"ttlSecondsAfterFinished":3600}}'
    job.batch/location-geocoding-batch patched
    Example 4: Update JobPost Job Annotations
    # Add annotation for monitoring
kubectl annotate job jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts monitoring.anihpj.com/alert=enabled
    job.batch/jobpost-search-indexing annotated
    Example 5: Resume Suspended Backup Job
    # Resume suspended job
kubectl patch job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
    job.batch/multi-service-backup patched

    Update Job Status

    Update the status subresource of the specified Job (typically used by the Job controller).

    Example 1: Monitor Skills Job Status Updates
    # Watch status changes
kubectl get job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills -w -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].type}'
    Progressing Progressing Complete Complete
    Example 2: Check Author Job Status Transitions
    # Monitor condition changes
kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=author-data-processing -n anihpj-authors
    Example 3: Track Location Job Progress
    # Watch completion progress
kubectl get job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -w -o custom-columns="ACTIVE:.status.active,SUCCEEDED:.status.succeeded"
    Example 4: Monitor JobPost Job Completion
    # Track job completion
kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts --timeout=600s
    Example 5: Check Backup Job Status History
    # Get status change history
kubectl describe job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system | grep -A 10 "Events:"

    Patch Job

    Partially update the specified Job resource using strategic merge patch, JSON merge patch, or JSON patch.

    Example 1: Patch Skills Job with Strategic Merge
    # Add label using strategic merge patch
kubectl patch job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"environment":"production"}}}'
    job.batch/skills-validation patched
    Example 2: Patch Author Job Parallelism
    # Update parallelism (before job starts)
kubectl patch job author-data-processing -n anihpj-authors -p '{"spec":{"parallelism":5}}'
    job.batch/author-data-processing patched
    Example 3: Patch Location Job with JSON Patch
    # Add annotation using JSON patch
kubectl patch job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations --type='json' -p='[{"op":"add","path":"/metadata/annotations/last-updated","value":"2024-01-15T15:30:00Z"}]'
    job.batch/location-geocoding-batch patched
    Example 4: Patch JobPost Job Deadline
    # Update active deadline seconds
kubectl patch job jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts -p '{"spec":{"activeDeadlineSeconds":7200}}'
    job.batch/jobpost-search-indexing patched
    Example 5: Patch Backup Job Suspend State
    # Toggle suspend state
kubectl patch job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
    job.batch/multi-service-backup patched

    Patch Job Status

    Partially update the status subresource of the specified Job using patch operations.

    Example 1: Monitor Skills Job Status Patching
    # Watch status patch operations
kubectl get events --field-selector reason=SuccessfulCreate,involvedObject.name=skills-validation -n anihpj-skills
    LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 2m Normal SuccessfulCreate job/skills-validation Created pod: skills-validation-abc123 1m Normal SuccessfulCreate job/skills-validation Created pod: skills-validation-def456
    Example 2: Track Author Job Status Changes
    # Monitor controller status updates
kubectl logs -n kube-system -l app=job-controller --tail=20
    I0115 14:30:15.123456 job_controller.go:315] Job author-data-processing: status updated I0115 14:30:16.234567 job_controller.go:403] Job author-data-processing: pod succeeded I0115 14:30:17.345678 job_controller.go:315] Job author-data-processing: completion count updated
    Example 3: Observe Location Job Status Patching
    # Watch status field updates
kubectl get job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations -o jsonpath='{.status}' -w
    Example 4: Monitor JobPost Job Status Updates
    # Track completion status changes
kubectl get events --field-selector reason=Completed,involvedObject.name=jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts
    Example 5: Check Backup Job Status Modifications
    # Monitor status field changes
kubectl get job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system -o json -w | jq '.status'

    Delete Job

    Delete the specified Job resource and optionally its associated pods.

    Example 1: Delete Completed Skills Job
    # Delete specific job
kubectl delete job skills-validation -n anihpj-skills
    job.batch "skills-validation" deleted
    Example 2: Delete Author Job with Grace Period
    # Delete with custom grace period
kubectl delete job author-data-processing -n anihpj-authors --grace-period=30
    job.batch "author-data-processing" deleted
    Example 3: Force Delete Stuck Location Job
    # Force delete without waiting
kubectl delete job location-geocoding-batch -n anihpj-locations --force --grace-period=0
    warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. job.batch "location-geocoding-batch" force deleted
    Example 4: Delete JobPost Job and Keep Pods
    # Delete job but keep pods running
kubectl delete job jobpost-search-indexing -n anihpj-jobposts --cascade=orphan
    job.batch "jobpost-search-indexing" deleted
    Example 5: Delete Backup Job with Confirmation
    # Delete with confirmation
kubectl delete job multi-service-backup -n anihpj-system --wait=true
    job.batch "multi-service-backup" deleted

    Delete Collection of Jobs

    Delete multiple Job resources using label selectors or field selectors.

    Example 1: Delete Completed Skills Jobs
    # Delete all completed jobs in skills namespace
kubectl delete jobs -n anihpj-skills --field-selector=status.successful=1
    Example 2: Delete Author Jobs by Label
    # Delete jobs with specific label
kubectl delete jobs -n anihpj-authors -l batch=data-processing
    job.batch "author-data-processing" deleted job.batch "author-profile-sync" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Old Location Jobs
    # Delete jobs older than 24 hours
kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-locations -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.creationTimestamp < "'$(date -d '24 hours ago' --iso-8601)'") | .metadata.name' | xargs -r kubectl delete job -n anihpj-locations
    job.batch "location-old-geocoding" deleted job.batch "location-legacy-sync" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Failed JobPost Jobs
    # Delete all failed jobs
kubectl delete jobs -n anihpj-jobposts --field-selector=status.failed=1
    job.batch "jobpost-failed-index" deleted job.batch "jobpost-retry-failed" deleted
    Example 5: Delete All System Backup Jobs
    # Delete all backup jobs
kubectl delete jobs -n anihpj-system -l jobType=backup --all
    job.batch "daily-backup" deleted job.batch "weekly-backup" deleted job.batch "multi-service-backup" deleted

    CronJob

    CronJob Overview

    CronJobSpec - Job schedule and template specification
    CronJobStatus - Current execution status and history
    CronJobList - Collection of CronJob resources
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: CronJob v1 API | API Version: batch/v1

    CronJob represents the configuration of a single cron job that creates Jobs on a time-based schedule for recurring tasks.

    Full Description

    CronJob represents the configuration of a single cron job that creates Jobs on a time-based schedule. In Django applications, CronJobs are essential for recurring tasks like database maintenance, report generation, log cleanup, and periodic data synchronization. Unlike regular Jobs that run once, CronJobs automatically create new Jobs according to their schedule.

    CronJobs are perfect for automating routine Django tasks such as cleaning expired sessions, sending scheduled notifications, backing up user data, updating search indexes, and processing recurring batch operations. Each CronJob creates Job objects that handle the actual execution, providing reliable scheduling for your anihpj Django application maintenance tasks.

    Syntax

    # Create a CronJob from YAML file
kubectl create -f cronjob.yaml
# List all CronJobs
kubectl get cronjobs
# List all CronJobs (short form)
kubectl get cj
# Get detailed information about a specific CronJob
kubectl describe cronjob {name}
# Get detailed information about a specific CronJob (short form)
kubectl describe cj {name}
# Suspend/resume CronJob executions
kubectl patch cronjob {name} -p '{"spec":{"suspend":true}}'
kubectl patch cronjob {name} -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
# Delete a specific CronJob
kubectl delete cronjob {name}
# Delete a specific CronJob (short form)
kubectl delete cj {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-django-cronjob
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: maintenance
spec:
  schedule: "0 2 * * *"  # Daily at 2 AM
  timeZone: "UTC"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 300
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: maintenance
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: django-task
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "cleanup_expired_jobs"]
            env:
            - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
              value: "anihpj.settings"
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "128Mi"
                cpu: "100m"
              limits:
                memory: "256Mi"
                cpu: "200m"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure

    Parameters Description

    • apiVersion: Always "batch/v1" for CronJob. This indicates the API version being used.
    • kind: Must be "CronJob" to specify the resource type.
    • metadata.name: Unique name for the CronJob within the namespace. Use descriptive names like "anihpj-django-cronjob".
    • metadata.namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the CronJob will be created. Defaults to "default".
    • metadata.labels: Key-value pairs for organizing and selecting CronJobs. Useful for grouping Django app maintenance tasks.
    • spec.schedule: Cron format schedule expression. For Django apps, common patterns are daily, weekly, or hourly tasks.
    • spec.timeZone: Time zone for the schedule. Important for Django apps with location-specific requirements.
    • spec.concurrencyPolicy: How to handle concurrent job executions. Options: Allow, Forbid, Replace.
    • spec.startingDeadlineSeconds: Deadline in seconds for starting jobs if they miss their scheduled time.
    • spec.jobTemplate: Template for the Jobs that will be created on schedule.
    • spec.successfulJobsHistoryLimit: Number of successful completed jobs to retain for history.
    • spec.failedJobsHistoryLimit: Number of failed jobs to retain for troubleshooting.

    Return Value

    Returns the CronJob object with its current state, including status information about last schedule time, active jobs, and execution history.

    • 200 OK: CronJob successfully created/updated
    • 201 Created: CronJob successfully created
    • 400 Bad Request: Invalid CronJob specification or schedule format
    • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
    • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
    • 404 Not Found: Namespace not found
    • 409 Conflict: CronJob already exists
    • 422 Unprocessable Entity: Invalid field values or schedule expression

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: Skills Model Cleanup (jobpost app)

    This example demonstrates deploying a CronJob for cleaning up expired Skills data daily, ensuring the Skills model in the jobpost app maintains current and relevant job skills.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-skills-cleanup
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: skills-cleanup
spec:
  schedule: "0 3 * * *"  # Daily at 3 AM
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: jobpost
            service: skills-cleanup
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: skills-cleanup
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "cleanup_expired_skills"]
            env:
            - name: DJANGO_APP
              value: "jobpost"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "skills"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Apply the Skills cleanup CronJob
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-skills-cleanup.yaml
    cronjob.batch/anihpj-jobpost-skills-cleanup created
    Example 2: Author Model Analytics (jobpost app)

    This example shows how to deploy weekly analytics generation for the Author model, creating reports on author activity and job post metrics.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-author-analytics
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: author-analytics
spec:
  schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # Mondays at 9 AM
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: jobpost
            service: author-analytics
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: author-analytics
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "generate_author_analytics"]
            env:
            - name: DJANGO_APP
              value: "jobpost"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "author"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Apply the Author analytics CronJob
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-author-analytics.yaml
    cronjob.batch/anihpj-jobpost-author-analytics created
    Example 3: Location Model Data Sync (jobpost app)

    This example deploys hourly synchronization for Location model data, keeping location information current with external location services.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-location-sync
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: location-sync
spec:
  schedule: "0 * * * *"  # Every hour
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 300
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: jobpost
            service: location-sync
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: location-sync
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "sync_location_data"]
            env:
            - name: DJANGO_APP
              value: "jobpost"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "location"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Apply the Location sync CronJob
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-location-sync.yaml
    cronjob.batch/anihpj-jobpost-location-sync created
    Example 4: JobPost Model Archival (jobpost app)

    This example implements monthly archival of expired JobPost records, maintaining database performance while preserving historical data.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-archive
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: jobpost-archive
spec:
  schedule: "0 2 1 * *"  # First day of month at 2 AM
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 5
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 3600
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: jobpost
            service: jobpost-archive
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: jobpost-archive
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "archive_expired_jobposts"]
            env:
            - name: DJANGO_APP
              value: "jobpost"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "jobpost"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Apply the JobPost archival CronJob
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-archive.yaml
    cronjob.batch/anihpj-jobpost-archive created
    Example 5: User Model Notifications (jobpost app)

    This example sets up daily notification sending for User model, delivering job alerts and updates to registered users of the anihpj jobpost application.

    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-user-notifications
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost
    service: user-notifications
spec:
  schedule: "0 8 * * *"  # Daily at 8 AM
  timeZone: "America/New_York"
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: jobpost
            service: user-notifications
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: user-notifications
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "send_daily_notifications"]
            env:
            - name: DJANGO_APP
              value: "jobpost"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "user"
            - name: EMAIL_BACKEND
              value: "django.core.mail.backends.smtp.EmailBackend"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Apply the User notifications CronJob
kubectl apply -f anihpj-jobpost-user-notifications.yaml
    cronjob.batch/anihpj-jobpost-user-notifications created
    spec: template: spec: containers: - name: django-task image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0 command: ["python", "manage.py", "cleanup"] restartPolicy: OnFailure
    Example 1: Daily Database Cleanup

    Clean expired JobPost records daily

    Models: JobPost with expiry dates
    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: jobpost-cleanup
  namespace: anihpj-system
spec:
  schedule: "0 3 * * *"
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: cleanup
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "cleanup_expired_jobs"]
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    kubectl apply -f jobpost-cleanup-cronjob.yaml cronjob.batch/jobpost-cleanup created kubectl get cronjobs -n anihpj-system NAME SCHEDULE SUSPEND ACTIVE LAST SCHEDULE AGE jobpost-cleanup 0 3 * * * False 0 <none> 10s
    Example 2: Weekly Report Generation

    Generate weekly analytics reports

    Models: JobPost, Author, Skills analytics
    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: weekly-reports
  namespace: anihpj-system
spec:
  schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # Mondays at 9 AM
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: reports
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "generate_weekly_reports"]
            env:
            - name: REPORT_TYPE
              value: "analytics"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    kubectl apply -f weekly-reports-cronjob.yaml cronjob.batch/weekly-reports created kubectl describe cronjob weekly-reports -n anihpj-system Name: weekly-reports Namespace: anihpj-system Schedule: 0 9 * * 1 Starting Deadline Seconds: <unset> Concurrency Policy: Allow Suspend: False Successful Job History Limit: 3 Failed Job History Limit: 1
    Example 3: Hourly Cache Refresh

    Refresh Skills and Location cache hourly

    Models: Skills, Location caching
    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: cache-refresh
  namespace: anihpj-system
spec:
  schedule: "0 * * * *"  # Every hour
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: cache-refresh
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "refresh_cache", "--models=skills,location"]
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    kubectl apply -f cache-refresh-cronjob.yaml cronjob.batch/cache-refresh created kubectl get jobs -n anihpj-system -l app=cache-refresh NAME COMPLETIONS DURATION AGE cache-refresh-27845620 1/1 45s 1h cache-refresh-27845680 1/1 42s 1m
    Example 4: Monthly Archive Process

    Archive old JobPost records monthly

    Models: JobPost archival and cleanup
    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: monthly-archive
  namespace: anihpj-system
spec:
  schedule: "0 2 1 * *"  # 1st day of month at 2 AM
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 3600
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 7200
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: archive
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "archive_old_posts", "--months=6"]
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "256Mi"
                cpu: "200m"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    kubectl apply -f monthly-archive-cronjob.yaml cronjob.batch/monthly-archive created kubectl get cronjob monthly-archive -n anihpj-system -o wide NAME SCHEDULE SUSPEND ACTIVE LAST SCHEDULE AGE monthly-archive 0 2 1 * * False 0 <none> 30s
    Example 5: Notification Scheduler

    Send daily job alerts to users

    Models: User, JobPost notifications
    apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: daily-notifications
  namespace: anihpj-system
spec:
  schedule: "0 8 * * *"  # Daily at 8 AM
  timeZone: "America/New_York"
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 5
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: notifications
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "send_daily_notifications"]
            env:
            - name: EMAIL_BACKEND
              value: "django.core.mail.backends.smtp.EmailBackend"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    kubectl apply -f daily-notifications-cronjob.yaml cronjob.batch/daily-notifications created kubectl logs -n anihpj-system job/daily-notifications-27845740 Processing notification queue... Sent 150 job alerts to subscribers Notification batch completed successfully

    CronJobSpec

    CronJobSpec describes how the job execution will look like and when it will actually run. For Django applications, this specification defines the schedule, job template, and execution policies for recurring maintenance tasks.

    Syntax
    spec:
  schedule: "0 2 * * *"
  timeZone: "UTC"
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 300
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  suspend: false
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: scheduled-task
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: django-task
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:latest
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "maintenance"]
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    Parameters Description
    • schedule (string): Cron format schedule expression. Required field. Common Django patterns: "0 2 * * *" (daily 2 AM), "0 0 * * 0" (weekly Sunday), "*/30 * * * *" (every 30 minutes).
    • timeZone (string): Time zone name for the schedule. Optional. Important for Django apps with location-specific requirements. Examples: "America/New_York", "Europe/London".
    • jobTemplate (JobTemplateSpec): Required. Template specification for the Jobs created by this CronJob. Must include valid Job spec.
    • startingDeadlineSeconds (int64): Optional deadline for starting jobs if missed. For Django apps, typically 300-900 seconds to allow for startup time.
    • concurrencyPolicy (string): How to handle concurrent executions. Allow (default), Forbid (prevent overlaps), Replace (stop current, start new).
    • suspend (boolean): Suspend future executions without deleting the CronJob. Useful for maintenance or debugging Django scheduled tasks.
    • successfulJobsHistoryLimit (int32): Number of successful jobs to retain. Default 3. For Django apps, balance between history and storage.
    • failedJobsHistoryLimit (int32): Number of failed jobs to retain. Default 1. Important for debugging Django task failures.
    Return Value

    Returns the CronJobSpec object containing the complete schedule configuration and job template for the Django application's recurring tasks.

    • 200 OK: CronJobSpec successfully retrieved
    • 400 Bad Request: Invalid schedule format or specification
    • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
    • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions to access CronJobSpec
    • 404 Not Found: CronJob or namespace not found
    5 Practical Django Examples
    Example 1: Skills Model Daily Maintenance

    This example shows a CronJobSpec for daily Skills model maintenance, cleaning up expired or invalid skill entries from the jobpost app.

    spec:
  schedule: "0 3 * * *"  # Daily at 3 AM
  timeZone: "UTC"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: skills-maintenance
            model: skills
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: skills-cleanup
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "cleanup_skills"]
            env:
            - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
              value: "anihpj.settings"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "skills"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Get the CronJobSpec for skills maintenance
kubectl get cronjob skills-maintenance -o jsonpath='{.spec}' | jq
    { "concurrencyPolicy": "Forbid", "failedJobsHistoryLimit": 1, "jobTemplate": { "spec": { "template": { "metadata": { "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "component": "skills-maintenance", "model": "skills" } }, "spec": { "containers": [ { "command": ["python", "manage.py", "cleanup_skills"], "env": [ {"name": "DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", "value": "anihpj.settings"}, {"name": "MODEL_FOCUS", "value": "skills"} ], "image": "anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0", "name": "skills-cleanup" } ], "restartPolicy": "OnFailure" } } } }, "schedule": "0 3 * * *", "successfulJobsHistoryLimit": 3, "timeZone": "UTC" }
    Example 2: Author Model Weekly Reports

    This example demonstrates a CronJobSpec for generating weekly Author analytics reports, tracking author activity and job posting metrics.

    spec:
  schedule: "0 9 * * 1"  # Mondays at 9 AM
  timeZone: "America/New_York"
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 600
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 5
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: author-reports
            model: author
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: author-analytics
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "generate_author_reports"]
            env:
            - name: REPORT_TYPE
              value: "weekly"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "author"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Apply the Author reports CronJobSpec
kubectl apply -f author-reports-cronjob.yaml
    cronjob.batch/author-reports created
    Example 3: Location Model Hourly Sync

    This example shows a CronJobSpec for hourly Location model synchronization with external geocoding services to keep location data current.

    spec:
  schedule: "0 * * * *"  # Every hour
  concurrencyPolicy: Replace
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 300
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 1800
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: location-sync
            model: location
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: location-sync
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "sync_locations"]
            env:
            - name: SYNC_MODE
              value: "incremental"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "location"
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "128Mi"
                cpu: "100m"
          restartPolicy: Never
    # Check the location sync CronJobSpec schedule
kubectl get cronjob location-sync -o jsonpath='{.spec.schedule}'
    0 * * * *
    Example 4: JobPost Model Archive Process

    This example demonstrates a CronJobSpec for monthly JobPost archival, moving expired job postings to archive storage while maintaining database performance.

    spec:
  schedule: "0 2 1 * *"  # First day of month at 2 AM
  timeZone: "UTC"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  startingDeadlineSeconds: 1800
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      activeDeadlineSeconds: 7200
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: jobpost-archive
            model: jobpost
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: jobpost-archiver
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
            command: ["python", "manage.py", "archive_jobposts", "--months=6"]
            env:
            - name: ARCHIVE_THRESHOLD_MONTHS
              value: "6"
            - name: MODEL_FOCUS
              value: "jobpost"
            resources:
              requests:
                memory: "256Mi"
                cpu: "200m"
              limits:
                memory: "512Mi"
                cpu: "500m"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
    # Verify the JobPost archive CronJobSpec configuration
kubectl describe cronjob jobpost-archive | grep -A 10 "Schedule:"
    Schedule: 0 2 1 * * Concurrency Policy: Forbid Suspend: False Successful Job History Limit: 3 Failed Job History Limit: 3 Starting Deadline Seconds: 1800s Job Template: Metadata: Labels: app=anihpj component=jobpost-archive model=jobpost
    Example 5: User Model Notification System

    This example shows an advanced CronJobSpec for User model notification processing, sending personalized job alerts and system updates to registered users.

    spec:
  schedule: "0 8,12,18 * * *"  # 8 AM, 12 PM, 6 PM daily
  timeZone: "America/New_York"
  concurrencyPolicy: Allow
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 10
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 5
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: anihpj
            component: user-notifications
            model: user
          annotations:
            batch.kubernetes.io/cronjob: "user-notifications"
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: notification-processor
            image: anihpj/jobpost-app:v1.0.0
