kubectl get hpa -n anihpj-system -o wide
    NAME REFERENCE TARGETS MINPODS MAXPODS REPLICAS AGE jobpost-api-hpa Deployment/jobpost-api cpu: 72%/70%, memory: 68%/80% 2 20 8 5d user-auth-hpa Deployment/user-auth auth_requests_per_second: 25/30 3 15 3 3d skills-external-hpa Deployment/skills-service pubsub.googleapis.com: 45/30 1 10 6 2d location-object-hpa Deployment/location requests-per-second: 800m/1k 2 12 4 1d author-container-hpa Deployment/author-service cpu (author-container): 58%/60% 1 8 4 12h
    Example 3: List HPAs by Label Selector

    This example shows how to filter HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources using label selectors to find specific API service autoscalers.

    # Filter HPAs by component label
kubectl get hpa -n anihpj-system -l component=api
    NAME REFERENCE TARGETS MINPODS MAXPODS REPLICAS AGE jobpost-api-hpa Deployment/jobpost-api 72%/70%, 68%/80% 2 20 8 5d skills-api-hpa Deployment/skills-api skill_matches: 18/20 2 18 6 4d
    Example 4: Export HPA Configurations

    This example demonstrates how to export all HPA configurations in YAML format for backup, migration, or configuration management purposes.

    # Export all HPA configurations in YAML format
kubectl get hpa -n anihpj-system -o yaml > anihpj-hpa-backup.yaml
    apiVersion: v1 items: - apiVersion: autoscaling/v2 kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler metadata: name: jobpost-api-hpa namespace: anihpj-system spec: maxReplicas: 20 minReplicas: 2 scaleTargetRef: apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment name: jobpost-api status: currentReplicas: 8 desiredReplicas: 8 kind: List
    Example 5: Watch HPA Changes in Real-Time

    This example shows how to monitor HorizontalPodAutoscaler changes in real-time to observe scaling decisions and metric fluctuations as they occur.

    # Watch HPA changes in real-time
kubectl get hpa -n anihpj-system -w
    NAME REFERENCE TARGETS MINPODS MAXPODS REPLICAS AGE jobpost-api-hpa Deployment/jobpost-api 72%/70%, 68%/80% 2 20 8 5d user-auth-hpa Deployment/user-auth 25/30 3 15 3 3d jobpost-api-hpa Deployment/jobpost-api 75%/70%, 68%/80% 2 20 8 5d jobpost-api-hpa Deployment/jobpost-api 75%/70%, 68%/80% 2 20 10 5d

    ⚙️ Operations

    Complete set of operations for managing HorizontalPodAutoscaler v2 resources in anihpj Django applications.

    🔍 Get HorizontalPodAutoscaler

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers/{name}

    Read the specified HorizontalPodAutoscaler with enhanced v2 metrics and status information.

    # Get specific HPA with v2 metrics
kubectl get hpa jobpost-api-hpa -n anihpj-system -o yaml
    apiVersion: autoscaling/v2 kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler metadata: name: jobpost-api-hpa namespace: anihpj-system spec: maxReplicas: 20 minReplicas: 2 metrics: - resource: name: cpu target: averageUtilization: 70 type: Utilization type: Resource - resource: name: memory target: averageUtilization: 80 type: Utilization type: Resource scaleTargetRef: apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment name: jobpost-api status: currentMetrics: - resource: current: averageUtilization: 72 name: cpu type: Resource - resource: current: averageUtilization: 68 name: memory type: Resource currentReplicas: 8 desiredReplicas: 8
    📊 Get HorizontalPodAutoscaler Status

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers/{name}/status

    Read the status subresource of the specified HorizontalPodAutoscaler with current metrics and conditions.

    # Get HPA status with current metrics
kubectl get hpa user-auth-hpa -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq
    { "conditions": [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "message": "recommended size matches current size", "reason": "ReadyForNewScale", "status": "True", "type": "AbleToScale" }, { "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "message": "the HPA was able to successfully calculate a replica count from pods metric auth_requests_per_second", "reason": "ValidMetricFound", "status": "True", "type": "ScalingActive" } ], "currentMetrics": [ { "pods": { "current": { "averageValue": "25" }, "metric": { "name": "auth_requests_per_second" } }, "type": "Pods" } ], "currentReplicas": 3, "desiredReplicas": 3 }
    📋 List HorizontalPodAutoscalers

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers

    List or watch objects of kind HorizontalPodAutoscaler in a specific namespace.

    # List all HPAs in namespace with v2 features
kubectl get hpa -n anihpj-system --output=custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,TARGET:.spec.scaleTargetRef.name,MIN:.spec.minReplicas,MAX:.spec.maxReplicas,CURRENT:.status.currentReplicas,DESIRED:.status.desiredReplicas"
    NAME TARGET MIN MAX CURRENT DESIRED jobpost-api-hpa jobpost-api 2 20 8 8 user-auth-hpa user-auth-service 3 15 3 3 skills-external-hpa skills-service 1 10 6 6 location-object-hpa location-service 2 12 4 4 author-container-hpa author-service 1 8 4 4
    🌐 List HorizontalPodAutoscalers (All Namespaces)

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/autoscaling/v2/horizontalpodautoscalers

    List or watch HorizontalPodAutoscaler objects across all namespaces.

    # List HPAs across all namespaces
kubectl get hpa --all-namespaces
    NAMESPACE NAME REFERENCE TARGETS MINPODS MAXPODS REPLICAS AGE anihpj-system jobpost-api-hpa Deployment/jobpost-api 72%/70%, 68%/80% 2 20 8 5d anihpj-system user-auth-hpa Deployment/user-auth 25/30 3 15 3 3d anihpj-prod jobpost-prod-hpa Deployment/jobpost-prod cpu: 45%/60% 5 50 12 7d anihpj-staging skills-staging-hpa Deployment/skills-staging skill_processing: 15/25 2 15 4 2d
    ➕ Create HorizontalPodAutoscaler

    HTTP Request: POST /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers

    Create a new HorizontalPodAutoscaler with enhanced v2 metrics and scaling behavior.

    # Create advanced HPA with multiple metrics and behavior
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: advanced-hpa
  namespace: anihpj-system
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: jobpost-analytics
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 30
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 60
  - type: Pods
    pods:
      metric:
        name: analytics_jobs_per_second
      target:
        type: AverageValue
        averageValue: "40"
  behavior:
    scaleUp:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 60
      policies:
      - type: Percent
        value: 100
        periodSeconds: 15
      - type: Pods
        value: 4
        periodSeconds: 15
      selectPolicy: Max
    scaleDown:
      stabilizationWindowSeconds: 300
      policies:
      - type: Percent
        value: 10
        periodSeconds: 60
EOF
    horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling/advanced-hpa created
    ✏️ Update HorizontalPodAutoscaler

    HTTP Request: PUT /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers/{name}

    Replace the specified HorizontalPodAutoscaler with updated configuration.

    # Update HPA with new metrics configuration
kubectl patch hpa jobpost-api-hpa -n anihpj-system --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"maxReplicas":25,"metrics":[{"type":"Resource","resource":{"name":"cpu","target":{"type":"Utilization","averageUtilization":65}}},{"type":"Resource","resource":{"name":"memory","target":{"type":"Utilization","averageUtilization":75}}}]}}'
    horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling/jobpost-api-hpa patched
    📊 Update HorizontalPodAutoscaler Status

    HTTP Request: PUT /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers/{name}/status

    Replace the status of the specified HorizontalPodAutoscaler (typically used by controllers).

    # Status updates are typically performed by the HPA controller
# Check current status
kubectl get hpa user-auth-hpa -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}' | jq
    [ { "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "message": "recommended size matches current size", "reason": "ReadyForNewScale", "status": "True", "type": "AbleToScale" }, { "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "message": "the HPA was able to successfully calculate a replica count", "reason": "ValidMetricFound", "status": "True", "type": "ScalingActive" } ]
    🔧 Patch HorizontalPodAutoscaler

    HTTP Request: PATCH /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers/{name}

    Partially update the specified HorizontalPodAutoscaler with new configuration.

    # Add external metric to existing HPA
kubectl patch hpa skills-api-hpa -n anihpj-system --type='json' -p='[
  {
    "op": "add",
    "path": "/spec/metrics/-",
    "value": {
      "type": "External",
      "external": {
        "metric": {
          "name": "skills_processing_queue_length"
        },
        "target": {
          "type": "Value",
          "value": "100"
        }
      }
    }
  }
]'
    horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling/skills-api-hpa patched
    📊 Patch HorizontalPodAutoscaler Status

    HTTP Request: PATCH /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers/{name}/status

    Partially update the status of the specified HorizontalPodAutoscaler.

    # Status patches are typically done by controllers
# View recent status changes
kubectl describe hpa location-object-hpa -n anihpj-system | grep -A 5 Events
    Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal SuccessfulRescale 2m horizontal-pod-autoscaler New size: 6; reason: external metric requests-per-second above target Normal SuccessfulRescale 5m horizontal-pod-autoscaler New size: 4; reason: All metrics below target
    🗑️ Delete HorizontalPodAutoscaler

    HTTP Request: DELETE /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers/{name}

    Delete the specified HorizontalPodAutoscaler and stop automatic scaling.

    # Delete specific HPA
kubectl delete hpa author-container-hpa -n anihpj-system
    horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling "author-container-hpa" deleted
    🗑️ Delete HorizontalPodAutoscaler Collection

    HTTP Request: DELETE /apis/autoscaling/v2/namespaces/{namespace}/horizontalpodautoscalers

    Delete multiple HorizontalPodAutoscalers at once using label selectors or other criteria.

    # Delete HPAs by label
kubectl delete hpa -n anihpj-system -l component=analytics
    horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling "jobpost-analytics-hpa" deleted horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling "author-analytics-hpa" deleted
    # Delete all HPAs in namespace (be careful!)
kubectl delete hpa -n anihpj-system --all
    horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling "jobpost-api-hpa" deleted horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling "user-auth-hpa" deleted horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling "skills-external-hpa" deleted horizontalpodautoscaler.autoscaling "location-object-hpa" deleted

    ReplicaSet

    ReplicaSet Overview

    Docs: Kubernetes ReplicaSet Documentation

    ReplicaSetSpec - ReplicaSet specification
    ReplicaSetStatus - Current ReplicaSet state
    ReplicaSetList - Collection operations
    Operations - CRUD and management
    API Version: apps/v1 | Kind: ReplicaSet

    ReplicaSet ensures that a specified number of pod replicas are running at any given time.

    Full Description

    ReplicaSet is a Kubernetes workload resource that maintains a stable set of replica pods running at any given time. It ensures that a specified number of pod replicas are running, replacing pods that fail or are deleted. In your anihpj Django project, ReplicaSets are commonly used to ensure high availability for your Django application pods, managing Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services with consistent replica counts.

    ReplicaSets work by continuously monitoring the current state of pods and comparing it to the desired state. If a pod fails or gets deleted, the ReplicaSet controller automatically creates a new pod to replace it. While you typically don't create ReplicaSets directly (they're usually managed by Deployments), understanding them is crucial for debugging and advanced pod management in your Django application infrastructure.

    Syntax

    # Create a ReplicaSet from YAML file
kubectl create -f replicaset.yaml
# List all ReplicaSets
kubectl get replicasets
# List all ReplicaSets (short form)
kubectl get rs
# Get detailed information about a specific ReplicaSet
kubectl describe replicaset {name}
# Get detailed information about a specific ReplicaSet (short form)
kubectl describe rs {name}
# Get ReplicaSet in YAML format
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o yaml
# Scale a ReplicaSet to specific number of replicas
kubectl scale replicaset {name} --replicas={count}
# Delete a specific ReplicaSet
kubectl delete replicaset {name}
# Delete a specific ReplicaSet (short form)
kubectl delete rs {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-api-replicaset
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: skills-api
    tier: backend
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: skills-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: skills-api
        tier: backend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: skills-api
        image: anihpj/skills-api:v1.2.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          value: "postgresql://anihpj:password@db:5432/anihpj_skills"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"

    Parameters Description

    • apiVersion: Always "apps/v1" for ReplicaSets. This specifies the API version for the resource.
    • kind: Must be "ReplicaSet" to specify the resource type.
    • metadata.name: Unique name for the ReplicaSet within the namespace. Use descriptive names like "anihpj-skills-api-rs".
    • metadata.namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the ReplicaSet will be created. Defaults to "default".
    • metadata.labels: Key-value pairs for organizing and selecting ReplicaSets. Useful for grouping Django app components.
    • spec.replicas: Desired number of pod replicas. This determines how many instances of your Django service will run.
    • spec.selector: Label selector that identifies which pods belong to this ReplicaSet. Must match pod template labels.
    • spec.template: Pod template specification that defines the pods to be created by this ReplicaSet.
    • spec.template.metadata.labels: Labels applied to pods created by this ReplicaSet. Must match the selector.
    • spec.template.spec: Pod specification including containers, volumes, and other pod configuration.
    • status.replicas: Current number of pod replicas managed by the ReplicaSet.
    • status.readyReplicas: Number of pods that are ready and available to serve traffic.

    Return Value

    Returns the ReplicaSet object with its current state, including status information about current and ready replicas, and conditions indicating the ReplicaSet's health.

    • 200 OK: ReplicaSet successfully created/updated
    • 201 Created: ReplicaSet successfully created
    • 400 Bad Request: Invalid ReplicaSet specification
    • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
    • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
    • 404 Not Found: Namespace not found
    • 409 Conflict: ReplicaSet already exists
    • 422 Unprocessable Entity: Invalid field values

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: Skills Model API ReplicaSet

    This example demonstrates creating a ReplicaSet for the Skills model API service in the anihpj Django project, ensuring 3 replicas are always running to handle skills management operations.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-api-rs
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: skills-api
    model: skills
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: skills-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: skills-api
        model: skills
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: skills-api
        image: anihpj/skills-api:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
    # Create the Skills API ReplicaSet
kubectl apply -f skills-api-replicaset.yaml
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-skills-api-rs created
    # Check ReplicaSet status
kubectl get rs anihpj-skills-api-rs -n anihpj-system
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 2m
    Example 2: User Authentication Service ReplicaSet

    This example shows a ReplicaSet for User model authentication service, maintaining 2 replicas for high availability of user authentication in the anihpj Django application.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-auth-rs
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: user-auth
    model: user
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: user-auth
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: user-auth
        model: user
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: user-auth
        image: anihpj/user-auth:v2.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
    # Create User authentication ReplicaSet
kubectl create -f user-auth-replicaset.yaml
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-user-auth-rs created
    # Describe ReplicaSet for detailed information
kubectl describe rs anihpj-user-auth-rs -n anihpj-system
    Name: anihpj-user-auth-rs Namespace: anihpj-system Selector: app=anihpj,component=user-auth Labels: app=anihpj component=user-auth model=user Annotations: Replicas: 2 current / 2 desired Pods Status: 2 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed
    Example 3: JobPost Processing ReplicaSet

    This example creates a ReplicaSet for JobPost model processing service, ensuring consistent availability for job posting operations in the anihpj Django system.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost-processor
    model: jobpost
spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: jobpost-processor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost-processor
        model: jobpost
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-processor
        image: anihpj/jobpost-processor:v1.5.2
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
    # Apply JobPost processor ReplicaSet
kubectl apply -f jobpost-processor-rs.yaml
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs created
    # Scale the ReplicaSet up to 6 replicas
kubectl scale rs anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs --replicas=6 -n anihpj-system
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs scaled
    Example 4: Author Profile Service ReplicaSet

    This example demonstrates a ReplicaSet for Author model profile service, maintaining 2 replicas to handle author profile management efficiently.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-profile-rs
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: author-profile
    model: author
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: author-profile
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: author-profile
        model: author
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: author-profile
        image: anihpj/author-profile:v1.3.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
    # Create Author profile ReplicaSet
kubectl create -f author-profile-rs.yaml
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-author-profile-rs created
    # Get ReplicaSet in YAML format for backup
kubectl get rs anihpj-author-profile-rs -n anihpj-system -o yaml > author-profile-rs-backup.yaml
    # Output written to author-profile-rs-backup.yaml
    Example 5: Location Service ReplicaSet

    This example shows a ReplicaSet for Location model service, ensuring 3 replicas are available for location-based operations in the anihpj Django application.

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-service-rs
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: location-service
    model: location
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: location-service
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: location-service
        model: location
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: location-service
        image: anihpj/location-service:v2.0.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
    # Apply Location service ReplicaSet
kubectl apply -f location-service-rs.yaml
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-location-service-rs created
    # Delete the ReplicaSet (pods will be deleted too)
kubectl delete rs anihpj-location-service-rs -n anihpj-system
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-location-service-rs deleted

    ReplicaSetSpec

    API Version: apps/v1 | Kind: ReplicaSetSpec

    ReplicaSetSpec is the specification of a ReplicaSet, defining the desired state and behavior for pod replicas.

    Full Description

    ReplicaSetSpec defines the specification of a ReplicaSet, containing all the configuration needed to maintain a desired number of pod replicas. In your anihpj Django project, the ReplicaSetSpec determines how many instances of your Django services will run, which pods belong to the ReplicaSet (through selectors), and what template to use when creating new pods for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services.

    The specification includes the replica count, label selectors for identifying managed pods, and the pod template that defines the containers and configuration for new pods. Understanding ReplicaSetSpec is essential for managing your Django application's scalability and ensuring consistent deployment patterns across your microservices architecture.

    Syntax

    # View ReplicaSetSpec in YAML format
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o yaml | grep -A 20 "spec:"
# Get ReplicaSetSpec as JSON for programmatic access
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o jsonpath='{.spec}'
# Describe ReplicaSet to see spec details
kubectl describe replicaset {name}
# Edit ReplicaSetSpec directly
kubectl edit replicaset {name}
# Patch ReplicaSetSpec for specific changes
kubectl patch replicaset {name} --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"replicas":5}}'
# Scale ReplicaSet using spec.replicas
kubectl scale replicaset {name} --replicas={count}
# View current ReplicaSet spec configuration
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o jsonpath='{.spec}' | jq .

    YAML Structure

    spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: skills-api
      model: skills
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: skills-api
        model: skills
        version: v1.0.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: skills-api
        image: anihpj/skills-api:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-secrets
              key: database-url
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5

    Parameters Description

    • replicas: Desired number of pod replicas. This integer value determines how many instances of your Django service will run simultaneously.
    • selector: Label selector that identifies which pods belong to this ReplicaSet. Must match the pod template labels exactly.
    • selector.matchLabels: Map of key-value pairs that pods must have to be managed by this ReplicaSet. Essential for proper pod selection.
    • selector.matchExpressions: List of label selector requirements for more complex selection criteria beyond simple equality.
    • template: Pod template specification that defines the pods to be created by this ReplicaSet when scaling up.
    • template.metadata: Metadata applied to pods created from this template, including labels and annotations.
    • template.metadata.labels: Labels applied to all pods created from this template. Must match the selector matchLabels.
    • template.spec: Pod specification including containers, volumes, security context, and other pod-level configuration.
    • template.spec.containers: Array of container specifications that define the application containers running in each pod.
    • minReadySeconds: Minimum number of seconds for which a newly created pod should be ready without any container crashes.

    Return Value

    Returns the ReplicaSetSpec object containing the specification configuration, including replica count, selector configuration, and pod template definition for the ReplicaSet.

    • 200 OK: ReplicaSetSpec successfully retrieved
    • 400 Bad Request: Invalid specification format
    • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
    • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
    • 404 Not Found: ReplicaSet not found
    • 422 Unprocessable Entity: Invalid field values in specification

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: Skills API ReplicaSetSpec with Health Checks

    This example shows a complete ReplicaSetSpec for the Skills model API service, including health checks and resource limits for the anihpj Django project.

    spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: skills-api
      model: skills
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: skills-api
        model: skills
        version: v1.2.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: skills-api
        image: anihpj/skills-api:v1.2.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /api/skills/health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /api/skills/ready/
            port: 8000
    # View the Skills API ReplicaSetSpec
kubectl get rs anihpj-skills-api-rs -o jsonpath='{.spec}' | jq .
    { "replicas": 3, "selector": { "matchLabels": { "app": "anihpj", "component": "skills-api", "model": "skills" } }, "template": { "metadata": { "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "component": "skills-api", "model": "skills", "version": "v1.2.0" } } } }
    Example 2: User Service ReplicaSetSpec with Environment Configuration

    This example demonstrates a ReplicaSetSpec for User model authentication service with comprehensive environment variable configuration.

    spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: user-auth
      model: user
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: user-auth
        model: user
        tier: backend
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: user-auth
        image: anihpj/user-auth:v2.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
        - name: DATABASE_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: anihpj-secrets
              key: database-url
        - name: REDIS_URL
          value: "redis://redis:6379/0"
    # Edit User auth ReplicaSetSpec to change replicas
kubectl patch rs anihpj-user-auth-rs --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"replicas":4}}'
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-user-auth-rs patched
    Example 3: JobPost Processor ReplicaSetSpec with Resource Limits

    This example shows a ReplicaSetSpec for JobPost model processing with defined resource requests and limits for optimal performance.

    spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: jobpost-processor
      model: jobpost
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost-processor
        model: jobpost
        version: v1.5.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-processor
        image: anihpj/jobpost-processor:v1.5.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
        env:
        - name: WORKER_PROCESSES
          value: "4"
    # Scale JobPost processor ReplicaSet using spec modification
kubectl scale rs anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs --replicas=6
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs scaled
    Example 4: Author Profile ReplicaSetSpec with Complex Selectors

    This example demonstrates a ReplicaSetSpec for Author model service using matchExpressions for more sophisticated pod selection.

    spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchExpressions:
    - key: app
      operator: In
      values: [anihpj]
    - key: component
      operator: In
      values: [author-profile, author-api]
    - key: model
      operator: In
      values: [author]
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: author-profile
        model: author
        environment: production
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: author-profile
        image: anihpj/author-profile:v1.3.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE
          value: "anihpj.settings.production"
    # View Author profile ReplicaSetSpec with complex selectors
kubectl describe rs anihpj-author-profile-rs | grep -A 10 "Selector:"
    Selector: app in (anihpj),component in (author-profile,author-api),model in (author) Labels: app=anihpj component=author-profile model=author Annotations: Replicas: 2 current / 2 desired Pods Status: 2 Running / 0 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed
    Example 5: Location Service ReplicaSetSpec with MinReadySeconds

    This example shows a ReplicaSetSpec for Location model service with minReadySeconds configuration for stable deployments.

    spec:
  replicas: 3
  minReadySeconds: 30
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: location-service
      model: location
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: location-service
        model: location
        version: v2.1.0
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: location-service
        image: anihpj/location-service:v2.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /api/locations/health/
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
    # View Location service ReplicaSetSpec including minReadySeconds
kubectl get rs anihpj-location-service-rs -o yaml | grep -A 25 "spec:"
    spec: minReadySeconds: 30 replicas: 3 selector: matchLabels: app: anihpj component: location-service model: location template: metadata: creationTimestamp: null labels: app: anihpj component: location-service model: location version: v2.1.0

    ReplicaSetStatus

    API Version: apps/v1 | Kind: ReplicaSetStatus

    ReplicaSetStatus represents the current status of a ReplicaSet, providing real-time information about replica counts and conditions.

    Full Description

    ReplicaSetStatus provides comprehensive information about the current state of a ReplicaSet, including the actual number of running pods, ready replicas, and various conditions that indicate the health and status of the ReplicaSet. In your anihpj Django project, monitoring ReplicaSetStatus is crucial for understanding how well your Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services are performing and whether they're meeting their desired replica targets.

    The status includes real-time metrics such as current replica counts, available replicas, ready replicas, and detailed conditions that help diagnose issues with pod creation, scaling, or health. Understanding ReplicaSetStatus helps you monitor the health of your Django application infrastructure and troubleshoot scaling issues or pod failures.

    Syntax

    # View ReplicaSetStatus information
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o yaml | grep -A 20 "status:"
# Get ReplicaSetStatus as JSON
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o jsonpath='{.status}'
# Check specific status fields
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o jsonpath='{.status.replicas}'
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}'
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o jsonpath='{.status.availableReplicas}'
# Monitor ReplicaSetStatus changes in real-time
kubectl get replicaset {name} -w
# View ReplicaSetStatus conditions
kubectl get replicaset {name} -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*]}'
# Describe ReplicaSet to see status details
kubectl describe replicaset {name}

    Status Structure

    status:
  replicas: 3
  readyReplicas: 3
  availableReplicas: 3
  fullyLabeledReplicas: 3
  observedGeneration: 1
  conditions:
  - type: ReplicaFailure
    status: "False"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    reason: "NoFailures"
    message: "All replicas are healthy"
  - type: Ready
    status: "True"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:25:00Z"
    reason: "AllReplicasReady"
    message: "All 3 replicas are ready and available"

    Parameters Description

    • replicas: Current number of non-terminating pods targeted by this ReplicaSet. Shows actual running pod count.
    • readyReplicas: Number of pods that are ready and can serve traffic. Indicates pods that passed readiness probes.
    • availableReplicas: Number of available pods for at least minReadySeconds. These pods are stable and serving traffic.
    • fullyLabeledReplicas: Number of pods that have labels matching the ReplicaSet's pod template labels.
    • observedGeneration: Generation of the most recently observed ReplicaSet. Used for tracking configuration changes.
    • conditions: Array of current conditions observed for this ReplicaSet, indicating various states and issues.
    • conditions.type: Type of ReplicaSet condition, such as "ReplicaFailure" or custom condition types.
    • conditions.status: Status of the condition - "True", "False", or "Unknown".
    • conditions.lastTransitionTime: Last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.
    • conditions.reason: Machine-readable reason for the condition's last transition.
    • conditions.message: Human-readable message indicating details about the transition.

    Return Value

    Returns the ReplicaSetStatus object containing current state information, including replica counts, availability metrics, and condition details for monitoring and troubleshooting.

    • 200 OK: ReplicaSetStatus successfully retrieved
    • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
    • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
    • 404 Not Found: ReplicaSet not found

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: Skills API ReplicaSetStatus Monitoring

    This example demonstrates monitoring the status of a Skills model API ReplicaSet, showing all replicas healthy and ready in the anihpj Django project.

    # Check Skills API ReplicaSetStatus
kubectl get rs anihpj-skills-api-rs -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq .
    { "replicas": 3, "readyReplicas": 3, "availableReplicas": 3, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 3, "observedGeneration": 1, "conditions": [ { "type": "ReplicaFailure", "status": "False", "lastTransitionTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "reason": "NoFailures", "message": "All replicas are healthy" } ] }
    # Monitor Skills API ReplicaSet status changes
kubectl get rs anihpj-skills-api-rs -w
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 5m anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 5m anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 6m
    Example 2: User Auth Service ReplicaSetStatus During Scaling

    This example shows ReplicaSetStatus for User model authentication service during a scaling operation, displaying transitional states.

    # Check User auth ReplicaSetStatus during scaling
kubectl get rs anihpj-user-auth-rs -o jsonpath='{.status.replicas}'
    4
    # Check ready replicas vs total replicas
kubectl get rs anihpj-user-auth-rs -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}'
    3
    # Get detailed status including conditions
kubectl describe rs anihpj-user-auth-rs | grep -A 10 "Pod Status:"
    Pod Status: 3 Running / 1 Waiting / 0 Succeeded / 0 Failed Pod Template: Labels: app=anihpj component=user-auth model=user Containers: user-auth: Image: anihpj/user-auth:v2.0.0 Port: 8000/TCP Host Port: 0/TCP
    Example 3: JobPost Processor ReplicaSetStatus with Failure

    This example demonstrates ReplicaSetStatus when the JobPost model processor has replica failures, showing diagnostic information.

    # Check JobPost processor ReplicaSetStatus with issues
kubectl get rs anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs -o yaml | grep -A 15 "status:"
    status: replicas: 3 readyReplicas: 2 availableReplicas: 2 fullyLabeledReplicas: 3 observedGeneration: 2 conditions: - type: ReplicaFailure status: "True" lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T11:15:00Z" reason: "FailedCreate" message: "Failed to create pod: insufficient resources"
    # Get specific condition information
kubectl get rs anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="ReplicaFailure")].message}'
    Failed to create pod: insufficient resources
    Example 4: Author Profile ReplicaSetStatus Health Check

    This example shows comprehensive health checking of Author model profile service ReplicaSetStatus, including all status metrics.

    # Check all Author profile ReplicaSetStatus metrics
kubectl get rs anihpj-author-profile-rs -o jsonpath='{.status.replicas}{" replicas, "}{.status.readyReplicas}{" ready, "}{.status.availableReplicas}{" available"}'
    2 replicas, 2 ready, 2 available
    # Verify fully labeled replicas match total replicas
kubectl get rs anihpj-author-profile-rs -o jsonpath='{.status.fullyLabeledReplicas}'
    2
    # Check observed generation for configuration tracking
kubectl get rs anihpj-author-profile-rs -o jsonpath='{.status.observedGeneration}'
    1
    Example 5: Location Service ReplicaSetStatus Real-time Monitoring

    This example demonstrates real-time monitoring of Location model service ReplicaSetStatus for continuous health assessment.

    # Real-time monitoring of Location service ReplicaSetStatus
kubectl get rs anihpj-location-service-rs --watch
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-location-service-rs 3 3 3 2m anihpj-location-service-rs 3 3 3 2m anihpj-location-service-rs 5 3 3 2m anihpj-location-service-rs 5 5 3 2m anihpj-location-service-rs 5 5 5 3m
    # Get comprehensive status during scaling
kubectl get rs anihpj-location-service-rs -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq '.replicas, .readyReplicas, .availableReplicas'
    5 5 5

    ReplicaSetList

    API Version: apps/v1 | Kind: ReplicaSetList

    ReplicaSetList is a collection of ReplicaSets, providing batch operations and bulk management capabilities.

    Full Description

    ReplicaSetList represents a collection of ReplicaSet resources, enabling batch operations and comprehensive management of multiple ReplicaSets simultaneously. In your anihpj Django project, ReplicaSetList is useful for managing all the ReplicaSets across your microservices architecture, including those handling Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost model services. This resource type allows you to perform bulk operations, apply filters, and manage ReplicaSets across namespaces.

    ReplicaSetList operations are essential for cluster-wide management, monitoring multiple Django services simultaneously, and performing administrative tasks across your entire application infrastructure. You can use ReplicaSetList to view all ReplicaSets, apply bulk updates, and perform maintenance operations efficiently across your Django application ecosystem.

    Syntax

    # List all ReplicaSets in current namespace
kubectl get replicasets
# List all ReplicaSets in specific namespace
kubectl get replicasets -n {namespace}
# List all ReplicaSets across all namespaces
kubectl get replicasets --all-namespaces
# List ReplicaSets with labels filtering
kubectl get replicasets -l {label-selector}
# Get ReplicaSetList in YAML format
kubectl get replicasets -o yaml
# Get ReplicaSetList as JSON
kubectl get replicasets -o json
# Watch ReplicaSetList for changes
kubectl get replicasets -w
# List ReplicaSets with custom columns
kubectl get replicasets -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas

    List Structure

    apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSetList
metadata:
  resourceVersion: "12345"
  selfLink: /apis/apps/v1/replicasets
items:
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: ReplicaSet
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-skills-api-rs
    namespace: anihpj-system
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      component: skills-api
      model: skills
  spec:
    replicas: 3
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        component: skills-api
  status:
    replicas: 3
    readyReplicas: 3
- apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: ReplicaSet
  metadata:
    name: anihpj-user-auth-rs
    namespace: anihpj-system
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      component: user-auth
      model: user
  spec:
    replicas: 2
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        component: user-auth
  status:
    replicas: 2
    readyReplicas: 2

    Parameters Description

    • apiVersion: Always "apps/v1" for ReplicaSetList. Specifies the API version for the list resource.
    • kind: Must be "ReplicaSetList" to specify the collection resource type.
    • metadata: List metadata including resource version and self-link for the collection.
    • metadata.resourceVersion: Version of the resource collection, used for optimistic concurrency control.
    • metadata.selfLink: Self-referencing link to this ReplicaSetList resource.
    • items: Array of ReplicaSet objects contained in this list. Each item is a complete ReplicaSet resource.
    • items[].metadata: Individual ReplicaSet metadata including name, namespace, labels, and annotations.
    • items[].spec: Individual ReplicaSet specification defining desired state and pod template.
    • items[].status: Individual ReplicaSet status showing current state and replica counts.
    • continue: Continuation token for paginated lists when dealing with large numbers of ReplicaSets.
    • remainingItemCount: Number of remaining items in the list when using pagination.

    Return Value

    Returns the ReplicaSetList object containing an array of ReplicaSet resources, along with list metadata for pagination and resource versioning.

    • 200 OK: ReplicaSetList successfully retrieved
    • 400 Bad Request: Invalid list parameters or selectors
    • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
    • 403 Forbidden: Insufficient permissions
    • 404 Not Found: Namespace not found

    5 Practical Django Examples

    Example 1: List All Django Model ReplicaSets

    This example demonstrates listing all ReplicaSets for Django model services in the anihpj project, showing a comprehensive view of all microservices.

    # List all ReplicaSets in anihpj-system namespace
kubectl get replicasets -n anihpj-system
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 2d anihpj-user-auth-rs 2 2 2 2d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 4 4 4 1d anihpj-author-profile-rs 2 2 2 1d anihpj-location-service-rs 3 3 3 1d
    # Get detailed ReplicaSetList in YAML format
kubectl get replicasets -n anihpj-system -o yaml | head -20
    apiVersion: v1 items: - apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: annotations: deployment.kubernetes.io/desired-replicas: "3" deployment.kubernetes.io/max-replicas: "4" deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1" creationTimestamp: "2024-01-13T10:00:00Z" generation: 1 labels: app: anihpj component: skills-api model: skills name: anihpj-skills-api-rs namespace: anihpj-system
    Example 2: Filter ReplicaSets by Django Model Type

    This example shows how to filter ReplicaSetList to show only specific Django model services using label selectors.

    # List ReplicaSets for User model services only
kubectl get replicasets -l model=user -n anihpj-system
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-user-auth-rs 2 2 2 2d
    # List ReplicaSets for specific app component
kubectl get replicasets -l app=anihpj,component=jobpost-processor -n anihpj-system
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 4 4 4 1d
    # Get ReplicaSetList with multiple model types
kubectl get replicasets -l 'model in (skills,author)' -n anihpj-system
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 2d anihpj-author-profile-rs 2 2 2 1d
    Example 3: Monitor All Django Services ReplicaSets

    This example demonstrates real-time monitoring of all Django service ReplicaSets using watch functionality for the anihpj project.

    # Watch all ReplicaSets for changes
kubectl get replicasets -n anihpj-system -w
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 2d anihpj-user-auth-rs 2 2 2 2d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 4 4 4 1d anihpj-author-profile-rs 2 2 2 1d anihpj-location-service-rs 3 3 3 1d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 4 4 4 1d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 6 4 4 1d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 6 6 4 1d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 6 6 6 1d
    # Get custom view of ReplicaSetList with specific columns
kubectl get replicasets -n anihpj-system -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,MODEL:.metadata.labels.model,REPLICAS:.spec.replicas,READY:.status.readyReplicas,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME MODEL REPLICAS READY AGE anihpj-skills-api-rs skills 3 3 2024-01-13T10:00:00Z anihpj-user-auth-rs user 2 2 2024-01-13T10:00:00Z anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs jobpost 6 6 2024-01-14T08:00:00Z anihpj-author-profile-rs author 2 2 2024-01-14T12:00:00Z anihpj-location-service-rs location 3 3 2024-01-14T14:00:00Z
    Example 4: Cross-Namespace Django ReplicaSet Management

    This example shows managing ReplicaSets across multiple namespaces for different environments in the anihpj Django project.

    # List ReplicaSets across all namespaces
kubectl get replicasets --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj
    NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-production anihpj-skills-api-rs 5 5 5 5d anihpj-staging anihpj-skills-api-rs 2 2 2 3d anihpj-development anihpj-skills-api-rs 1 1 1 1d anihpj-production anihpj-user-auth-rs 3 3 3 5d anihpj-staging anihpj-user-auth-rs 2 2 2 3d
    # Get ReplicaSetList JSON for programmatic processing
kubectl get replicasets --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, namespace: .metadata.namespace, replicas: .spec.replicas, ready: .status.readyReplicas}'
    { "name": "anihpj-skills-api-rs", "namespace": "anihpj-production", "replicas": 5, "ready": 5 } { "name": "anihpj-skills-api-rs", "namespace": "anihpj-staging", "replicas": 2, "ready": 2 }
    Example 5: Django ReplicaSet Health Summary

    This example demonstrates generating a comprehensive health summary of all Django service ReplicaSets in the anihpj project.

    # Get comprehensive ReplicaSet health status
kubectl get replicasets -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.replicas}{"\t"}{.status.readyReplicas}{"\t"}{.status.availableReplicas}{"\n"}{end}' | column -t
    anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 anihpj-user-auth-rs 2 2 2 anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 6 6 6 anihpj-author-profile-rs 2 2 2 anihpj-location-service-rs 3 3 3
    # Count total ReplicaSets by model type
kubectl get replicasets -n anihpj-system -o json | jq '.items | group_by(.metadata.labels.model) | map({model: .[0].metadata.labels.model, count: length})'
    [ { "model": "author", "count": 1 }, { "model": "jobpost", "count": 1 }, { "model": "location", "count": 1 }, { "model": "skills", "count": 1 }, { "model": "user", "count": 1 } ]

    ReplicaSet Operations

    ReplicaSet Operations Overview

    Docs: Kubernetes ReplicaSet Operations

    Read ReplicaSet - Get specified ReplicaSet
    Read Status ReplicaSet - Get ReplicaSet status
    List ReplicaSets - List ReplicaSets in namespace
    List All Namespaces ReplicaSet - List across namespaces
    Create ReplicaSet - Create new ReplicaSet
    Update ReplicaSet - Replace ReplicaSet
    Update Status ReplicaSet - Update ReplicaSet status
    Patch ReplicaSet - Partially update ReplicaSet
    Patch Status ReplicaSet - Patch ReplicaSet status
    Delete ReplicaSet - Delete ReplicaSet
    Delete Collection ReplicaSet - Delete multiple ReplicaSets

    📖 Read ReplicaSet

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets/{name}

    Read the specified ReplicaSet and retrieve its complete configuration and current status.

    # Read Skills API ReplicaSet
kubectl get replicaset anihpj-skills-api-rs -n anihpj-system -o yaml
    apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: ReplicaSet metadata: name: anihpj-skills-api-rs namespace: anihpj-system labels: app: anihpj component: skills-api model: skills spec: replicas: 3 selector: matchLabels: app: anihpj component: skills-api status: replicas: 3 readyReplicas: 3 availableReplicas: 3

    📊 Read ReplicaSet Status

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets/{name}/status

    Read the status of the specified ReplicaSet, focusing only on current state information.

    # Get User auth ReplicaSet status only
kubectl get replicaset anihpj-user-auth-rs -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status}'
    { "replicas": 2, "readyReplicas": 2, "availableReplicas": 2, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 2, "observedGeneration": 1 }

    📋 List ReplicaSets

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets

    List or watch objects of kind ReplicaSet in a specific namespace.

    # List all ReplicaSets in anihpj-system namespace
kubectl get replicasets -n anihpj-system
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-skills-api-rs 3 3 3 2d anihpj-user-auth-rs 2 2 2 2d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 4 4 4 1d anihpj-author-profile-rs 2 2 2 1d anihpj-location-service-rs 3 3 3 1d

    🌐 List ReplicaSets Across All Namespaces

    HTTP Request: GET /apis/apps/v1/replicasets

    List or watch objects of kind ReplicaSet across all namespaces.

    # List all ReplicaSets across all namespaces
kubectl get replicasets --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj
    NAMESPACE NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-production anihpj-skills-api-rs 5 5 5 5d anihpj-staging anihpj-skills-api-rs 2 2 2 3d anihpj-development anihpj-skills-api-rs 1 1 1 1d anihpj-system anihpj-user-auth-rs 2 2 2 2d

    ➕ Create ReplicaSet

    HTTP Request: POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets

    Create a new ReplicaSet resource with the specified configuration.

    # Create JobPost analytics ReplicaSet
kubectl apply -f - << EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-analytics-rs
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost-analytics
    model: jobpost
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: jobpost-analytics
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: jobpost-analytics
        model: jobpost
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: jobpost-analytics
        image: anihpj/jobpost-analytics:v1.0.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
EOF
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-analytics-rs created

    🔄 Update ReplicaSet

    HTTP Request: PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets/{name}

    Replace the specified ReplicaSet with an updated configuration.

    # Update Skills API ReplicaSet with new image
kubectl get replicaset anihpj-skills-api-rs -n anihpj-system -o yaml > skills-rs-backup.yaml
kubectl apply -f - << EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: ReplicaSet
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-api-rs
  namespace: anihpj-system
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: skills-api
    model: skills
spec:
  replicas: 4
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: anihpj
      component: skills-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: anihpj
        component: skills-api
        model: skills
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: skills-api
        image: anihpj/skills-api:v1.1.0
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
EOF
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-skills-api-rs configured

    📊 Update ReplicaSet Status

    HTTP Request: PUT /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets/{name}/status

    Replace the status of the specified ReplicaSet. This is typically done by controllers.

    # Status updates are typically performed by controllers
# View current status before any updates
kubectl get replicaset anihpj-user-auth-rs -n anihpj-system -o jsonpath='{.status}'
    { "replicas": 2, "readyReplicas": 2, "availableReplicas": 2, "fullyLabeledReplicas": 2, "observedGeneration": 1 }

    🔧 Patch ReplicaSet

    HTTP Request: PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets/{name}

    Partially update the specified ReplicaSet, modifying only specific fields.

    # Scale Author profile ReplicaSet using patch
kubectl patch replicaset anihpj-author-profile-rs -n anihpj-system --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"replicas":3}}'
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-author-profile-rs patched
    # Add labels using strategic merge patch
kubectl patch replicaset anihpj-location-service-rs -n anihpj-system --type='strategic' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"version":"v2.0.0"}}}'
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-location-service-rs patched

    📊 Patch ReplicaSet Status

    HTTP Request: PATCH /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets/{name}/status

    Partially update the status of the specified ReplicaSet. Usually done by system controllers.

    # Status patches are typically done by controllers
# Monitor status changes after scaling
kubectl get replicaset anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs -n anihpj-system -w
    NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 4 4 4 1d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 6 4 4 1d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 6 6 4 1d anihpj-jobpost-processor-rs 6 6 6 1d

    🗑️ Delete ReplicaSet

    HTTP Request: DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets/{name}

    Delete the specified ReplicaSet and optionally its managed pods.

    # Delete specific ReplicaSet (and its pods)
kubectl delete replicaset anihpj-jobpost-analytics-rs -n anihpj-system
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-analytics-rs deleted
    # Delete ReplicaSet but keep pods orphaned
kubectl delete replicaset anihpj-skills-api-rs -n anihpj-system --cascade=orphan
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-skills-api-rs deleted

    🗑️ Delete Collection of ReplicaSets

    HTTP Request: DELETE /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/replicasets

    Delete multiple ReplicaSets at once using label selectors or other criteria.

    # Delete ReplicaSets by label selector
kubectl delete replicasets -n anihpj-system -l component=analytics
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-jobpost-analytics-rs deleted replicaset.apps/anihpj-user-analytics-rs deleted
    # Delete all ReplicaSets for specific model (be careful!)
kubectl delete replicasets -n anihpj-system -l model=author
    replicaset.apps/anihpj-author-profile-rs deleted
    # Delete all ReplicaSets in namespace (DANGEROUS!)
kubectl delete replicasets -n anihpj-test --all
    replicaset.apps/test-skills-rs deleted replicaset.apps/test-user-rs deleted replicaset.apps/test-jobpost-rs deleted

    PriorityClass

    PriorityClass

    PriorityClass Overview

    PriorityClass - Priority class mapping definition
    PriorityClassList - Collection of priority classes
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: PriorityClass v1 API | API Version: scheduling.k8s.io/v1

    PriorityClass defines mapping from a priority class name to the priority integer value.

    Full Description

    PriorityClass is a Kubernetes resource that defines mapping from a priority class name to the priority integer value. When developing your anihpj Django application, PriorityClasses enable you to assign different priority levels to your pods, ensuring that critical services like the JobPost API or User authentication services get scheduled before less critical components. Higher priority pods can preempt lower priority ones when cluster resources are constrained.

    This is particularly useful in Django deployments where you want to ensure that your core jobpost application services (handling Skills, User management, Author content, Location services, and JobPost processing) maintain different priority levels based on their criticality to your business operations.

    Syntax

    # Create a PriorityClass from YAML file
kubectl create -f priorityclass.yaml

# List all PriorityClasses
kubectl get priorityclasses

# Get detailed information about a specific PriorityClass
kubectl describe priorityclass {name}

# Get PriorityClass in YAML format
kubectl get priorityclass {name} -o yaml

# Delete a specific PriorityClass
kubectl delete priorityclass {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-high-priority
value: 1000
globalDefault: false
description: "High priority for critical anihpj Django services"
preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority

    Parameters

    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "scheduling.k8s.io/v1" for PriorityClass
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "PriorityClass"
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata including name and labels
    • value (int32, required): Integer value of this priority class that pods receive when using this class
    • description (string): Arbitrary string providing guidelines on when this priority class should be used
    • globalDefault (boolean): Whether this PriorityClass should be the default priority for pods without priority class
    • preemptionPolicy (string): Policy for preempting pods with lower priority (Never, PreemptLowerPriority)

    Return Value

    • PriorityClass object: Complete priority class specification with metadata and priority value
    • metadata: Object metadata including creation timestamp and resource version
    • value: The actual priority integer that will be assigned to pods using this class

    5 Django Service Examples

    Example 1: High Priority for JobPost API

    Create high priority class for critical JobPost API services.

    # Create high priority class for JobPost API
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-high
value: 1000
description: "High priority for JobPost API services"
preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-high created
    Example 2: Medium Priority for User Services

    Create medium priority class for User authentication and management.

    # Create medium priority for User services
kubectl create -f user-priority.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-user-medium created
    Example 3: Low Priority for Skills Processing

    Create low priority class for background Skills processing tasks.

    # Create low priority for Skills processing
kubectl apply -f skills-priority.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-low created
    Example 4: Critical Priority for Author Content

    Create critical priority class for Author content management.

    # Create critical priority for Author services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-critical
value: 2000
description: "Critical priority for Author content services"
globalDefault: false
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-author-critical created
    Example 5: Default Priority for Location Services

    Create default priority class for Location services across the cluster.

    # Create default priority for Location services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-default
value: 100
description: "Default priority for Location services"
globalDefault: true
preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-location-default created

    PriorityClassList

    PriorityClassList is a collection of priority classes. This list format is returned when querying multiple priority classes and provides a standardized way to handle collections of priority configurations in your Django application infrastructure.

    Syntax
    # List all PriorityClasses
kubectl get priorityclasses

# List PriorityClasses with labels
kubectl get priorityclasses -l app=anihpj

# Get PriorityClassList in JSON format
kubectl get priorityclasses -o json
    Parameters
    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "scheduling.k8s.io/v1" for PriorityClassList
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "PriorityClassList"
    • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resource version and continue token
    • items ([]PriorityClass, required): Array of PriorityClass objects
    Return Value
    • PriorityClassList object: Collection containing multiple PriorityClass resources
    • items array: List of individual PriorityClass objects with their priority values
    • metadata: List metadata for pagination and resource versioning
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All JobPost Priority Classes

    List all PriorityClasses used by JobPost application components.

    # List JobPost priority classes
kubectl get priorityclasses -l component=jobpost
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE anihpj-jobpost-high 1000 false 5m anihpj-jobpost-medium 500 false 4m
    Example 2: List User Service Priority Classes

    Get all priority classes for User authentication services.

    # List User priority classes with details
kubectl get priorityclasses -o wide --selector=service=user
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE anihpj-user-medium 750 false 3m anihpj-user-auth-high 1200 false 2m
    Example 3: List Skills Processing Priority Classes

    View priority classes for Skills processing and background tasks.

    # List Skills priority classes in JSON
kubectl get priorityclasses -l service=skills -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
    "anihpj-skills-low" "anihpj-skills-batch"
    Example 4: List Author Content Priority Classes

    Get priority classes sorted by value for Author content services.

    # List Author priority classes sorted by value
kubectl get priorityclasses --sort-by=.value
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE anihpj-location-default 100 true 6m anihpj-skills-low 200 false 5m anihpj-author-critical 2000 false 1m
    Example 5: List All Application Priority Classes

    Get comprehensive list of all anihpj application priority classes.

    # List all anihpj priority classes
kubectl get priorityclasses -l app=anihpj --show-labels
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE LABELS anihpj-location-default 100 true 6m app=anihpj,service=location anihpj-skills-low 200 false 5m app=anihpj,service=skills anihpj-user-medium 750 false 3m app=anihpj,service=user anihpj-jobpost-high 1000 false 5m app=anihpj,service=jobpost anihpj-author-critical 2000 false 1m app=anihpj,service=author

    Operations

    Operations Overview

    Read - GET single PriorityClass details
    List - GET multiple PriorityClasses
    Create - POST new PriorityClass
    Update - PUT existing PriorityClass
    Patch - PATCH PriorityClass fields
    Delete - DELETE PriorityClass resource
    Delete Collection - DELETE multiple PriorityClasses

    Operations allow you to interact with PriorityClass resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage priority levels for Django application components, ensuring proper scheduling precedence.

    Read PriorityClass

    Read the specified PriorityClass to get complete priority configuration and metadata for your Django application deployments.

    Syntax
    kubectl get priorityclass {name} -o yaml
kubectl get priorityclass {name} -o json
kubectl describe priorityclass {name}
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the PriorityClass
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (PriorityClass): OK - Returns the complete PriorityClass object
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Read JobPost High Priority Class

    Get complete JobPost priority class configuration in YAML format.

    # Get JobPost priority class details
kubectl get priorityclass anihpj-jobpost-high -o yaml
    apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1 kind: PriorityClass metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-09-14T12:00:00Z" name: anihpj-jobpost-high resourceVersion: "12345" uid: abc123-def456-ghi789 value: 1000 description: "High priority for JobPost API services" preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority globalDefault: false
    Example 2: Read User Medium Priority Details

    Get detailed description of User service priority class.

    # Describe User priority class
kubectl describe priorityclass anihpj-user-medium
    Name: anihpj-user-medium Labels: Annotations: Value: 750 GlobalDefault: false PreemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority Description: Medium priority for User authentication services Events:
    Example 3: Read Skills Low Priority JSON

    Get Skills priority class in JSON format for processing.

    # Get Skills priority class in JSON
kubectl get priorityclass anihpj-skills-low -o json | jq '.value'
    200
    Example 4: Read Author Critical Priority

    Get Author priority class with custom output format.

    # Get Author priority class with custom columns
kubectl get priorityclass anihpj-author-critical -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,VALUE:.value,DEFAULT:.globalDefault
    NAME VALUE DEFAULT anihpj-author-critical 2000 false
    Example 5: Read Location Default Priority

    Get Location default priority class configuration details.

    # Get Location default priority details
kubectl get priorityclass anihpj-location-default -o jsonpath='{.value}' && echo
    100

    List PriorityClasses

    List PriorityClasses across the cluster, supporting label selectors and field selectors for filtering Django application priority configurations.

    Syntax
    kubectl get priorityclasses
kubectl get priorityclasses -l app=anihpj
kubectl get priorityclasses --sort-by=.value
    Parameters
    • labelSelector (string): Filter by labels (e.g., "app=anihpj")
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter by fields
    • watch (boolean): Watch for changes
    Return Value
    • 200 (PriorityClassList): OK - Returns list of PriorityClass objects
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All JobPost Priority Classes

    List all PriorityClasses for JobPost services with label filtering.

    # List JobPost priority classes
kubectl get priorityclasses -l service=jobpost
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE anihpj-jobpost-high 1000 false 10m anihpj-jobpost-medium 500 false 8m
    Example 2: List User Priority Classes

    List User authentication priority classes with detailed output.

    # List User priority classes
kubectl get priorityclasses -l service=user -o wide
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE anihpj-user-medium 750 false 9m anihpj-user-auth-high 1200 false 7m
    Example 3: List Skills Priority Classes

    List Skills processing priority classes showing labels.

    # List Skills priority classes with labels
kubectl get priorityclasses -l service=skills --show-labels
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE LABELS anihpj-skills-low 200 false 12m app=anihpj,service=skills anihpj-skills-batch 150 false 10m app=anihpj,service=skills,type=batch
    Example 4: List Author Priority Classes Sorted

    List Author content priority classes sorted by value.

    # List Author priority classes sorted by value
kubectl get priorityclasses -l service=author --sort-by=.value
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE anihpj-author-medium 800 false 6m anihpj-author-critical 2000 false 4m
    Example 5: List All Application Priority Classes

    List all PriorityClasses for the entire anihpj application.

    # List all anihpj priority classes
kubectl get priorityclasses -l app=anihpj
    NAME VALUE GLOBAL-DEFAULT AGE anihpj-location-default 100 true 15m anihpj-skills-low 200 false 12m anihpj-user-medium 750 false 9m anihpj-jobpost-high 1000 false 10m anihpj-author-critical 2000 false 4m

    Create PriorityClass

    Create a new PriorityClass from specification to define priority levels for Django application components.

    Syntax
    kubectl create -f priorityclass.yaml
kubectl apply -f priorityclass.yaml
    Parameters
    • body (PriorityClass, required): Complete PriorityClass specification
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without creation
    • fieldManager (string): Field management identifier
    Return Value
    • 200 (PriorityClass): OK - PriorityClass successfully created
    • 201 (PriorityClass): Created - PriorityClass created
    • 202 (PriorityClass): Accepted - Creation in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Create JobPost Critical Priority

    Create a new critical priority class for JobPost processing services.

    # Create JobPost critical priority class
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-critical
value: 1500
description: "Critical priority for JobPost processing"
preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-critical created
    Example 2: Create User Auth Priority

    Create User authentication priority class with specific preemption policy.

    # Create User auth priority class
kubectl create -f user-auth-priority.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-user-auth created
    Example 3: Create Skills Batch Priority

    Create Skills batch processing priority class with low value.

    # Create Skills batch priority class
kubectl apply -f skills-batch-priority.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-batch created
    Example 4: Create Author Content Priority

    Create Author content priority class with medium value.

    # Create Author content priority class
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-content
value: 800
description: "Priority for Author content management"
globalDefault: false
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-author-content created
    Example 5: Create Location Service Priority

    Create Location service priority class with preemption settings.

    # Create Location service priority class
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-service
value: 600
description: "Priority for Location services"
preemptionPolicy: Never
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-location-service created

    Update PriorityClass

    Replace the specified PriorityClass with updated configuration to modify priority values for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl replace -f updated-priorityclass.yaml
kubectl apply -f updated-priorityclass.yaml
    Parameters
    • body (PriorityClass, required): Complete updated PriorityClass specification
    • name (string, required): Name of the PriorityClass to update
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without update
    Return Value
    • 200 (PriorityClass): OK - PriorityClass successfully updated
    • 201 (PriorityClass): Created - PriorityClass was created
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Update JobPost Priority Value

    Update JobPost priority class with increased priority value.

    # Update JobPost priority class value
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-high
value: 1200
description: "Updated high priority for JobPost API services"
preemptionPolicy: PreemptLowerPriority
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-high configured
    Example 2: Update User Priority Description

    Update User priority class with modified description.

    # Update User priority class description
kubectl replace -f updated-user-priority.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-user-medium replaced
    Example 3: Update Skills Priority Policy

    Update Skills priority class with different preemption policy.

    # Update Skills priority preemption policy
kubectl apply -f skills-priority-updated.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-low configured
    Example 4: Update Author Priority to Global Default

    Update Author priority class to be the global default.

    # Update Author priority to global default
kubectl replace -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-critical
value: 2000
description: "Critical priority for Author content services - Global Default"
globalDefault: true
EOF
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-author-critical replaced
    Example 5: Update Location Priority Configuration

    Update Location priority class with comprehensive configuration changes.

    # Update Location priority configuration
kubectl apply -f location-priority-updated.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-location-default configured

    Patch PriorityClass

    Partially update the specified PriorityClass with patch data to modify specific fields without replacing the entire priority class.

    Syntax
    kubectl patch priorityclass {name} -p '{patch-data}'
kubectl patch priorityclass {name} --type='json' -p='[patch-operations]'
    Parameters
    • body (Patch, required): Patch data in specified format
    • name (string, required): Name of the PriorityClass to patch
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without patching
    Return Value
    • 200 (PriorityClass): OK - PriorityClass successfully patched
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Patch JobPost Priority Value

    Patch JobPost priority class to update only the priority value.

    # Patch JobPost priority value
kubectl patch priorityclass anihpj-jobpost-high -p '{"value": 1300}'
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-high patched
    Example 2: Patch User Priority Description

    Update User priority class description using JSON patch.

    # Patch User priority description
kubectl patch priorityclass anihpj-user-medium --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/description", "value": "Updated medium priority for User services"}]'
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-user-medium patched
    Example 3: Patch Skills Preemption Policy

    Update Skills priority class preemption policy using strategic merge.

    # Patch Skills preemption policy
kubectl patch priorityclass anihpj-skills-low -p '{"preemptionPolicy": "Never"}'
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-low patched
    Example 4: Patch Author Global Default

    Change Author priority class global default setting.

    # Patch Author global default
kubectl patch priorityclass anihpj-author-critical --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/globalDefault", "value": false}]'
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-author-critical patched
    Example 5: Patch Location Priority Multiple Fields

    Update multiple fields in Location priority class simultaneously.

    # Patch Location priority multiple fields
kubectl patch priorityclass anihpj-location-default -p '{
  "value": 150,
  "description": "Updated default priority for Location services",
  "preemptionPolicy": "PreemptLowerPriority"
}'
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io/anihpj-location-default patched

    Delete PriorityClass

    Delete the specified PriorityClass to remove priority definitions that are no longer needed for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete priorityclass {name}
kubectl delete -f priorityclass.yaml
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the PriorityClass to delete
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - PriorityClass successfully deleted
    • 202 (Status): Accepted - Deletion in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete JobPost Priority

    Delete obsolete JobPost priority class.

    # Delete JobPost priority class
kubectl delete priorityclass anihpj-jobpost-medium
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-medium" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Priority with Confirmation

    Delete User priority class with dry-run verification.

    # Verify deletion with dry-run first
kubectl delete priorityclass anihpj-user-auth --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete priorityclass anihpj-user-auth
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-user-auth" deleted (dry run) priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-user-auth" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Priority by File

    Delete Skills priority class using YAML file.

    # Delete Skills priority by file
kubectl delete -f skills-batch-priority.yaml
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-batch" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Priority with Grace Period

    Delete Author priority class with custom grace period.

    # Delete Author priority with grace period
kubectl delete priorityclass anihpj-author-content --grace-period=30
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-author-content" deleted
    Example 5: Delete Location Priority with Wait

    Delete Location priority class and wait for completion.

    # Delete Location priority with wait
kubectl delete priorityclass anihpj-location-service --wait=true
# Verify deletion
kubectl get priorityclass anihpj-location-service
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-location-service" deleted Error from server (NotFound): priorityclasses.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-location-service" not found

    Delete Collection of PriorityClasses

    Delete multiple PriorityClasses based on label selectors to clean up related priority resources for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete priorityclasses -l {label-selector}
kubectl delete priorityclasses --all
    Parameters
    • labelSelector (string): Filter priority classes by labels
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter priority classes by fields
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - Collection deletion successful
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete All JobPost Priority Classes

    Delete all PriorityClasses related to JobPost services.

    # Delete all JobPost priority classes
kubectl delete priorityclasses -l service=jobpost
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-high" deleted priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-critical" deleted
