    Example 2: Delete User Priority Classes by Environment

    Delete User priority classes for specific environment.

    # Delete User test priority classes
kubectl delete priorityclasses -l service=user,env=test
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-user-test" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Development Priority Classes

    Delete Skills priority classes in development environment.

    # Delete Skills dev priority classes
kubectl delete priorityclasses -l service=skills,env=development
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-dev" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Priority Classes with Dry Run

    Preview Author priority class deletion before execution.

    # Preview Author priority deletion
kubectl delete priorityclasses -l service=author --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete priorityclasses -l service=author
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-author-critical" deleted (dry run) priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-author-content" deleted (dry run) priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-author-critical" deleted priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-author-content" deleted
    Example 5: Delete All Application Priority Classes

    Delete all PriorityClasses for the entire anihpj application.

    # Delete all anihpj priority classes
kubectl delete priorityclasses -l app=anihpj
# Verify deletion
kubectl get priorityclasses -l app=anihpj
    priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-location-default" deleted priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-low" deleted priorityclass.scheduling.k8s.io "anihpj-user-medium" deleted No resources found

    DeviceTaintRule v1alpha3

    DeviceTaintRule

    DeviceTaintRule Overview

    DeviceTaintRule - Device taint rule for pod scheduling
    DeviceTaintRuleSpec - Device selector and taint specification
    DeviceTaintRuleList - Collection of device taint rules
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: DeviceTaintRule v1alpha3 API | API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3

    DeviceTaintRule describes when to taint a device in a node for pod scheduling constraints.

    Full Description

    DeviceTaintRule is a Kubernetes alpha-level resource that defines rules for automatically tainting devices in nodes based on device properties and conditions. When developing your anihpj Django application, DeviceTaintRules enable you to control which devices are available for scheduling pods that require specific hardware capabilities, such as GPU-accelerated JobPost processing, AI-driven Skills analysis, or specialized hardware for User data processing.

    This is particularly valuable in Django deployments where different services (Skills processing, User authentication, Author content management, Location services, and JobPost operations) require specific device types or need to avoid certain device conditions. DeviceTaintRules automatically apply taints to devices that match specified criteria, preventing pods from being scheduled on inappropriate hardware.

    Syntax

    # Create a DeviceTaintRule from YAML file
kubectl create -f devicetaintrule.yaml

# List all DeviceTaintRules
kubectl get devicetaintrules

# Get detailed information about a specific DeviceTaintRule
kubectl describe devicetaintrule {name}

# Get DeviceTaintRule in YAML format
kubectl get devicetaintrule {name} -o yaml

# Delete a specific DeviceTaintRule
kubectl delete devicetaintrule {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-gpu-fault-taint
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: device.driver == "nvidia.com/gpu"
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/gpu-fault
    value: "true"
    effect: NoSchedule

    Parameters

    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3" for DeviceTaintRule
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "DeviceTaintRule"
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata including name and labels
    • spec (DeviceTaintRuleSpec): Specification of device selector and taint configuration

    Return Value

    • DeviceTaintRule object: Complete device taint rule specification with metadata and device selector
    • metadata: Object metadata including creation timestamp and resource version
    • spec: Device selector criteria and taint configuration for matching devices

    5 Django Service Examples

    Example 1: Taint Faulty GPU Devices for JobPost Processing

    Create DeviceTaintRule to taint faulty GPU devices used by JobPost AI processing.

    # Create GPU fault taint rule for JobPost services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.driver == "nvidia.com/gpu" && device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] < "8Gi"'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/jobpost-gpu-insufficient
    value: "memory-low"
    effect: NoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault created
    Example 2: Taint High-Temperature Devices for User Services

    Create DeviceTaintRule to taint overheating devices for User authentication services.

    # Create temperature-based taint rule for User services
kubectl create -f user-device-temp-taint.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-temp-taint created
    Example 3: Taint Outdated Network Devices for Skills Processing

    Create DeviceTaintRule to taint network devices with old firmware for Skills services.

    # Create network device firmware taint rule
kubectl apply -f skills-network-taint.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-network-taint created
    Example 4: Taint Power-Constrained Devices for Author Content

    Create DeviceTaintRule to taint power-limited devices for Author content processing.

    # Create power constraint taint rule for Author services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-power-constrained
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.attributes["power.available"] < "100W"'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/author-power-limited
    value: "insufficient-power"
    effect: NoExecute
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-power-constrained created
    Example 5: Taint Legacy Storage Devices for Location Services

    Create DeviceTaintRule to taint legacy storage devices for Location data services.

    # Create legacy storage taint rule for Location services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-legacy-storage
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.driver == "storage.local/disk" && device.attributes["storage.type"] == "HDD"'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/location-storage-legacy
    value: "hdd-performance"
    effect: NoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-legacy-storage created

    DeviceTaintRuleSpec

    DeviceTaintRuleSpec defines the specification for device selection criteria and taint configuration. This specification enables precise control over which devices get tainted based on CEL expressions and device attributes, allowing your Django application to avoid problematic hardware.

    Syntax
    # View DeviceTaintRuleSpec structure
kubectl get devicetaintrule {name} -o jsonpath='{.spec}'

# Validate DeviceTaintRuleSpec
kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f devicetaintrule.yaml
    Parameters
    • deviceSelector ([]DeviceSelector, required): Array of device selector expressions using CEL
    • taint (Taint, required): Taint configuration to apply to matching devices
    • cel (string): CEL expression for device selection criteria
    Return Value
    • DeviceTaintRuleSpec object: Complete specification with device selectors and taint configuration
    • deviceSelector array: Collection of CEL-based device selection criteria
    • taint object: Taint configuration including key, value, and effect
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: CEL Expression for JobPost GPU Selection

    DeviceTaintRuleSpec with complex CEL expression for JobPost GPU device selection.

    # Create JobPost GPU CEL selector
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-advanced
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: |
        device.driver == "nvidia.com/gpu" && 
        device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "16Gi" &&
        device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.compute"] >= "7.5" &&
        !device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.error"]
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/jobpost-gpu-premium
    value: "high-performance"
    effect: NoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-advanced created
    Example 2: Multi-Condition Device Selection for User Services

    DeviceTaintRuleSpec with multiple device selectors for User service hardware.

    # Create multi-condition User device selector
kubectl create -f user-multi-device-taint.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-multi-device created
    Example 3: Network Device Specifications for Skills Processing

    DeviceTaintRuleSpec targeting network devices for Skills data processing.

    # Create Skills network device specification
kubectl apply -f skills-network-spec.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-network-spec created
    Example 4: Storage Performance Criteria for Author Content

    DeviceTaintRuleSpec with storage performance CEL expressions for Author services.

    # Create Author storage performance spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-storage-perf
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: |
        device.driver == "storage.ssd/nvme" &&
        device.attributes["storage.iops"] < "10000" ||
        device.attributes["storage.latency"] > "1ms"
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/author-storage-slow
    value: "performance-degraded"
    effect: PreferNoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-storage-perf created
    Example 5: Comprehensive Device Health Check for Location Services

    DeviceTaintRuleSpec with comprehensive health checks for Location service devices.

    # Create Location device health specification
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-device-health
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: |
        device.attributes["health.status"] != "healthy" ||
        device.attributes["temperature"] > "80C" ||
        device.attributes["utilization"] > "90%" ||
        device.attributes["error.count"] > "5"
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/location-device-unhealthy
    value: "health-check-failed"
    effect: NoExecute
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-device-health created

    DeviceTaintRuleList

    DeviceTaintRuleList is a collection of device taint rules. This list format is returned when querying multiple device taint rules and provides a standardized way to handle collections of device taint configurations in your Django application infrastructure.

    Syntax
    # List all DeviceTaintRules
kubectl get devicetaintrules

# List DeviceTaintRules with labels
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l app=anihpj

# Get DeviceTaintRuleList in JSON format
kubectl get devicetaintrules -o json
    Parameters
    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3" for DeviceTaintRuleList
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "DeviceTaintRuleList"
    • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resource version and continue token
    • items ([]DeviceTaintRule, required): Array of DeviceTaintRule objects
    Return Value
    • DeviceTaintRuleList object: Collection containing multiple DeviceTaintRule resources
    • items array: List of individual DeviceTaintRule objects with their device selectors and taints
    • metadata: List metadata for pagination and resource versioning
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All JobPost Device Taint Rules

    List all DeviceTaintRules used by JobPost application components.

    # List JobPost device taint rules
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l component=jobpost
    NAME AGE anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault 5m anihpj-jobpost-gpu-advanced 3m
    Example 2: List User Service Device Taint Rules

    Get all device taint rules for User authentication services.

    # List User device taint rules with details
kubectl get devicetaintrules -o wide --selector=service=user
    NAME AGE DEVICE-SELECTOR anihpj-user-temp-taint 4m device.attributes["temperature"] > "70C" anihpj-user-multi-device 2m Multiple selectors
    Example 3: List Skills Processing Device Taint Rules

    View device taint rules for Skills processing and background tasks.

    # List Skills device taint rules in JSON
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l service=skills -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
    "anihpj-skills-network-taint" "anihpj-skills-network-spec"
    Example 4: List Author Content Device Taint Rules

    Get device taint rules sorted by creation time for Author content services.

    # List Author device taint rules sorted by creation time
kubectl get devicetaintrules --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME AGE anihpj-author-power-constrained 6m anihpj-author-storage-perf 2m
    Example 5: List All Application Device Taint Rules

    Get comprehensive list of all anihpj application device taint rules.

    # List all anihpj device taint rules
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l app=anihpj --show-labels
    NAME AGE LABELS anihpj-location-legacy-storage 7m app=anihpj,service=location anihpj-location-device-health 1m app=anihpj,service=location anihpj-skills-network-taint 5m app=anihpj,service=skills anihpj-user-temp-taint 4m app=anihpj,service=user anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault 5m app=anihpj,service=jobpost anihpj-author-power-constrained 6m app=anihpj,service=author

    Operations

    Operations Overview

    Read - GET single DeviceTaintRule details
    List - GET multiple DeviceTaintRules
    Create - POST new DeviceTaintRule
    Update - PUT existing DeviceTaintRule
    Patch - PATCH DeviceTaintRule fields
    Delete - DELETE DeviceTaintRule resource
    Delete Collection - DELETE multiple DeviceTaintRules

    Operations allow you to interact with DeviceTaintRule resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage device tainting rules for Django application components, ensuring proper device availability and scheduling constraints.

    Read DeviceTaintRule

    Read the specified DeviceTaintRule to get complete device taint configuration and metadata for your Django application deployments.

    Syntax
    kubectl get devicetaintrule {name} -o yaml
kubectl get devicetaintrule {name} -o json
kubectl describe devicetaintrule {name}
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the DeviceTaintRule
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (DeviceTaintRule): OK - Returns the complete DeviceTaintRule object
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Read JobPost GPU Taint Rule

    Get complete JobPost GPU device taint rule configuration in YAML format.

    # Get JobPost GPU taint rule details
kubectl get devicetaintrule anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault -o yaml
    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3 kind: DeviceTaintRule metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-09-14T12:00:00Z" name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault resourceVersion: "12345" uid: abc123-def456-ghi789 spec: deviceSelector: - cel: 'device.driver == "nvidia.com/gpu" && device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] < "8Gi"' taint: key: anihpj.com/jobpost-gpu-insufficient value: "memory-low" effect: NoSchedule
    Example 2: Read User Temperature Taint Details

    Get detailed description of User service temperature taint rule.

    # Describe User temperature taint rule
kubectl describe devicetaintrule anihpj-user-temp-taint
    Name: anihpj-user-temp-taint Namespace: Labels: service=user Annotations: API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3 Kind: DeviceTaintRule Spec: Device Selector: Cel: device.attributes["temperature"] > "70C" Taint: Effect: NoSchedule Key: anihpj.com/user-temperature-high Value: overheating Events:
    Example 3: Read Skills Network Taint Rule JSON

    Get Skills network taint rule in JSON format for processing.

    # Get Skills network taint rule in JSON
kubectl get devicetaintrule anihpj-skills-network-taint -o json | jq '.spec.taint.key'
    "anihpj.com/skills-network-outdated"
    Example 4: Read Author Power Taint Rule

    Get Author power constraint taint rule with custom output format.

    # Get Author power taint rule with custom columns
kubectl get devicetaintrule anihpj-author-power-constrained -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,TAINT-KEY:.spec.taint.key,EFFECT:.spec.taint.effect
    NAME TAINT-KEY EFFECT anihpj-author-power-constrained anihpj.com/author-power-limited NoExecute
    Example 5: Read Location Storage Taint Rule

    Get Location storage taint rule CEL expression details.

    # Get Location storage taint rule CEL expression
kubectl get devicetaintrule anihpj-location-legacy-storage -o jsonpath='{.spec.deviceSelector[0].cel}' && echo
    device.driver == "storage.local/disk" && device.attributes["storage.type"] == "HDD"

    List DeviceTaintRules

    List DeviceTaintRules across the cluster, supporting label selectors and field selectors for filtering Django application device taint configurations.

    Syntax
    kubectl get devicetaintrules
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l app=anihpj
kubectl get devicetaintrules --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    Parameters
    • labelSelector (string): Filter by labels (e.g., "app=anihpj")
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter by fields
    • watch (boolean): Watch for changes
    Return Value
    • 200 (DeviceTaintRuleList): OK - Returns list of DeviceTaintRule objects
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All JobPost Device Taint Rules

    List all DeviceTaintRules for JobPost services with label filtering.

    # List JobPost device taint rules
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l service=jobpost
    NAME AGE anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault 10m anihpj-jobpost-gpu-advanced 8m
    Example 2: List User Device Taint Rules

    List User authentication device taint rules with detailed output.

    # List User device taint rules
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l service=user -o wide
    NAME AGE DEVICE-SELECTOR anihpj-user-temp-taint 9m device.attributes["temperature"] > "70C" anihpj-user-multi-device 7m Multiple device selectors
    Example 3: List Skills Device Taint Rules

    List Skills processing device taint rules showing labels.

    # List Skills device taint rules with labels
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l service=skills --show-labels
    NAME AGE LABELS anihpj-skills-network-taint 12m app=anihpj,service=skills anihpj-skills-network-spec 10m app=anihpj,service=skills,type=network
    Example 4: List Author Device Taint Rules Sorted

    List Author content device taint rules sorted by creation time.

    # List Author device taint rules sorted by creation time
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l service=author --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME AGE anihpj-author-power-constrained 16m anihpj-author-storage-perf 12m
    Example 5: List All Application Device Taint Rules

    List all DeviceTaintRules for the entire anihpj application.

    # List all anihpj device taint rules
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l app=anihpj
    NAME AGE anihpj-location-legacy-storage 17m anihpj-location-device-health 11m anihpj-skills-network-taint 12m anihpj-user-temp-taint 9m anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault 10m anihpj-author-power-constrained 16m

    Create DeviceTaintRule

    Create a new DeviceTaintRule from specification to define device tainting rules for Django application components.

    Syntax
    kubectl create -f devicetaintrule.yaml
kubectl apply -f devicetaintrule.yaml
    Parameters
    • body (DeviceTaintRule, required): Complete DeviceTaintRule specification
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without creation
    • fieldManager (string): Field management identifier
    Return Value
    • 200 (DeviceTaintRule): OK - DeviceTaintRule successfully created
    • 201 (DeviceTaintRule): Created - DeviceTaintRule created
    • 202 (DeviceTaintRule): Accepted - Creation in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Create JobPost GPU Performance Taint Rule

    Create a new GPU performance taint rule for JobPost AI processing services.

    # Create JobPost GPU performance taint rule
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-performance
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.driver == "nvidia.com/gpu" && device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.performance"] < "high"'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/jobpost-gpu-slow
    value: "performance-limited"
    effect: PreferNoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-performance created
    Example 2: Create User Network Security Taint Rule

    Create User authentication network security taint rule with specific conditions.

    # Create User network security taint rule
kubectl create -f user-network-security-taint.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-network-security created
    Example 3: Create Skills Memory Constraint Taint Rule

    Create Skills processing memory constraint taint rule for insufficient memory devices.

    # Create Skills memory constraint taint rule
kubectl apply -f skills-memory-constraint-taint.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-memory-constraint created
    Example 4: Create Author CPU Throttling Taint Rule

    Create Author content CPU throttling taint rule for performance monitoring.

    # Create Author CPU throttling taint rule
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-cpu-throttling
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.attributes["cpu.throttling"] == "true" || device.attributes["cpu.temperature"] > "85C"'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/author-cpu-throttled
    value: "thermal-throttling"
    effect: NoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-cpu-throttling created
    Example 5: Create Location Disk Health Taint Rule

    Create Location service disk health taint rule with health monitoring.

    # Create Location disk health taint rule
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-disk-health
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.driver == "storage.local/disk" && (device.attributes["smart.status"] != "PASSED" || device.attributes["bad.sectors"] > "0")'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/location-disk-failing
    value: "smart-failure"
    effect: NoExecute
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-disk-health created

    Update DeviceTaintRule

    Replace the specified DeviceTaintRule with updated configuration to modify device tainting criteria for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl replace -f updated-devicetaintrule.yaml
kubectl apply -f updated-devicetaintrule.yaml
    Parameters
    • body (DeviceTaintRule, required): Complete updated DeviceTaintRule specification
    • name (string, required): Name of the DeviceTaintRule to update
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without update
    Return Value
    • 200 (DeviceTaintRule): OK - DeviceTaintRule successfully updated
    • 201 (DeviceTaintRule): Created - DeviceTaintRule was created
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Update JobPost GPU Memory Threshold

    Update JobPost GPU taint rule with modified memory threshold criteria.

    # Update JobPost GPU memory threshold
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.driver == "nvidia.com/gpu" && device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] < "12Gi"'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/jobpost-gpu-insufficient
    value: "memory-very-low"
    effect: NoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault configured
    Example 2: Update User Temperature Threshold

    Update User temperature taint rule with different temperature threshold.

    # Update User temperature threshold
kubectl replace -f updated-user-temp-taint.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-temp-taint replaced
    Example 3: Update Skills Network Criteria

    Update Skills network taint rule with enhanced network criteria.

    # Update Skills network criteria
kubectl apply -f skills-network-updated.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-network-taint configured
    Example 4: Update Author Power Policy

    Update Author power constraint taint rule with modified power policy.

    # Update Author power policy
kubectl replace -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1alpha3
kind: DeviceTaintRule
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-power-constrained
spec:
  deviceSelector:
    - cel: 'device.attributes["power.available"] < "150W" || device.attributes["power.efficiency"] < "80%"'
  taint:
    key: anihpj.com/author-power-limited
    value: "insufficient-power-updated"
    effect: PreferNoSchedule
EOF
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-power-constrained replaced
    Example 5: Update Location Storage Configuration

    Update Location storage taint rule with comprehensive storage configuration changes.

    # Update Location storage configuration
kubectl apply -f location-storage-updated.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-legacy-storage configured

    Patch DeviceTaintRule

    Partially update the specified DeviceTaintRule with patch data to modify specific fields without replacing the entire device taint rule.

    Syntax
    kubectl patch devicetaintrule {name} -p '{patch-data}'
kubectl patch devicetaintrule {name} --type='json' -p='[patch-operations]'
    Parameters
    • body (Patch, required): Patch data in specified format
    • name (string, required): Name of the DeviceTaintRule to patch
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without patching
    Return Value
    • 200 (DeviceTaintRule): OK - DeviceTaintRule successfully patched
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Patch JobPost Taint Effect

    Patch JobPost GPU taint rule to update only the taint effect.

    # Patch JobPost taint effect
kubectl patch devicetaintrule anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault -p '{"spec":{"taint":{"effect":"PreferNoSchedule"}}}'
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault patched
    Example 2: Patch User Taint Value

    Update User temperature taint rule value using JSON patch.

    # Patch User taint value
kubectl patch devicetaintrule anihpj-user-temp-taint --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/taint/value", "value": "critically-overheating"}]'
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-temp-taint patched
    Example 3: Patch Skills CEL Expression

    Update Skills network taint rule CEL expression using strategic merge.

    # Patch Skills CEL expression
kubectl patch devicetaintrule anihpj-skills-network-taint -p '{"spec":{"deviceSelector":[{"cel":"device.driver == \"network.interface/ethernet\" && device.attributes[\"firmware.version\"] < \"2.0\""}]}}'
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-network-taint patched
    Example 4: Patch Author Taint Key

    Change Author power constraint taint rule key using JSON patch.

    # Patch Author taint key
kubectl patch devicetaintrule anihpj-author-power-constrained --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/taint/key", "value": "anihpj.com/author-power-critical"}]'
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-power-constrained patched
    Example 5: Patch Location Multiple Taint Fields

    Update multiple taint fields in Location storage taint rule simultaneously.

    # Patch Location multiple taint fields
kubectl patch devicetaintrule anihpj-location-legacy-storage -p '{
  "spec": {
    "taint": {
      "key": "anihpj.com/location-storage-deprecated",
      "value": "legacy-hdd-performance",
      "effect": "NoExecute"
    }
  }
}'
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-legacy-storage patched

    Delete DeviceTaintRule

    Delete the specified DeviceTaintRule to remove device tainting rules that are no longer needed for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete devicetaintrule {name}
kubectl delete -f devicetaintrule.yaml
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the DeviceTaintRule to delete
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - DeviceTaintRule successfully deleted
    • 202 (Status): Accepted - Deletion in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete JobPost GPU Performance Taint

    Delete obsolete JobPost GPU performance taint rule.

    # Delete JobPost GPU performance taint rule
kubectl delete devicetaintrule anihpj-jobpost-gpu-performance
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-performance" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Network Security Taint with Confirmation

    Delete User network security taint rule with dry-run verification.

    # Verify deletion with dry-run first
kubectl delete devicetaintrule anihpj-user-network-security --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete devicetaintrule anihpj-user-network-security
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-network-security" deleted (dry run) devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-network-security" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Memory Taint by File

    Delete Skills memory constraint taint rule using YAML file.

    # Delete Skills memory taint by file
kubectl delete -f skills-memory-constraint-taint.yaml
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-memory-constraint" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author CPU Taint with Grace Period

    Delete Author CPU throttling taint rule with custom grace period.

    # Delete Author CPU taint with grace period
kubectl delete devicetaintrule anihpj-author-cpu-throttling --grace-period=30
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-cpu-throttling" deleted
    Example 5: Delete Location Disk Taint with Wait

    Delete Location disk health taint rule and wait for completion.

    # Delete Location disk taint with wait
kubectl delete devicetaintrule anihpj-location-disk-health --wait=true
# Verify deletion
kubectl get devicetaintrule anihpj-location-disk-health
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-disk-health" deleted Error from server (NotFound): devicetaintrules.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-disk-health" not found

    Delete Collection of DeviceTaintRules

    Delete multiple DeviceTaintRules based on label selectors to clean up related device taint resources for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete devicetaintrules -l {label-selector}
kubectl delete devicetaintrules --all
    Parameters
    • labelSelector (string): Filter device taint rules by labels
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter device taint rules by fields
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - Collection deletion successful
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete All JobPost Device Taint Rules

    Delete all DeviceTaintRules related to JobPost services.

    # Delete all JobPost device taint rules
kubectl delete devicetaintrules -l service=jobpost
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-fault" deleted devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-advanced" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Device Taint Rules by Environment

    Delete User device taint rules for specific environment.

    # Delete User test device taint rules
kubectl delete devicetaintrules -l service=user,env=test
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-test-temp" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Development Device Taint Rules

    Delete Skills device taint rules in development environment.

    # Delete Skills dev device taint rules
kubectl delete devicetaintrules -l service=skills,env=development
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-dev-network" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Device Taint Rules with Dry Run

    Preview Author device taint rule deletion before execution.

    # Preview Author device taint deletion
kubectl delete devicetaintrules -l service=author --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete devicetaintrules -l service=author
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-power-constrained" deleted (dry run) devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-storage-perf" deleted (dry run) devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-power-constrained" deleted devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-storage-perf" deleted
    Example 5: Delete All Application Device Taint Rules

    Delete all DeviceTaintRules for the entire anihpj application.

    # Delete all anihpj device taint rules
kubectl delete devicetaintrules -l app=anihpj
# Verify deletion
kubectl get devicetaintrules -l app=anihpj
    devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-legacy-storage" deleted devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-device-health" deleted devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-network-taint" deleted devicetaintrule.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-temp-taint" deleted No resources found

    ResourceClaim

    ResourceClaim

    ResourceClaim Overview

    ResourceClaim - Dynamic resource request for cluster devices
    ResourceClaimSpec - Resource claim specification and device requests
    ResourceClaimStatus - Allocation status and device tracking
    ResourceClaimList - Collection of resource claims
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: ResourceClaim v1 API | API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1

    ResourceClaim describes a request for access to resources in the cluster, for use by workloads.

    Full Description

    ResourceClaim is a Kubernetes resource that describes a request for access to specific devices or resources in the cluster for use by workloads. When developing your anihpj Django application, ResourceClaims enable dynamic allocation of specialized hardware like GPUs for JobPost AI processing, network accelerators for Skills analysis, or storage devices for User data management. This alpha-level feature requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.

    This is particularly useful in Django deployments where different services (Skills processing, User authentication, Author content management, Location services, and JobPost operations) require specific hardware resources that need to be dynamically allocated and managed throughout the pod lifecycle.

    Syntax

    # Create a ResourceClaim from YAML file
kubectl create -f resourceclaim.yaml

# List all ResourceClaims
kubectl get resourceclaims

# Get detailed information about a specific ResourceClaim
kubectl describe resourceclaim {name}

# Get ResourceClaim in YAML format
kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o yaml

# Delete a specific ResourceClaim
kubectl delete resourceclaim {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-gpu-claim
  namespace: anihpj-prod
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: gpu-request
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu
        count: 1
        selectors:
        - cel:
            expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "8Gi"'

    Parameters

    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1" for ResourceClaim
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ResourceClaim"
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata including name, namespace, and labels
    • spec (ResourceClaimSpec, required): Specification describing what resources are being requested
    • status (ResourceClaimStatus): Status describing allocation state and allocated resources

    Return Value

    • ResourceClaim object: Complete resource claim specification with metadata and allocation status
    • metadata: Object metadata including creation timestamp and resource version
    • spec: Device requests and configuration parameters for resource allocation
    • status: Allocation results and current resource binding state

    5 Django Service Examples

    Example 1: GPU Resource Claim for JobPost AI Processing

    Create ResourceClaim for GPU resources needed by JobPost AI analysis services.

    # Create GPU resource claim for JobPost AI
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim
  namespace: anihpj-prod
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: ai-gpu
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-high-memory
        count: 2
        selectors:
        - cel:
            expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "16Gi" && device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.compute"] >= "8.0"'
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim created
    Example 2: Network Accelerator Claim for User Authentication

    Create ResourceClaim for network acceleration hardware for User authentication services.

    # Create network accelerator claim for User services
kubectl create -f user-network-claim.yaml
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-network-claim created
    Example 3: Storage Device Claim for Skills Data Processing

    Create ResourceClaim for high-performance storage devices for Skills processing.

    # Create storage device claim for Skills processing
kubectl apply -f skills-storage-claim.yaml
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-storage-claim created
    Example 4: FPGA Resource Claim for Author Content Analysis

    Create ResourceClaim for FPGA devices needed by Author content analysis.

    # Create FPGA resource claim for Author services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-fpga-claim
  namespace: anihpj-prod
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: content-fpga
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: intel-fpga
        count: 1
        capacity:
          requests:
            intel.com/fpga.dsp: "100"
            intel.com/fpga.memory: "8Gi"
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-fpga-claim created
    Example 5: Multi-Device Claim for Location Services

    Create ResourceClaim with multiple device types for Location geospatial services.

    # Create multi-device claim for Location services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-multi-claim
  namespace: anihpj-prod
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: gps-accelerator
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: gps-hardware
        count: 1
    - name: mapping-gpu
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu
        count: 1
        selectors:
        - cel:
            expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "12Gi"'
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-multi-claim created

    ResourceClaimSpec

    ResourceClaimSpec defines what is being requested in a ResourceClaim and how to configure it. The specification is immutable once created and defines device requests, constraints, and configuration parameters for dynamic resource allocation.

    Syntax
    # View ResourceClaimSpec structure
kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o jsonpath='{.spec}'

# Validate ResourceClaimSpec
kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f resourceclaim.yaml
    Parameters
    • devices (DeviceClaim): Defines how to request devices with specific criteria
    • devices.requests ([]DeviceRequest, required): Individual requests for distinct devices
    • devices.constraints ([]DeviceConstraint): Constraints that must be satisfied by allocated devices
    • devices.config ([]DeviceClaimConfiguration): Configuration parameters for drivers
    Return Value
    • ResourceClaimSpec object: Complete specification with device requests and constraints
    • devices: Device claim configuration including requests and selectors
    • constraints: Device allocation constraints and requirements
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Complex Device Request Spec for JobPost Processing

    ResourceClaimSpec with advanced device selection and constraints for JobPost AI processing.

    # Create advanced JobPost device claim spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-advanced-claim
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: primary-gpu
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-enterprise
        count: 1
        selectors:
        - cel:
            expression: |
              device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "24Gi" &&
              device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.compute"] >= "8.6" &&
              device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.nvlink"] == "true"
        capacity:
          requests:
            nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "20Gi"
    constraints:
    - matchAttribute: "numa.node"
      requests: ["primary-gpu"]
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-advanced-claim created
    Example 2: User Service Device Constraints

    ResourceClaimSpec with device constraints for User authentication hardware.

    # Create User service device constraints
kubectl create -f user-device-constraints.yaml
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-constrained-claim created
    Example 3: Skills Processing Multiple Device Requests

    ResourceClaimSpec with multiple device requests for Skills data processing.

    # Create Skills multi-device request spec
kubectl apply -f skills-multi-device-spec.yaml
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-multi-claim created
    Example 4: Author Content Device Configuration

    ResourceClaimSpec with device configuration parameters for Author content services.

    # Create Author device configuration spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-config-claim
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: content-processor
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: fpga-content-analyzer
        count: 1
    config:
    - opaque:
        driver: content.fpga.example.com
        parameters:
          runtime.Object:
            kind: ContentConfig
            apiVersion: v1
            processingMode: "high-quality"
            bufferSize: "2Gi"
      requests: ["content-processor"]
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-config-claim created
    Example 5: Location Service FirstAvailable Device Selection

    ResourceClaimSpec using FirstAvailable selection for Location geospatial processing.

    # Create Location FirstAvailable device spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-flexible-claim
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: geo-processor
      firstAvailable:
      - name: high-end-gpu
        deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-enterprise
        count: 1
        selectors:
        - cel:
            expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "16Gi"'
      - name: standard-gpu
        deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-standard
        count: 2
        selectors:
        - cel:
            expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "8Gi"'
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-flexible-claim created

    ResourceClaimStatus

    ResourceClaimStatus tracks whether the resource has been allocated and what the result of that allocation was. It provides information about allocated devices, their configuration, and current binding state.

    Syntax
    # View ResourceClaimStatus
kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Check allocation status
kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o jsonpath='{.status.allocation}'

# View allocated devices
kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o jsonpath='{.status.devices}'
    Parameters
    • allocation (AllocationResult): Set once the claim has been allocated successfully
    • reservedFor ([]ResourceClaimConsumerReference): Entities currently allowed to use the claim
    • devices ([]AllocatedDeviceStatus): Status of each allocated device as reported by drivers
    Return Value
    • ResourceClaimStatus object: Complete status with allocation and device information
    • allocation: Allocation results including device details and node selector
    • reservedFor: List of consumers that have reserved this claim
    • devices: Detailed status information for each allocated device
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Check JobPost GPU Allocation Status

    View allocation status for JobPost GPU resource claim.

    # Check JobPost GPU allocation status
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim -o jsonpath='{.status.allocation.devices.results[0]}' | jq .
    { "device": "gpu-0", "driver": "nvidia.com/gpu", "pool": "worker-node-1", "request": "ai-gpu", "consumedCapacity": { "nvidia.com/gpu.memory": "20Gi" } }
    Example 2: Monitor User Service Device Conditions

    Check device conditions for User authentication hardware allocation.

    # Monitor User device conditions
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-user-network-claim -o jsonpath='{.status.devices[0].conditions}' | jq .
    [ { "type": "Ready", "status": "True", "lastTransitionTime": "2024-09-14T12:30:00Z", "reason": "DeviceConfigured", "message": "Network accelerator successfully configured" } ]
    Example 3: Verify Skills Storage Claim Reservations

    Check which pods have reserved the Skills storage resource claim.

    # Check Skills claim reservations
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-skills-storage-claim -o jsonpath='{.status.reservedFor}' | jq .
    [ { "name": "skills-processor-pod-1", "resource": "pods", "uid": "abc123-def456-ghi789", "apiGroup": "" } ]
    Example 4: View Author FPGA Device Status

    Get detailed device status for Author content processing FPGA.

    # View Author FPGA device status
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-author-fpga-claim -o yaml | grep -A 20 "devices:"
    devices: - device: fpga-card-1 driver: intel.com/fpga pool: compute-node-3 conditions: - type: Ready status: "True" lastTransitionTime: "2024-09-14T12:35:00Z" reason: "FPGAProgrammed" message: "FPGA successfully programmed with content analysis bitstream" data: processingUnits: 1024 memoryBandwidth: "500GB/s"
    Example 5: Location Service Multi-Device Allocation

    Check allocation results for Location service multi-device claim.

    # Check Location multi-device allocation
kubectl describe resourceclaim anihpj-location-multi-claim
    Name: anihpj-location-multi-claim Namespace: anihpj-prod API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1 Kind: ResourceClaim Status: Allocation: Devices: Results: Device: gps-card-0 Driver: location.gps.example.com Pool: node-1 Request: gps-accelerator Device: gpu-2 Driver: nvidia.com/gpu Pool: node-1 Request: mapping-gpu Consumed Capacity: nvidia.com/gpu.memory: 12Gi Reserved For: Name: location-service-pod Resource: pods UID: xyz789-abc123-def456

    ResourceClaimList

    ResourceClaimList is a collection of resource claims. This list format is returned when querying multiple resource claims and provides a standardized way to handle collections of dynamic resource allocations in your Django application infrastructure.

    Syntax
    # List all ResourceClaims
kubectl get resourceclaims

# List ResourceClaims with labels
kubectl get resourceclaims -l app=anihpj

# Get ResourceClaimList in JSON format
kubectl get resourceclaims -o json
    Parameters
    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1" for ResourceClaimList
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ResourceClaimList"
    • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resource version and continue token
    • items ([]ResourceClaim, required): Array of ResourceClaim objects
    Return Value
    • ResourceClaimList object: Collection containing multiple ResourceClaim resources
    • items array: List of individual ResourceClaim objects with their specifications and status
    • metadata: List metadata for pagination and resource versioning
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All JobPost Resource Claims

    List all ResourceClaims used by JobPost application components.

    # List JobPost resource claims
kubectl get resourceclaims -l component=jobpost
    NAME AGE READY anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim 5m True anihpj-jobpost-advanced-claim 3m True
    Example 2: List User Service Resource Claims

    Get all resource claims for User authentication services.

    # List User resource claims with details
kubectl get resourceclaims -l service=user -o wide
    NAME AGE READY DEVICES anihpj-user-network-claim 4m True network-accelerator-1 anihpj-user-constrained-claim 2m True crypto-card-0
    Example 3: List Skills Processing Resource Claims

    View resource claims for Skills processing and background tasks.

    # List Skills resource claims in JSON
kubectl get resourceclaims -l service=skills -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
    "anihpj-skills-storage-claim" "anihpj-skills-multi-claim"
    Example 4: List Author Content Resource Claims

    Get resource claims sorted by creation time for Author content services.

    # List Author resource claims sorted by creation time
kubectl get resourceclaims -l service=author --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME AGE anihpj-author-fpga-claim 6m anihpj-author-config-claim 4m
    Example 5: List All Application Resource Claims

    Get comprehensive list of all anihpj application resource claims.

    # List all anihpj resource claims
kubectl get resourceclaims -l app=anihpj --show-labels
    NAME AGE READY LABELS anihpj-location-multi-claim 7m True app=anihpj,service=location anihpj-location-flexible-claim 5m True app=anihpj,service=location anihpj-skills-storage-claim 6m True app=anihpj,service=skills anihpj-user-network-claim 4m True app=anihpj,service=user anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim 5m True app=anihpj,service=jobpost anihpj-author-fpga-claim 6m True app=anihpj,service=author

    Operations

    Operations Overview

    Read - GET single ResourceClaim details
    Read Status - GET ResourceClaim status
    List - GET multiple ResourceClaims (namespaced)
    List All - GET all ResourceClaims (cluster-wide)
    Create - POST new ResourceClaim
    Update - PUT existing ResourceClaim
    Update Status - PUT ResourceClaim status
    Patch - PATCH ResourceClaim fields
    Patch Status - PATCH ResourceClaim status
    Delete - DELETE ResourceClaim resource
    Delete Collection - DELETE multiple ResourceClaims

    Operations allow you to interact with ResourceClaim resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage dynamic resource allocation for Django application components, ensuring proper device allocation and availability.

    Read ResourceClaim

    Read the specified ResourceClaim to get complete resource allocation configuration and metadata for your Django application deployments.

    Syntax
    kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o yaml
kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o json
kubectl describe resourceclaim {name}
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the ResourceClaim
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace of the ResourceClaim
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceClaim): OK - Returns the complete ResourceClaim object
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Read JobPost GPU Resource Claim

    Get complete JobPost GPU resource claim configuration in YAML format.

    # Get JobPost GPU claim details
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim -n anihpj-prod -o yaml
    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1 kind: ResourceClaim metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-09-14T12:00:00Z" name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim namespace: anihpj-prod resourceVersion: "12345" uid: abc123-def456-ghi789 spec: devices: requests: - name: ai-gpu exactly: deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-high-memory count: 2 status: allocation: devices: results: - device: gpu-0 driver: nvidia.com/gpu pool: worker-node-1 request: ai-gpu
    Example 2: Read User Network Resource Claim Details

    Get detailed description of User service network resource claim.

    # Describe User network resource claim
kubectl describe resourceclaim anihpj-user-network-claim -n anihpj-prod
    Name: anihpj-user-network-claim Namespace: anihpj-prod Labels: service=user Annotations: API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1 Kind: ResourceClaim Spec: Devices: Requests: Name: network-accelerator Exactly: Device Class Name: intel-network-accelerator Count: 1 Status: Allocation: Devices: Results: Device: nic-0 Driver: intel.com/network Pool: node-2 Request: network-accelerator Events:
    Example 3: Read Skills Storage Claim JSON

    Get Skills storage resource claim in JSON format for processing.

    # Get Skills storage claim in JSON
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-skills-storage-claim -n anihpj-prod -o json | jq '.status.allocation.devices.results[0].device'
    "nvme-0"
    Example 4: Read Author FPGA Resource Claim

    Get Author FPGA resource claim with custom output format.

    # Get Author FPGA claim with custom columns
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-author-fpga-claim -n anihpj-prod -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,READY:.status.allocation,DEVICES:.status.devices[*].device
    NAME READY DEVICES anihpj-author-fpga-claim fpga-card-1
    Example 5: Read Location Multi-Device Claim

    Get Location multi-device resource claim allocation details.

    # Get Location multi-device claim allocation
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-location-multi-claim -n anihpj-prod -o jsonpath='{.status.allocation.devices.results[*].device}' && echo
    gps-card-0 gpu-2

    Read ResourceClaim Status

    Read the status of the specified ResourceClaim to get allocation results and device information for your Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl get resourceclaim {name} -o jsonpath='{.status}'
curl -k -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  $APISERVER/apis/resource.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/resourceclaims/{name}/status
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the ResourceClaim
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace of the ResourceClaim
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceClaim): OK - Returns the ResourceClaim with status information
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Check JobPost GPU Allocation Status

    Read allocation status for JobPost GPU resource claim.

    # Check JobPost GPU allocation status
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim -n anihpj-prod -o jsonpath='{.status.allocation}' | jq .
    { "allocationTimestamp": "2024-09-14T12:05:00Z", "devices": { "results": [ { "device": "gpu-0", "driver": "nvidia.com/gpu", "pool": "worker-node-1", "request": "ai-gpu", "consumedCapacity": { "nvidia.com/gpu.memory": "20Gi" } } ] } }
    Example 2: Monitor User Network Claim Reservations

    Check reservation status for User network resource claim.

    # Monitor User claim reservations
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-user-network-claim -n anihpj-prod -o jsonpath='{.status.reservedFor}' | jq .
    [ { "name": "user-auth-pod-1", "resource": "pods", "uid": "def456-ghi789-abc123", "apiGroup": "" } ]
    Example 3: Verify Skills Storage Device Status

    Read device status for Skills storage resource claim.

    # Verify Skills storage device status
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-skills-storage-claim -n anihpj-prod -o jsonpath='{.status.devices[0].conditions}' | jq .
    [ { "type": "Ready", "status": "True", "lastTransitionTime": "2024-09-14T12:10:00Z", "reason": "DeviceReady", "message": "NVMe storage device successfully mounted" } ]
    Example 4: Check Author FPGA Configuration Status

    Read configuration status for Author FPGA resource claim.

    # Check Author FPGA configuration status
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-author-fpga-claim -n anihpj-prod -o jsonpath='{.status.devices[0].data}' | jq .
    { "bitstream": "content-analyzer-v2.1", "configurationStatus": "loaded", "processingUnits": 1024, "memoryBandwidth": "500GB/s" }
    Example 5: View Location Multi-Device Allocation Status

    Read allocation status for Location multi-device resource claim.

    # View Location multi-device allocation status
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-location-multi-claim -n anihpj-prod -o jsonpath='{.status.allocation.devices.results[*].{device:.device,driver:.driver}}' | jq .
    [ { "device": "gps-card-0", "driver": "location.gps.example.com" }, { "device": "gpu-2", "driver": "nvidia.com/gpu" } ]

    Create ResourceClaim

    Create a new ResourceClaim from specification to define dynamic resource allocation requests for Django application components.

    Syntax
    kubectl create -f resourceclaim.yaml
kubectl apply -f resourceclaim.yaml
    Parameters
    • body (ResourceClaim, required): Complete ResourceClaim specification
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace for the ResourceClaim
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without creation
    • fieldManager (string): Field management identifier
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceClaim): OK - ResourceClaim successfully created
    • 201 (ResourceClaim): Created - ResourceClaim created
    • 202 (ResourceClaim): Accepted - Creation in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Create JobPost Machine Learning Resource Claim

    Create a new ML resource claim for JobPost analysis services.

    # Create JobPost ML resource claim
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-ml-claim
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: jobpost
    component: ml-processing
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: ml-accelerator
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-ml
        count: 1
        selectors:
        - cel:
            expression: |
              device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "32Gi" &&
              device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.compute"] >= "9.0"
        capacity:
          requests:
            nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "24Gi"
            nvidia.com/gpu.compute: "80%"
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-ml-claim created
    Example 2: Create User Crypto Hardware Claim

    Create User authentication crypto hardware resource claim.

    # Create User crypto hardware claim
kubectl create -f user-crypto-claim.yaml
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-crypto-claim created
    Example 3: Create Skills Analytics Resource Claim

    Create Skills analytics processing resource claim with multiple devices.

    # Create Skills analytics resource claim
kubectl apply -f skills-analytics-claim.yaml
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-analytics-claim created
    Example 4: Create Author Video Processing Claim

    Create Author video content processing resource claim.

    # Create Author video processing claim
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-video-claim
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: author
    component: video-processing
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: video-encoder
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: intel-qsv-encoder
        count: 2
        tolerations:
        - key: "video.encoding.heavy"
          operator: "Equal"
          value: "true"
          effect: "NoSchedule"
    - name: video-storage
      exactly:
        deviceClassName: nvme-fast-storage
        count: 1
        capacity:
          requests:
            storage.nvme.bandwidth: "10Gbps"
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-video-claim created
    Example 5: Create Location Real-time Processing Claim

    Create Location real-time geospatial processing resource claim.

    # Create Location real-time processing claim
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaim
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-realtime-claim
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: location
    component: realtime-processing
spec:
  devices:
    requests:
    - name: edge-compute
      firstAvailable:
      - name: high-performance
        deviceClassName: edge-gpu-premium
        count: 1
        allocationMode: "ExactCount"
      - name: standard-performance
        deviceClassName: edge-gpu-standard
        count: 2
        allocationMode: "ExactCount"
    constraints:
    - matchAttribute: "edge.location.zone"
      requests: ["edge-compute"]
EOF
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-realtime-claim created

    Delete ResourceClaim

    Delete the specified ResourceClaim to release dynamic resource allocations that are no longer needed for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete resourceclaim {name}
kubectl delete -f resourceclaim.yaml
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the ResourceClaim to delete
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace of the ResourceClaim
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - ResourceClaim successfully deleted
    • 202 (Status): Accepted - Deletion in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete JobPost ML Resource Claim

    Delete obsolete JobPost ML resource claim.

    # Delete JobPost ML resource claim
kubectl delete resourceclaim anihpj-jobpost-ml-claim -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-ml-claim" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Crypto Claim with Verification

    Delete User crypto resource claim with dry-run verification.

    # Verify deletion with dry-run first
kubectl delete resourceclaim anihpj-user-crypto-claim -n anihpj-prod --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete resourceclaim anihpj-user-crypto-claim -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-crypto-claim" deleted (dry run) resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-crypto-claim" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Analytics Claim by File

    Delete Skills analytics resource claim using YAML file.

    # Delete Skills analytics claim by file
kubectl delete -f skills-analytics-claim.yaml
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-analytics-claim" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Video Claim with Grace Period

    Delete Author video processing resource claim with custom grace period.

    # Delete Author video claim with grace period
kubectl delete resourceclaim anihpj-author-video-claim -n anihpj-prod --grace-period=60
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-video-claim" deleted
    Example 5: Delete Location Real-time Claim with Wait

    Delete Location real-time processing resource claim and wait for completion.

    # Delete Location real-time claim with wait
kubectl delete resourceclaim anihpj-location-realtime-claim -n anihpj-prod --wait=true
# Verify deletion
kubectl get resourceclaim anihpj-location-realtime-claim -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-realtime-claim" deleted Error from server (NotFound): resourceclaims.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-realtime-claim" not found

    Delete Collection of ResourceClaims

    Delete multiple ResourceClaims based on label selectors to clean up related dynamic resource allocations for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete resourceclaims -l {label-selector}
kubectl delete resourceclaims --all
    Parameters
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace to delete ResourceClaims from
    • labelSelector (string): Filter resource claims by labels
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter resource claims by fields
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - Collection deletion successful
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete All JobPost Resource Claims

    Delete all ResourceClaims related to JobPost services.

    # Delete all JobPost resource claims
kubectl delete resourceclaims -l service=jobpost -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-claim" deleted resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-ml-claim" deleted resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-advanced-claim" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Resource Claims by Environment

    Delete User resource claims for specific environment.

    # Delete User test resource claims
kubectl delete resourceclaims -l service=user,env=test -n anihpj-test
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-test-claim" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Development Resource Claims

    Delete Skills resource claims in development environment.

    # Delete Skills dev resource claims
kubectl delete resourceclaims -l service=skills,env=development -n anihpj-dev
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-dev-claim" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Resource Claims with Dry Run

    Preview Author resource claim deletion before execution.

    # Preview Author resource claim deletion
kubectl delete resourceclaims -l service=author -n anihpj-prod --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete resourceclaims -l service=author -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-claim" deleted (dry run) resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-video-claim" deleted (dry run) resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-claim" deleted resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-video-claim" deleted
    Example 5: Delete All Application Resource Claims

    Delete all ResourceClaims for the entire anihpj application.

    # Delete all anihpj resource claims
kubectl delete resourceclaims -l app=anihpj -n anihpj-prod
# Verify deletion
kubectl get resourceclaims -l app=anihpj -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-multi-claim" deleted resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-realtime-claim" deleted resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-storage-claim" deleted resourceclaim.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-network-claim" deleted No resources found in anihpj-prod namespace.

    ResourceClaimTemplate

    ResourceClaimTemplate

    ResourceClaimTemplate Overview

    ResourceClaimTemplate - Template for generating ResourceClaim objects
    ResourceClaimTemplateSpec - Template specification and metadata
    ResourceClaimTemplateList - Collection of resource claim templates
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: ResourceClaimTemplate v1 API | API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1

    ResourceClaimTemplate is used to produce ResourceClaim objects dynamically for workloads.

    Full Description

    ResourceClaimTemplate is used to produce ResourceClaim objects automatically when workloads need dynamic resource allocation. When developing your anihpj Django application, ResourceClaimTemplates enable automatic generation of device claims for different services like JobPost AI processing, Skills analysis, User authentication hardware, Author content processing, and Location geospatial services. This alpha-level feature requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.

    Templates are particularly useful in Django deployments where you want to standardize resource allocation patterns across multiple pods or services. Instead of manually creating individual ResourceClaims, templates automatically generate them based on predefined specifications when pods are scheduled.

    Syntax

    # Create a ResourceClaimTemplate from YAML file
kubectl create -f resourceclaimtemplate.yaml

# List all ResourceClaimTemplates
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates

# Get detailed information about a specific ResourceClaimTemplate
kubectl describe resourceclaimtemplate {name}

# Get ResourceClaimTemplate in YAML format
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate {name} -o yaml

# Delete a specific ResourceClaimTemplate
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplate {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-gpu-template
  namespace: anihpj-prod
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      component: ai-processing
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: gpu-request
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu
          count: 1
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "8Gi"'

    Parameters

    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1" for ResourceClaimTemplate
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ResourceClaimTemplate"
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata including name, namespace, and labels
    • spec (ResourceClaimTemplateSpec, required): Template specification for generating ResourceClaims

    Return Value

    • ResourceClaimTemplate object: Complete template specification with metadata and generation parameters
    • metadata: Object metadata including creation timestamp and resource version
    • spec: Template specification that will be used to generate ResourceClaims

    5 Django Service Examples

    Example 1: GPU Template for JobPost AI Processing

    Create ResourceClaimTemplate for automatic GPU allocation for JobPost AI services.

    # Create GPU template for JobPost AI
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-template
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: jobpost
    component: ai-processing
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: jobpost
      auto-generated: "true"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: ai-gpu
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-ml
          count: 1
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: |
                device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "16Gi" &&
                device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.compute"] >= "8.0"
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-template created
    Example 2: Network Accelerator Template for User Services

    Create ResourceClaimTemplate for User authentication network acceleration.

    # Create network template for User services
kubectl create -f user-network-template.yaml
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-network-template created
    Example 3: Storage Template for Skills Processing

    Create ResourceClaimTemplate for Skills data processing storage requirements.

    # Create storage template for Skills processing
kubectl apply -f skills-storage-template.yaml
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-storage-template created
    Example 4: FPGA Template for Author Content Processing

    Create ResourceClaimTemplate for Author content analysis FPGA devices.

    # Create FPGA template for Author services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-fpga-template
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: author
    component: content-processing
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: author
      device-type: fpga
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: content-fpga
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: intel-fpga-content
          count: 1
          capacity:
            requests:
              intel.com/fpga.dsp: "200"
              intel.com/fpga.memory: "4Gi"
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-fpga-template created
    Example 5: Multi-Device Template for Location Services

    Create ResourceClaimTemplate with multiple device types for Location geospatial processing.

    # Create multi-device template for Location services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-multi-template
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: location
    component: geospatial-processing
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: location
      multi-device: "true"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: gps-accelerator
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: gps-hardware
          count: 1
      - name: mapping-gpu
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu
          count: 1
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "8Gi"'
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-multi-template created

    ResourceClaimTemplateSpec

    ResourceClaimTemplateSpec contains the metadata and fields for a ResourceClaim template. This specification defines what ResourceClaim objects will be generated when the template is used by workloads.

    Syntax
    # View ResourceClaimTemplateSpec structure
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate {name} -o jsonpath='{.spec}'

# Validate ResourceClaimTemplateSpec
kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f resourceclaimtemplate.yaml
    Parameters
    • spec (ResourceClaimSpec, required): Complete ResourceClaim specification that will be copied to generated claims
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Labels and annotations to be copied to generated ResourceClaims
    Return Value
    • ResourceClaimTemplateSpec object: Complete template specification with metadata and claim spec
    • spec: ResourceClaim specification that will be used for generation
    • metadata: Template metadata to be applied to generated claims
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: JobPost AI Template Specification

    Advanced ResourceClaimTemplateSpec for JobPost machine learning workloads.

    # Create advanced JobPost template spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-ml-template
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: jobpost
      workload-type: machine-learning
      tier: production
    annotations:
      resource.anihpj.com/usage: "ai-processing"
      resource.anihpj.com/priority: "high"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: ml-gpu
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-enterprise
          count: 2
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: |
                device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "32Gi" &&
                device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.compute"] >= "9.0" &&
                device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.nvlink"] == "true"
      constraints:
      - matchAttribute: "numa.node"
        requests: ["ml-gpu"]
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-ml-template created
    Example 2: User Authentication Template Specification

    ResourceClaimTemplateSpec for User service crypto hardware requirements.

    # Create User crypto template spec
kubectl create -f user-crypto-template.yaml
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-crypto-template created
    Example 3: Skills Analytics Template Specification

    ResourceClaimTemplateSpec with multiple device types for Skills processing.

    # Create Skills analytics template spec
kubectl apply -f skills-analytics-template.yaml
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-analytics-template created
    Example 4: Author Content Template with Configuration

    ResourceClaimTemplateSpec with device configuration for Author content processing.

    # Create Author content template with config
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-content-template
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: author
      processing-type: content-analysis
    annotations:
      config.anihpj.com/fpga-bitstream: "content-analyzer-v3.0"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: content-processor
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: intel-fpga-optimized
          count: 1
      config:
      - opaque:
          driver: content.fpga.anihpj.com
          parameters:
            runtime.Object:
              kind: ContentConfig
              apiVersion: v1
              mode: "high-throughput"
              bufferSize: "4Gi"
              parallelStreams: 8
        requests: ["content-processor"]
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-content-template created
    Example 5: Location Geospatial Template Specification

    ResourceClaimTemplateSpec with FirstAvailable device selection for Location services.

    # Create Location geospatial template spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-geospatial-template
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: location
      processing-type: geospatial
    annotations:
      scaling.anihpj.com/auto-scale: "true"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: geo-accelerator
        firstAvailable:
        - name: premium-gpu
          deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-premium
          count: 1
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "24Gi"'
        - name: standard-gpu
          deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-standard
          count: 2
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "12Gi"'
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-geospatial-template created

    ResourceClaimTemplateList

    ResourceClaimTemplateList is a collection of claim templates. This list format is returned when querying multiple resource claim templates and provides a standardized way to handle collections of templates in your Django application infrastructure.

    Syntax
    # List all ResourceClaimTemplates
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates

# List ResourceClaimTemplates with labels
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -l app=anihpj

# Get ResourceClaimTemplateList in JSON format
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -o json
    Parameters
    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1" for ResourceClaimTemplateList
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ResourceClaimTemplateList"
    • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resource version and continue token
    • items ([]ResourceClaimTemplate, required): Array of ResourceClaimTemplate objects
    Return Value
    • ResourceClaimTemplateList object: Collection containing multiple ResourceClaimTemplate resources
    • items array: List of individual ResourceClaimTemplate objects with their specifications
    • metadata: List metadata for pagination and resource versioning
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All JobPost Resource Claim Templates

    List all ResourceClaimTemplates used by JobPost application components.
