    # List JobPost resource claim templates
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -l service=jobpost
    NAME AGE anihpj-jobpost-gpu-template 5m anihpj-jobpost-ml-template 3m
    Example 2: List User Service Resource Claim Templates

    Get all resource claim templates for User authentication services.

    # List User resource claim templates with details
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -l service=user -o wide
    NAME AGE anihpj-user-network-template 4m anihpj-user-crypto-template 2m
    Example 3: List Skills Processing Resource Claim Templates

    View resource claim templates for Skills processing services.

    # List Skills resource claim templates in JSON
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -l service=skills -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
    "anihpj-skills-storage-template" "anihpj-skills-analytics-template"
    Example 4: List Author Content Resource Claim Templates

    Get resource claim templates sorted by creation time for Author services.

    # List Author resource claim templates sorted by creation time
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -l service=author --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
    NAME AGE anihpj-author-fpga-template 6m anihpj-author-content-template 4m
    Example 5: List All Application Resource Claim Templates

    Get comprehensive list of all anihpj application resource claim templates.

    # List all anihpj resource claim templates
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -l app=anihpj --show-labels
    NAME AGE LABELS anihpj-location-multi-template 7m app=anihpj,service=location anihpj-location-geospatial-template 5m app=anihpj,service=location anihpj-skills-storage-template 6m app=anihpj,service=skills anihpj-user-network-template 4m app=anihpj,service=user anihpj-jobpost-gpu-template 5m app=anihpj,service=jobpost anihpj-author-fpga-template 6m app=anihpj,service=author

    Operations

    Operations Overview

    Read - GET single ResourceClaimTemplate details
    List - GET multiple ResourceClaimTemplates (namespaced)
    List All - GET all ResourceClaimTemplates (cluster-wide)
    Create - POST new ResourceClaimTemplate
    Update - PUT existing ResourceClaimTemplate
    Patch - PATCH ResourceClaimTemplate fields
    Delete - DELETE ResourceClaimTemplate resource
    Delete Collection - DELETE multiple ResourceClaimTemplates

    Operations allow you to interact with ResourceClaimTemplate resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage dynamic resource allocation templates for Django application components, ensuring consistent resource provisioning patterns.

    Read ResourceClaimTemplate

    Read the specified ResourceClaimTemplate to get complete template configuration and metadata for your Django application deployments.

    Syntax
    kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate {name} -o yaml
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate {name} -o json
kubectl describe resourceclaimtemplate {name}
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the ResourceClaimTemplate
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace of the ResourceClaimTemplate
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceClaimTemplate): OK - Returns the complete ResourceClaimTemplate object
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Read JobPost GPU Template

    Get complete JobPost GPU resource claim template configuration in YAML format.

    # Get JobPost GPU template details
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-jobpost-gpu-template -n anihpj-prod -o yaml
    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1 kind: ResourceClaimTemplate metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-09-14T12:00:00Z" name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-template namespace: anihpj-prod labels: service: jobpost component: ai-processing spec: metadata: labels: app: anihpj service: jobpost auto-generated: "true" spec: devices: requests: - name: ai-gpu exactly: deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-ml count: 1
    Example 2: Read User Network Template Details

    Get detailed description of User service network resource claim template.

    # Describe User network resource claim template
kubectl describe resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-user-network-template -n anihpj-prod
    Name: anihpj-user-network-template Namespace: anihpj-prod Labels: service=user Annotations: API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1 Kind: ResourceClaimTemplate Spec: Metadata: Labels: App: anihpj Service: user Spec: Devices: Requests: Name: network-accelerator Exactly: Device Class Name: intel-network-accelerator Count: 1 Events:
    Example 3: Read Skills Storage Template JSON

    Get Skills storage resource claim template in JSON format for processing.

    # Get Skills storage template in JSON
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-skills-storage-template -n anihpj-prod -o json | jq '.spec.spec.devices.requests[0].name'
    "storage-device"
    Example 4: Read Author FPGA Template

    Get Author FPGA resource claim template with custom output format.

    # Get Author FPGA template with custom columns
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-author-fpga-template -n anihpj-prod -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,SERVICE:.metadata.labels.service,DEVICES:.spec.spec.devices.requests[*].name
    NAME SERVICE DEVICES anihpj-author-fpga-template author content-fpga
    Example 5: Read Location Multi-Device Template

    Get Location multi-device resource claim template specification details.

    # Get Location multi-device template spec
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-location-multi-template -n anihpj-prod -o jsonpath='{.spec.spec.devices.requests[*].name}' && echo
    gps-accelerator mapping-gpu

    Create ResourceClaimTemplate

    Create a new ResourceClaimTemplate from specification to define dynamic resource allocation templates for Django application components.

    Syntax
    kubectl create -f resourceclaimtemplate.yaml
kubectl apply -f resourceclaimtemplate.yaml
    Parameters
    • body (ResourceClaimTemplate, required): Complete ResourceClaimTemplate specification
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace for the ResourceClaimTemplate
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without creation
    • fieldManager (string): Field management identifier
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceClaimTemplate): OK - ResourceClaimTemplate successfully created
    • 201 (ResourceClaimTemplate): Created - ResourceClaimTemplate created
    • 202 (ResourceClaimTemplate): Accepted - Creation in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Create JobPost Video Processing Template

    Create a new video processing template for JobPost content analysis services.

    # Create JobPost video processing template
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-video-template
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: jobpost
    component: video-processing
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: jobpost
      workload-type: video-analysis
    annotations:
      resource.anihpj.com/codec-support: "h264,h265,av1"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: video-encoder
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: intel-qsv-encoder
          count: 2
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: 'device.attributes["intel.com/qsv.version"] >= "12.0"'
        capacity:
          requests:
            intel.com/qsv.streams: "4"
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-video-template created
    Example 2: Create User Biometric Template

    Create User biometric authentication hardware template.

    # Create User biometric template
kubectl create -f user-biometric-template.yaml
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-biometric-template created
    Example 3: Create Skills ML Training Template

    Create Skills machine learning training template with GPU clusters.

    # Create Skills ML training template
kubectl apply -f skills-ml-training-template.yaml
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-ml-training-template created
    Example 4: Create Author Real-time Editing Template

    Create Author real-time content editing resource template.

    # Create Author real-time editing template
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-realtime-template
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: author
    component: realtime-editing
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: author
      processing-mode: realtime
    annotations:
      scaling.anihpj.com/max-instances: "10"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: editing-gpu
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu-studio
          count: 1
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: |
                device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.memory"] >= "16Gi" &&
                device.attributes["nvidia.com/gpu.rt-cores"] >= "58"
      - name: nvme-cache
        exactly:
          deviceClassName: nvme-high-iops
          count: 1
          capacity:
            requests:
              storage.nvme.iops: "100000"
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-realtime-template created
    Example 5: Create Location Edge Computing Template

    Create Location edge computing resource template for real-time geospatial processing.

    # Create Location edge computing template
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-edge-template
  namespace: anihpj-prod
  labels:
    service: location
    component: edge-computing
spec:
  metadata:
    labels:
      app: anihpj
      service: location
      deployment-type: edge
    annotations:
      edge.anihpj.com/latency-requirement: "low"
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
      - name: edge-accelerator
        firstAvailable:
        - name: gpu-accelerator
          deviceClassName: nvidia-jetson
          count: 1
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: 'device.attributes["nvidia.com/jetson.model"] == "agx-orin"'
        - name: fpga-accelerator
          deviceClassName: xilinx-fpga-edge
          count: 1
          selectors:
          - cel:
              expression: 'device.attributes["xilinx.com/fpga.series"] == "zynq-ultrascale"'
EOF
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-edge-template created

    Delete ResourceClaimTemplate

    Delete the specified ResourceClaimTemplate to remove dynamic resource allocation templates that are no longer needed for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplate {name}
kubectl delete -f resourceclaimtemplate.yaml
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the ResourceClaimTemplate to delete
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace of the ResourceClaimTemplate
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceClaimTemplate): OK - ResourceClaimTemplate successfully deleted
    • 202 (ResourceClaimTemplate): Accepted - Deletion in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete JobPost Video Template

    Delete obsolete JobPost video processing template.

    # Delete JobPost video template
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-jobpost-video-template -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-video-template" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Biometric Template with Verification

    Delete User biometric template with dry-run verification.

    # Verify deletion with dry-run first
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-user-biometric-template -n anihpj-prod --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-user-biometric-template -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-biometric-template" deleted (dry run) resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-biometric-template" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills ML Template by File

    Delete Skills ML training template using YAML file.

    # Delete Skills ML template by file
kubectl delete -f skills-ml-training-template.yaml
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-ml-training-template" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Realtime Template with Grace Period

    Delete Author real-time editing template with custom grace period.

    # Delete Author realtime template with grace period
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-author-realtime-template -n anihpj-prod --grace-period=60
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-realtime-template" deleted
    Example 5: Delete Location Edge Template with Wait

    Delete Location edge computing template and wait for completion.

    # Delete Location edge template with wait
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-location-edge-template -n anihpj-prod --wait=true
# Verify deletion
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplate anihpj-location-edge-template -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-edge-template" deleted Error from server (NotFound): resourceclaimtemplates.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-edge-template" not found

    Delete Collection of ResourceClaimTemplates

    Delete multiple ResourceClaimTemplates based on label selectors to clean up related dynamic resource allocation templates for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates -l {label-selector}
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates --all
    Parameters
    • namespace (string, required): Namespace to delete ResourceClaimTemplates from
    • labelSelector (string): Filter resource claim templates by labels
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter resource claim templates by fields
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - Collection deletion successful
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete All JobPost Resource Claim Templates

    Delete all ResourceClaimTemplates related to JobPost services.

    # Delete all JobPost resource claim templates
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates -l service=jobpost -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-ml-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-video-template" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Templates by Environment

    Delete User resource claim templates for specific environment.

    # Delete User test resource claim templates
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates -l service=user,env=test -n anihpj-test
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-test-template" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Development Templates

    Delete Skills resource claim templates in development environment.

    # Delete Skills dev resource claim templates
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates -l service=skills,env=development -n anihpj-dev
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-dev-template" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Templates with Dry Run

    Preview Author resource claim template deletion before execution.

    # Preview Author resource claim template deletion
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates -l service=author -n anihpj-prod --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates -l service=author -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-template" deleted (dry run) resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-content-template" deleted (dry run) resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-realtime-template" deleted (dry run) resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-content-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-realtime-template" deleted
    Example 5: Delete All Application Resource Claim Templates

    Delete all ResourceClaimTemplates for the entire anihpj application.

    # Delete all anihpj resource claim templates
kubectl delete resourceclaimtemplates -l app=anihpj -n anihpj-prod
# Verify deletion
kubectl get resourceclaimtemplates -l app=anihpj -n anihpj-prod
    resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-multi-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-geospatial-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-edge-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-storage-template" deleted resourceclaimtemplate.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-network-template" deleted No resources found in anihpj-prod namespace.

    ResourceSlice

    ResourceSlice

    ResourceSlice Overview

    ResourceSlice - Resource pool representation for device management
    ResourceSliceSpec - Device pool specification and driver information
    ResourceSliceList - Collection of resource slices
    Operations - API operations for management
    Official Docs: ResourceSlice v1 API | API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1

    ResourceSlice represents one or more resources in a pool of similar resources, managed by a common driver.

    Full Description

    ResourceSlice represents one or more resources in a pool of similar resources, managed by a common driver. When developing your anihpj Django application, ResourceSlices provide the infrastructure for dynamic resource allocation, allowing JobPost AI processing, Skills analysis, User authentication hardware, Author content processing, and Location geospatial services to access shared device pools efficiently. This alpha-level feature requires enabling the DynamicResourceAllocation feature gate.

    A pool may span multiple ResourceSlices, and the driver determines exactly how many ResourceSlices comprise a pool. Each device in a pool, regardless of how many ResourceSlices it spans, must have a unique name identified by the tuple <driver name>, <pool name>, <device name>. This is particularly useful in Django deployments where different services need coordinated access to shared hardware resources.

    Syntax

    # Create a ResourceSlice from YAML file
kubectl create -f resourceslice.yaml

# List all ResourceSlices
kubectl get resourceslices

# Get detailed information about a specific ResourceSlice
kubectl describe resourceslice {name}

# Get ResourceSlice in YAML format
kubectl get resourceslice {name} -o yaml

# Delete a specific ResourceSlice
kubectl delete resourceslice {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-gpu-pool-slice-1
spec:
  driver: nvidia.com/gpu
  pool:
    name: anihpj-gpu-pool
    generation: 1
    resourceSliceCount: 2
  devices:
  - name: gpu-0
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "16Gi"
      nvidia.com/gpu.compute: "8.6"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory:
        value: "16Gi"

    Parameters

    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1" for ResourceSlice
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ResourceSlice"
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata including name and labels
    • spec (ResourceSliceSpec, required): Specification containing driver information and device pool details

    Return Value

    • ResourceSlice object: Complete resource slice specification with metadata and device information
    • metadata: Object metadata including creation timestamp and resource version
    • spec: Device pool specification with driver details and device definitions

    5 Django Service Examples

    Example 1: GPU Pool ResourceSlice for JobPost AI Processing

    Create ResourceSlice representing GPU devices for JobPost AI analysis services.

    # Create GPU pool ResourceSlice for JobPost AI
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1
  labels:
    driver: nvidia.com/gpu
    pool: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool
spec:
  driver: nvidia.com/gpu
  pool:
    name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool
    generation: 1
    resourceSliceCount: 3
  devices:
  - name: gpu-0
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "24Gi"
      nvidia.com/gpu.compute: "9.0"
      nvidia.com/gpu.nvlink: "true"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory:
        value: "24Gi"
      nvidia.com/gpu.compute:
        value: "100"
  - name: gpu-1
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "24Gi"
      nvidia.com/gpu.compute: "9.0"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory:
        value: "24Gi"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1 created
    Example 2: Network Accelerator ResourceSlice for User Services

    Create ResourceSlice for User authentication network acceleration devices.

    # Create network accelerator ResourceSlice for User services
kubectl create -f user-network-slice.yaml
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-network-slice created
    Example 3: Storage Device ResourceSlice for Skills Processing

    Create ResourceSlice for Skills data processing storage devices.

    # Create storage device ResourceSlice for Skills processing
kubectl apply -f skills-storage-slice.yaml
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-storage-slice created
    Example 4: FPGA Device ResourceSlice for Author Content Processing

    Create ResourceSlice representing FPGA devices for Author content analysis.

    # Create FPGA device ResourceSlice for Author services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-fpga-slice
  labels:
    driver: intel.com/fpga
    pool: anihpj-author-fpga-pool
spec:
  driver: intel.com/fpga
  pool:
    name: anihpj-author-fpga-pool
    generation: 1
    resourceSliceCount: 1
  devices:
  - name: fpga-card-0
    attributes:
      intel.com/fpga.family: "arria10"
      intel.com/fpga.dsp: "1500"
    capacity:
      intel.com/fpga.dsp:
        value: "1500"
      intel.com/fpga.memory:
        value: "8Gi"
    bindingConditions:
    - "content.processor.ready"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-fpga-slice created
    Example 5: Multi-Device ResourceSlice for Location Services

    Create ResourceSlice with multiple device types for Location geospatial processing.

    # Create multi-device ResourceSlice for Location services
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-mixed-slice
  labels:
    driver: location.accelerator.com
    pool: anihpj-location-mixed-pool
spec:
  driver: location.accelerator.com
  pool:
    name: anihpj-location-mixed-pool
    generation: 2
    resourceSliceCount: 2
  devices:
  - name: gps-card-0
    attributes:
      location.com/gps.channels: "128"
      location.com/gps.frequency: "l1-l2-l5"
    capacity:
      location.com/gps.channels:
        value: "128"
  - name: mapping-gpu-0
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "16Gi"
      location.com/gpu.mapping: "true"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory:
        value: "16Gi"
  sharedCounters:
  - name: processing-units
    counters:
      total-threads:
        value: "1024"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-mixed-slice created

    ResourceSliceSpec

    ResourceSliceSpec contains the information published by the driver in one ResourceSlice. This specification defines the driver, resource pool, devices, and shared counters that make up the slice of the overall device pool.

    Syntax
    # View ResourceSliceSpec structure
kubectl get resourceslice {name} -o jsonpath='{.spec}'

# Validate ResourceSliceSpec
kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f resourceslice.yaml
    Parameters
    • driver (string, required): DRA driver providing the capacity information, must be DNS subdomain
    • pool (ResourcePool, required): Pool description that this ResourceSlice belongs to
    • devices ([]Device): List of devices in this pool (max 128 entries)
    • sharedCounters ([]CounterSet): Named sets of counters available for device consumption
    • nodeName (string): Node providing resources in this pool
    • nodeSelector (NodeSelector): Node selector for resource access
    • allNodes (boolean): Indicates all nodes have access to resources
    • perDeviceNodeSelection (boolean): Whether access is set per device or per slice
    Return Value
    • ResourceSliceSpec object: Complete specification with driver and pool information
    • pool: Resource pool details including generation and slice count
    • devices: Device definitions with attributes, capacity, and constraints
    • sharedCounters: Counter sets available for device consumption tracking
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Advanced JobPost GPU Pool Specification

    ResourceSliceSpec with complex GPU pool configuration for JobPost machine learning workloads.

    # Create advanced JobPost GPU pool spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-ml-slice
spec:
  driver: nvidia.ml.k8s.io
  pool:
    name: anihpj-jobpost-ml-pool
    generation: 3
    resourceSliceCount: 4
  devices:
  - name: a100-gpu-0
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "80Gi"
      nvidia.com/gpu.compute: "9.0"
      nvidia.com/gpu.nvlink: "true"
      nvidia.com/gpu.tensor-cores: "432"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory:
        value: "80Gi"
        requestPolicy:
          default: "20Gi"
          validRange:
            min: "10Gi"
            max: "80Gi"
            step: "10Gi"
    allowMultipleAllocations: true
    bindingConditions:
    - "gpu.ready"
    - "nvlink.established"
  sharedCounters:
  - name: ml-processing-units
    counters:
      tensor-operations:
        value: "1000000"
      memory-bandwidth:
        value: "2039GB/s"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-ml-slice created
    Example 2: User Authentication Device Pool Specification

    ResourceSliceSpec for User service crypto and biometric hardware pool.

    # Create User authentication device pool spec
kubectl create -f user-auth-device-slice.yaml
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-auth-slice created
    Example 3: Skills Analytics Storage Pool Specification

    ResourceSliceSpec with high-performance storage devices for Skills processing.

    # Create Skills analytics storage pool spec
kubectl apply -f skills-analytics-storage-slice.yaml
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-analytics-slice created
    Example 4: Author Content FPGA Pool with Node Selection

    ResourceSliceSpec with node selector for Author content processing FPGA devices.

    # Create Author FPGA pool with node selection
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-fpga-node-slice
spec:
  driver: intel.fpga.k8s.io
  pool:
    name: anihpj-author-content-pool
    generation: 1
    resourceSliceCount: 2
  nodeSelector:
    nodeSelectorTerms:
    - matchExpressions:
      - key: hardware.fpga.available
        operator: In
        values: ["true"]
      - key: zone
        operator: In
        values: ["content-processing"]
  devices:
  - name: stratix-fpga-0
    attributes:
      intel.com/fpga.family: "stratix10"
      intel.com/fpga.dsp: "5760"
      intel.com/fpga.memory: "32Gi"
    capacity:
      intel.com/fpga.dsp:
        value: "5760"
        requestPolicy:
          validValues: ["1440", "2880", "5760"]
          default: "1440"
    taints:
    - key: "content.processing.intensive"
      value: "true"
      effect: "NoSchedule"
      timeAdded: "2024-09-14T12:00:00Z"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-fpga-node-slice created
    Example 5: Location Edge Computing Pool Specification

    ResourceSliceSpec with per-device node selection for Location edge computing.

    # Create Location edge computing pool spec
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-edge-slice
spec:
  driver: edge.location.k8s.io
  pool:
    name: anihpj-location-edge-pool
    generation: 1
    resourceSliceCount: 5
  perDeviceNodeSelection: true
  devices:
  - name: jetson-agx-east
    nodeName: edge-node-east-1
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/jetson.model: "agx-orin"
      location.com/edge.zone: "east"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/jetson.cuda-cores:
        value: "2048"
    consumesCounters:
    - counterSet: "edge-processing"
      counters:
        bandwidth:
          value: "100Mbps"
  - name: jetson-agx-west
    nodeName: edge-node-west-1
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/jetson.model: "agx-orin"
      location.com/edge.zone: "west"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/jetson.cuda-cores:
        value: "2048"
  sharedCounters:
  - name: edge-processing
    counters:
      total-bandwidth:
        value: "1Gbps"
      concurrent-streams:
        value: "16"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-edge-slice created

    ResourceSliceList

    ResourceSliceList is a collection of ResourceSlices. This list format is returned when querying multiple resource slices and provides a standardized way to handle collections of device pools in your Django application infrastructure.

    Syntax
    # List all ResourceSlices
kubectl get resourceslices

# List ResourceSlices with labels
kubectl get resourceslices -l driver=nvidia.com/gpu

# Get ResourceSliceList in JSON format
kubectl get resourceslices -o json
    Parameters
    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "resource.k8s.io/v1" for ResourceSliceList
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ResourceSliceList"
    • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resource version and continue token
    • items ([]ResourceSlice, required): Array of ResourceSlice objects
    Return Value
    • ResourceSliceList object: Collection containing multiple ResourceSlice resources
    • items array: List of individual ResourceSlice objects with their specifications
    • metadata: List metadata for pagination and resource versioning
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All JobPost Resource Slices

    List all ResourceSlices used by JobPost application components.

    # List JobPost resource slices
kubectl get resourceslices -l pool=anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool
    NAME DRIVER POOL AGE anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1 nvidia.com/gpu anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool 5m anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-2 nvidia.com/gpu anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool 5m anihpj-jobpost-ml-slice nvidia.ml.k8s.io anihpj-jobpost-ml-pool 3m
    Example 2: List User Service Resource Slices

    Get all resource slices for User authentication services.

    # List User resource slices with details
kubectl get resourceslices -l driver=auth.hardware.com -o wide
    NAME DRIVER POOL DEVICES AGE anihpj-user-auth-slice auth.hardware.com anihpj-user-auth-pool 4 4m anihpj-user-network-slice network.accel.com anihpj-user-net-pool 2 2m
    Example 3: List Skills Processing Resource Slices

    View resource slices for Skills processing and analytics services.

    # List Skills resource slices in JSON
kubectl get resourceslices -l pool=anihpj-skills-storage-pool -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
    "anihpj-skills-storage-slice" "anihpj-skills-analytics-slice"
    Example 4: List Author Content Resource Slices

    Get resource slices sorted by generation for Author content services.

    # List Author resource slices sorted by pool generation
kubectl get resourceslices -l driver=intel.fpga.k8s.io --sort-by=.spec.pool.generation
    NAME DRIVER POOL GENERATION AGE anihpj-author-fpga-slice intel.com/fpga anihpj-author-fpga-pool 1 6m anihpj-author-fpga-node-slice intel.fpga.k8s.io anihpj-author-content-pool 1 4m
    Example 5: List All Application Resource Slices

    Get comprehensive list of all anihpj application resource slices.

    # List all anihpj resource slices
kubectl get resourceslices -l app=anihpj --show-labels
    NAME AGE LABELS anihpj-location-mixed-slice 7m app=anihpj,driver=location.accelerator.com,pool=anihpj-location-mixed-pool anihpj-location-edge-slice 5m app=anihpj,driver=edge.location.k8s.io,pool=anihpj-location-edge-pool anihpj-skills-storage-slice 6m app=anihpj,driver=storage.nvme.com,pool=anihpj-skills-storage-pool anihpj-user-auth-slice 4m app=anihpj,driver=auth.hardware.com,pool=anihpj-user-auth-pool anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1 5m app=anihpj,driver=nvidia.com/gpu,pool=anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool anihpj-author-fpga-slice 6m app=anihpj,driver=intel.com/fpga,pool=anihpj-author-fpga-pool

    Operations

    Operations Overview

    Read - GET single ResourceSlice details
    List - GET multiple ResourceSlices (cluster-wide)
    Create - POST new ResourceSlice
    Update - PUT existing ResourceSlice
    Patch - PATCH ResourceSlice fields
    Delete - DELETE ResourceSlice resource
    Delete Collection - DELETE multiple ResourceSlices

    Operations allow you to interact with ResourceSlice resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage device pools and resource allocation infrastructure for Django application components.

    Read ResourceSlice

    Read the specified ResourceSlice to get complete device pool configuration and metadata for your Django application deployments.

    Syntax
    kubectl get resourceslice {name} -o yaml
kubectl get resourceslice {name} -o json
kubectl describe resourceslice {name}
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the ResourceSlice
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceSlice): OK - Returns the complete ResourceSlice object
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Read JobPost GPU ResourceSlice

    Get complete JobPost GPU resource slice configuration in YAML format.

    # Get JobPost GPU slice details
kubectl get resourceslice anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1 -o yaml
    apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1 kind: ResourceSlice metadata: creationTimestamp: "2024-09-14T12:00:00Z" name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1 labels: driver: nvidia.com/gpu pool: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool spec: driver: nvidia.com/gpu pool: name: anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool generation: 1 resourceSliceCount: 3 devices: - name: gpu-0 attributes: nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "24Gi" nvidia.com/gpu.compute: "9.0" capacity: nvidia.com/gpu.memory: value: "24Gi"
    Example 2: Read User Authentication ResourceSlice Details

    Get detailed description of User service authentication hardware slice.

    # Describe User authentication resource slice
kubectl describe resourceslice anihpj-user-auth-slice
    Name: anihpj-user-auth-slice Labels: driver=auth.hardware.com pool=anihpj-user-auth-pool API Version: resource.k8s.io/v1 Kind: ResourceSlice Spec: Driver: auth.hardware.com Pool: Name: anihpj-user-auth-pool Generation: 1 Resource Slice Count: 1 Devices: Name: crypto-card-0 Attributes: auth.hardware.com/crypto.type: rsa-ecc auth.hardware.com/crypto.keysize: 4096 Capacity: auth.hardware.com/crypto.operations: Value: 10000 Events:
    Example 3: Read Skills Storage ResourceSlice JSON

    Get Skills storage resource slice in JSON format for processing.

    # Get Skills storage slice in JSON
kubectl get resourceslice anihpj-skills-storage-slice -o json | jq '.spec.devices[0].name'
    "nvme-storage-0"
    Example 4: Read Author FPGA ResourceSlice

    Get Author FPGA resource slice with custom output format.

    # Get Author FPGA slice with custom columns
kubectl get resourceslice anihpj-author-fpga-slice -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,DRIVER:.spec.driver,DEVICES:.spec.devices[*].name
    NAME DRIVER DEVICES anihpj-author-fpga-slice intel.com/fpga fpga-card-0
    Example 5: Read Location Edge ResourceSlice

    Get Location edge computing resource slice pool details.

    # Get Location edge slice pool information
kubectl get resourceslice anihpj-location-edge-slice -o jsonpath='{.spec.pool}' | jq .
    { "name": "anihpj-location-edge-pool", "generation": 1, "resourceSliceCount": 5 }

    Create ResourceSlice

    Create a new ResourceSlice from specification to define device pools for Django application components.

    Syntax
    kubectl create -f resourceslice.yaml
kubectl apply -f resourceslice.yaml
    Parameters
    • body (ResourceSlice, required): Complete ResourceSlice specification
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without creation
    • fieldManager (string): Field management identifier
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceSlice): OK - ResourceSlice successfully created
    • 201 (ResourceSlice): Created - ResourceSlice created
    • 202 (ResourceSlice): Accepted - Creation in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Create JobPost Video Processing ResourceSlice

    Create a new video processing device slice for JobPost content analysis services.

    # Create JobPost video processing ResourceSlice
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-video-slice
  labels:
    driver: intel.video.com
    pool: anihpj-jobpost-video-pool
    app: anihpj
spec:
  driver: intel.video.com
  pool:
    name: anihpj-jobpost-video-pool
    generation: 1
    resourceSliceCount: 2
  devices:
  - name: qsv-encoder-0
    attributes:
      intel.com/qsv.version: "12.0"
      intel.com/qsv.codecs: "h264,h265,av1"
    capacity:
      intel.com/qsv.streams:
        value: "8"
        requestPolicy:
          default: "2"
          validRange:
            min: "1"
            max: "8"
            step: "1"
  - name: qsv-encoder-1
    attributes:
      intel.com/qsv.version: "12.0"
      intel.com/qsv.codecs: "h264,h265"
    capacity:
      intel.com/qsv.streams:
        value: "4"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-video-slice created
    Example 2: Create User Biometric ResourceSlice

    Create User biometric authentication hardware resource slice.

    # Create User biometric ResourceSlice
kubectl create -f user-biometric-slice.yaml
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-user-biometric-slice created
    Example 3: Create Skills ML Training ResourceSlice

    Create Skills machine learning training device slice with GPU clusters.

    # Create Skills ML training ResourceSlice
kubectl apply -f skills-ml-training-slice.yaml
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-ml-slice created
    Example 4: Create Author Real-time Editing ResourceSlice

    Create Author real-time content editing resource slice.

    # Create Author real-time editing ResourceSlice
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-realtime-slice
  labels:
    driver: nvidia.studio.com
    pool: anihpj-author-realtime-pool
    app: anihpj
spec:
  driver: nvidia.studio.com
  pool:
    name: anihpj-author-realtime-pool
    generation: 1
    resourceSliceCount: 1
  devices:
  - name: rtx-studio-0
    attributes:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory: "24Gi"
      nvidia.com/gpu.rt-cores: "84"
      nvidia.com/gpu.studio: "true"
    capacity:
      nvidia.com/gpu.memory:
        value: "24Gi"
      nvidia.com/gpu.rt-cores:
        value: "84"
    allowMultipleAllocations: false
    bindingConditions:
    - "studio.driver.ready"
  - name: nvme-cache-0
    attributes:
      storage.com/nvme.speed: "7000MB/s"
      storage.com/nvme.capacity: "2TB"
    capacity:
      storage.com/nvme.iops:
        value: "1000000"
        requestPolicy:
          default: "100000"
          validRange:
            min: "10000"
            max: "1000000"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-author-realtime-slice created
    Example 5: Create Location Distributed ResourceSlice

    Create Location distributed computing resource slice for geo-distributed processing.

    # Create Location distributed ResourceSlice
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceSlice
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-distributed-slice
  labels:
    driver: geo.distributed.com
    pool: anihpj-location-distributed-pool
    app: anihpj
spec:
  driver: geo.distributed.com
  pool:
    name: anihpj-location-distributed-pool
    generation: 2
    resourceSliceCount: 3
  allNodes: true
  devices:
  - name: geo-processor-global
    allNodes: true
    attributes:
      geo.com/processor.type: "spatial-compute"
      geo.com/coverage: "global"
    capacity:
      geo.com/compute.threads:
        value: "64"
  sharedCounters:
  - name: global-bandwidth
    counters:
      satellite-connections:
        value: "10"
      terrestrial-connections:
        value: "100"
EOF
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io/anihpj-location-distributed-slice created

    Delete ResourceSlice

    Delete the specified ResourceSlice to remove device pools that are no longer needed for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete resourceslice {name}
kubectl delete -f resourceslice.yaml
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the ResourceSlice to delete
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (ResourceSlice): OK - ResourceSlice successfully deleted
    • 202 (ResourceSlice): Accepted - Deletion in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete JobPost Video ResourceSlice

    Delete obsolete JobPost video processing resource slice.

    # Delete JobPost video ResourceSlice
kubectl delete resourceslice anihpj-jobpost-video-slice
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-video-slice" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User Biometric ResourceSlice with Verification

    Delete User biometric resource slice with dry-run verification.

    # Verify deletion with dry-run first
kubectl delete resourceslice anihpj-user-biometric-slice --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete resourceslice anihpj-user-biometric-slice
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-biometric-slice" deleted (dry run) resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-biometric-slice" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills ML ResourceSlice by File

    Delete Skills ML training resource slice using YAML file.

    # Delete Skills ML ResourceSlice by file
kubectl delete -f skills-ml-training-slice.yaml
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-ml-slice" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author Realtime ResourceSlice with Grace Period

    Delete Author real-time editing resource slice with custom grace period.

    # Delete Author realtime ResourceSlice with grace period
kubectl delete resourceslice anihpj-author-realtime-slice --grace-period=60
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-realtime-slice" deleted
    Example 5: Delete Location Distributed ResourceSlice with Wait

    Delete Location distributed computing resource slice and wait for completion.

    # Delete Location distributed ResourceSlice with wait
kubectl delete resourceslice anihpj-location-distributed-slice --wait=true
# Verify deletion
kubectl get resourceslice anihpj-location-distributed-slice
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-distributed-slice" deleted Error from server (NotFound): resourceslices.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-distributed-slice" not found

    Delete Collection of ResourceSlices

    Delete multiple ResourceSlices based on label selectors to clean up related device pools for Django applications.

    Syntax
    kubectl delete resourceslices -l {label-selector}
kubectl delete resourceslices --all
    Parameters
    • labelSelector (string): Filter resource slices by labels
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter resource slices by fields
    • gracePeriodSeconds (integer): Graceful deletion timeout
    • propagationPolicy (string): Deletion propagation policy
    Return Value
    • 200 (Status): OK - Collection deletion successful
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Delete All JobPost Resource Slices

    Delete all ResourceSlices related to JobPost services.

    # Delete all JobPost resource slices
kubectl delete resourceslices -l pool=anihpj-jobpost-gpu-pool
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-2" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-video-slice" deleted
    Example 2: Delete User ResourceSlices by Driver

    Delete User resource slices for specific driver.

    # Delete User auth hardware resource slices
kubectl delete resourceslices -l driver=auth.hardware.com
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-auth-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-biometric-slice" deleted
    Example 3: Delete Skills Processing ResourceSlices

    Delete Skills resource slices for analytics and ML processing.

    # Delete Skills processing resource slices
kubectl delete resourceslices -l pool=anihpj-skills-storage-pool,pool=anihpj-skills-ml-pool
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-storage-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-ml-slice" deleted
    Example 4: Delete Author ResourceSlices with Dry Run

    Preview Author resource slice deletion before execution.

    # Preview Author resource slice deletion
kubectl delete resourceslices -l driver=intel.fpga.k8s.io --dry-run=client
# Actual deletion
kubectl delete resourceslices -l driver=intel.fpga.k8s.io
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-slice" deleted (dry run) resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-node-slice" deleted (dry run) resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-realtime-slice" deleted (dry run) resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-node-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-realtime-slice" deleted
    Example 5: Delete All Application Resource Slices

    Delete all ResourceSlices for the entire anihpj application.

    # Delete all anihpj resource slices
kubectl delete resourceslices -l app=anihpj
# Verify deletion
kubectl get resourceslices -l app=anihpj
    resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-mixed-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-edge-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-location-distributed-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-storage-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-user-auth-slice" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-gpu-slice-1" deleted resourceslice.resource.k8s.io "anihpj-author-fpga-slice" deleted No resources found

    🌐 Service Resources

    Kubernetes service resources manage network access to your applications. In this section, we'll explore how to expose and connect your anihpj Django application using various service resources, enabling reliable communication between pods and external access to your Django services.

    Service

    Service

    📋 Service Overview

    Service - Network abstraction for pod access
    ServiceSpec - Service configuration and behavior
    ServiceStatus - Current service state and load balancer status
    ServiceList - Collection of services
    Operations - API operations for management

    ✨ Enhanced Documentation: This guide features professional syntax highlighting powered by Prism.js, matching the quality of industry-standard documentation. All examples use your exact Django models with realistic outputs.

    Official Docs: Service v1 API | API Version: v1

    Service is a named abstraction of software service consisting of local port that the proxy listens on, and the selector that determines which pods will answer requests sent through the proxy.

    Full Description

    Service is a named abstraction of software service (for example, mysql) consisting of local port (for example 3306) that the proxy listens on, and the selector that determines which pods will answer requests sent through the proxy. When developing your anihpj Django application, Services provide stable network endpoints for accessing your JobPost API, Skills processing, User authentication, Author content management, and Location services, regardless of pod IP changes or restarts.

    Services enable reliable communication between different components of your Django application and provide external access to your services through various service types (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName). This ensures your anihpj application components can discover and communicate with each other effectively.

    Syntax

    # Create a Service from YAML file
kubectl create -f service.yaml

# List all Services
kubectl get services

# Get detailed information about a specific Service
kubectl describe service {name}

# Get Service in YAML format
kubectl get service {name} -o yaml

# Delete a specific Service
kubectl delete service {name}

    YAML Structure

    apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj-jobpost
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
  type: ClusterIP

    Parameters

    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "v1" for Service
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "Service"
    • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata including name and labels
    • spec (ServiceSpec): Service specification defining behavior, ports, and selectors
    • status (ServiceStatus): Current service status, populated by the system (read-only)

    Return Value

    • Service object: Complete service specification with metadata and status
    • metadata: Object metadata including creation timestamp and resource version
    • spec: Service specification with ports, selectors, and service type configuration
    • status: Current service state including load balancer status when applicable

    5 Django Service Examples

    Example 1: JobPost API Service with ClusterIP

    Create ClusterIP Service for JobPost API endpoints accessible within cluster.

    # Create JobPost API Service
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-api
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost-api
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost-api
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
  - port: 443
    targetPort: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    name: https
  type: ClusterIP
EOF
    service/anihpj-jobpost-api created
    Example 2: User Authentication Service with NodePort

    Create NodePort Service for User authentication accessible externally.

    # Create User authentication Service
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-auth
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: user-auth
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: user-auth
  ports:
  - port: 8080
    targetPort: 8000
    nodePort: 30080
    protocol: TCP
    name: auth-http
  type: NodePort
EOF
    service/anihpj-user-auth created
    Example 3: Skills Processing LoadBalancer Service

    Create LoadBalancer Service for Skills processing with external access.

    # Create Skills processing LoadBalancer Service
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-lb
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: skills-processing
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: skills-processing
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
  type: LoadBalancer
  loadBalancerSourceRanges:
  - "10.0.0.0/8"
  - "192.168.0.0/16"
EOF
    service/anihpj-skills-lb created
    Example 4: Author Content Service with Session Affinity

    Create Service for Author content management with client IP session affinity.

    # Create Author content Service with session affinity
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-content
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: author-content
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: author-content
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: content-http
  sessionAffinity: ClientIP
  sessionAffinityConfig:
    clientIP:
      timeoutSeconds: 3600
  type: ClusterIP
EOF
    service/anihpj-author-content created
    Example 5: Location Service with ExternalName

    Create ExternalName Service for Location geospatial services pointing to external service.

    # Create Location ExternalName Service
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-external
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: location-geo
spec:
  type: ExternalName
  externalName: maps.googleapis.com
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
    name: https
EOF
    service/anihpj-location-external created

    ServiceSpec

    ServiceSpec describes the attributes that a user creates on a service. This specification defines how the service behaves, which pods it targets, what ports are exposed, and how traffic is distributed.

    Syntax
    # View ServiceSpec structure
kubectl get service {name} -o jsonpath='{.spec}'

# Validate ServiceSpec
kubectl apply --dry-run=client -f service.yaml
    Parameters
    • selector (map[string]string): Route service traffic to pods with matching label keys and values
    • ports ([]ServicePort, required): List of ports exposed by the service
    • type (string): Service type (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName)
    • clusterIP (string): Service IP address, usually assigned randomly
    • clusterIPs ([]string): List of IP addresses for dual-stack services
    • externalIPs ([]string): List of external IP addresses for the service
    • sessionAffinity (string): Session affinity type (ClientIP or None)
    • loadBalancerIP (string): IP address for LoadBalancer type (deprecated)
    • loadBalancerSourceRanges ([]string): Restrict traffic through load balancer
    • externalName (string): External reference for ExternalName type
    • externalTrafficPolicy (string): How nodes distribute external traffic (Local or Cluster)
    • internalTrafficPolicy (string): How nodes distribute internal traffic (Local or Cluster)
    Return Value
    • ServiceSpec object: Complete service specification
    • ports: Array of ServicePort objects defining exposed ports
    • selector: Label selector for targeting pods
    • sessionAffinityConfig: Session affinity configuration when applicable
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: JobPost API ServiceSpec with Multiple Ports

    ServiceSpec for JobPost API with HTTP and gRPC ports.

    # Create JobPost API with multiple ports
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-multi
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost-api
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
    appProtocol: http
  - port: 9090
    targetPort: 9000
    protocol: TCP
    name: grpc
    appProtocol: grpc
  - port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
    protocol: TCP
    name: metrics
  type: ClusterIP
EOF
    service/anihpj-jobpost-multi created
    Example 2: User Service with Traffic Policies

    ServiceSpec for User authentication with local traffic policy.

    # Create User service with traffic policies
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-local
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: user-auth
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    nodePort: 30800
    protocol: TCP
  type: NodePort
  externalTrafficPolicy: Local
  internalTrafficPolicy: Local
EOF
    service/anihpj-user-local created
    Example 3: Skills Service with Load Balancer Class

    ServiceSpec for Skills processing with specific load balancer implementation.

    # Create Skills service with load balancer class
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-advanced-lb
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: skills-processing
  ports:
  - port: 443
    targetPort: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    name: https
  type: LoadBalancer
  loadBalancerClass: "nginx"
  allocateLoadBalancerNodePorts: false
EOF
    service/anihpj-skills-advanced-lb created
    Example 4: Author Service with Health Check NodePort

    ServiceSpec for Author content with health check configuration.

    # Create Author service with health check
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-health
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: author-content
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: content
  type: LoadBalancer
  externalTrafficPolicy: Local
  healthCheckNodePort: 32000
EOF
    service/anihpj-author-health created
    Example 5: Location Service with Dual-Stack IP

    ServiceSpec for Location services with IPv4 and IPv6 support.

    # Create Location service with dual-stack
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-dual-stack
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: location-geo
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
    name: geo-http
  ipFamilyPolicy: PreferDualStack
  ipFamilies:
  - IPv4
  - IPv6
  type: ClusterIP
EOF
    service/anihpj-location-dual-stack created

    ServiceStatus

    ServiceStatus represents the current status of a service, including conditions and load balancer status when applicable.

    Syntax
    # View ServiceStatus
kubectl get service {name} -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Get service status with conditions
kubectl describe service {name}
    Parameters
    • conditions ([]Condition): Current service state conditions
    • loadBalancer (LoadBalancerStatus): Load balancer status when type is LoadBalancer
    Return Value
    • ServiceStatus object: Current service status information
    • conditions: Array of condition objects describing service state
    • loadBalancer: LoadBalancerStatus with ingress points when applicable
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Check JobPost Service Status

    View JobPost API service status and conditions.

    # Get JobPost service status
kubectl get service anihpj-jobpost-api -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq .
    { "loadBalancer": {} }
    Example 2: Check User LoadBalancer Service Status

    View User service LoadBalancer status with external IP.

    # Describe User LoadBalancer service
kubectl describe service anihpj-user-loadbalancer
    Name: anihpj-user-loadbalancer Namespace: default Labels: app=anihpj component=user-auth Selector: app=anihpj,component=user-auth Type: LoadBalancer IP Family Policy: SingleStack IP Families: IPv4 IP: 10.96.0.10 IPs: 10.96.0.10 LoadBalancer Ingress: 203.0.113.5 Port: auth-http 80/TCP TargetPort: 8000/TCP NodePort: auth-http 31234/TCP Endpoints: 192.168.1.10:8000,192.168.1.11:8000 Session Affinity: None External Traffic Policy: Cluster Events: Type Reason Age From Message ---- ------ ---- ---- ------- Normal EnsuringLoadBalancer 3m service-controller Ensuring load balancer Normal EnsuredLoadBalancer 2m service-controller Ensured load balancer
    Example 3: Monitor Skills Service Endpoints

    Check Skills processing service endpoints and ready status.

    # Get Skills service with endpoints
kubectl get service anihpj-skills-processing -o wide
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE SELECTOR anihpj-skills-processing ClusterIP 10.96.128.45 80/TCP 5m app=anihpj,component=skills
    Example 4: View Author Service Conditions

    Check Author content service conditions and health status.

    # Get Author service conditions
kubectl get service anihpj-author-content -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*]}' | jq .
    []
    Example 5: Location Service LoadBalancer Ingress

    View Location service LoadBalancer ingress details.

    # Get Location LoadBalancer ingress status
kubectl get service anihpj-location-lb -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0]}' | jq .
    { "ip": "198.51.100.10", "ports": [ { "port": 80, "protocol": "TCP" } ] }

    ServiceList

    ServiceList holds a list of services. This list format is returned when querying multiple services and provides a standardized way to handle collections of services in your Django application infrastructure.

    Syntax
    # List all Services
kubectl get services

# List Services with labels
kubectl get services -l app=anihpj

# Get ServiceList in JSON format
kubectl get services -o json
    Parameters
    • apiVersion (string, required): API version, always "v1" for ServiceList
    • kind (string, required): Resource type, always "ServiceList"
    • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata including resource version and continue token
    • items ([]Service, required): Array of Service objects
    Return Value
    • ServiceList object: Collection containing multiple Service resources
    • items array: List of individual Service objects with their specifications
    • metadata: List metadata for pagination and resource versioning
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All anihpj Application Services

    List all services related to the anihpj Django application.

    # List anihpj services
kubectl get services -l app=anihpj
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE anihpj-jobpost-api ClusterIP 10.96.0.45 80/TCP 10m anihpj-user-auth NodePort 10.96.0.67 8080:30080/TCP 8m anihpj-skills-lb LoadBalancer 10.96.0.89 203.0.113.10 80:31456/TCP 6m anihpj-author-content ClusterIP 10.96.0.123 80/TCP 4m anihpj-location-external ExternalName maps.googleapis.com 443/TCP 2m
    Example 2: List Services by Component

    Get services filtered by specific application component.

    # List JobPost component services
kubectl get services -l component=jobpost-api -o wide
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE SELECTOR anihpj-jobpost-api ClusterIP 10.96.0.45 80/TCP 10m app=anihpj,component=jobpost-api anihpj-jobpost-multi ClusterIP 10.96.0.156 80/TCP,9090/TCP,8080/TCP 5m app=anihpj,component=jobpost-api
    Example 3: List LoadBalancer Services

    Get all LoadBalancer type services with external access.

    # List LoadBalancer services
kubectl get services --field-selector spec.type=LoadBalancer
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE anihpj-skills-lb LoadBalancer 10.96.0.89 203.0.113.10 80:31456/TCP 6m anihpj-skills-advanced-lb LoadBalancer 10.96.0.201 203.0.113.20 443:32001/TCP 3m anihpj-author-health LoadBalancer 10.96.0.234 203.0.113.30 80:32000/TCP 2m
    Example 4: List Services in JSON Format

    Get services in JSON format for programmatic processing.

    # Get services JSON and extract names
kubectl get services -l app=anihpj -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
    "anihpj-jobpost-api" "anihpj-user-auth" "anihpj-skills-lb" "anihpj-author-content" "anihpj-location-external"
    Example 5: List Services with Custom Columns

    Display services with custom column output showing specific information.

    # List services with custom columns
kubectl get services -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,TYPE:.spec.type,CLUSTER-IP:.spec.clusterIP,EXTERNAL-IP:.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ip
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP anihpj-jobpost-api ClusterIP 10.96.0.45 anihpj-user-auth NodePort 10.96.0.67 anihpj-skills-lb LoadBalancer 10.96.0.89 203.0.113.10 anihpj-author-content ClusterIP 10.96.0.123 anihpj-location-external ExternalName

    Operations

    Operations Overview

    Read - GET single Service details
    Status - GET Service status information
    List - GET multiple Services (namespaced)
    List All - GET multiple Services (cluster-wide)
    Create - POST new Service
    Update - PUT existing Service
    Update Status - PUT Service status subresource
    Patch - PATCH Service fields
    Patch Status - PATCH Service status
    Delete - DELETE Service resource
    Delete Collection - DELETE multiple Services

    Operations allow you to interact with Service resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to manage Django application network access, expose services, and monitor connectivity status.

    Read Service

    Read the specified Service to get complete network configuration and endpoint information for your Django application services.

    Syntax
    # Get Service details in YAML format
kubectl get service {name} -o yaml

# Get Service details in JSON format  
kubectl get service {name} -o json

# Get detailed Service description
kubectl describe service {name}
    HTTP API Syntax
    GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/services/{name}
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the Service
    • namespace (string, required): Object namespace (default: "default")
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (Service): OK - Returns the complete Service object
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Read JobPost API Service Configuration

    Get detailed JobPost API service configuration for the main job posting endpoints in the anihpj Django project.

    # Get JobPost API service details
kubectl get service anihpj-jobpost-api -o yaml
    apiVersion: v1 kind: Service metadata: name: anihpj-jobpost-api namespace: default labels: app: anihpj component: jobpost-api spec: ports: - name: http port: 80 protocol: TCP targetPort: 8000 selector: app: anihpj component: jobpost-api sessionAffinity: None type: ClusterIP status: loadBalancer: {}
    Example 2: Read User Authentication Service Details

    Get User authentication service configuration for Django user management and login endpoints.

    # Describe User authentication service
kubectl describe service anihpj-user-auth
    Name: anihpj-user-auth Namespace: default Labels: app=anihpj component=user-auth Selector: app=anihpj,component=user-auth Type: NodePort IP Family Policy: SingleStack IP Families: IPv4 IP: 10.96.0.67 IPs: 10.96.0.67 Port: auth-http 8080/TCP TargetPort: 8000/TCP NodePort: auth-http 30080/TCP Endpoints: 192.168.1.15:8000,192.168.1.16:8000 Session Affinity: None Events:
    Example 3: Read Skills Processing LoadBalancer Service

    Get Skills processing service LoadBalancer configuration for external job skill matching API access.

    # Get Skills LoadBalancer service in JSON
kubectl get service anihpj-skills-lb -o json | jq '{name: .metadata.name, type: .spec.type, clusterIP: .spec.clusterIP, loadBalancer: .status.loadBalancer}'
    { "name": "anihpj-skills-lb", "type": "LoadBalancer", "clusterIP": "10.96.0.89", "loadBalancer": { "ingress": [ { "ip": "203.0.113.10" } ] } }
    Example 4: Read Author Content Service with Session Affinity

    Get Author content management service configuration with ClientIP session affinity for consistent content editing sessions.

    # Get Author service session affinity configuration
kubectl get service anihpj-author-content -o jsonpath='{.spec.sessionAffinity}{"\n"}{.spec.sessionAffinityConfig}'
    ClientIP {"clientIP":{"timeoutSeconds":3600}}
    Example 5: Read Location ExternalName Service

    Get Location geospatial service ExternalName configuration pointing to external mapping API for location-based job searches.

    # Get Location ExternalName service external reference
kubectl get service anihpj-location-external -o jsonpath='{.spec.type}{"\n"}{.spec.externalName}'
    ExternalName maps.googleapis.com

    Read Service Status

    Read the status of the specified Service to get load balancer status and service health information for your Django application services.

    Syntax
    # Get Service status information
kubectl get service {name} -o jsonpath='{.status}'

# Get Service status in YAML format
kubectl get service {name} -o yaml | grep -A 20 "status:"

# Describe Service to see status and events
kubectl describe service {name}
    HTTP API Syntax
    GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/services/{name}/status
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
    Parameters
    • name (string, required): Name of the Service
    • namespace (string, required): Object namespace (default: "default")
    • pretty (string): Pretty-print the output
    Return Value
    • 200 (Service): OK - Returns the Service object with status
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Check JobPost LoadBalancer Status

    Monitor JobPost API LoadBalancer service status to verify external IP allocation for job posting endpoints.

    # Check JobPost LoadBalancer status
kubectl get service anihpj-jobpost-lb -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer}' | jq .
    { "ingress": [ { "ip": "203.0.113.25" } ] }
    Example 2: Monitor User Service Load Balancer Ingress

    Check User authentication service LoadBalancer ingress status for external user login access.

    # Monitor User service LoadBalancer ingress
kubectl describe service anihpj-user-lb | grep -A 5 "LoadBalancer Ingress"
    LoadBalancer Ingress: 203.0.113.30 Port: auth-http 80/TCP TargetPort: 8000/TCP NodePort: auth-http 32145/TCP Endpoints: 192.168.1.20:8000,192.168.1.21:8000
    Example 3: Check Skills Service Conditions

    Verify Skills processing service status conditions for skill matching API health monitoring.

    # Check Skills service status conditions
kubectl get service anihpj-skills-processing -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*]}' | jq .
    []
    Example 4: Monitor Author Service Status

    Monitor Author content management service status including endpoint readiness for Django CMS functionality.

    # Get Author service complete status
kubectl get service anihpj-author-content -o yaml | grep -A 10 "status:"
    status: loadBalancer: {}
    Example 5: Check Location Service Load Balancer Ports

    Verify Location geospatial service LoadBalancer port status for mapping API connectivity.

    # Check Location service LoadBalancer port status
kubectl get service anihpj-location-lb -o jsonpath='{.status.loadBalancer.ingress[0].ports[*]}' | jq .
    { "port": 80, "protocol": "TCP" }

    List Services

    List or watch objects of kind Service in a specific namespace to monitor all Django application network services.

    Syntax
    # List all Services in current namespace
kubectl get services

# List Services with labels
kubectl get services -l app=anihpj

# Watch Services for changes
kubectl get services --watch
    HTTP API Syntax
    GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/services
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
    Parameters
    • namespace (string, required): Object namespace (default: "default")
    • labelSelector (string): Filter services by labels
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter services by fields
    • watch (boolean): Watch for changes
    • limit (integer): Maximum number of results
    Return Value
    • 200 (ServiceList): OK - Returns the ServiceList object
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All anihpj Application Services

    List all services for the anihpj Django application to monitor network configurations.

    # List anihpj application services
kubectl get services -l app=anihpj
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE anihpj-jobpost-api ClusterIP 10.96.0.45 80/TCP 2h anihpj-user-auth NodePort 10.96.0.67 8080:30080/TCP 1h anihpj-skills-lb LoadBalancer 10.96.0.89 203.0.113.10 80:31456/TCP 45m anihpj-author-content ClusterIP 10.96.0.123 80/TCP 30m anihpj-location-geo LoadBalancer 10.96.0.156 203.0.113.20 80:32001/TCP 15m
    Example 2: List Services by Component Type

    Filter services by component to monitor specific Django application functionality.

    # List services by component
kubectl get services -l component=jobpost-api,user-auth
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE anihpj-jobpost-api ClusterIP 10.96.0.45 80/TCP 2h anihpj-user-auth NodePort 10.96.0.67 8080:30080/TCP 1h
    Example 3: List LoadBalancer Services

    List all LoadBalancer type services for external access monitoring.

    # List LoadBalancer services
kubectl get services --field-selector spec.type=LoadBalancer
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE anihpj-skills-lb LoadBalancer 10.96.0.89 203.0.113.10 80:31456/TCP 45m anihpj-location-geo LoadBalancer 10.96.0.156 203.0.113.20 80:32001/TCP 15m
    Example 4: Watch Services for Changes

    Monitor Skills processing services for real-time changes during deployments.

    # Watch Skills services for changes
kubectl get services -l component=skills-processing --watch
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE anihpj-skills-lb LoadBalancer 10.96.0.89 203.0.113.10 80:31456/TCP 45m anihpj-skills-internal ClusterIP 10.96.0.234 80/TCP 10m
    Example 5: List Services with Custom Output

    List Author and Location services with custom column output for reporting.

    # List services with custom columns
kubectl get services -l component=author-content,location-geo -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,TYPE:.spec.type,CLUSTER-IP:.spec.clusterIP
    NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP anihpj-author-content ClusterIP 10.96.0.123 anihpj-location-geo LoadBalancer 10.96.0.156

    List Services (All Namespaces)

    List or watch Services across all namespaces to get a cluster-wide view of Django application services.

    Syntax
    # List Services across all namespaces
kubectl get services --all-namespaces

# List Services across all namespaces with labels
kubectl get services --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj

# Watch Services across all namespaces
kubectl get services --all-namespaces --watch
    HTTP API Syntax
    GET /api/v1/services
Accept: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
    Parameters
    • labelSelector (string): Filter services by labels
    • fieldSelector (string): Filter services by fields
    • watch (boolean): Watch for changes
    • limit (integer): Maximum number of results
    • continue (string): Continue token for pagination
    Return Value
    • 200 (ServiceList): OK - Returns the ServiceList object across all namespaces
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: List All anihpj Services Cluster-Wide

    Get cluster-wide view of all anihpj Django application services across environments.

    # List anihpj services across all namespaces
kubectl get services --all-namespaces -l app=anihpj
    NAMESPACE NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE default anihpj-jobpost-api ClusterIP 10.96.0.45 80/TCP 2h production anihpj-jobpost-prod LoadBalancer 10.96.1.45 203.0.113.50 80:30080/TCP 1d staging anihpj-jobpost-staging ClusterIP 10.96.2.45 80/TCP 12h development anihpj-jobpost-dev NodePort 10.96.3.45 80:32000/TCP 6h
    Example 2: List LoadBalancer Services Cluster-Wide

    Monitor all LoadBalancer services across namespaces for external access management.

    # List all LoadBalancer services cluster-wide
kubectl get services --all-namespaces --field-selector spec.type=LoadBalancer
    NAMESPACE NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE production anihpj-jobpost-prod LoadBalancer 10.96.1.45 203.0.113.50 80:30080/TCP 1d production anihpj-skills-prod LoadBalancer 10.96.1.89 203.0.113.51 80:30081/TCP 1d staging anihpj-user-staging LoadBalancer 10.96.2.67 203.0.113.52 80:30082/TCP 12h
    Example 3: Monitor User Services Across Environments

    Track User authentication services across development, staging, and production environments.

    # List User services across all namespaces
kubectl get services --all-namespaces -l component=user-auth
    NAMESPACE NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE production anihpj-user-prod LoadBalancer 10.96.1.67 203.0.113.60 80:30090/TCP 1d staging anihpj-user-staging LoadBalancer 10.96.2.67 203.0.113.61 80:30091/TCP 12h development anihpj-user-dev NodePort 10.96.3.67 80:32010/TCP 6h
    Example 4: Watch Skills Services Across Namespaces

    Monitor Skills processing services for changes across all environments in real-time.

    # Watch Skills services across all namespaces
kubectl get services --all-namespaces -l component=skills-processing --watch
    NAMESPACE NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE production anihpj-skills-prod LoadBalancer 10.96.1.89 203.0.113.70 80:30100/TCP 1d staging anihpj-skills-staging ClusterIP 10.96.2.89 80/TCP 12h
    Example 5: List Services with Namespace and Labels

    Get comprehensive view of Author and Location services with namespace information.

    # List Author and Location services with namespace details
kubectl get services --all-namespaces -l component=in=author-content,location-geo -o wide
    NAMESPACE NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE SELECTOR production anihpj-author-prod ClusterIP 10.96.1.123 80/TCP 1d app=anihpj,component=author-content staging anihpj-location-staging ClusterIP 10.96.2.156 80/TCP 12h app=anihpj,component=location-geo

    Create Service

    Create a new Service from specification to expose Django application pods and enable network access to your anihpj services.

    Syntax
    # Create Service from YAML file
kubectl create -f service.yaml

# Apply Service configuration
kubectl apply -f service.yaml

# Create Service using kubectl expose command
kubectl expose deployment {deployment-name} --type=LoadBalancer --port=80
    HTTP API Syntax
    POST /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/services
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {token}
    Parameters
    • namespace (string, required): Object namespace (default: "default")
    • body (Service, required): Service specification object
    • dryRun (string): Validation mode without creation
    • fieldManager (string): Field management identifier
    Return Value
    • 200 (Service): OK - Service successfully created
    • 201 (Service): Created - Service created
    • 202 (Service): Accepted - Creation in progress
    • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required
    5 Django Service Examples
    Example 1: Create JobPost API ClusterIP Service

    Create internal ClusterIP service for JobPost API endpoints accessible within the Kubernetes cluster for the anihpj Django application.

    # Create JobPost API ClusterIP service
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-internal
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost-api
    environment: production
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: jobpost-api
  ports:
  - name: http
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
    protocol: TCP
  - name: admin
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
    protocol: TCP
  type: ClusterIP
EOF
    service/anihpj-jobpost-internal created
    Example 2: Create User Authentication NodePort Service

    Create NodePort service for User authentication endpoints with external access for Django admin and user login functionality.

    # Create User authentication NodePort service
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-nodeport
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    component: user-auth
spec:
  selector:
    app: anihpj
    component: user-auth
  ports:
  - name: auth-http
    port: 8000
    targetPort: 8000
    nodePort: 30800
    protocol: TCP
  type: NodePort
  externalTrafficPolicy: Local
EOF
    service/anihpj-user-nodeport created
    Example 3: Create Skills Processing LoadBalancer Service

    Create LoadBalancer service for Skills processing API with external load balancer for public skill matching and search endpoints.

    # Create Skills LoadBalancer service with source IP restrictions
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
